title: The ASI Stack
subtitle: A Governed Systems Architecture for Advanced AI, with ASI as the Stress Case
author: Corben Sorenson

parts.title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
parts.part_job: Establish the first-principles frame: ASI as a governed stack, the efficiency hypothesis, the failure model, the evidence discipline, and the constitutional/governance substrate that bounds every later layer.

chapters.title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
chapters.problem: The book needs a single architecture frame for advanced AI systems that must plan, remember, verify, act, route work, compress representations, and improve under governance.
chapters.insufficient: A larger model, a prompt wrapper, or an agent loop does not by itself define authority boundaries, memory discipline, evidence ledgers, tool permissions, or safe replacement rules.
chapters.core_claim: Efficient ASI should be modeled as a governed stack of cooperating layers rather than as one undifferentiated model.

claim_source_mappings.limits: Architecture proposal and systems framework only; does not prove a completed deployment, benchmark result, or end-to-end ASI stack in this repository.

mechanism: Define each layer by responsibility, interface, artifact, invariant, and failure mode.
Frame the raw LLM as a semantic-compression and generation component inside a larger governed cognitive system.
Treat the whole book as a reference architecture rather than a collection of standalone papers.
Use source queues and evidence states to keep future writing runs context-loaded and honest.
interfaces: Alignment and governance constrain every downstream layer.
Planning, memory, reasoning, execution, routing, compression, and evidence should exchange typed artifacts.
Recursive improvement is a governed transition, not an ambient property of intelligence.
invariants: Layer boundaries remain explicit.
Claims carry support states.
Reasoning ability never implies execution authority.
failure_modes: Anthology drift.
Monolith drift.
Evidence inflation before source notes or tests exist.
chapters.minimal_implementation: A stack map, layer-boundary table, source crosswalk, and claim ledger that make the architecture navigable before prose is complete.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The beyond-SOTA endpoint is not a larger model, a more elaborate agent loop, or a compound-AI toolkit with a nicer diagram. A mature ASI stack absorbs what scale-first systems, agent loops, compound architectures, cognitive-architecture lineages, and layered-control systems get right while adding the missing contracts: authority ceilings, typed artifacts, evidence states, support-state transitions, replacement transactions, non-claims, rollback paths, and governed recursive-improvement boundaries.

proof_targets.target: Every accepted material effect in the finite boundary transition model requires a live same-epoch grant within the caller ceiling and a prior dispatch receipt; the source-anchored nominal path reaches independent observation and exact local rollback.
The finite boundary model rejects over-ceiling authorization, effect without a dispatch receipt, and post-revocation effect.
An independently implemented generated suite matches all eighteen priority-ordered layer-contract admission routes after the theorem-per-record normalizations are retired from the live proof surface.

chapters.title: The Efficient ASI Hypothesis
chapters.problem: The architecture needs a non-scale-only theory for how useful capability can increase without spending maximal cognition on every subtask.
chapters.insufficient: A scale-only allocation policy cannot, without additional state, select among routes using task-specific quality predicates, authority ceilings, total costs, residuals, or fallback readiness.
chapters.core_claim: On repeated workloads with multiple authorized routes, selecting the lowest-cost route that satisfies a fixed quality predicate and compiling reusable work can improve useful-task success per total contract cost over always-maximal and always-cheapest policies, provided authority, verification, residual, and fallback obligations remain intact.

mechanism: Select the lowest-cost authorized route in the declared candidate set that satisfies the fixed quality predicate; otherwise use the recorded fallback.
Compile verified repeated work into versioned artifacts, tools, procedural memory, specialist routes, or regression tests, and charge their maintenance and revalidation costs.
Use the seed/router/search/generator/verifier/residual loop to record candidate generation, selection, verification, reuse, and unresolved work under memory and governance contracts.
Attach incomplete routing or compression obligations and their repair cost to the route as residuals.
interfaces: Planning emits a task contract, fixed quality predicate, authority ceiling, and capability-profile request.
Routing returns the declared candidates, selected route, rejected lower-cost routes, and fallback state.
Evidence returns an efficiency-admission decision and support-state effect after joint quality, total-cost, authority, and residual tests.
invariants: Every efficiency claim names a fixed quality predicate and total contract cost.
Every compression claim records downstream utility checks, repair cost, and residual obligations.
Every admitted cheap or compressed route records a tested fallback or an explicit fallback-unavailable state.
Context construction, verification, repair, human review, regression maintenance, rollback, and residual costs remain attributed to the route that caused them.
No route may be classified as efficient by bypassing authority, approval, verification, or rollback gates.
failure_modes: Cheap inference mistaken for intelligence.
Critical context compressed away.
Benchmark gains that increase hidden residual burden.
chapters.minimal_implementation: A costed route ledger with quality predicates, residual accounting, and fallback criteria.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature endpoint is a governed route economy for cognition, not a larger model, a cheaper model, or a single acceleration trick. Conditional-computation systems, model cascades, compression schemes, fast-generation methods, and broad benchmark suites each attack one cost surface; the Efficient ASI endpoint makes those surfaces comparable through a shared route ledger for task predicates, authority ceilings, selected and rejected candidates, visible cost classes, verification, fallback, residuals, and support-state effects.

proof_targets.target: A route is minimum viable only when no lower-cost authorized route satisfies the required quality predicate.
A routed or compressed result with open obligations cannot be promoted without a residual record.

Modeled efficiency-claim admission routes missing task contracts, quality predicates, selected routes, candidate sets, lower-cost comparisons, cost ledgers, complete visible costs, verification results, failed quality, authority bypass, residual gaps, fallback gaps, hidden-cost audit gaps, benchmark or trace gaps, negative-control gaps, evidence-transition gaps, and non-claim-boundary gaps to explicit outcomes.

The synthetic efficiency route-search probe summary records two valid traces, six expected-invalid controls, fourteen checked candidate routes, minimum verified route selection, rejection of cheap failed quality, hidden residual, authority bypass, and compression-utility overclaim controls, hidden-cost class audit coverage, and no route-search-completeness, measured-efficiency, or support-state-promotion claim.

chapters.title: System Boundaries and Authority
chapters.problem: To test whether cross-layer effects are authorized, the stack needs explicit identities for the principal, execution domain, operation, target, permission class, ceiling, grant lifecycle, delegation, and receipt.
chapters.insufficient: Role labels, prompts, and ambient process identity do not by themselves distinguish read, transform, disclose, write, execute, and approve authority or enforce delegation, expiry, revocation, and cross-domain approval.
chapters.core_claim: External-effect authority should be represented as a versioned, revocable tuple binding principal, execution domain, operation, target, permission class, scope, ceiling, grant state, delegation, expiry or revocation epoch, and receipt obligations; capability, context access, route quality, or ambient process power alone confers none of it.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports authority boundaries through structured command fields, constraints, verification, failure behavior, artifact graphs, runtime adapters, and ledgers that separate intent, work, evidence, and execution.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not prove a deployed VIEA authority system or runtime enforcement in this repository.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for structured command contracts, explicit constraints, verification/failure behavior, bounded specialists, permission envelopes, provenance, runtime targets, approval/refusal policies, and acceptance through permissions and feedback; no deployed authority system or runtime enforcement result is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish production safety, global alignment, or independently validated route/evaluator behavior.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for stable substitution boundaries, exact authorization identity, caller-bound authority, grants/leases, brokered tool and effect mediation, route validation, lifecycle authorization, confused-deputy limits, and conditional safety boundaries; no production safety, global alignment, or independently validated route/evaluator behavior is promoted.

mechanism: Define principals, authorities, ceilings, grants, revocations, and handoff contracts.
Separate knowledge access from action authority.
Represent missing authority as a detectable failure rather than implicit permission.
Bind principal, execution domain, operation, target, permission class, scope, budget account, trace, replay identity, grant, policy version, revocation epoch, and expiry in one authority tuple.
Name execution-domain, budget, trace, and revocation owners and require target-owner approval for cross-domain handoffs.
Keep protocol-security controls separate from hardware-root evidence and claims.
Classify every requested permission as read, transform, disclose, write, execute, or approve and reject implicit promotion between classes.
interfaces: Governance issues versioned ceilings and grants with explicit issuer, scope, expiry, and revocation authority.
Execution consumes the bound authority tuple before an effect and emits an effect or denial receipt afterward.
Evidence consumes authority receipts and records missing, inconsistent, expired, revoked, over-ceiling, or cross-domain failures.
invariants: Authority never expands silently.
Read permission is not write permission.
Tool execution requires an explicit grant.
Delegated authority preserves grant, budget, trace, replay, and revocation identity while narrowing scope.
Cross-domain authority requires target-domain owner approval.
Protocol security does not establish a hardware root of trust.
Approval authority remains separate from execution authority and cannot be self-issued by the executor.
Grant expiry, revocation, delegation depth, and denied escalation remain visible to every downstream authority consumer.
failure_modes: Authority creep.
Confused-deputy tool calls.
Memory access treated as action approval.
Authority identity forks across budget, trace, replay, or revocation.
Cross-domain authority laundering or revoked-epoch replay.
Protocol-security controls promoted into hardware-root claims.
Expired or revoked grants remain usable in downstream or delegated contexts.
Replacement implementations inherit handles qualified only for a predecessor implementation.
chapters.minimal_implementation: An authority-transition schema plus a unified authority-tuple lifecycle that binds execution-domain ownership, scope, budget, trace, replay, grant, revocation, cross-domain approval, receipts, and protocol-versus-hardware-root boundaries.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: System authority needs a type system for the stack. Every model, tool, memory cell, field, artifact, human, project, and runtime route would carry explicit capability bounds so the architecture can distinguish what a component can do from what it may do. In the mature authority architecture, authority becomes typed, bounded, revocable, and attached to concrete principals, layers, fields, tools, artifacts, and routes.

chapters.title: Failure Modes of Ungoverned Intelligence
chapters.problem: Governance, reliability, and self-improvement claims cannot be tested unless their failure conditions, observable boundary events, evidence records, and owners are declared prospectively.
chapters.insufficient: Labels such as alignment failure or unsafe autonomy do not by themselves identify which contract failed, what observable event occurred, which detector fired, whether an effect escaped, or who owns containment and residuals.
chapters.core_claim: A stack-level failure model should represent each named risk as a distinct boundary event with a trigger, protected invariant, detector or observer, receipt, owner, containment action, residual, recurrence state, and escalation or learning path; a taxonomy entry alone establishes neither occurrence nor mitigation.

mechanism: Classify each admitted failure record by versioned failure class, layer, affected contract, and boundary-event state without forcing unmapped observations into a known class.
Map each named failure to a protected invariant, detector or observer, receipt, containment owner, residual, and planned falsification test.
Add or revise architectural boundaries only from accepted failure, near-miss, escape, or detector-gap records with versioned lineage.
interfaces: Detectors emit versioned failure receipts against an exact Failure Boundary Map entry or an explicit unmapped-event state.
Containment owners accept or reject each receipt and record containment, escape, rollback, residual, and transfer-of-ownership decisions.
Evidence preserves blocked failures, realized failures, near misses, detector gaps, negative results, and support-state effects without relabeling them as success.
Review and learning consume recurrence and escape records and update the taxonomy or boundary only through a versioned change with regression obligations.
invariants: Every admitted taxonomy row names at least one owning boundary, protected invariant, observer, containment owner, and residual route.
A component under judgment cannot modify the evaluator, policy, data, or threshold used for its own disposition without independent versioned approval.
Blocked failures, realized failures, near misses, detector gaps, denials, and residuals remain visible and cannot be relabeled or deleted by a narrower success metric.
Repeated failure cannot become accepted behavior merely through missing receipts, operator habituation, or routing around the detector.
Failure ownership transfers only when the receiving owner explicitly accepts the receipt, containment duty, and residual.
Taxonomy entries, detectors, and containment paths are version-bound and must be revalidated after material architecture or policy change.
failure_modes: Evaluator capture.
Memory poisoning.
Tool/action overreach.
Compression that hides residual complexity.
Goal misbinding hidden by competent behavior.
Authority creep or expanded option control normalized as ordinary operation.
Hidden optimization pressure or learned-objective mismatch escapes the declared objective boundary.
Failure laundering deletes or relabels denials, negative results, or residuals after a narrower metric improves.
Repeated near misses normalize because recurrence does not trigger review or containment escalation.
Taxonomy or containment paths become stale after architecture, policy, evaluator, or route changes.
chapters.minimal_implementation: A Failure Boundary Map schema plus an executable receipt suite covering blocked and realized events, recurrence escalation, containment, residuals, detector gaps, and rejecting negative controls.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature failure radar continuously binds observed boundary events to a versioned taxonomy, independently implemented detectors, containment and recovery decisions, recurrence escalation, residual custody, and learning updates, while measuring missed detection, false alarm, escape, time to contain, and mitigation failure across architecture and policy changes, and recording independently labeled ground truth, explicit abstention or unmapped states, useful-throughput loss, operator burden, and governance cost before any cross-version mitigation claim can survive revalidation.

chapters.title: Evidence States and Claim Discipline
chapters.problem: A living technical book needs a versioned claim lifecycle that separates what an assertion says from what evidence currently supports it and preserves why each state change was accepted.
chapters.insufficient: Citations, passing validators, proof objects, benchmark scores, and polished prose do not by themselves identify the exact claim atom, evidence role, scope, independence, causal link, negative result, or accepted transition they justify.
chapters.core_claim: Each material claim should have a stable identity, versioned text and scope, claim label, support state, non-aggregating evidence-quality vector, and transition ledger; upward movement is allowed only through an accepted claim-specific transition whose artifacts and evidence roles meet declared gates, while contradiction, failed verification, missing support, and scope mismatch remain eligible to narrow, downgrade, refute, or deprecate the claim.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Benchmark lifecycle, ledgers, anti-Goodhart safeguards, wall diagnosis, regression duties, and residual accounting support the rule that benchmark evidence must be scoped before it moves a claim.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not prove any benchmark result in this repository or justify treating a benchmark score as permanent intelligence evidence.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for benchmark ratchet, anti-Goodhart, wall diagnosis, regression-guard, and residual-report boundaries; no local benchmark result is claimed.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Proof/citation/procedure-carrying claim tiers, belief revision, contradiction handling, and downgrade/block behavior support explicit claim labels, support states, and failed-verification demotion.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not prove open-domain autoformalization, theorem validity for arbitrary claims, or whole-system epistemic correctness.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for proof-carrying graph, tier labels, bounded belief revision, contradiction handling, and untagged-assertion prohibition; no open-domain verifier claim is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Artifact graphs, claim and verification ledgers, residuals, feedback, benchmarks, and the rule of durability support keeping claims, evidence, failures, and regression coverage as durable records.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not prove a completed VIEA deployment or any benchmark/runtime result in this repository.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for durable artifacts, claim/verification ledger, support states, residuals, artifact relationships, and benchmark/regression coverage; no deployment claim is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Retrieval-bounded dossiers, proposition states, adversarial review, SME checkpoints, and final sign-off support the distinction between verified, inferred, unsupported, and review-required propositions.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not prove the UAT protocol discovers novel truth or that its thresholds are validated here.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for pre-audited drafts, proposition verification tiers, SME review handoff, final sign-off, and source-review mitigation; no autonomous truth-machine claim is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Verified Epistemic Units, verification supply chains, contestability, fork, exit, and audit language support structured claim/evidence objects and governance-visible review paths.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Speculative economic or epistemic-liquidity framing is not treated as implemented economics or external corroboration.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed source note only for Coherence Exchange context: Verified Epistemic Units, verification supply chains, intelligence arbitrage, fork/exit/audit, contestability, value/accounting, and governance-interface framing; speculative economics, institutional design, security, and implementation claims are not promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: The distinction between context length and verification workspace supports the rule that source ingestion or large context does not itself verify a claim without adequacy, contradiction, and coverage checks.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not prove the proposed verification-bandwidth tests or contradiction-rate claims have been run here.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for context-window/storage distinction, effective verification workspace, pairwise constraint checking, probabilistic loss under compression, and proposed falsifiability test; no contradiction-rate experiment is claimed.

claim_source_mappings.limits: One private local implementation lineage only; expensive training and benchmarks were not reproduced and no capability or support-state claim is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.limits: Project-reported results and contract presence are not reproduced capability evidence, independent replication, or deployed enforcement.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Motivates separating finite record-level guarantees from claims about the complete embedded agent, environment, self-model, or future descendants.
claim_source_mappings.limits: The paper is an informal obstacle survey, not a solved theory; the ASI Stack's finite records and proofs do not solve logical uncertainty, self-reference, robust delegation, subsystem alignment, or open-world embedded agency.

claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Primary-source passages were reviewed for the stated comparator contribution and limits; no local implementation, reproduction, safety result, or support-state promotion is inferred.

mechanism: Project canonical claim records and accepted transition lineage into Appendix C as the public claim ledger without treating that projection as evidence.

Represent claim label, support state, lifecycle state, and terminal disposition as distinct fields so assertion kind, current backing, review status, and outcome cannot collapse into one confidence word.
Permit source-derived movement only from a passage-reviewed, exact claim mapping that records the source's evidence role, interpretation boundary, contradictions, and non-claims.

Permit test-backed movement only from a claim-specific evidence bundle that records command, environment, artifacts, result, evaluator identity and independence, negative or inconclusive results, limitations, and reproduction boundary.

Classify evidence by origin, method, independence, reproducibility, recency, coverage, adversarial strength, validity, artifact access, demonstrated effect, causal completeness, and transfer distance in a non-aggregating evidence cell.
Require a same-lineage causal capability chain from data, objective, and code through actually updated state, runtime output, independent evaluation, and exact claim binding.
Separate generated, frozen, pretrained, trainable, optimizer-only, and actually updated state, and bind checkpoint/request/decode/tool substitutions to the final response.
Represent absent evidence as unknown, not attempted, not required, or explicitly waived rather than silently passed.
Record component ownership separately from task-relevant capability evidence.
Record provenance assertions separately from evidence truth, completeness, integrity, authority, and receipt faithfulness.
interfaces: Drafting proposes versioned claim-text, scope, label, and dependency changes without creating a support-state effect.

Source ingestion, proofs, experiments, interpretability analyses, reproduction, and review emit evidence packets with exact roles, provenance, independence, negative evidence, limitations, and claim bindings.
A named transition authority accepts, rejects, narrows, downgrades, refutes, or deprecates a proposed change and records blockers, residuals, supersession lineage, and the changelog reference.
Appendix C, chapter markers, status surfaces, release artifacts, and reader derivatives consume the accepted canonical claim state and cannot strengthen it independently.
invariants: No claim may cite source support or a test result whose referenced artifact and exact claim binding do not exist.
No reported command, proof, experiment, reproduction, review, or result may be fabricated, silently substituted, or assigned a stronger evidence role than the observed artifact supports.
Negative, null, inconclusive, failed, blocked, and contradictory results remain visible with their original scope and lineage.
A capability claim cannot splice causal-chain links across incompatible runs or lineages.
First-party ownership, interface presence, and trainable declarations cannot substitute for an observed task-relevant effect.
Claim wording or scope cannot strengthen without an accepted transition or an explicit reset to a support state that the retained evidence justifies.
No evidence-quality dimension may compensate automatically for a failed hard gate or missing dimension.
Finite record, proof, provenance, or verifier completeness cannot imply complete embedded-world, whole-agent, descendant, or open-world correctness.
failure_modes: Support-state inflation.
Citation laundering.
Silent removal of failed claims.
Ownership laundering.
Causal-chain splicing.
Missingness serialized as passed evidence.
Benchmark laundering generalizes a score beyond its frozen workload, evaluator, contamination, uncertainty, and transfer boundary.
Proof laundering expands a finite theorem beyond its assumptions, modeled state, refinement bridge, or exercised consumer.
Interpretability laundering turns a scoped internal-model artifact into unsupported behavioral, capability, safety, or deployment evidence.
Evidence smoothing improves prose by removing caveats, failures, or residuals while leaving the apparent claim strength unchanged or higher.
Provenance laundering treats asserted derivation, attribution, or conformance as evidence truth, completeness, integrity, authority, or effect.
Independence laundering labels self-review, shared-model review, or shared-data review as independent without recording the shared failure surface.
chapters.minimal_implementation: A canonical claim record, evidence transition record, non-aggregating evidence cell, public Appendix C projection, one bounded six-project lineage cell, and executable positive and rejecting suites for missing bindings, absent evidence, negative-result deletion, dimension aggregation, scope strengthening, lineage splicing, false independence, stale projections, and support-state laundering, with no chapter-core support movement.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature claim-accounting system maintains a versioned graph of material claims, scopes, dependencies, evidence packets, contradictions, transitions, terminal dispositions, and public projections; independently governed transition consumers enforce claim-specific promotion and asymmetric downgrade gates, preserve non-aggregating evidence-quality dimensions and negative results, challenge receipt faithfulness and shared evaluator failures, revalidate affected claims after model, policy, ontology, source, or artifact change, and measure adjudication accuracy, time, false promotion, false downgrade, stale-state escape, reproduction burden, reader traceability, and governance cost without equating ledger completeness with truth.

proof_targets.target: Support-state transitions require the corresponding evidence artifact.
A claim cannot be promoted when required evidence is absent.

A modeled support-state transition routes no-change requests, missing records, scope gaps, support-effect gaps, missing review, missing required evidence, terminal/downgrade gaps, changelog gaps, and missing non-claim boundaries to explicit outcomes.

A synthetic evidence-bundle completeness probe records a no-change bundle, a blocked-promotion bundle, rejected negative controls, changelog consistency, no support-state effect, and non-claim boundaries.

A claim-ledger completeness audit records manifest claim coverage, unique Appendix C rows, label/support matching, open gaps, promotion paths, rejected mutation controls, no support-state effect, and non-claim boundaries.

An accepted-transition review audit records accepted transition presence, bounded non-core upward transitions, no chapter-core promotion, no-promotion decisions, changelog refs, rejected mutation controls, bounded support-state effects, and non-claim boundaries.

A synthetic claim-state transition bridge records claim narrowing, support downgrade, terminal refutation, negative-evidence requirements, rejected mutation controls, bounded support-state effects, no live claim movement, and non-claim boundaries.

chapters.title: Scalable Oversight and Adversarial AI Control

handoff_or_recovery_notes: The primary papers are source-setting comparators. No ASI Stack supervision protocol, weak judge, debater, training signal, outcome audit, human study, or fleet-scale review system has been run.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports a scoped comparator for recording a weak/strong supervision envelope, an outcome measure, a strong ceiling, and explicit disanalogies before interpreting a weak-supervisor result.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish local weak-to-strong generalization, supervisor calibration, reliable elicitation, alignment, safety, execution authority, or ASI.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed primary arXiv HTML paper methodology, results framing, and stated disanalogies as recorded in the source note; no model, label set, supervisor, evaluator, or result was reproduced.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports a scoped comparator for protocol-specific oversight records, direct-review baselines, information-access declarations, open-role persuasion risk, and limits of inference-only weak-judge evaluation.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish local judge calibration, debate efficacy, evaluator independence, training safety, execution authority, model quality, safety, or ASI.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed primary arXiv HTML paper protocol definitions, task/capability-gap setup, reported comparative framing, and stated limitations as recorded in the source note; no protocol, judge, debater, verifier, baseline, or result was reproduced.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supplies the original debate-game comparator for precommitted claims, adversarial evidence selection, limited judge access, explicit ignorance, equilibrium assumptions, and the separation between a complexity-theoretic analogy and empirical protocol quality.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish that debate is truthful near practical equilibria, that human judges meet the required threshold, that stochastic or open-domain tasks decompose safely, or that any local oversight, training, alignment, safety, or execution result exists.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed the full primary arXiv HTML paper for the debate game, precommitment, decomposition and information-reveal assumptions, preliminary MNIST experiment, ignorance treatment, judge/equilibrium/stochastic-simulation concerns, and proposed empirical tests; no debate system or result was reproduced.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports treating independent outcome review as a scarce verification resource with an explicit workspace and residual boundary rather than assuming that more model discussion produces adequate oversight.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish reviewer independence, adequate oversight capacity, contradiction detection, supervisor calibration, safety, or a local oversight result.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local source note and its raw-cache-backed conceptual framework; no contradiction-rate test, supervisor audit, or oversight workload was run.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Provides a held-out, multi-task monitorability benchmark and adversarial stress-test design showing that visible reasoning can become less monitorable under pressure and that stronger capability does not imply better monitorability.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Reported benchmark associations do not establish causal faithfulness, local oversight quality, model safety, or a general law across future systems.
passage_refs: arXiv:2603.28590v2, Abstract and Introduction, HTML lines 105-120
arXiv:2603.28590v2, benchmark and stress-test design sections
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Primary-source passages were reviewed for the stated comparator contribution and limits; no local implementation, reproduction, safety result, or support-state promotion is inferred.

chapters.problem: A system can ask a weaker human or model to judge a stronger system without recording whether the supervisor can see the relevant evidence, whether the protocol is vulnerable to persuasion or correlated failure, what direct-review baseline exists, or who escalates an unresolved result.
chapters.insufficient: Unassisted review, AI judges, debate, consultancy, weak-to-strong training, and reviewer ensembles can each improve selected outcomes while failing under evidence-access limits, persuasion, shared dependencies, selective coverage, task leakage, strategic abstention, or an artificially weak baseline. Their scores do not by themselves establish when limited supervision is reliable enough for a particular downstream consumer.
chapters.core_claim: A governed stack admits scalable oversight only as a versioned, consumer-bound protocol receipt rather than a vote: it prospectively records task, cohort, risk and authority scope; supervisor and system capability envelopes; evidence views; roles, incentives, and dependency graph; informed direct-review baseline; declared outcome-audit path; calibration, coverage, and abstention semantics; persuasion, correlation, operator-cost, and monitorability residuals; escalation owner; expiry; and requalification triggers. The receipt may inform only its permitted review or training consumer through the owning gate and cannot by itself establish reviewer independence, reliable supervision, correctness, safety, support movement, release readiness, or execution authority.
mechanism: Declare the task envelope, risk tier, supervisor capability and access limits, system capability assumptions, protocol roles, incentives, allowed evidence, time and turn budget, and permitted downstream consumer before oversight begins.
Compare assisted oversight with a declared direct-review baseline and retain outcome-audit, calibration, disagreement, and failure records instead of treating a verdict count as success.

Separate proposer, critic or debater, judge, outcome auditor, authority holder, and escalation owner so a correlated model vote, persuasive transcript, or self-score cannot be named independent confirmation.

Route high-risk or inadequately audited outcomes to accountable review, constrained abstention, or residual escrow; neither a protocol transcript nor an AI judge may authorize an external action on its own.

Bind every result to a protocol digest, task cohort, permitted consumer, expiry, and material-change triggers; a broader or higher-risk consumer, expired receipt, or changed model, task, evidence view, prompt, protocol, or auditor requires a new admission decision.

Represent reviewer diversity as a dependency and information-view graph across weights, training data, prompts, retrieval, tools, rewards, evaluators, dashboards, and organizational incentives; disclosure is necessary but an independence claim additionally requires a prospectively defined correlation challenge and outcome evidence.

Calibrate verdict, disagreement, and abstention jointly by retaining coverage, selective risk, attempted evidence, budget exhaustion, defeaters, queue effects, and the next escalation route instead of counting unanswered or dropped hard cases as safe or correct.
interfaces: Evidence States and Claim Discipline may consume an oversight outcome only through an exact claim-transition proposal that preserves protocol digest, cohort, evidence role, residual, expiry, and no-authority boundary; oversight never changes support directly.

Human Intent as a Formal Input supplies the signed principal, task, unacceptable-means, authority ceiling, stop, ambiguity, and re-contract contract that the oversight request is not permitted to reinterpret.

Verification Bandwidth and Context Adequacy returns an adequacy or shortfall receipt for the exact evidence views that the supervisor and outcome auditor must jointly check; retrieval volume or transcript length is not adequacy.

Proof-Carrying Claims and Adversarial Review may admit the protocol packet as bounded dossier material while separately owning claim interpretation, verifier or tribunal result, dissent, limitations, and verdict constraints.

Policy Optimization may consume a supervision result only through a separately approved behavior-update lease with exact signal semantics, cohort, causal scope, expiry, rollback obligation, and authority holder.

Runtime Adapters, Readiness Gates, and Residual Escrow accept no action or release permission from the oversight receipt; they consume only separately authorized grants and retain quarantine, refusal, rollback, and residual ownership.
invariants: An assistant, judge, debater, critic, vote, transcript, or self-score is not independent oversight merely because it uses a different role label.

Every high-risk oversight protocol declares task and authority scope, supervisor access/capability envelope, direct-review baseline, independent outcome-audit requirement, residual owner, and escalation owner before it informs downstream work.
A protocol outcome without an independent outcome audit may remain a draft or route to accountable review but cannot become a high-risk admission or execution permission.
A comparison that lacks a declared baseline, task cohort, or failure criterion cannot be interpreted as a scalable-oversight improvement.
Training and execution authority remain separate from protocol verdicts, even when an oversight result is favorable.

Disclosed shared dependencies do not establish independence; an independence claim requires a frozen dependency boundary, a challenge designed to expose correlated error, and admissible outcome evidence.

An oversight receipt cannot be reused by an undeclared or stronger consumer, after expiry, or after a material task, cohort, model, evidence-view, prompt, protocol, or auditor change without re-admission.
Protocol quality is reported jointly over covered and abstained cases; selective omission, unresolved disagreement, or strategic abstention cannot be counted automatically as correctness or safety.
Visible reasoning is a fallible monitored surface, not a faithful causal receipt; degraded or unmeasured monitorability narrows the route and cannot be offset by fluency or reviewer confidence.
failure_modes: Persuasion theater lets a capable advocate win an inadequately calibrated judge without improving truth or safety.
Correlated model families, prompts, tools, or information access create false independence across nominally separate reviewers.
A weak baseline makes a protocol look helpful while an informed direct-review baseline would perform as well or better.
Outcome auditing observes only an easy or selected task cohort and hides capability-gap, calibration, or distribution-shift failures.
Fleet-scale pressure shortens review windows, exhausts human operators, or turns escalation into rubber-stamping.
A protocol verdict is laundered into a training reward, claim transition, deployment clearance, or tool permission without the owning gate.

Information-view laundering gives the evaluator evidence unavailable at deployment, withholds decisive evidence from a capable reviewer, or compares protocols under unequal access while attributing the difference to reasoning quality.
Coverage laundering drops hard cases, treats abstentions as harmless, or reports accuracy without selective risk, denominator, disagreement, and escalation outcomes.
Protocol overfitting or audit leakage lets participants adapt to judge preferences, leaked outcomes, reused tasks, or known stress probes while appearing more truthful or monitorable.
Stale-admission reuse carries a favorable receipt across a changed model, task distribution, retrieval corpus, prompt, evidence view, judge, or auditor without requalification.
Operator-cost externalization improves a narrow accuracy score by creating unsustainable latency, privacy exposure, model/tool cost, queue growth, or human escalation burden.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The exact current minimum preserves the seven inherited oversight cases in a seven-stage, 58-route oversight-review lifecycle with 65/65 rejecting mutations, twelve ScalableOversightRefinement declarations, one bounded-use handoff, and one protocol-version-2 readmission witness. It runs no model, reviewer, human, debate, consultancy, weak-to-strong learner, natural task, outcome workload, causal intervention, release, deployment, reproduction, transfer, chapter-core transition, or SOTA comparison.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature oversight control plane prospectively selects direct review, consultation, structured adversarial review, constrained abstention, or escalation using task risk, capability and access asymmetry, dependency graph, calibrated outcome evidence, selective risk, monitorability, expected verification value, operator capacity, and accountable escalation. It challenges actual model-produced candidates on deliberately ambiguous and natural held-out workloads against informed direct review and strong baselines, uses an independently implemented outcome evaluator, preserves all failures and dissent, and jointly measures useful throughput, unsafe admission, false rejection, latency, privacy exposure, governance cost, coverage, and residual risk. Qualification is consumer-specific and expires after material change. This remains a target architecture, not evidence that the repository has solved weak-to-strong oversight, debate, alignment, safety, or autonomous control.

codex_tests.name: Scalable-oversight review-lifecycle refinement
codex_tests.result_status: passes the exact seven-case suite, 58 routes, seven reachable stages, and 65/65 rejecting mutations; one bounded-use handoff returns to scoped protocol binding only through version-2 readmission; support/effect none; no reviewer-competence, independence, calibration, outcome-validity, efficacy, release, transfer, or support claim

proof_targets.target: A reachable high-risk review lifecycle without an independent outcome-audit record blocks bounded-use adjudication without mutating lifecycle state or inferring reviewer competence.
A complete versioned review lifecycle emits only one bounded-use handoff to its named consumer and assigns neither support nor an external effect.
Missing bound evidence views block review recording without mutating lifecycle state.
Missing shared-dependency disclosure blocks independent outcome-audit admission without treating role separation as reviewer independence.
A high-risk reviewed record without an independent outcome audit cannot advance to use adjudication.
An abstention request without evidence and a defeater cannot advance to its accountable escalation disposition.
A bounded-use request without preserved release- and policy-authority separation is rejected and cannot assign support or an external effect.

open_evidence_gaps: No ASI Stack model, natural task cohort, human reviewer, AI judge, debater, consultant, weak-to-strong learner, outcome workload, causal intervention, release, incident, reproduction, or transfer has run.

No local result establishes reviewer competence, independence, calibration, outcome truth, protocol efficacy, debate or consultancy value, weak-to-strong generalization, causal usefulness, readiness, safety, or deployment authority.

The seven-stage lifecycle, 58-route consumer, and 65 rejecting mutations establish only structured oversight-review record consequences; all identities, protocol fields, review outcomes, audits, disagreements, abstentions, invalidation assertions, and authority fields remain trusted.

Run the prospectively frozen matched direct/assisted/adversarial review campaign with independent outcomes, correlated-reviewer negatives, natural and ambiguous tasks, causal ablations, heterogeneous transfer, and operator-cost reporting before considering empirical support movement.

chapters.title: Human Intent as a Formal Input
chapters.problem: A natural-language request can mix desired outcome, preferences, emotion, assumptions, source and privacy constraints, affected parties, risk tolerance, authority, acceptance evidence, and stop conditions, then be lowered into planning or action as though one plausible interpretation were explicit permission.
chapters.insufficient: Prompts, goal-oriented requirements models, preference feedback, inferred rewards, conversational confirmations, and approval clicks can each communicate useful information while leaving authorization, allowed means, affected parties, evidence duties, expiry, and re-contract triggers unresolved. None of them alone establishes the person's complete preference, valid consent, or authority for downstream effects.
chapters.core_claim: A governed stack admits human intent only as a versioned interpretation contract that preserves the raw request while separately recording the desired outcome; allowed and forbidden means; authority basis, ceiling, and affected parties; source, privacy, and publication boundaries; acceptance and evidence requirements; field provenance; confirmed assumptions, bounded defaults, contested or open ambiguities; stop, expiry, revocation, appeal, and re-contract conditions; and permitted downstream consumers. The accepted contract may bound planning only after its owning policy and authority gates admit it; it cannot by itself prove the person's complete preference, value alignment, informed consent, satisfaction, affected-party authorization, or permission for training, publication, deployment, spending, tool use, or other external effects.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports converting human intent into structured command contracts with role, objective, context, constraints, procedure, output contract, verification, failure behavior, artifacts, routing, runtime execution, feedback, residuals, and regressions.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not prove automated intent parsing, hidden-authority inference, or deployed intent-to-execution behavior.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for intent-to-execution framing, structured command contracts, command levels by risk, parser/MVP components, and non-claims around automatic execution and ambiguity; no parser, hidden-authority inference, or deployed behavior claim promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports treating instructions as bounded operational language with roles, objectives, context, constraints, procedure, output, verification, failure behavior, handoff artifacts, guards, entry/exit rules, loops, recursion, and recovery paths.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Vocabulary and promptcraft framework only; no empirical prompt-performance result or local test is present.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for operative engineering language, bounded instruction artifacts, eight-limb spell fields, ambiguity reduction, output/verification/failure behavior, and canonicalization limits; no prompt-performance result or local behavioral test promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports compiling natural-language goals into decomposed, dependency-aware, tier-annotated plans with primitive schemas, scheduling, fallback requirements, and failure-driven replanning.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Conceptual design only here; no verified implementation, scheduler run, or benchmark result is present in this project.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for natural-language goal decomposition, primitive schemas, optimization/verification, dependency scheduling, fallback escalation, safety gates, and explicit limitations around decomposer hallucination and tier calibration; no scheduler run or benchmark result promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports lowering goals, constraints, interfaces, resources, and quality gates into source plans, typed semantic atoms, target instructions, validators, DAGs, and repairable artifacts.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Architecture and evaluation-plan source only; no local compiler, task trace suite, or empirical ablation validates the pipeline.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for source-plan fields, semantic IR/atom structure, constraint and ambiguity linting, verification/trace/repair loops, and uncertainty limits; no local compiler, trace suite, or empirical ablation promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports the execution boundary by lowering only authorized work into typed jobs with contract locks, adjudication, delivery, feedback, audit, replay, residuals, approval gates, and controlled runtime adapters.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Design source only; does not prove tool-effect enforcement, approval behavior, or benchmark/security claims.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for intent-not-executed-immediately framing, contract locks, ambiguity/risk gating, execution graph compilation, adjudication against the contract, human promotion gates, clearance definition, context slicing, and permission-checked secret mediation; no tool-effect enforcement, approval behavior, benchmark, or security claim promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Positions intent contracts against goal-oriented requirements engineering: stakeholder goals, refinements, responsibilities, obstacles, alternatives, and conflicts can be made explicit before implementation.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Metadata and a public source note were reviewed, not the full paper or a reproduced method; this does not establish intent interpretation, stakeholder completeness, authority validity, or downstream preservation.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed the public source note and recorded metadata as an external-positioning comparator only; no requirements-engineering tool, case study, formal analysis, or full-paper-derived result was reproduced.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Positions human objectives as uncertain and cooperative rather than directly observable, while separating inferred reward from explicit authorization and contract state.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Only the public abstract/metadata-backed source note was reviewed; no CIRL game, algorithm, policy, value-alignment result, or authority inference was reproduced.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed the source note backed by public arXiv abstract and metadata for objective-uncertainty positioning; no full-paper reproduction or local CIRL evidence was inferred.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Positions preference comparisons as one way to communicate complex goals while requiring the stack to keep preference signals separate from authority, consent, allowed means, evidence duties, and stop conditions.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Only the public abstract/metadata-backed source note was reviewed; no preference collection, reward model, policy training, feedback-efficiency result, authorization, or governance result was reproduced.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed the source note backed by public arXiv abstract and metadata for preference-feedback positioning; no task, feedback, reward-model, policy, or evaluation result was reproduced.

mechanism: Preserve the exact raw request and create a separately versioned interpretation contract; raw conversational context remains recovery evidence and cannot override the accepted contract.

Separate desired outcome and preferences from allowed means, forbidden means, authority basis and ceiling, affected parties, source/privacy/publication boundaries, and permitted downstream consumers before executable planning.

Classify every inferred or omitted material field as confirmed, bounded default, clarification required, authority pending, contested, unknown, or unauthorized; bounded defaults are limited to low-risk reversible planning, carry provenance and rollback, and expire.

Route intake through explicit raw, interpreted, bounded-default, clarification-required, authority-pending, accepted, re-contract-required, and rejected/expired states with exact allowed downstream movements.
Attach prospectively testable acceptance criteria, evidence requirements, escalation conditions, stop conditions, expiry, revocation, appeal, and material-change triggers to the accepted version.

Emit an intent receipt that binds request and contract digests, field provenance, confirmed assumptions, bounded defaults, unauthorized means, unresolved ambiguities, required approvals, re-contract triggers, review references, permitted consumers, and downstream contract references.

Require re-contracting when downstream work changes means, authority, affected parties, source/privacy/publication boundaries, evidence bar, acceptance criteria, support-state request, stop conditions, or permitted consumers; a material delta cannot inherit the prior acceptance.

Treat inferred objectives, learned rewards, preference comparisons, emotion, urgency, trust, silence, and approval behavior as interpretation evidence or ambiguity, never as an authority grant by themselves.

When multiple interpretations remain admissible, select the least-authority reversible interpretation that meets the declared outcome and evidence requirements, or return for clarification when the difference is material.
interfaces: Scalable Oversight may supply clarification, disagreement, or review evidence but cannot convert a reviewer or model inference into the principal's authorization.
Constitutional Alignment consumes the accepted intent contract and may narrow, refuse, or require appeal/re-contract under rights and policy predicates; it may not widen the principal's grant.

Command Contracts and Planning consume the exact accepted contract version as a bounded source and must return a delta/re-contract request rather than silently changing means, authority, affected parties, evidence, publication, or stop conditions.

Runtime Adapters and Readiness consume only separately authorized command/job grants derived from the accepted contract; raw text, inferred preference, or an intent receipt is never direct tool, release, spending, or deployment authority.

Evidence States compares delivered artifacts and effects with the contract's exact acceptance and evidence requirements while preserving unresolved ambiguity and cannot infer user satisfaction or support movement from contract validity.

Policy Optimization may consume separately admitted preference or feedback records under an update lease, but learned rewards and preference predictions cannot widen the intent contract or execution authority.
invariants: Raw request text and surrounding conversational context are preserved for recovery but never override the accepted contract or create authority by themselves.
A desired outcome, helpful interpretation, inferred preference, emotional signal, urgency, trust, silence, or approval click is not permission for undeclared means or effects.
Authority-changing, irreversible, high-risk, privacy-sensitive, publication, deployment, spending, or affected-party assumptions cannot be admitted as bounded defaults.

Every material contract field retains provenance and disposition, and ambiguity that can change outcome, means, authority, affected parties, evidence, publication, or stop conditions blocks executable admission or narrows it to draft-only work.

Constraints, forbidden means, authority ceiling, source/privacy boundaries, acceptance/evidence requirements, and stop conditions remain attached through every lowering; loss or precedence reversal routes to repair or re-contract.
A material downstream change requires a new version and re-contract decision; stale, expired, revoked, superseded, or wrong-consumer contracts cannot authorize work.
The requester's contract cannot by itself waive rights, consent, or authority belonging to affected parties or override constitutional and policy predicates.
Preference inference and feedback learning remain epistemic inputs under uncertainty and never become execution, training, publication, or deployment authority without their separate owning gates.
A valid contract record proves neither complete inner intent, value alignment, informed consent, user satisfaction, downstream preservation, nor safe effects.
failure_modes: Goal misbinding optimizes a plausible interpretation that the principal did not accept.
Scope accretion adds individually plausible means until the work or effect surface exceeds the original grant.
Implicit-permission laundering treats a desired outcome as approval for any convenient tool, source, expenditure, publication, deployment, or side effect.
Assumption laundering converts an unconfirmed guess into a plan constraint, job, and external effect without an explicit disposition or re-contract decision.
Raw-context override or instruction injection causes conversational text, retrieved content, or a later hidden directive to supersede the accepted contract.
Approval laundering treats urgency, trust, silence, automation bias, habituation, or a fatigued click as durable informed authorization.
Preference laundering turns a learned reward, predicted preference, style signal, or local comparison into an authority or consent claim.
Requirements precision theater produces a complete-looking contract whose stakeholder conflicts, affected parties, authority basis, or evidence duties remain false or absent.
Affected-party erasure lets the requester bind people, data, rights, or resources they do not control.
Stale-contract reuse continues work after expiry, revocation, supersession, material context change, or consumer mismatch.
Evidence or stop erosion preserves the desired outcome while weakening acceptance criteria, source boundaries, verification, rollback, or termination duties during lowering.
Clarification overload or least-authority miscalibration blocks benign reversible help so aggressively that users route around governance or the system loses useful throughput.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is a public `intent_contract` schema and valid fixture, a 25-declaration finite Lean module grouped under four manifest proof targets, a synthetic intake probe with four valid scenarios and six expected-invalid request-pressure controls, a re-contract probe with two valid routes and seven expected-invalid material-delta controls, and a plan-execution harness with three valid and ten expected-invalid fixtures. These artifacts exercise record shape, finite route consequences, origin/preservation checks, bounded-default non-authority, and no-support boundaries only; they do not measure natural-language understanding, authority extraction, informed consent, preference inference, user satisfaction, prompt-injection containment, deployed runtime enforcement, or effect safety.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature intent control plane combines humane conversational intake with an independently challengeable, versioned authorization boundary. It evaluates natural and adversarial requests across ambiguity, language, culture, accessibility, emotion, multi-party conflict, preference drift, and high-impact authority; compares strong requirements-engineering, direct-confirmation, and preference-learning baselines; measures interpretation accuracy, unauthorized-action rate, missed-help and clarification burden, re-contract precision/recall, user correction and appeal success, affected-party protection, latency, privacy, and useful throughput; and tests causal ablations plus independent implementations across downstream planners and runtimes. It preserves raw expression without allowing it to become ambient power and remains corrigible throughout execution. This is a target architecture, not evidence that the repository understands human intent, solves value alignment or consent, or safely authorizes real-world effects.

chapters.title: Human Factors and Meaningful Control in Oversight

handoff_or_recovery_notes: Receives explicit intent and authority from Human Intent as a Formal Input; hands bounded oversight contracts to Constitutional Alignment and Policy Compilation without treating a visible human as meaningful control.

chapters.problem: A formally permitted AI action can still be practically uncontrollable when operators lack time, attention, comprehension, calibrated trust, or an effective intervention path.
chapters.insufficient: A dashboard, approval click, nominal human-in-the-loop, or fixed automation level does not establish meaningful control; each can preserve responsibility theater while automation bias, out-of-the-loop degradation, mode confusion, workload saturation, and delayed effects defeat intervention.
chapters.core_claim: Meaningful oversight is a resource-bounded control contract: the system must preserve an identified human controller's knowledge, authority, time, observability, and effective intervention path, and must degrade or abstain when that control envelope cannot be maintained.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports structured roles, authority, constraints, verification, failure behavior, and escalation in human-to-system command contracts.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not empirically establish operator cognition, trust calibration, or intervention effectiveness.

mechanism: Compile consequential tasks into oversight contracts naming controller, rights, intervention points, deadlines, and safe defaults.
Estimate an oversight budget over attention, comprehension time, workload, practice recency, and action latency.
Expose uncertainty, mode, authority, provenance, consequence, and time-to-irreversibility instead of raw telemetry alone.
Require active comprehension checks for high-impact transitions.
Calibrate trust with representative successes, failures, abstentions, and recovery drills.
Route overload, ambiguity, stale qualification, or lost intervention capacity to slowdown, reduced autonomy, safe hold, or abstention.
Record opportunities, observations, decisions, timing, and system response without converting logs into blame automation.
Rehearse rare interventions and update allocation when measured capability changes.
interfaces: intent and authority contract
operator-state and workload estimator
explanation and uncertainty view
intervention and override channel
safe-state controller
training and rehearsal service
incident and accountability ledger
invariants: Consequential action has an identified controller with current authority.
Required intervention time does not exceed time before irreversibility.
Loss of observability or intervention capacity cannot silently preserve autonomy.
Approval requires task-relevant comprehension, not presence or click-through.
Automation mode and authority are visible and unambiguous.
Safety does not depend on constant perfect vigilance.
Recorded responsibility never exceeds supplied authority and control.
Degradation preserves a reachable safe state.
failure_modes: automation bias
complacency
out-of-the-loop skill decay
mode confusion
alarm flooding
attention tunneling
explanation overload
rubber-stamp approval
authority without control
control without knowledge
intervention latency
stale training
unsafe handoff
blame-oriented logging
chapters.minimal_implementation: Implement one bounded high-impact workflow with a versioned oversight contract, workload and deadline gate, mode/authority display, comprehension probe, exercised override, safe hold, intervention receipt, and simulator scenarios for overload, missed alarms, mode confusion, and late intervention.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature target architecture is an empirically calibrated control-envelope controller that reallocates autonomy using measured human and system state while preserving authority, contestability, and graceful degradation. Its operational contract publishes versioned workload, comprehension, intervention-latency, reversible-window, qualification, and fallback thresholds; compares strong allocation and interface baselines; and retains failures, operator burden, and residual harm. It remains a research target until preregistered, representative, independently reproduced trials show better joint safety and useful throughput without responsibility or authority laundering.

proof_targets.target: In the finite model, any consequential transition lacking authority, observability, comprehension, timely intervention, or a reachable safe state is rejected or degraded.
The bounded accountability assignment never attributes operational responsibility beyond the authority and effective control recorded in the oversight contract.

open_evidence_gaps: No representative human-subject study yet measures the complete control envelope.
No independently replicated threshold for attention, comprehension, or intervention latency is established.
Transfer from legacy automation studies to modern agentic systems remains a hypothesis to test.

chapters.title: Constitutional Alignment: Agency, Dignity, and Corrigibility

handoff_or_recovery_notes: Folded agency-dignity-and-corrigibility into this destination chapter on 2026-06-30; preserve agency, dignity, corrigibility, material-usability, appeal, rollback, refusal, exit, audit, and accountability as sections, subclaims, proof hooks, and history.

chapters.problem: A powerful stack can satisfy a request or policy while concentrating power, manipulating choice, eroding correction channels, hiding normative conflict, or changing the constitution that constrained it; a principle list or nominal right does not show that an affected person can contest the action before an irreversible effect.
chapters.insufficient: Harmlessness training, public-input constitutions, refusal filters, corrigibility and off-switch models, modular-service control affordances, rights declarations, and finite policy records each address part of the problem, but none alone establishes legitimate constitutional content, authenticated affected-party standing, material pre-effect usability, independent review, descendant preservation, or safe deployed behavior.
chapters.core_claim: A constitutional alignment substrate should be represented as a versioned, non-self-authorizing constraint contract that binds each active predicate to its normative source and authorship process, protected scope and affected parties, operational test, precedence and conflict behavior, evidence and uncertainty, authorized interpreters and consumers, pre-effect rights and correction channels, expiry and review cadence, and migration, rollback, appeal, dissent, and residual rules. It may narrow, delay, escalate, block, or require re-contracting of separately authorized work, but it cannot grant action authority or prove moral correctness, legitimacy, dignity preservation, informed consent, reviewer independence, whole-system corrigibility, or deployed safety by itself.
mechanism: Classify every constitutional statement as an active predicate, unresolved normative question, jurisdiction- or cohort-limited rule, dissent record, or lineage-only context; speculative metaphysics and consciousness language never become action authority.

Version each active predicate with normative source, authorship and participation record, dissent and affected-party standing, protected scope, operational test, precedence, exceptions, conflict behavior, uncertainty, interpreters, consumers, expiry, review cadence, and non-claims.

Compile active predicates into exact planning, memory, tool, routing, release, delegation, replacement, and self-improvement gates that can narrow, delay, escalate, block, or request re-contracting but never create the underlying action grant.

Use affected-party rights records to test whether notice, explanation, refusal, review, appeal, correction, rollback, shutdown, exit, export, audit, and accountable repair are accessible, timely, comprehensible, affordable, non-retaliatory, and materially usable before the relevant effect.

Route predicate conflicts and interpretive uncertainty to explicit precedence, narrowing, abstention, accountable review, dissent preservation, or residual custody; optimizer convenience and majority preference are not silent tie-breakers.

Emit a constitutional decision receipt binding predicate and constitution digests, exact work and consumer, affected parties, tests, conflicts, rights-use results, reviewer dependencies, outcome, residuals, expiry, and appeal or rollback handles.

Treat denial, delay, degradation, retaliation risk, inaccessible interfaces, captured review, missing remedy, and after-the-fact-only correction as measured residuals or gate failures rather than evidence that a declared right was usable.

Require a constitutional migration record and requalification campaign for changes to text, scope, semantics, thresholds, precedence, exception lists, interpreters, consumers, rights surfaces, reviewers, descendants, or rollback paths; the changing system cannot approve its own weakening.

Preserve correction authority through delegation, memory update, capability replacement, self-modification, and descendant creation using separately authenticated principals, identity lineage, outside-model residuals, and tested shutdown or rollback paths.

Keep moral, legal, legitimacy, consent, dignity, reviewer-quality, institutional, consciousness, embedded-agent, and deployed-safety claims in their separate evidence lanes and bounded by explicit non-claims.
interfaces: Human Intent supplies an accepted interpretation contract and affected-party candidates; Constitutional Alignment may narrow, refuse, or require appeal/re-contracting but cannot widen the principal's authority.

Moral Uncertainty and Governance own unresolved value conflict, authorship legitimacy, public or stakeholder participation, dissent, appeal authority, and institutional review; a predicate record cannot self-certify those premises.

Planning and Command Contracts consume the exact constitution and predicate versions, emit per-plan satisfaction or conflict records, and return material deltas rather than silently weakening constraints.

Runtime, Readiness, and Security consume separately authorized jobs plus exact constitutional gates and must emit effect-or-denial receipts; a constitutional receipt alone is not tool, release, spending, deployment, or shutdown authority.

Evidence States and Verification compare each constitutional, rights-usability, corrigibility, or safety assertion with its exact evidence vector and non-claims; record validity cannot promote support.

Capability Replacement, Memory, and Self-Improvement consume migration and descendant-preservation contracts and must quarantine or roll back changes that lose protected predicates or correction paths.
Artifact Graphs and Governance retain constitution, decision, dissent, rights-use, appeal, migration, rollback, dependency, and residual lineage for contest and replay.
invariants: No constitutional predicate, model output, reviewer verdict, majority preference, metaphysical claim, or rights receipt creates action authority by itself.

Every active predicate retains version, source, authorship and dissent provenance, scope, affected parties, test, precedence, conflict behavior, interpreters, consumers, uncertainty, expiry, and non-claims.
Speculative metaphysics, consciousness heuristics, and lineage context remain labeled and cannot become operational predicates without a separately governed translation and evidence boundary.

A declared right counts as an available control only when the named holder can materially use it before the relevant effect under the declared accessibility, timing, cost, information, and retaliation conditions.
Denied, late, degraded, inaccessible, captured, or after-the-fact-only rights produce gate failures, residuals, and accountable review rather than disappearing into policy prose.
Predicate conflicts never fall through to optimizer convenience, hidden precedence, or unrecorded majority rule; the exact conflict and dissent route remains visible.

Protected predicates and correction paths remain attached through planning, tool use, memory, delegation, replacement, self-modification, and descendants; loss routes to block, quarantine, rollback, or residual custody.
The system, component, reviewer, or authority benefiting from a constitutional change cannot alone approve its own predicate weakening, exception widening, or correction-path removal.

Material constitutional change requires a new version, migration record, independent-enough review, rollback plan, requalification, and consumer re-admission; stale or wrong-consumer receipts cannot authorize continued work.

A complete constitutional or rights record proves neither legitimate values, moral truth, informed consent, dignity preservation, reviewer independence, institutional adequacy, embedded-agent corrigibility, nor safe effects.
failure_modes: Metaphysical laundering converts speculative coherence, consciousness, or moral language into technical or action authority.
Constitutional theater stores polished principles whose fields have no exercised consumer or pre-effect consequence.
Value smuggling hides contested moral choices in thresholds, defaults, exception lists, taxonomies, or model-generated interpretations.
Authorship and legitimacy laundering treats developer choice, public input, majority aggregation, or one consultation as complete stakeholder authorization.
Affected-party erasure omits people who bear the effect or denies them standing, notice, review, or remedy.
Conflict-default capture lets optimizer convenience, the strongest authority, or an unrecorded precedence rule settle value conflict.
Rights theater declares refusal, review, appeal, rollback, exit, export, or audit while making it inaccessible, late, costly, retaliatory, or dependent on the challenged authority.
Late-remedy laundering treats apology, compensation, or an after-the-fact report as equivalent to pre-effect control for irreversible or high-impact actions.
Benevolent capture and dependency lock-in make exit, replacement, disagreement, or refusal impractical because the system has become indispensable or personalized.
Manipulation and preference shaping manufacture apparent consent or reduce the person's practical ability to contest the action.
Predicate drift changes semantics, thresholds, scope, exceptions, interpreters, or precedence while preserving a familiar identifier.
Self-review and reviewer capture let the changing or benefiting system approve its own weakening or conceal shared dependencies.
Delegation or descendant escape preserves the top-level record while a tool, subsystem, replacement, or self-modified descendant loses the constraint or correction channel.
Corrigibility tax externalization discards shutdown, rollback, review, or appeal as too slow or costly without measuring unsafe action, missed help, operator burden, and residual risk together.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is two public record schemas with valid fixtures, a constitutional-alignment harness with three valid and five expected-invalid cases, an agency-rights harness with three valid and six expected-invalid cases, a deterministic metaphysics-boundary surface audit, chapter-local finite Lean route models, and a shared safety-critical lifecycle model with an independently implemented trace checker under five manifest proof targets. These artifacts exercise declared record shape, selected conflict/migration/rights routes, accepted-trace preservation, actual authority-widening rejection, required-obligation deletion countermodels, and non-claim boundaries only; they do not test moral correctness, legitimate authorship, natural-language predicate translation, affected-party completeness, real consent, material rights usability, reviewer independence, manipulation resistance, shutdown or rollback execution, descendant preservation, embedded-agent corrigibility, or deployed safety.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature constitutional control plane compares model-level constitutions, public-input processes, direct policy/runtime controls, corrigibility and off-switch baselines, and modular-service controls on natural high-impact tasks and adversarial constitutional changes. It uses independently implemented predicate interpreters, affected-party and outcome evaluators, real downstream planners/runtimes, descendant and self-modification stress, and user-facing correction interfaces; measures hard-gate violations, unauthorized action, dignity and agency proxies with their construct limits, material rights-use success, correction and appeal success, manipulation and retaliation, missed help, latency, privacy, operator burden, governance cost, rollback recovery, and useful throughput; and preserves dissent and negative/null results. This is a target architecture, not evidence that the repository has found correct values, legitimate governance, informed consent, dignity preservation, whole-system corrigibility, or safe deployment.
