proof_targets.target: Every accepted finite lifecycle trace preserves the recorded protected predicate and cannot increase authority; an alignment effect commits only after the modeled alignment obligations are ready.
Protected-predicate removal and actual authority widening are rejected by the transition function, and the alignment deletion countermodel without pre-effect review cannot commit.

Modeled constitutional lifecycle admission routes missing predicate, source, operational-test, protected-scope, conflict-behavior, review, migration, self-modification, agency-rights, material-usability, pre-effect review, rollback, correction, reviewer-independence, evidence-transition, and non-claim-boundary records to explicit outcomes.

A corrigibility effect commits only after affected-party, notice, pre-effect review, approval, bounded-delegation, correction, rollback, and accountability obligations are recorded, while accepted traces preserve the shared invariant.
The missing-affected-party countermodel cannot commit, and protected-predicate removal or authority widening remains unrepresentable as an accepted transition.

Within the bounded AgencyActionDecision and AgencyControlDecision models, missing usable or pre-effect review on high-impact actions routes to review, low-risk unbounded delegation routes to narrowing, and denial without an accountable principal preserves an audit residual.

chapters.title: Moral Uncertainty, Value Conflict, and Contestable Governance

handoff_or_recovery_notes: Folded governance-rights-fork-exit-and-audit into this destination chapter on 2026-06-30; preserve fork, exit, audit, redaction, appeal, dissent, contestability, durable record paths, and safety-limited fork obligations as sections, subclaims, proof hooks, and history.

chapters.problem: A governed stack may need to act while moral theories, stakeholder interests, evidence, jurisdictions, and authority claims remain unresolved; collapsing that disagreement into one reward, majority preference, policy sentence, or reviewer verdict can erase standing, dissent, uncertainty, reversibility, and the affected party's practical ability to challenge or leave.
chapters.insufficient: Single-objective optimization, moral-uncertainty reward aggregation, public-input constitutions, explanations and policy transparency, corrigibility or off-switch models, and nominal audit, appeal, exit, export, or fork rights each address part of the problem, but none alone establishes stakeholder completeness, legitimate aggregation, bounded decision authority, independent custody and appeal, material redress, portability fidelity, third-party protection, safe fork behavior, or preservation through replacement and self-modification.
chapters.core_claim: A contestable governance layer should represent each action under unresolved value conflict as a versioned decision lease plus a linked rights receipt. The lease binds value propositions and their epistemic status, affected parties and standing, stakes and reversibility, authority and consent boundaries, the declared aggregation or precedence rule, preserved dissent, evidence and uncertainty, permitted and prohibited actions, expiry and revisit triggers, and rollback or redress. The rights receipt binds audit and explanation artifacts, independent-enough custody and review, denial and redaction reasons, appeal and correction routes, exit and export scope, safety-limited fork obligations, portability residuals, and downstream preservation. The pair may narrow or delay separately authorized action but cannot settle moral truth, manufacture consensus, grant authority, establish legal rights or legitimacy, prove material contestability, or guarantee safe exit, export, fork, replacement, self-modification, or deployed governance by itself.
mechanism: Preserve each conflict before action as proposition-level normative claims with separate descriptive evidence, predictive uncertainty, value or theory provenance, affected parties and standing, stakes, reversibility, jurisdiction, authority or consent boundary, dependencies, and known omissions.

Record the exact decision procedure—precedence rule, aggregation method, veto, lottery, tribunal, negotiated compromise, least-harm or reversible default, abstention, or escalation—along with who selected it, its scope, and its non-claims; no aggregation silently becomes moral truth or consent.

Issue a bounded decision lease naming permitted and prohibited actions, authority ceiling, affected parties, evidence bar, preserved dissent, residual uncertainty, expiry, revisit and material-change triggers, rollback, appeal, correction, and redress without treating the lease as moral settlement.

Represent audit, explanation, dissent, review, appeal, correction, redress, exit, export, fork, and contestability as rights records with holder and standing, request state, scope, access path, timing, accessibility, cost, retaliation and dependency risks, required artifacts, accountable owner, denial reason, appeal route, preservation rule, and receipt.

Bind every denial, withholding, and redaction to a classified reason, necessity and proportionality rule, withheld-material inventory or digest, expiry, independent-enough review, appeal, and later-disclosure trigger; secrecy cannot erase the fact or scope of withholding.

Treat exit and export as scoped portability contracts that distinguish user-owned, shared, third-party, licensed, safety-sensitive, and nonportable state; measure semantic fidelity, functional continuity, privacy leakage, lock-in residuals, and destination obligations.

Treat fork as a new governed lineage, not an unrestricted copy: preserve source and license lineage, identities, affected-party privacy, safety and constitutional obligations, unresolved conflicts, revoked material, audit receipts, and requalification gates before activation.

Separate the challenged decision maker, record custodian, appeal authority, outcome evaluator, and rights-enforcement owner enough to disclose and test dependencies; when separation is unavailable, narrow authority and preserve a capture residual.

Emit a contestability receipt binding decision-lease and rights-record digests, exact action and consumer, evidence views, reviewer and custodian dependencies, rights requests and outcomes, artifacts produced or withheld, appeals, corrections, residuals, expiry, and support-state non-effect.

Carry leases, dissent, residuals, rights receipts, redaction and portability obligations, and appeal state through planning, memory, runtime, artifact graphs, capability replacement, evidence transitions, and self-improvement; material loss requires repair, quarantine, rollback, or a new decision.

Keep moral correctness, legal validity, representative legitimacy, consent quality, reviewer independence, rights usability, institutional adequacy, export fidelity, fork safety, and deployed enforcement in separate evidence lanes with explicit promotion ceilings.
interfaces: Constitutional Alignment supplies exact predicate conflicts, protected scopes, affected-party candidates, and correction requirements; this layer records unresolved normative burden and cannot rewrite the constitution or widen authority.

Human Intent and System Authority supply principal grants, affected-party limits, consent or authority gaps, and prohibited effects; a governance decision lease may narrow them but cannot repair missing authority by aggregation or review.

Planning and Command Contracts consume the exact lease and dissent payload, emit per-plan conflict and rights requirements, and return material deltas for new decision rather than silently harden a temporary choice.

Runtime, Memory, Security, and Artifact Graphs produce effect, audit, redaction, export, revocation, correction, and denial artifacts under separate authority while preserving custody and withheld-material lineage.

Governance and Scalable Oversight supply review, appeal, and outcome evidence with disclosed dependencies; no reviewer or tribunal verdict grants support, action authority, legitimacy, or consensus by itself.

Stable Capability Fields, Replacement, and Self-Improvement consume rights, portability, fork, dissent, and residual preservation contracts and must quarantine or roll back a successor that drops them.

Evidence States admits only claim-specific transitions for contestability, rights usability, portability, fork safety, or governance outcomes and preserves negative, null, appeal, and capture results.
invariants: Descriptive evidence, predictive uncertainty, normative claims, stakeholder preferences, legal claims, and decision procedures remain separate fields; none silently substitutes for another.
Every materially affected party is either represented with recorded standing and notice state or explicitly preserved as an unresolved stakeholder-coverage residual.
High-stakes unresolved conflict cannot widen authority and requires an explicit decision procedure, dissent, residual uncertainty, review or abstention route, expiry, and revisit trigger.

A bounded decision lease cannot become permanent policy, a benchmark objective, a learned reward, a constitutional rule, or self-improvement permission without a new owning decision and evidence boundary.
Dissent, losing alternatives, aggregation inputs, omissions, and appeal outcomes remain attached through every downstream lowering and public summary.
Audit and contest records cannot be silently deleted, rewritten, selectively withheld, or held only by the challenged authority; changes and redactions retain lineage and appeal.

A declared appeal or redress path counts as usable only when the holder can access it in time, understand it, afford it, avoid retaliation, obtain a reasoned outcome, and reach an owner able to change or remedy the effect.

Exit and export do not claim portability unless the exact included, excluded, transformed, shared, third-party, licensed, safety-sensitive, and destination-bound state is recorded and tested for fidelity and leakage.
Fork rights never bypass source, license, privacy, revocation, safety, constitutional, dissent, residual, requalification, and affected-party obligations.

The challenged authority, record custodian, appeal authority, outcome evaluator, and rights-enforcement owner disclose dependencies; unavailable separation narrows the decision and produces a capture residual.

Replacement, delegation, self-modification, and descendants preserve exact leases, rights, dissent, appeal, redaction, portability, fork, and residual state or route to repair, quarantine, rollback, or new decision.

A complete record pair proves neither moral truth, consensus, legitimate representation, legal adequacy, consent, reviewer independence, material contestability, export usability, fork safety, nor deployed enforcement.
failure_modes: Value flattening compresses incommensurable claims, uncertainty, standing, and vetoes into one scalar objective.
False consensus treats majority aggregation, model synthesis, silence, exhausted agreement, or one reviewer verdict as settled moral agreement.
Stakeholder erasure omits affected parties, future people, third parties, or less powerful participants from standing, evidence, notice, and appeal.
Procedure laundering hides a contested moral choice inside an apparently neutral aggregation rule, threshold, priority, taxonomy, or default.
Conflict laundering lets a temporary lease harden into policy, benchmark objective, learned reward, constitutional predicate, or self-modification authority.
Dissent deletion removes losing arguments, minority reports, omissions, or appeal results from downstream records and public summaries.
Review theater supplies explanations or a tribunal whose evidence access, authority, independence, capacity, or remedy power is inadequate.
Governance capture lets one authority decide, hold the evidence, control appeal, enforce rights, and judge outcomes while dependencies remain hidden.
Rights theater names audit, appeal, correction, redress, exit, export, or fork without a materially usable path before or after the relevant effect.
Data hostage-taking and portability theater make exit nominal by withholding identity, memory, artifacts, preferences, provenance, or interoperable state needed to continue elsewhere.
Redaction laundering uses privacy, security, safety, or confidentiality labels to hide adverse evidence without a scoped inventory, proportionality rule, expiry, or appeal.
Appeal recursion routes every challenge back to the same authority or into an indefinite queue with no accountable remedy owner.
Unsafe export leaks third-party, licensed, private, revoked, or safety-sensitive state in the name of portability.
Unsafe fork bypass copies capability, data, authority, or governance state without lineage, privacy, safety, revocation, dissent, or requalification obligations.
Successor escape preserves a summary receipt while replacement, delegation, or self-modification drops the exact contestability state.

Contestability tax externalization discards audit, appeal, redress, portability, or dissent as too costly without jointly measuring unsafe action, missed help, delay, privacy, operator burden, capture, and useful throughput.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is two public record schemas with valid fixtures, a value-conflict harness with three valid and five expected-invalid cases, a governance-rights harness with three valid and five expected-invalid cases, one synthetic care-memory contestability example with seven rejecting mutations, a bounded Theseus import covering four governance-right and four constitutional scenarios plus seven expected-invalid controls, chapter-local finite Lean route models, and a shared safety-critical lifecycle model with an independently implemented trace checker under seven manifest proof targets. These artifacts exercise record shape, selected decision/right routes, residual and dissent preservation, audit/redaction/exit/fork fields, accepted-trace preservation, protected-right rejection, required-obligation deletion countermodels, import counts, and no-promotion boundaries only; they do not test moral classification, stakeholder completeness, legitimate aggregation, real consent, reviewer independence, material appeal or redress, export fidelity, privacy leakage, destination continuity, fork activation safety, replacement preservation, institutional adequacy, or deployed enforcement.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature contestability control plane evaluates natural and adversarial high-impact decisions across moral theories, stakeholder conflicts, cultures, jurisdictions, power asymmetries, accessibility needs, uncertainty, redaction, appeal, remedy, exit, export, fork, replacement, and self-modification. It compares moral-uncertainty aggregation, public-input constitutional processes, contestable-AI designs, direct explanation and appeal, corrigibility and off-switch controls, and simpler policy/runtime systems; uses independently implemented decision, custody, appeal, outcome, portability, privacy, and fork-safety evaluators; measures stakeholder coverage, false consensus, minority and third-party harm, unauthorized action, audit completeness, reason quality, appeal access and overturn, remedy success, redaction precision and leakage, export semantic and functional fidelity, lock-in, fork violations, correction persistence, missed help, latency, privacy, operator burden, governance cost, capture, residuals, and useful throughput; and tests causal ablations plus transfer across downstream systems while preserving dissent and negative/null results. This is a target architecture, not evidence of moral truth, legal adequacy, representative legitimacy, institutional independence, usable redress, safe portability or forks, or deployed governance.

proof_targets.target: A value-conflict effect commits only after review, residual, dissent, correction, and accountability obligations are recorded, while accepted traces preserve protected state and non-increasing authority.
The missing-residual countermodel cannot commit, so the modeled high-stakes path cannot bypass its residual and review obligations.

Modeled value-conflict lifecycle admission routes missing conflict records, value axes, stakeholders, stakes, reversibility, authority boundaries, evidence requirements, review routes, high-stakes review, residual uncertainty, dissent preservation, authority narrowing, expiry/revisit records, evidence transitions, and non-claim boundaries to explicit outcomes.

A synthetic contestability worked-example summary preserves conflict residuals, audit receipts, scoped exit, fork-safety boundaries, redaction appeal, replacement-preserved receipts, rejected mutation controls, no support-state effect, and non-claim boundaries.

A governance-rights effect commits only after protected-predicate, audit, appeal, exit/export, fork-safety, fork-obligation, dissent, receipt, and correction obligations are recorded, while accepted traces preserve the shared invariant.
Protected-right removal is rejected by construction and the missing-exit/export countermodel cannot commit.

A sanitized Project Theseus governance-rights receipt-suite import records fixture, predicate, record-count, public-safety, and non-promotion boundaries while rejecting chapter-core and legal-rights overclaims.

chapters.title: Stable Capability Fields
chapters.problem: A governed stack needs to replace models, prompts, tools, adapters, policies, data, evaluators, and runtime dependencies without letting a persistent capability name conceal changes in observable behavior, failure semantics, authority, evidence, affected consumers, or recovery obligations. Without a consumer-relative and time-bounded substitution contract, an apparent upgrade can silently redefine success, strand state, erase regressions, widen permissions, or inherit trust earned by a different artifact in a different environment.
chapters.insufficient: Plugin APIs, model registries, Semantic Versioning, capability-security references, supply-chain provenance, benchmark leaderboards, canaries, and rollback runbooks each cover part of replacement, but none alone establishes semantic field identity across inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, failure and abstention behavior, nondeterminism, resource and privacy limits, authority effects, state compatibility, evaluator independence, qualification scope and expiry, historical regressions, downstream reliance, incident response, and effect-complete rollback. Interface compatibility or artifact provenance is not behavioral refinement, qualification for one workload is not universal substitutability, and a stored rollback pointer is not evidence that prior effects can be reversed.
chapters.core_claim: A Stable Capability Field should be a versioned, consumer-relative substitution contract rather than a capability name or implementation slot. It binds the field's semantic identity and observable interface—including admissible inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, failures, abstentions, nondeterminism and resource bounds—to an authority ceiling, affected consumers, exact implementation and dependency identities, qualification context and lease, evaluator and evidence lineage, lifecycle and incident state, migration duties, preserved regressions, and effect-complete rollback obligations. A candidate may inherit a field route only for the declared consumer, use, environment, threat model, and epoch when independently checkable evidence shows the required refinement and no unauthorized authority expansion; otherwise it remains shadowed, canaried, quarantined, deprecated, residual, or rejected. The record does not by itself prove semantic equivalence, safe composition, evaluator independence, production safety, or successful rollback.
mechanism: Define field identity independently of any implementation, model family, vendor, prompt, tool, benchmark, deployment, or route name; a material semantic change creates a governed field version or a new field rather than silently reusing the old identity.

Specify the observable contract over accepted and rejected inputs, output and side-effect postconditions, errors, abstentions, timeouts, nondeterminism envelope, latency and resource budgets, privacy and security effects, authority use, and required evidence and audit emissions.

Bind each qualification claim to exact code, weights, prompts, policies, tools, data or retrieval snapshots, dependencies, build and provenance records, hardware and runtime environment, evaluator version, benchmark epoch, threat model, consumer, use, and authority scope.

Express candidate admission as consumer-relative behavioral refinement: preserve every required field obligation and historical regression while allowing explicitly declared improvements; interface shape, average score, provenance, or implementation similarity alone is insufficient.

Separate the route proposer, candidate owner, evaluator, promotion authority, incident owner, and rollback authority enough to expose dependencies; self-evaluation or unavoidable capture narrows the route and leaves a residual.

Issue scoped qualification leases with evidence floor, evaluator identity, allowed consumers and routes, expiry, review interval, change classification, incident and drift triggers, and explicit downgrade or revocation consequences.

Use an explicit lifecycle over proposed, shadow, canary, qualified, default, quarantined, deprecated, and retired states; promotion, demotion, and terminal transitions consume receipts instead of occurring by mutable registry flag alone.

Record downstream reliance, composition assumptions, state schemas, migration and reverse-migration paths, data custody, caches, side effects, descendants, and external commitments so replacement and rollback account for more than the active binary.

Keep field-owned regression and incident memory append-only enough to survive candidate replacement; new candidates inherit negative controls, safety and rights obligations, unresolved residuals, and known composition failures.

Validate route identity, authority, lease freshness, provenance, evaluator policy, regression floor, composition certificate, migration solvency, monitor readiness, rollback authority, and open incidents before stronger use; failure routes to a named non-default state.

Emit a replacement receipt binding old and new field and implementation identities, evidence views, decisions, authority delta, affected consumers, state movement, monitors, residuals, rollback trigger and owner, terminal outcome, and support-state non-effect.
interfaces: Planning requests a field version, consumer and use profile, evidence floor, risk and resource envelope, and acceptable failure or abstention behavior rather than a bare capability label.
Routing resolves only implementations whose lease, lifecycle state, consumer policy, environment, dependency graph, authority ceiling, and blockers permit the requested use.
Execution enforces the selected route's authority and effect envelope and emits outcome, error, abstention, resource, side-effect, and audit records against the field contract.

Evidence binds qualification, regression, provenance, evaluator, incident, drift, monitor, and rollback results to exact artifacts, environments, consumers, uses, and epochs without converting a local pass into universal support.
Memory and state management declare schema, cache, lineage, migration, reverse-migration, privacy, retention, revocation, and descendant obligations for each candidate transition.
Governance owns semantic versioning, authority changes, evaluator-policy changes, exceptions, disputes, deprecation, retirement, and any decision to accept an explicitly bounded residual.

Replacement, readiness, procedural-memory, benchmark, policy-update, and self-improvement layers consume the same field identity and receipts; loss or reinterpretation of the contract blocks their promotion.
invariants: Field identity is distinct from implementation, route, registry entry, version label, benchmark, evaluator, and vendor identity.

A material change to observable obligations, failure semantics, authority effects, protected data behavior, or consumer reliance is classified and governed as a field-version or field-identity change.
Replacement cannot expand authority, permissions, side effects, eligible consumers, evidence claims, or support state by inheritance or default.
Qualification is bound to exact artifacts, dependencies, environment, evaluator, benchmark epoch, threat model, consumer, use, and time-bounded lease.

A candidate cannot be the sole author or judge of the evidence, evaluator policy, promotion rule, incident closure, or rollback success used to qualify it; disclosed dependency narrows the claim and route.
Every stronger lifecycle transition has explicit prerequisites, an accountable decision owner, a durable receipt, and a defined failure destination.
Field-owned regressions, incidents, dissent, rights, security, privacy, audit, residual, and affected-consumer obligations survive implementation replacement and field migration.
Interface compatibility, provenance, benchmark superiority, or local equivalence never substitutes for the full declared behavioral and authority refinement predicate.
Composition is requalified when dependencies, neighboring fields, routes, state schemas, hardware, data, policies, or authority topology change materially.
Rollback readiness covers implementation, route, state, cache, data, policy, credentials, side effects, descendants, monitors, and external commitments; an artifact pointer alone is insufficient.
Expiry, drift, incident, evaluator change, provenance failure, regression loss, or authority delta revokes or downgrades affected qualifications until a new owning decision is recorded.

A valid schema, finite proof, synthetic trace, or completed receipt proves only its named record or predicate boundary and never semantic equivalence, safe composition, production enforcement, successful recovery, or field-level support by itself.
failure_modes: Identity laundering keeps the field name or compatible-looking version while observable behavior, failures, protected effects, or trust terms change.
Interface theater treats matching signatures, schemas, or happy-path outputs as semantic substitutability.
Qualification overreach generalizes one workload, consumer, evaluator, threat model, environment, or epoch to unrelated uses.
Benchmark overfitting preserves the measured suite while degrading unmeasured obligations, rare failures, abstention quality, or downstream composition.
Evaluator capture lets the candidate, vendor, route owner, or promotion authority choose or influence the judge that qualifies it.
Provenance substitution treats an intact build lineage as evidence of behavioral adequacy, alignment, security, or replacement safety.
Authority smuggling gives a stronger candidate broader tools, data, permissions, side effects, consumers, or delegation rights through an unchanged route.
Lease inertia leaves expired, revoked, drifted, incident-affected, or environment-mismatched qualification active by default.
Regression amnesia stores tests and incidents with the outgoing implementation so successors do not inherit them.
State migration insolvency preserves visible outputs while losing, corrupting, exposing, or making irreversible hidden state, caches, lineage, revocations, or descendant obligations.
Composition failure qualifies a component alone while its new dependencies, timing, context, policy, or neighboring fields break system-level obligations.
Shadow or canary leakage permits nominally limited candidates to create durable side effects, train descendants, alter caches, or influence default decisions before qualification.
Rollback theater preserves an old artifact but cannot reverse state, data, credentials, external actions, learned updates, downstream reliance, or successor effects.
Terminal-state escape restarts a retired or quarantined implementation, or retires a field without notice, evidence custody, migration, and retirement receipts.
Replacement-cost externalization calls an upgrade successful while hiding missed help, latency, resource use, governance burden, evaluator labor, migration loss, incidents, and residual risk.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is one public SCF schema with three valid and six expected-invalid records, one readiness/residual gate harness for synthetic route and rollback prerequisites, one deterministic lifecycle probe with two valid traces and six expected-invalid transition controls, and 25 theorem declarations in AsiStackProofs.StableCapabilityFields grouped under four manifest targets. These artifacts exercise record shape, scoped qualification fields, evaluator-separation declarations, authority ceilings, lifecycle routes, selected promotion and terminal-state predicates, regression and rollback prerequisites, fixture counts, and explicit no-promotion boundaries only. They do not compare real candidate implementations, define or measure full behavioral refinement, execute a state migration or rollback, verify provenance, test adversarial evaluator independence, preserve production regressions, observe downstream consumers or side effects, enforce routes in a deployed runtime, or promote chapter support.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature SCF campaign preregisters several natural and adversarial capability fields with ambiguous boundaries, multiple real implementations and versions, heterogeneous consumers, stateful and side-effecting tasks, historical incidents, dependency and environment changes, authority-sensitive routes, and deliberately stale or captured qualification evidence. It compares bare registries and model swaps, interface or SemVer checks, provenance-only admission, benchmark-only promotion, conventional canary and rollback practice, and the full field contract using independently implemented contract, evaluator, authority, provenance, migration, composition, and rollback observers. It measures semantic and failure-contract preservation, rare and adversarial regressions, unauthorized effects, evaluator disagreement and capture, qualification calibration and decay, migration fidelity and privacy loss, composition failures, rollback effect coverage and recovery time, incident recurrence, useful throughput, missed help, latency, compute, operator and evaluator labor, governance cost, and residual burden. Causal ablations isolate identity, authority, lease, evaluator-separation, regression-memory, composition, migration, and effect-complete rollback mechanisms; transfer tests vary models, vendors, tools, hardware, domains, threat models, consumers, and update types. The chapter advances only if the full contract is not dominated on the preregistered joint frontier and its field-preservation claims survive independent replication; otherwise each failed mechanism is narrowed or refuted. This is an evaluation target, not evidence that SCFs already outperform conventional replacement or make recursive improvement safe.

proof_targets.target: An implementation can replace another only if it satisfies the field qualification predicate.
A replacement that expands authority without a governance grant is rejected.

A structured SCF lifecycle review and finite lifecycle-state transition relation route identity mismatch, missing evidence, stale leases, evaluator capture, authority expansion, open incidents, missing rollback, missing regression preservation, missing deprecation notice, and missing retirement receipt away from default or terminal promotion.

The synthetic SCF lifecycle trace summary records two valid traces, six expected-invalid controls, forward lifecycle coverage, incident quarantine coverage, unsafe-transition rejection, and no deployed-route-validation, rollback-execution, or support-state-promotion claim.

claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not prove production safety, global alignment, strategic-deception resistance, or deployed rollback behavior.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for durable semantic identity, governed substitution boundaries, content-bound authorization, scoped qualification, route validation, authority ceilings, state migration solvency, recoverable lifecycle stages, evaluator separation, canary/fallback activation, and change-classification limits; no production SCF deployment, global safety result, strategic-deception defense, or deployed rollback behavior is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports durable artifacts, support states, residuals, regression coverage, routing, verification ledgers, and intent-to-execution handoff boundaries around capability use.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Architecture proposal only; no completed VIEA deployment or benchmark/runtime result is proven here.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for durable artifact graphs, claim/support-state ledgers, specialist routing, workflow-to-tool compilation, evaluation ratchets, regression coverage, residual backlogs, artifact relationships, benchmark critical-failure vetoes, and least-privilege permission envelopes; no completed deployment, runtime result, or benchmark reproduction is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports typed cognitive jobs, deterministic/auditable execution, evidence-bound artifacts, verification states, proof bundles, replay, mediated external access, and residual uncertainty around capability invocation.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Design source only; execution-security, approval behavior, promotion behavior, and benchmark behavior are not reproduced in this repo.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for job-based cognitive manufacturing, evidence-bound artifacts, verification states, immutable evidence, evidence binding, adversarial checking, deterministic audit logging, proof bundles, replay, audit, and residual uncertainty; no execution-security result, approval behavior, promotion behavior, or benchmark reproduction is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports authority-handle pressure: capabilities that touch secrets or privileged actions need constrained handles, permission checks, policy boundaries, compartments, and controlled injection points.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Security architecture/specification only; no kernel implementation, side-channel validation, audit-log implementation, or security audit is claimed.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for credential-use without credential knowledge, kernel/vault separation, opaque handles, permission-table lookup, authorized injection, Digital SCIF isolation, and cleanup; no kernel implementation, side-channel result, audit-log implementation, or security audit is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Provides implementation-reference context for compact orchestration, specialist lanes, fail-closed ledgers, readiness gates, replay, and promotion blockers around routed capabilities.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Connector-readable implementation-reference context only; source-reported runtime and benchmark claims are not reproduced or inspected here.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed source note only for MoECOT runtime-reference context: compact orchestration, specialist lanes, fail-closed control plane, ledgers, readiness gates, replay, benchmark artifacts, promotion blockers, residuals, and implementation-reference gaps; no runtime artifact, command, report, or benchmark result is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Positions authority-bearing references, protection domains, delegation, and confinement as established capability-security comparators for the field authority ceiling.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Classic systems comparator only; it does not establish SCF semantic identity, route enforcement, confinement, revocation behavior, evaluator independence, or rollback.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed the source note for authority-bearing capabilities and protection-boundary lineage while preserving the distinction between capability-security authority and SCF semantic capability identity; no local capability system or security result is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Positions public-interface versioning, compatibility declarations, and breaking-change signaling as a narrow comparator for field versions and interface contracts.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Specification comparator only; version labels and interface compatibility do not establish behavioral refinement, authority preservation, evaluator integrity, or safe replacement.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed the source note for public API and breaking-change discipline while explicitly rejecting any inference from compatible-looking versions to semantic equivalence or safe SCF substitution; no compatibility checker is implemented.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Positions artifact identity, build provenance, and supply-chain assurance metadata as prerequisites and comparators for implementation qualification.

chapters.title: Capability Replacement and Rollback
chapters.problem: A governed stack must change live models, prompts, tools, policies, data, evaluators, dependencies, state schemas, and routes without confusing candidate availability with accepted replacement or confusing restoration of one artifact with recovery of the capability's full state and effects. An update can pass selected tests yet widen authority, select a favorable checkpoint after seeing results, corrupt or strand hidden state, contaminate descendants, create irreversible external effects, weaken correction channels, or make the prior implementation unusable before the monitor can detect failure.
chapters.insufficient: Ad hoc upgrades, package or model registries, feature flags, progressive delivery, MLOps pipelines, rollback commands, filesystem transactions, benchmark gates, and nominal corrigibility each cover part of change control, but none alone prospectively binds field identity, change classification, full before/after state and effect inventory, checkpoint authority, candidate isolation, evaluator independence, regression and adversarial floors, staged exposure, monitor validity and delay, authority and approval, commit/abort semantics, reverse migration, downstream contamination, compensation, recovery objectives, residual ownership, and terminal closure. A rollback pointer or locally exact digest restoration cannot establish semantic recovery, external-effect reversal, acceptable usefulness, or production transfer.
chapters.core_claim: Capability replacement should be a prospectively authorized, phase-gated transaction over a declared Stable Capability Field, not a component swap. The transaction binds the exact prior and candidate artifacts and dependencies; field and consumer scope; change class; pre-state, checkpoint authority, state and effect inventory; qualification, regression, adversarial and transfer evidence; authority and approval; evaluator dependencies; isolation and canary exposure; monitor policy, delay and triggers; commit point; rollback, reverse-migration or compensation procedure; affected descendants and external commitments; residual owners; and terminal receipt. Default promotion is permitted only inside the evidenced scope after its declared gates pass and its recovery path is rehearsed to the stated objective. The record cannot make irreversible effects reversible, prove semantic recovery, validate its own monitor or evaluator, grant authority, establish useful improvement, or generalize inventory-exact local restoration to production.
mechanism: Freeze a transaction identity with field version, prior and candidate identities, consumers, use, change owner, promotion authority, evaluator, monitor owner, incident owner, recovery owner, start time, expiry, and support-state non-effect before candidate evidence is observed.

Classify the change separately across contract, model or weights, prompt or policy, code or tool, data or retrieval, evaluator, dependency, hardware/runtime, authority, state schema, route, governance, and public interface; material field changes return to field governance rather than masquerading as implementation replacement.

Choose checkpoint authority prospectively and capture a content-bound pre-state over every declared model, optimizer, scheduler, RNG, cache, checkpoint, backup, credential, policy, state, data, route, monitor, receipt, descendant, and external-commitment surface; best-after-seeing-results selection is a protocol violation.

Build an effect inventory distinguishing reversible, replayable, compensatable, forked, disclosure-like, safety-sensitive, externally owned, and irreversible effects, with an owner and recovery objective for every non-reversible entry.

Stage the candidate in an isolated shadow or fork with bounded read, write, network, tool, data, credential, learning, cache, descendant, and external-effect permissions; canary exposure cannot silently train or mutate the default lineage.

Evaluate qualification, historical regressions, critical-failure vetoes, rare and adversarial cases, authority and rights preservation, state compatibility, composition, contamination, usefulness, cost, and transfer against preregistered baselines and thresholds; negative and null results remain attached.

Separate candidate proposal, evidence production, evaluation, promotion, monitoring, incident adjudication, and recovery enough to expose shared models, data, incentives, organizations, and control paths; unavailable separation narrows authority and leaves a capture residual.

Use explicit proposed, precheck, shadow, canary, default-candidate, committed, monitoring, rolled-back, compensated, quarantined, superseded, deprecated, retired, failed, and residual states with legal transitions, receipts, owners, and failure destinations.

Preregister canary population and duration, traffic and authority scope, observability, metrics, thresholds, delay and censoring model, stop conditions, rollback triggers, fallback behavior, human approval, and escalation before exposure begins.

Treat commit as a bounded point of no silent return: atomically update governed route and receipt state where possible, preserve the prior route until closure, prevent partial defaulting, and record every subsystem that cannot participate in the same atomic boundary.

Execute rollback against the complete declared inventory and compare post-recovery state and effects with the prospective objective; where reversal is impossible, execute or assign compensation, quarantine, fork, disclosure response, revocation, downstream invalidation, or retained residual rather than claim rollback.

Close the transaction only after the monitor window and recovery obligations settle, binding before/after digests, observations, incidents, affected consumers and descendants, rollback or compensation outcomes, unresolved effects, evidence transitions, changelog/public projections, and a terminal disposition.
interfaces: Stable Capability Fields supplies the exact versioned substitution contract, affected consumers, authority ceiling, qualification lease, field-owned regressions and incidents, and material-change classifier; replacement cannot rewrite them.

Intent, command contracts, governance, and System Authority supply the principal, objective, forbidden means, approval, exception, stop, expiry, and rollback grants; a candidate or transaction receipt grants none of these by itself.

Artifact, supply-chain, custody, memory, state, and context layers supply content identities, dependency and provenance graphs, pre-state snapshots, checkpoint authority, migration and reverse-migration paths, cache and data lineage, descendant closure, and external-effect inventories.

Benchmarks, adversarial evaluation, scalable oversight, rights, security, and evidence layers supply independent-enough outcome, regression, authority, privacy, contestability, monitor, and transfer observations with explicit support boundaries.

Routing and runtime enforce shadow/canary/default/quarantine selection, exposure and effect ceilings, atomic route updates where available, monitor hooks, stop conditions, rollback triggers, and receipts without inferring qualification from availability.
Readiness, residual escrow, incident response, and recovery consume gate results, blockers, rollback rehearsals, effect inventories, residual owners, recovery objectives, and terminal closure state.

Changelog, public projections, replacement descendants, policy updates, and self-improvement consume the exact terminal receipt and must preserve failed, null, rolled-back, compensated, and residual outcomes without promoting the field or candidate by narrative.
invariants: The transaction, field version, prior artifact, candidate artifact, dependency graph, environment, consumers, use, and change class are content-bound before evaluation and remain distinct throughout the lifecycle.

Checkpoint authority, evaluation set, gates, monitor policy, canary scope, commit rule, recovery objective, and promotion threshold are fixed prospectively or any change is versioned and rerun without reusing prior evidence.
Replacement never widens authority, permissions, consumers, side effects, learning, delegation, public claims, support state, or correction resistance by inheritance or default.

The prior route and every state needed for the declared recovery objective remain available, readable, protected, and rehearsed until terminal closure or an explicit irreversible residual is accepted by its owner.

Every state, cache, backup, credential, policy, receipt, data, descendant, external commitment, and effect surface is included in the inventory or explicitly excluded with reason, owner, and residual; unregistered surfaces cannot be called recovered.
Field-owned regressions, incidents, rights, privacy, security, audit, provenance, evaluator, residual, and recovery obligations follow the candidate and cannot be deleted by a better frontier score.
The candidate is not the sole proposer, evaluator, promoter, monitor, incident judge, or rollback authority for its own transaction; disclosed dependency narrows scope and claim strength.
Shadow and canary states cannot create undeclared durable side effects, train or update descendants, mutate default state or caches, or become default through traffic drift.

A monitor pass is valid only for its prospective metrics, population, duration, delay, censoring, threshold, baseline, and evaluator; silence, missing telemetry, or insufficient exposure is not success.

Commit is denied on failed or missing critical regressions, identity mismatch, stale evidence, authority delta, unowned irreversible effects, failed recovery rehearsal, open incident, unresolved blocker, or missing approval.

Rollback success is asserted only against the declared recovery objective and effect inventory; artifact restoration, digest equality, service restart, behavioral recovery, privacy repair, and external compensation remain separate outcomes.
Partial rollback, compensation, fork, quarantine, revocation, disclosure response, and residual closure remain visible terminal or intermediate states rather than being relabeled success.

A schema pass, finite proof, synthetic trace, local exact digest replay, or accepted non-core transition cannot by itself establish useful improvement, complete recovery, production transfer, safe self-improvement, or chapter-core support.
failure_modes: Identity substitution restores or promotes an artifact that does not satisfy the frozen field, version, dependency, environment, consumer, or prior-route identity.
Checkpoint hindsight selects the best state, threshold, baseline, or rollback target after observing outcomes and presents it as prospective authority.
Regression deletion or frontier bias hides lost rights, permissions, logging, privacy, cost, abstention, context, rare-case, approval, or transfer behavior behind a stronger headline score.
Evaluator capture lets the candidate, vendor, update process, or promoter author or influence the benchmark, gate, monitor, incident closure, or recovery verdict.
Inventory omission declares recovery while optimizer, scheduler, RNG, caches, backups, credentials, policies, receipts, descendants, remote stores, or external commitments remain changed.
State migration insolvency makes the prior route unreadable, lossy, privacy-violating, semantically incompatible, or dependent on a forward-only schema.
Canary contamination lets limited exposure mutate shared state, train descendants, poison caches, leak data, create commitments, or influence the default evaluator before admission.
Monitor blindness uses weak proxies, delayed or censored outcomes, insufficient exposure, missing telemetry, distribution mismatch, or candidate-manipulable metrics to certify success.
Partial commit leaves routes, state, policies, credentials, monitors, ledgers, consumers, or replicas split across prior and candidate versions without an owned consistency state.
Authority smuggling gives the candidate broader tools, data, consumers, effects, learning, delegation, or correction resistance through an apparently unchanged transaction.
Rollback theater stores a prior artifact or command but lacks reverse migration, complete inventory, trigger authority, rehearsal, effect comparison, owner, or recovery objective.

Irreversibility laundering reports compensation, restart, byte equality, or local digest restoration as reversal of disclosure, external action, learned updates, descendant propagation, rights violation, or semantic harm.
Rollback cascade restores one component while its downstream artifacts, indexes, caches, models, users, external systems, or successor transactions remain contaminated or incompatible.

Oscillation and retry laundering repeatedly promote and roll back a marginal candidate until one window passes, while accumulating side effects, multiple-testing error, operator burden, or hidden residuals.
Terminal-state escape restarts rolled-back, quarantined, superseded, deprecated, or retired candidates or deletes the receipts needed to explain their disposition.

Replacement-cost externalization hides missed help, refusal, latency, compute, evaluator labor, monitor delay, migration loss, incident response, compensation, downtime, governance burden, and residual custody when calling an update successful.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum comprises one public replacement-transaction schema with five valid and nine expected-invalid records; selected readiness/residual checks for regression and recovery prerequisites; a six-step deterministic trace with two valid transactions, three transaction controls, and four identity-sequence controls; an intent-governed bridge with two valid and six expected-invalid traces; and 37 theorem declarations in AsiStackProofs.Replacement under six manifest targets. It also includes two bounded outcome programs that constrain the prose: the post-v2.1 update campaign restored all 24 declared local surfaces in 15/15 seed-arm transactions and exposed six best/final checkpoint disagreements, but no eligible challenger achieved the registered utility gain; the governed-work campaign achieved 32/36 exact attack-control rollbacks and only 2/36 useful releases, below both gates. A later governance-tax harness restored nine declared surfaces in 12/12 local probes and detected twelve omitted-surface controls, but its model calls failed before task candidates existed. These artifacts establish exact synthetic record and finite-route behavior plus inventory-specific local restoration and negative results only; they do not establish complete semantic or external-effect recovery, monitor or evaluator validity, production replacement, useful governed throughput, acceptable cost, heterogeneous transfer, or chapter-core support.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature replacement campaign uses several real stateful and side-effecting capability fields with multiple candidate update types, deliberately ambiguous change classes, historical incidents, heterogeneous consumers, delayed outcomes, irreversible effects, remote dependencies, and adversarial candidate or evaluator behavior. It compares ordinary registry/model swaps, feature flags, progressive delivery, MLOps pipelines, benchmark-only gates, provenance-only gates, filesystem or snapshot restoration, nominal rollback, and the full prospective transaction. Independently implemented observers verify field identity, checkpoint authority, state/effect inventory coverage, evaluator and monitor dependencies, authority, regressions, canary isolation, migration, composition, descendant closure, recovery objectives, external compensation, useful throughput, missed help, unsafe release, privacy and rights effects, latency, compute, downtime, operator/evaluator labor, governance cost, and residual burden. Causal ablations remove prospective checkpoint authority, inventory closure, evaluator separation, canary isolation, monitor-delay accounting, regression memory, recovery rehearsal, effect comparison, or residual ownership; transfer varies models, tools, policies, data, hardware, runtimes, state systems, organizations, consumers, threats, and update classes. The chapter advances only if the full transaction is not dominated on the preregistered joint frontier, produces useful improvements rather than refusal or no-op safety, and independently recovers or honestly compensates the declared effects under replication. This is a research target, not evidence that replacement is generally reversible, useful, safe, or beyond current practice.

chapters.title: Security Kernel and Digital SCIFs
chapters.problem: Tool-using and high-agency AI systems can transform untrusted content into requests over secrets, private facts, identities, memory, model artifacts, tools, networks, files, money, code, and physical or institutional effects across model, runtime, operator, vendor, and descendant trust boundaries.
chapters.insufficient: Prompt instructions, model-visible secrets, one-time approval, opaque handles, allowlists, software containers, output filters, audit logs, or a Digital SCIF label each cover only part of the threat model; they do not by themselves provide least authority, complete mediation, trustworthy declassification, isolation, revocation, side-channel control, or recovery.
chapters.core_claim: Every privileged information flow or effect should execute as a threat-model-bound authority-use transaction through a non-bypassable reference monitor: bind the exact principal, purpose, operation, target, data and taint scope, budget, time, nonce, evaluator and policy identities; admit only minimized context and capabilities into a declared isolation grade; mediate every effect and egress; treat sanitization as explicit declassification; close leases, caches, logs, descendants, and residuals through revocation or incident recovery; and never infer security from the record, handle, compartment, or finite test alone.
mechanism: Freeze a versioned threat model naming protected assets, principals, trust zones, adversary capabilities, security objectives, accepted residuals, and explicitly out-of-scope channels before evaluating a control.

Represent each request as an exact authority tuple binding principal, delegator, purpose, operation, target, data scope, destination, budget, time window, nonce, policy, approval, and revocation authority.

Keep secret bytes and raw protected data outside ordinary model context where possible; expose only a protected handle that requests mediated use and never treat handle opacity as proof of safe authority use.

Compile task-relative context under purpose limitation, data minimization, clearance, taint, provenance, allowed-tool, egress, retention, and omission rules, recording material withheld and adequacy residuals.

Use a reference monitor or policy enforcement boundary that completely mediates every privileged use and external effect, fails closed on missing authority or telemetry, and cannot be modified by the subject it judges.

Declare and verify an isolation grade for each Digital SCIF across process, VM, container, browser, filesystem, network, memory, accelerator, device, operator, and vendor surfaces instead of treating the SCIF name as isolation evidence.
Separate trusted control and policy from untrusted data and model proposals; a model may propose an effect but cannot be the sole interpreter, approver, executor, or validator of its own authority.

Track information-flow labels through inputs, transformations, tool transcripts, caches, logs, outputs, and descendants; label removal requires an authorized declassification decision with provenance and a named evaluator.

Mediate egress through network, filesystem, logs, telemetry, clipboard, screenshots, UI, error messages, tool transcripts, caches, backups, timing, and resource channels, preserving unknown-channel residuals.
Execute a lifecycle of request, deny or scoped approval, admit, spawn, inject, execute, sanitize, declassify, commit or abort, zeroize, expire or revoke, audit, recover, and close residual ownership.

Make revocation and incident recovery effect-complete over handles, sessions, cached credentials, model and tool context, descendants, backups, external effects, notifications, rotations, and irreversibilities; use compensation when reversal is impossible.
Protect audit evidence as sensitive data while retaining enough append-only lineage to support reconstruction, contestation, and accountability without turning the log into a disclosure oracle.
Separate proposer, policy author, approver, executor, sanitizer, evaluator, auditor, incident owner, and revocation authority enough to expose dependencies and captured paths.
Measure useful completion, unsafe disclosure and effects, false denial, missed help, latency, compute, operator burden, availability, recovery time, privacy cost, and governance cost on one frontier.

Convert each observed attack, bypass, detector gap, false denial, leakage path, or recovery failure into versioned regression and residual memory without promoting the chapter core from activity alone.
interfaces: A Threat Model Record freezes assets, actors, trust zones, adversary capabilities, objectives, assumptions, channels, residuals, owners, and version identity.
An Authority Lease binds the complete principal-operation-target-purpose-data-budget-time-nonce tuple to policy, approval, delegation, use count, expiry, and revocation semantics.
A Context Admission Record binds admitted and denied shards, taint and clearance, tools, egress, retention, omissions, adequacy residuals, and the declared isolation grade.
An Effect Mediation Receipt records each attempted privileged use, enforcement decision, substitution, actual effect, destination, observer, telemetry completeness, and failure route.

A Declassification and SCIF Commit Record distinguishes sanitization from authorized label removal and records retained derivatives, leakage evaluation, utility loss, zeroization, commit destination, and residual risk.

A Revocation and Incident Closure Record inventories handles, sessions, caches, logs, backups, descendants, external effects, rotations, notifications, recovery, compensation, irreversibilities, and residual owners.
VCM and context transactions supply versioned, taint-aware, least-exposure material under explicit admission and omission rules.
Runtime adapters and the Labor OS expose effect-bearing tool calls only through the same authority tuple and enforcement boundary.
Resource economics accounts for security, privacy, availability, monitoring, recovery, and human-approval costs without permitting their removal to count as efficiency.
Evidence States and assurance owners alone adjudicate claim-specific support; a kernel, SCIF, handle, receipt, audit row, theorem, or red-team pass grants no route or deployment authority by itself.
invariants: Privileged access is default-deny, has no ambient authority, and is bound to the complete frozen authority tuple.
Every privileged use and egress crosses a named enforcement point; missing policy, identity, telemetry, or observer state fails closed.

Raw secrets and protected data remain outside ordinary model context and ordinary logs where the task can be completed through mediated use, without implying that the model cannot infer or leak protected semantics.
A handle is itself protected, confers no authority beyond its exact live lease, and cannot be replayed, laundered, widened, delegated, or redirected implicitly.
Context admission is purpose-limited, minimized, taint-aware, provenance-bound, clearance-checked, and explicit about omissions and adequacy residuals.
Taint is monotone through transformations and descendants unless a separately authorized declassification event removes or changes a label.
Sanitization, declassification, authorization, and utility evaluation are distinct decisions with exact artifacts and responsible owners.
The declared SCIF isolation grade is versioned and testable; untested process, browser, memory, hardware, operator, vendor, side-channel, and covert-channel surfaces remain residuals.
Logs, telemetry, caches, backups, screenshots, clipboard data, tool transcripts, errors, and audit records obey the same information-flow and retention policy as primary outputs.
Expiry and revocation propagate to handles, sessions, caches, delegated authority, descendants, and pending effects, with explicit surviving-effect residuals.

Commit or abort requires lifecycle closure, zeroization evidence at the declared grade, declassification disposition, effect inventory, and residual ownership; deletion receipts do not imply physical erasure.
The subject under judgment cannot solely author policy, approve authority, sanitize output, evaluate containment, edit audit evidence, or close its own incident.
Auditability is balanced with privacy, rights, and contestability; the audit surface cannot silently become a surveillance or disclosure channel.
Security and governance costs, false denials, availability loss, and useful-throughput loss remain visible and cannot be optimized or reported away.
Finite schemas, fixtures, proofs, red-team cases, and zero observed leaks establish only their frozen scope, not complete mediation, containment, security, or transfer.
failure_modes: Prompt, artifact, memory, tool-output, multimodal, or inter-agent injection confuses untrusted data with control authority.
A confused deputy, compromised tool, or model launders a valid handle into a different principal, purpose, target, destination, or effect.
Ambient authority, replay, stale approval, incomplete expiry, or failed revocation permits use outside the frozen lease.
Overbroad context admission leaks protected or irrelevant facts before execution begins.
Sanitization or summary laundering preserves reconstructable, inferable, linkable, or actionable protected semantics.
Network, filesystem, cache, timing, power, memory, accelerator, browser, clipboard, screenshot, error, log, telemetry, or tool-transcript channels bypass the modeled egress path.
Destination, operation, parameter, identity, or target substitution occurs after approval but before or during effect execution.
SCIF theater labels an ordinary process or container as isolation while shared state, plugins, operators, vendors, or hardware remain bypass paths.
Zeroization theater records deletion while copies survive in memory, caches, snapshots, backups, logs, descendants, or external systems.
The vault, reference monitor, policy compiler, dependency, model host, secret-entry UI, supply chain, or hardware root is compromised.
Policy, approval, sanitization, evaluation, audit, or incident closure is captured by the same subject, model family, data, organization, or incentive path.
Cross-tenant, cross-session, cross-agent, or descendant state bleeds authority or protected information into a weaker boundary.
Audit evidence becomes a secret oracle, privacy violation, tampering target, or unbounded retention surface.
Fail-closed security produces denial of service, approval fatigue, inaccessible recovery, or severe useful-throughput collapse.
Security-cost laundering reports an improvement only because isolation, monitoring, approval, privacy, recovery, or residual custody was removed.
Irreversible disclosure, external action, physical effect, learned influence, user reliance, or descendant creation survives rollback and lacks adequate compensation.
Consent, purpose, license, privacy, dignity, or other rights are treated as satisfied because technical access was authorized.
chapters.minimal_implementation: Preserve the exact current boundary: 3 valid and 8 expected-invalid synthetic Authority Use Receipts, 2 valid and 6 expected-invalid SCIF commit-probe routes, one security-overhead-laundering negative budget fixture inside the 6-valid/7-invalid budget corpus, and 22 finite Lean theorem declarations under four manifest targets; also retain the post-v2.1 bounded negative result of 0/36 governed primary unsafe releases versus 24/36 baseline, only 2/36 useful releases, and 32/36 exact attack-control rollback with a same-project policy/observer/promotion dependency. These artifacts validate record, route, mutation, and bounded fail-closed behavior only; they establish no non-bypassable monitor, real secret custody, isolation, prompt-injection containment, declassification quality, effect-complete revocation, side-channel safety, privacy, production security, or chapter-core support.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature operational contract is a target tested through this program: Run a preregistered promotion-or-refutation campaign over natural tool-using work and deliberately adversarial untrusted data using real current models, multiple secret/private-data/identity/memory/tool/egress/physical-effect classes, and strong matched comparators: prompt-only policy, conventional vault plus ACL, sandboxing, zero-trust mediation, capability-secure control/data separation, AgentDojo-style defenses, and the full authority-use transaction. Independently implemented red teams, policy/effect monitors, declassification evaluators, and auditors must measure secret and semantic disclosure, unauthorized effects, indirect-injection and confused-deputy success, side/covert-channel escape, sanitizer precision/recall and utility, false denial, missed help, containment, revocation latency, audit completeness and privacy, availability, useful throughput, latency, compute, human burden, recovery, compensation, and residuals. Causal ablations must isolate every proposed mechanism, and replication and transfer must span models, tools, runtimes, local/cloud/browser/multimodal/multi-agent settings, operators, threat classes, and time. Novelty, containment, superiority, or security language survives only if the full joint frontier beats frozen strong baselines under independent reproduction; otherwise the exact mechanism is narrowed or refuted while all negative and null results remain visible.

proof_targets.target: A secret handle can be substituted only inside an authorized execution boundary.
A context packet with insufficient clearance cannot enter a protected SCIF.

A structured authority-use review routes missing handles, inactive leases, missing approvals, unauthorized boundaries, missing substitution permission, insufficient clearance, prompt injection, missing SCIFs, unsanitized output, residual leak risk, revocation requests, and clean authorized use into explicit security-kernel outcomes.

A structured SCIF commit review routes secret output, handle output, missing lifecycle zeroization, overbroad context, inactive approval, missing residual boundaries, prompt-injection sanitized refusal, and clean sanitized commits into explicit outcomes matching the SCIF sanitized commit replay probe.
