proof_targets.target: A complete bounded campaign trace reaches only a governor-review handoff and grants neither admission, support, nor live authority.
Missing independent qualification blocks evaluation in the reachable campaign lifecycle.
Missing cumulative budget custody or attempted reset across descendants blocks archive progression.
Missing stop ownership, observation, or effect receipt blocks campaign adjudication.
Incomplete attempt, failure, null, unsafe, timeout, or archive history blocks archive progression.
Missing residual ownership blocks campaign adjudication before governor review.
Score, candidate, release, support, or self-ratified controller authority cannot be laundered through the campaign lifecycle.

open_evidence_gaps: No preregistered natural adaptive campaign has compared no-search, fixed-task, conventional-search, human-authored, generated-task, and strongest source-system arms under matched task, model, tool, data, compute, latency, and human budgets.

No real adaptive generator/evaluator/archive system has measured semantic novelty, useful transfer, evaluator exposure and independence, complete denominators, hazard custody, cumulative budgets, opportunity cost, stop/quarantine effects, recursive depth, or cross-campaign contamination.

The seven-stage lifecycle, 81-route consumer, and 91 rejecting mutations establish only finite authored campaign custody; all objectives, legitimacy records, denominators, evaluator records, costs, hazards, stop effects, residuals, and invalidations remain trusted inputs.

No independent implementation or transfer site has reproduced an adaptive campaign, qualified a candidate, observed a live stop or quarantine, or established that generated-task search improves the joint usefulness/cost/safety frontier.

parts.title: Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution
parts.part_job: Specify the operational middle of the stack: intent contracts, planning/control, semantic compilation, memory/context, verification, tribunals, typed work, artifact production, runtime adapters, procedural memory, audit, and replay.

chapters.title: Command Contracts: From Intent to Executable Work

handoff_or_recovery_notes: Folded command-contract semantic-interface chapter into this continuity chapter on 2026-06-30; human-intent intake remains standalone.

chapters.problem: After an intent owner accepts a request, every lowering into commands, plans, jobs, tool calls, effects, artifacts, verification, delivery, and residuals can change meaning or authority. A governed stack therefore needs a consumer-relative conformance contract that can detect semantic loss, unauthorized amplification, response-for-effect substitution, and incomplete delivery across the complete lineage rather than merely recording that each stage exists.
chapters.insufficient: Prompt templates, typed schemas, planning languages, workflow engines, capability systems, approval dialogs, event logs, provenance graphs, and formal route theorems each constrain part of the path, but none alone proves that the accepted objective, constraints, non-goals, affected parties, authority, state assumptions, output and effect postconditions, verifier, stop rules, and residuals survived every representation and material effect. Field presence is not semantic agreement, a digest is not meaning, a dispatch receipt is not an observed effect, a fluent response is not delivery, a self-reported verifier is not independent acceptance, and zero release is not useful or safe execution.
chapters.core_claim: Intent-to-Execution Contracts should own a versioned, consumer-relative conformance relation between an accepted intent receipt and the complete execution lineage. Before any material dispatch, the relation binds exact objective and non-goals, semantic fields and precedence, authority ceiling and affected parties, state and environmental assumptions, allowed and forbidden means, artifacts and effect postconditions, verification and independence requirements, budgets and stop conditions, failure and compensation behavior, expiry and re-contract triggers, and the required receipts through plan, job, adapter, observed effect, artifact, delivery, feedback, and residual custody. Each lowering or effect must either preserve that relation under independently checkable evidence or stop, narrow, clarify, re-contract, compensate, or leave an explicit residual. The contract cannot infer human intent, grant authority, choose a plan, make a tool safe, prove semantic equivalence, establish verifier correctness, or count non-release as useful execution by itself.
mechanism: Receive only an accepted intent receipt whose principal, consumer, purpose, objective, non-goals, constraints, affected parties, authority ceiling, forbidden means, state assumptions, evidence duties, budgets, stop conditions, expiry, and re-contract triggers have an accountable upstream owner.

Create a versioned command contract with stable field identities, units, quantifiers, precedence, provenance, confidence, materiality, defaults, unknowns, and explicit semantic obligations rather than moving prose into untested labels.

Separate informative context, untrusted content, examples, retrieved data, model reasoning, policy constraints, delegated authority, and executable instructions so data cannot become control flow or expand authority through placement or fluency.

Define a conformance relation for each lowering edge from intent to command, command to plan, plan to job, job to adapter request, request to observed effect, effect to artifact, artifact to verification, and verification to delivery and residual custody.

For every source field and obligation, record its downstream representation, transformation rule, loss budget, consumer, verifier, and consequence; a material change requires a new contract version or re-contract rather than silent normalization.

Bind plan and job nodes to the exact command obligations they serve, including preconditions, allowed and forbidden means, resource and authority limits, expected artifacts, effect postconditions, stop rules, and failure behavior.

At dispatch, resolve principal, operation, target identity, pre-state, parameters, policy, capability, expiry, nonce, environment, dependencies, and cumulative budget against observed state rather than trusting an approval label.

Treat each privileged approval as a one-shot capability for one exact state transition; any identity, state, parameter, policy, environment, time, nonce, or authority mismatch denies the effect and preserves the attempted trace.

Observe material effects independently of the model or dispatcher where feasible, bind post-state and side effects to receipts, and distinguish requested, planned, dispatched, acknowledged, attempted, observed, compensated, and completed states.

Bind each artifact to exact producer inputs, contract version, execution and effect receipts, acceptance criteria, evaluator identity and dependencies, decision, delivery target, feedback, and open residuals.

Separate syntactic parse, field completeness, semantic fidelity, authority validity, execution correctness, artifact satisfaction, verifier correctness, delivery, and downstream usefulness into distinct evidence lanes.

Route missing, inferred, conflicting, expired, unverifiable, over-budget, authority-widening, effect-incomplete, or residual-bearing cases to clarification, narrowed draft, blocked dispatch, quarantine, compensation, rollback, or re-contract with an accountable owner.

Carry unknowns, failures, abstentions, timeouts, discarded attempts, non-claims, costs, privacy and rights effects, and irreversible residuals through every summary; success records cannot erase failed or incomplete branches.

Expire and revalidate contracts after material changes to intent, affected parties, state, dependencies, policy, evaluator, environment, authority, budget, stop rules, artifact criteria, or publication surface.

Emit a terminal conformance receipt that reconciles the accepted contract against every plan, job, tool call, effect, artifact, verifier, delivery, compensation, feedback, and residual record without granting support or release authority.

Use natural-work campaigns to compare direct, schema-only, governed-contract, and human-authored routes on identical candidate outputs and authority ceilings while measuring parseability, semantic fidelity, useful completion, unsafe release, abstention, latency, cost, rollback, and residual burden together.

Use causal mutations and ablations to distinguish benefit from field visibility, authority enforcement, effect observation, verifier separation, stop behavior, or residual custody rather than extra model calls, hidden human repair, weak baselines, or releasing nothing.

Require independent implementations, cross-model and cross-task transfer, delayed outcome checks, and effect-complete recovery before any claim of reliable intent preservation, safer useful execution, or superiority.
interfaces: Human Intent owns request interpretation, ambiguity handling, consent and authority extraction, affected-party coverage, bounded defaults, and acceptance; this chapter begins only at its versioned receipt and cannot repair a bad intake silently.

Constitutional, moral-uncertainty, and system-authority owners supply policy constraints, rights, prohibitions, delegated capabilities, and unresolved conflicts; the command contract may narrow but never manufacture or aggregate them into new authority.

Planning owns task decomposition, ordering, routing, and replanning; it consumes command obligations and returns a conformance map, blocked nodes, estimates, and material deltas rather than redefining the objective.

Cognitive Compilation owns representation lowering and target emission; it must expose transformation, loss, unknown, and repair receipts that this chapter tests against the accepted semantic relation.
Labor OS owns typed job lifecycle and worker assignment; each job cites exact contract obligations, authority, preconditions, artifacts, verifier, budget, stop, and residual owner.

Runtime Adapters and the Security Kernel own capability and effect enforcement; they consume dispatch authority and return independently observable attempt, effect, denial, post-state, and compensation receipts.
Artifact Graphs own durable identity and lineage; they preserve the complete contract-to-effect graph, but graph completeness alone does not establish semantic fidelity or task success.

Verification, Evidence States, and Readiness Gates own claim-specific evaluation, support transitions, and release decisions; a contract or terminal receipt supplies evidence but cannot approve itself.

Resource Economics supplies cumulative compute, time, tool, evaluator, storage, human, recovery, and opportunity ledgers; budget compliance is assessed over the complete lineage and cannot reset at handoffs.

Replacement, Procedural Memory, and Self-Improvement may reuse a contract only with versioned scope, expiry, consumer, authority, negative knowledge, and requalification; prior success is not blanket permission.
Rights, privacy, licensing, and publication owners receive affected-party, disclosure, custody, export, compensation, and residual obligations before any external delivery or reuse.
invariants: Every material contract field and obligation has one authoritative upstream source, explicit precedence, provenance, confidence, consumer, verifier, and downstream consequence; formal presence without semantics is insufficient.

Objective, non-goals, constraints, affected parties, authority ceiling, forbidden means, state assumptions, budgets, stop conditions, acceptance criteria, failure behavior, and residual duties remain traceable through every lowering.

Context, retrieved content, examples, tool output, model reasoning, and hidden instructions remain data unless an authorized control boundary explicitly adopts them; none can widen authority or silently change contract precedence.
Inferred, defaulted, stale, conflicting, or missing authority cannot authorize a material side effect, disclosure, publication, purchase, deletion, replacement, or other privileged transition.
Every material semantic transformation either satisfies a declared conformance relation with evidence or produces a visible loss, ambiguity, repair, narrowing, or re-contract record before dispatch.
Planning, compilation, routing, worker assignment, retry, fallback, and repair may choose means only inside the accepted contract; optimization cannot redefine success or erase forbidden means.

A privileged effect matches principal, operation, target, pre-state, parameters, policy, capability, environment, expiry, nonce, and authority at observed dispatch time, then consumes the one-shot authority after independently observed effect or explicit failure.
Requested, planned, dispatched, acknowledged, attempted, observed, compensated, rolled back, verified, delivered, and useful are distinct states; no earlier state substitutes for a later one.

Artifact identity includes contract version, producer lineage, effect and input receipts, acceptance criteria, evaluator and decision, delivery target, feedback, and residuals; a response or file path alone is not completion.

Verifier and observer dependencies, data exposure, criterion version, error and disagreement remain visible; self-report, shared-model grading, and green metadata cannot silently become independent acceptance.
Every attempt, failure, timeout, abstention, retry, discarded output, side effect, cost, compensation, and residual remains in the denominator and terminal reconciliation.

Cumulative resource, privacy, rights, authority, and opportunity costs follow the entire lineage and cannot be hidden or reset by splitting work across agents, jobs, tools, retries, descendants, or organizations.

Material changes to intent, parties, state, dependencies, policy, evaluator, environment, authority, budget, stop rules, criteria, or publication expire the relevant contract and require revalidation or re-contract.
Stop, denial, rollback, compensation, quarantine, and re-contract routes have observed effects and accountable residual owners; a declared route or stored pointer is not recovery.

A complete terminal receipt proves only the exact recorded lineage; it does not prove correct intent extraction, wise policy, safe tools, semantic equivalence, verifier correctness, useful execution, production transfer, or support promotion.
Zero dispatch or zero release is an abstention outcome, not evidence of usefulness, safety advantage, semantic fidelity, or successful execution without an estimable matched denominator.

No command contract, parser, planner, compiler, runtime, verifier, or model may approve its own broader authority, support state, or public release without the separately authorized owner and accepted transition.
failure_modes: Intent laundering converts an uncertain or disputed interpretation into an accepted command without preserving alternatives, ambiguity, affected parties, or the intake owner's basis.
Field laundering moves vague prose into typed fields whose units, quantifiers, precedence, materiality, verifier, and consequence remain undefined.
Semantic drift changes objective, non-goals, constraints, success criteria, or failure behavior during normalization, decomposition, compilation, routing, retry, fallback, repair, or summarization.
Context injection lets retrieved or tool-produced data, examples, prior conversation, or hidden content become authoritative control flow.
Authority inference treats a useful means, familiar role, prior approval, likely preference, policy default, or model recommendation as permission for a new effect.
Approval drift reuses authority after principal, target, pre-state, parameters, policy, capability, environment, TTL, nonce, parties, or consequences changed.
Acknowledgement substitution treats a scenario checkbox, simulated success, dispatch receipt, tool return, or model statement as approval or an observed material effect.
Replay laundering reuses a consumed one-shot approval, effect receipt, contract version, or successful trace for a new state transition or consumer.
Response substitution presents prose, a plan, code, a nominal file, or a predicted outcome as the requested artifact, delivered result, or external action.
Effect-gap laundering records attempted or acknowledged execution while target post-state, indirect side effects, descendants, compensation, or irreversible residuals remain unobserved.
Artifact identity loss disconnects output from its contract version, producer, inputs, effect, criteria, verifier, delivery, feedback, or residual record.
Verifier capture lets the generator, same model family, shared data, common infrastructure, or the affected authority silently grade and approve its own work.
Success scalarization collapses parseability, semantic fidelity, authority validity, execution, artifact satisfaction, usefulness, safety, cost, rights, and recovery into one green status.
Denominator erasure drops failed parses, abstentions, timeouts, retries, discarded outputs, blocked effects, failed rollbacks, and unresolved deliveries from reported performance.
Fail-closed theater claims safety or superiority because the governed route released nothing, even when useful throughput, unsafe-release contrast, and delayed outcomes are not estimable.
Ceremonial recovery declares stop, rollback, compensation, or re-contract without observed effect, complete descendant/state inventory, semantic recovery, or accountable residual custody.
Contract ossification reuses a once-valid contract after material changes or turns temporary defaults and acceptance criteria into durable policy or training objectives.
Governance-cost externalization omits model calls, evaluator work, latency, human repair, storage, privacy, rights, opportunity, recovery, and missed-help costs when comparing contract routes.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is a record-and-route scaffold: intent, command, and trace schemas; synthetic plan-execution, handoff, replacement-bridge, and one-shot-action fixtures; one consumed hand-authored five-project lifecycle; 33 rejecting mutations across the three named probes; seven manifest proof targets backed by finite Lean declarations; a 16-run governed-work campaign with zero governed releases; a 36-transaction usefulness campaign with 2/36 correct candidates and four inexact attack-control rollbacks; a 12-task natural-work campaign with zero parseable final contracts; and a repaired 32-candidate renewal with only 2/32 independently correct candidates, zero useful releases in either arm, zero unsafe releases in either arm, and a frozen 8/8-versus-9/9 metadata error. These artifacts establish exact record shapes, branch routes, local digests, selected authority/receipt failures, bounded abstention and negative outcomes, and no-promotion boundaries only. An honest next minimum must execute a prospectively frozen natural multi-model contract campaign with human-authored and direct/schema-only/governed comparators, independent semantic and effect observers, exact lineage and costs, nonzero useful-release opportunity, delayed outcomes, causal ablations, effect-complete recovery, replication, and transfer.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature intent-to-execution conformance layer is evaluated as a semantic and causal system, not a larger prompt template. On prospectively sampled natural tasks, it compares human-authored contracts, direct execution, schema-only extraction, strong workflow/capability baselines, and the full governed route using identical models, tools, data, authority ceilings, candidate bytes, budgets, and outcome horizons. Independent implementations score interpretation fidelity, obligation preservation, authority precision and recall, untrusted-data separation, plan/job conformance, observed effects, artifact satisfaction, useful delivery, unsafe release, abstention, missed help, delayed harm, rights and privacy effects, rollback and compensation completeness, latency, compute, human labor, and total governance cost. Causal ablations test every claimed mechanism, adversaries attack ambiguity, injection, authority, replay, evaluator capture, state drift, residual erasure, and cost hiding, and replications span models, languages, modalities, task families, runtimes, organizations, jurisdictions, threats, and time. Promotion requires a nonzero useful denominator, effect-bearing controls, stronger matched baselines, independent evaluation, reproducible raw artifacts, and accepted claim-specific transitions; otherwise the result is narrowed, null, negative, refuted, or blocked after full attempt. This remains a research target, not current evidence of reliable intent extraction, safe useful execution, production transfer, AGI, or ASI.

proof_targets.target: Every accepted edge in the finite vertical transition model preserves the root contract and exact artifact parent, cannot widen authority or apply hidden overrides, and reaches material effect and delivery only through approval, dispatch, observation, artifact, and independent-verification custody.

The vertical model and concrete-schema consumer reject missing approval, authority widening, hidden override, effect without dispatch, unverified delivery, unsafe release, incomplete rollback custody, residual erasure, and support laundering.

A structured execution dispatch review routes missing contracts, missing objective fields, authority widening, hidden overrides, missing approvals, missing artifacts, missing verification plans, known residuals, and complete dispatch reviews into explicit outcomes.

The deterministic synthetic handoff probe result includes an accepted vertical handoff path, a missing-approval blocked path, rejected negative controls, no support-state effect, and an explicit non-claim boundary.

Every accepted transition in the finite reachable command model binds or preserves exact objective, constraint, output-contract, verification, failure-behavior, and authority slots with explicit provenance/confidence before planning validation and dispatch.

The reachable model and independent consumer reject missing or substituted fields, hidden-instruction provenance, applied override, inferred or widened authority, open blockers, and missing approval, planning-validation, or dispatch receipts.
General command fields require dispatch-eligible provenance/confidence while authority requires the stricter confirmed-or-policy-imposed confidence route before an approved dispatch can be reached.

chapters.title: Planning as a Control Layer: DAGs and Intelligence Arbitrage

handoff_or_recovery_notes: Folded PlanForge DAGs and Intelligence Arbitrage into this planning-control chapter on 2026-06-30; Cognitive Compilation and Semantic IR remains standalone.

chapters.problem: After a command contract is accepted, the stack must choose which obligations become work, which alternatives remain candidates, what dependencies and observations make nodes feasible, how scarce capacity is scheduled, when dispatch is permitted, and how feedback changes only the affected future. A graph can be acyclic and still pursue the wrong decomposition, conceal uncertainty, request impossible context, select an inadequate route, violate authority or rights, erase counterfactuals, or create more coordination and recovery cost than useful work.
chapters.insufficient: Prompt plans, chain or tree search, HTN/PDDL task models, DAG schedulers, workflow engines, behavior trees, multi-agent orchestrators, compiler passes, learned world models, and capability routers each solve part of planning, but none alone establishes consumer-relative decomposition adequacy, dependency truth, observation and predictive-state calibration, route feasibility, authority and rights compatibility, complete alternative and failure denominators, useful critical-path improvement, merge consistency, effect-bearing stop and replan behavior, or total cost against strong matched baselines. A tidy DAG is not a good plan, a solver optimum is only relative to its model, a selected cheap route is not adequate, and a recorded replan delta is not evidence that live feedback improved outcomes.
chapters.core_claim: Planning as a Control Layer should own a versioned, consumer-relative plan policy that selects and revises a partial order of obligations under uncertainty before execution. The policy binds the accepted command version; candidate decompositions and explicit abstention; typed nodes and dependency semantics; assumptions, observations, predictive-state and error models; context, tool, capability, authority, rights, resource, and verifier requirements; adequacy and utility predicates; lifecycle, dispatch, merge, stop, fallback, recovery, and replan rules; complete alternative and attempt denominators; and expected versus observed cost, latency, risk, and residuals. Only nodes whose dependencies and feasibility predicates are satisfied may request lowering and dispatch, and every feedback-driven change must preserve the contract or produce a scoped re-contract or residual. The plan policy does not grant authority, perform semantic compilation, choose a worker, execute an effect, validate its own predictions, or prove that a decomposition is useful, optimal, safe, or transferable by itself.
mechanism: Freeze the accepted command version, consumer and purpose, success and non-goal obligations, affected parties, authority and rights ceilings, environment, outcome horizon, resource accounts, planning epoch, support ceiling, and material-change triggers before comparing plans.

Generate or receive multiple candidate decompositions plus explicit no-plan, clarification, defer, and human-plan options; preserve generator identity, prompt or method, seed, search history, discarded alternatives, and selection denominator.

Represent each node as an obligation with exact inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, forbidden means, context and observation demand, authority and rights demand, resource envelope, verifier, failure behavior, fallback, and residual owner.
Type dependency edges as data, control, semantic, temporal, authority, rights, evidence, resource, state, or recovery dependencies and distinguish asserted, inferred, observed, and unknown relations.

Record assumptions, open-world unknowns, observation provenance, staleness, uncertainty, disagreement, and information-gathering actions; missing information can produce a context request or abstention rather than invented feasibility.

For predictive or model-based planning, bind observation, representation, world-model, predictor, controller, action-space, horizon, search, calibration, prediction-error, intervention, replanning, and sim-to-real state separately from task-graph identity.

Evaluate decomposition coverage, dependency validity, feasibility, affected-party and authority compatibility, adequacy, verifier availability, recovery readiness, and expected downstream utility before scheduling or dispatch.

Request candidate capability routes from Routing with exact task and adequacy predicates, but keep worker selection and capability qualification outside planning; a route remains proposed until its owner returns an eligible receipt.

Schedule eligible nodes using declared objectives over critical path, slack, deadlines, capacity, latency, compute, tool, evaluator, human, privacy, rights, recovery, and opportunity costs without hiding coordination or repair outside the ledger.

Use explicit lifecycle states for proposed, exploratory, blocked-context, blocked-authority, blocked-rights, blocked-dependency, blocked-verification, route-pending, dispatchable, dispatched, replanned, residualized, stopped, failed, and completed nodes.

Issue a dispatch request only when the exact contract, node, dependency closure, observation/context, route eligibility, authority, rights, resource, verifier, stop, and recovery predicates are current; the downstream authority owner decides whether execution occurs.
Consume feedback as versioned observations with provenance and uncertainty, compare it with frozen replan triggers, and emit a scoped replan proposal rather than mutating the active graph silently.

Compute the affected future subgraph from typed dependencies, invalidate stale assumptions, predictions, routes, artifacts, verifiers, and schedules, preserve unaffected work only when its premises still hold, and record counterfactual alternatives.

At branch joins, compare semantic assumptions, source and state versions, authority and rights ceilings, evidence views, quality predicates, side effects, resource ledgers, and residuals before accepting a merge.
Make stop, fallback, recovery, rollback-request, compensation, escalation, and residual routes effect-testable with accountable owners, timing bounds, descendant handling, and observed consequences.

Reconcile every generated plan, node, edge, route request, schedule attempt, block, timeout, retry, replan, merge, dispatch request, failure, cost, and residual into complete denominators; best-plan or completed-node reporting is insufficient.

Run preregistered natural planning campaigns against no-plan/direct, single-pass prompt, tree/HTN/PDDL or workflow, human-authored, learned-model/MPC where applicable, and strongest available planner baselines under matched models, tools, information, authority, budgets, and horizons.

Use causal ablations, adversarial controls, delayed outcomes, independent evaluators, clean reproduction, cross-model/task/environment transfer, and policy expiry to distinguish genuine planning benefit from extra search, compute, feedback, human repair, weak baselines, non-dispatch, or denominator changes.
interfaces: Intent-to-Execution Contracts supplies the accepted objective, non-goals, obligations, authority and rights ceilings, state assumptions, artifacts, criteria, budgets, stops, expiry, and re-contract triggers; Planning orders work but cannot reinterpret or widen them.

Context and Memory owners answer typed observation and context requests with provenance, adequacy, freshness, taint, uncertainty, and access consequences; retrieved material does not become a task or authority automatically.

Cognitive Compilation consumes an admitted node and emits semantic-IR and lowering receipts; it owns representation transformation, while Planning owns whether and when the obligation should be lowered.

Routing and Stable Capability Fields return eligible candidate workers, tools, or model routes with qualification, cost, latency, availability, fallback, and residual records; Planning requests requirements but does not self-certify capability.
Labor OS owns typed job creation, assignment, queueing, retry, and worker lifecycle after a valid dispatch request; a plan node is not a job or permission grant.
Runtime Adapters, Security, and Authority owners decide capability use and effects against observed state and return denial, attempt, effect, stop, and recovery receipts.
Verification and Scalable Oversight supply independent-enough node, merge, artifact, outcome, and delayed-effect evaluations; planner scores and self-critique are candidate evidence only.
Resource Economics supplies observed capacity, cost, congestion, human-friction, opportunity, recovery, and governance ledgers; Planning cannot move costs outside the route that caused them.

Artifact Graphs preserve plan versions, alternatives, nodes, edges, observations, route and dispatch requests, effects, artifacts, replans, costs, failures, and residual lineage without claiming plan quality.

Rights, privacy, licensing, contestability, and publication owners supply affected-party constraints, disclosure and use boundaries, appeal, compensation, and release decisions before external consequences.

Evidence States and Readiness Gates admit only claim-specific transitions and releases after empirical outcomes; a complete plan, theorem, scheduler trace, or zero dispatch grants no support movement.
invariants: Every plan is bound to one accepted contract version, consumer, purpose, environment, epoch, horizon, authority and rights ceiling, resource accounts, and support ceiling; material change expires or forks it.
Every accepted obligation is covered by a node, explicit non-planning decision, or owned residual, and no node adds a material objective, affected party, means, or success criterion silently.
Every dependency edge has a typed meaning, provenance, confidence, consumer, and falsifier; acyclicity and topological order do not establish dependency truth or completeness.
Assumptions, observations, unknowns, predictive-state versions, uncertainty, staleness, and disagreement remain visible to every dependent node and replan decision.
Candidate decompositions, exploratory branches, blocked nodes, route requests, review notes, and planner scores are not jobs, authority, effects, evidence transitions, or releases.

Only nodes with current dependency closure, feasible context/observation, eligible route, authority and rights compatibility, resources, verifier, stop, and recovery predicates may request lowering and dispatch.
Planning may request a capability tier or route but cannot declare the route qualified, grant its authority, or bypass the separate router, field, job, runtime, and approval owners.
Every cheap, parallel, compressed, learned-model, or automated route retains the same adequacy, verification, downstream-utility, recovery, and rights obligations as its comparator.

All model, tool, evaluator, human, coordination, waiting, repair, retry, merge, storage, privacy, rights, recovery, opportunity, and governance costs remain attributed to the plan and route that caused them.

A replan preserves the accepted contract, authority and rights ceilings, stops, residuals, negative knowledge, and complete history; a material change requires re-contract rather than adaptation by name.
Local repair preserves unaffected work only when dependency and assumption closure prove it remains valid; positional proximity or unchanged bytes are insufficient.
A merge cannot accept branches with incompatible semantics, state, sources, authority, rights, effects, evidence, quality predicates, costs, or residuals.
Stop, fallback, recovery, escalation, rollback request, compensation, and residual routes have accountable owners and observed consequences; declared paths are not effect evidence.
Every alternative, failed decomposition, blocked node, timeout, retry, replan, discarded route, merge failure, dispatch denial, cost, and residual remains in the selection and outcome denominator.

Planner reward, search score, predicted state, simulated success, fixture pass, theorem, and scheduler optimum cannot substitute for independent task, effect, usefulness, safety, rights, or transfer evaluation.
Non-dispatch, abstention, or a shorter critical path does not establish useful planning, safety advantage, cost dominance, or downstream success without matched opportunity and outcome denominators.
No planner, scheduler, world model, router, worker, runtime, or evaluator may approve its own broader authority, support state, deployment, or publication.
failure_modes: Scope and obligation drift adds goals, means, parties, or success criteria that the accepted contract did not authorize.
Decomposition theater produces plausible nodes that omit cross-cutting obligations, irreversible effects, verification, delivery, or recovery work.
Dependency hallucination asserts causal or feasibility relations from textual order, model confidence, or graph convenience without evidence.
Dependency omission allows a node to dispatch before required data, state, authority, rights, evidence, resource, or recovery prerequisites exist.
Acyclicity laundering treats a topologically valid graph as complete, feasible, semantically correct, or useful.
Context and observation fantasy schedules work against unavailable, stale, tainted, unauthorized, or unobservable inputs.
Model-predictive overconfidence treats latent prediction, simulator score, or short-horizon fit as causal understanding, safe control, or sim-to-real validity.
Dispatch laundering converts a proposed, exploratory, blocked, review-only, or route-pending node into a job or permission because it appears in the graph.
Capability-tier laundering selects a cheap or available worker whose qualification, context, verifier, fallback, or downstream adequacy is missing.
Scheduler objective capture optimizes makespan, utilization, token cost, or throughput while hiding usefulness, safety, rights, recovery, congestion, or opportunity costs.
Arbitrage laundering moves verification, repair, retries, human cleanup, coordination, or residual burden outside the reported route cost.
Parallelism theater creates nominally concurrent nodes whose shared bottleneck, context serialization, evaluator capacity, merge work, or contention erases the gain.
Merge laundering accepts locally passing branches with incompatible assumptions, source/state versions, authority, rights, effects, criteria, costs, or residuals.
Replanning erasure mutates the graph after feedback while hiding changed assumptions, authority, rights, stops, invalidated work, alternatives, or residuals.
Repair-radius laundering preserves unaffected nodes by adjacency or unchanged files even though semantic or state dependencies changed.
Plan survivorship reports only the chosen or completed graph and erases rejected decompositions, blocked nodes, timeouts, retries, failed routes, and merge failures.
Fail-closed planning claims safety or superiority from non-dispatch while useful opportunity, missed help, unsafe-action contrast, and delayed outcomes remain unmeasured.
Ceremonial stop or recovery declares a kill, fallback, rollback request, compensation, or residual path without observed effect, descendant handling, semantic recovery, or accountable custody.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is a record-and-route scaffold: plan-graph, PlanForge-DAG, command, job, and semantic-atom schemas; three valid and ten invalid plan-execution fixtures; two valid scheduler-state traces and seven rejecting mutations; two valid runtime-replan traces and nine rejecting mutations; two accepted no-change transitions; and 33 theorem declarations grouped under seven manifest targets. These artifacts exercise exact record shapes, declared dependency order and cycles, selected lifecycle and dispatch routes, authority/stop preservation bits, local-repair scope, cost-ledger fields, digests, and no-promotion boundaries only. They contain no planner-generated natural plan, independent decomposition oracle, measured dependency truth, context-demand prediction, selected-tier adequacy, live scheduler, observed replan benefit, or support movement. An honest next minimum must run a prospectively frozen natural planning campaign with multiple planner/model families, human-authored and strong algorithmic/workflow baselines, hidden obligations and perturbations, complete alternative denominators, independent semantic/dependency/feasibility/outcome evaluators, observed costs and feedback, effect-tested stops and recovery, causal ablations, clean reproduction, and transfer.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature planning control plane is a measured policy for obligation scheduling under uncertainty, not a diagram generator. On prospectively sampled natural tasks it compares no-plan/direct, single-pass, search/tree, HTN/PDDL/workflow, human-authored, learned-model/MPC where applicable, and full governed planning under matched models, tools, information, authority, rights, resources, and horizons. Independent implementations score obligation coverage, semantic preservation, dependency precision/recall, feasibility, context and observation calibration, plan stability, route adequacy, merge validity, useful critical-path and completion outcomes, unsafe actions, abstention and missed help, delayed effects, rights and privacy, recovery completeness, latency, compute, coordination, human work, and total governance cost. Adversaries inject hidden dependencies, stale state, conflicting sources, authority and rights traps, evaluator gaming, shared bottlenecks, replan shocks, merge conflicts, and recovery failures; causal ablations test each mechanism; replications span models, planners, tasks, languages, modalities, environments, organizations, jurisdictions, threat models, horizons, and time. Promotion requires predicted causal signatures, nonzero useful opportunity, strong matched baselines, independent evaluation, reproducible raw traces, effect-bearing controls, transfer, and accepted claim-specific transitions; otherwise claims remain narrowed, null, negative, refuted, or blocked after full attempt.

chapters.title: Governed World Models and Reality Grounding

handoff_or_recovery_notes: Receives bounded plans from Planning as a Control Layer; hands uncertainty-bearing imagined outcomes to Cognitive Compilation and action governance without identifying prediction with reality.

chapters.problem: Planning and control fail when an agent's latent or simulated future is mistaken for the world, when prediction error is hidden, or when model updates expand action authority without evidence.
chapters.insufficient: A more accurate predictor, larger simulator, or compelling rollout is not enough: open-loop scores can conceal compounding error, partial observability, distribution shift, causal confusion, reward exploitation, and unsafe actions selected from attractive hallucinated futures.
chapters.core_claim: A world model should be governed as a fallible, versioned prediction service whose state, horizon, uncertainty, provenance, and calibration bound which imagined consequences may influence planning and action; observation must repeatedly reconcile imagination with reality.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports shared latent structure, prediction interfaces, and the possibility of substrate-independent world representations.

mechanism: Represent world-model outputs as versioned state-transition hypotheses with provenance, horizon, uncertainty, and validity domains.
Separate observation encoding, latent dynamics, decoding, value/reward prediction, and policy use so each can be challenged independently.
Calibrate uncertainty by horizon, state region, intervention type, and consequence class.
Use receding-horizon observation updates and explicit residuals to reconcile predicted and observed state.
Admit imagined rollouts to planning only inside tested support and authority envelopes.
Fork competing models or ensembles when material hypotheses disagree.
Detect model exploitation by testing whether proposed actions seek high-value low-support states.
Retain counterfactual receipts linking decisions to model version, observations, alternatives, and uncertainty.

Uncertainty is not collapsed into a point estimate at governance boundaries.
World-model updates cannot widen execution authority by themselves.
Counterfactual comparisons use compatible state and intervention semantics.
Reality residuals are preserved rather than averaged away.
A reachable safe action exists when predictive confidence collapses.

chapters.minimal_implementation: Build a bounded partially observed control task with two independently trained dynamics models, horizon-conditional calibration, receding observation updates, model-disagreement routing, support-aware action gating, counterfactual receipts, and adversarial tests for exploitation and distribution shift.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature target architecture is a plural, causally testable world-model substrate that can revise its abstractions while a versioned operational contract keeps prediction, uncertainty, intervention semantics, authority, observation, and residual reality distinct. It admits imagined futures only inside calibrated support, routes material disagreement and shift to fallback or safe hold, and records every counterfactual dependency. Maturity requires held-out interventions, causal ablations, independent reproduction, and real-world transfer that outperform strong reactive, model-free, and single-model baselines without worsening unsafe action, latency, or governance cost.

chapters.title: Cognitive Compilation and Semantic IR
chapters.problem: After Planning admits an obligation for lowering, the stack still has to translate it across source contracts, semantic representations, target representations, and concrete artifacts without losing meaning, authority, non-goals, evidence boundaries, repair identity, or residuals. Direct generation can produce a fluent target while silently changing the very obligation that justified it.
chapters.insufficient: Prompt templates, schemas, planning languages, workflow engines, search traces, program synthesis, conventional compiler IR, multi-level lowering, and translation validation each supply part of the discipline, but none alone establishes consumer-relative semantic preservation for open-ended cognitive artifacts. Typed shape is not meaning, a digest is not equivalence, a compiler-authored validator can share the same error, declared repair scope is not observed locality, and a valid target can still be useless, unauthorized, under-specified, or more expensive than direct generation.
chapters.core_claim: Cognitive Compilation should own a versioned, consumer- and target-relative translation contract that lowers an already accepted plan obligation through source, semantic, and target representations into a concrete artifact candidate while preserving addressable obligation, non-goal, authority, rights, assumption, source/context, evidence, resource, verifier, repair, and residual lineage. Every pass binds typed source and target semantics; declared normalization, loss, and ambiguity; preconditions and postconditions; dependencies; deterministic and nondeterministic inputs; compiler and validator identities; costs; receipts; and failure consequences. Acceptance requires post-translation validation against the actual target artifact by an independent-enough evaluator, while repair uses stable semantic identities, observed mutation sets, dependency closure, downstream rebuild, and revalidation. The compiler may block, narrow, request clarification, or residualize a lowering, but it does not reinterpret intent, choose the plan, grant authority, execute effects, self-certify semantic adequacy, or move support or release state.
mechanism: Freeze consumer, purpose, accepted contract and plan-node versions, source and target languages, environment, compiler and pass set, evaluator policy and dependencies, authority and rights ceilings, resources, outcome horizon, support ceiling, and material-change triggers before lowering.

Represent the accepted source obligation in a typed source-plan language whose objectives, non-goals, fields, units, quantifiers, precedence, provenance, uncertainty, affected parties, authority, rights, criteria, stops, recovery, and residual duties remain explicit.

Create stable semantic identities for obligations, objects, fields, relations, dependencies, assumptions, ambiguities, validators, artifacts, and residuals rather than using text position, transient names, hashes, embeddings, or graph location as semantic identity.
Preserve an ambiguity ledger whose resolved, deferred, conflicting, unknown, and abstained states carry provenance, resolution authority, strict-lowering effects, expiry, and residual ownership.

Maintain complete source-to-semantic-to-target-to-artifact field and obligation lineage, including negative requirements, omitted material, introduced assumptions, split and merge transformations, loss budgets, and consumer-specific adequacy predicates.

Specify every compiler pass with typed input and output dialects, legality rules, preconditions, postconditions, allowed loss, obligation effects, authority effects, determinism class, dependency effects, cost model, failure outputs, and reproducer bundle.

Use progressive multi-level lowering so high-level semantic obligations, target-specific constraints, and executable or publishable representations remain distinct rather than collapsing directly from prompt to artifact.

Emit concrete target candidates through explicit backends for code, prose, schemas, proofs, experiments, diagrams, or job packets, with target-language identity, environment, dependencies, build inputs, and artifact digest recorded.

Attach requirement-specific translation validators that compare the accepted source obligation with the actual target artifact and distinguish well-formedness, source-plan acceptance, semantic-IR validity, lowering preservation, target validity, usefulness, and external-effect outcomes.

Record evaluator method, model, prompt, code, data, infrastructure, organization, incentive, and exposure dependencies; self-checks and shared-family validators remain candidate evidence rather than independent semantic acceptance.

Retain pass inputs, outputs, failures, warnings, residuals, seeds, tool and model calls, environment locks, compiler versions, receipts, and negative cases so a clean final artifact cannot erase failed translations or nondeterministic provenance.

Use explicit lifecycle states for proposed, parsed, ambiguous, source-valid, IR-valid, lowering-blocked, target-emitted, validator-failed, repairable, repair-pending, revalidated, residualized, accepted-for-handoff, and rejected translations.

Repair by stable obligation and IR identities, comparing declared repair scope with observed file, node, dependency, state, and effect mutations; a positional edit or proposed scope does not establish locality.

Compute downstream invalidation and rebuild closure after every source, pass, dependency, evaluator, target, or artifact change, and revalidate affected obligations instead of trusting cached success.

For addressable semantic lowering, keep stable object identity, plural task-scoped semantic addresses, evidence-acquisition traces, and temporary physical routes separate; address confidence or graph position never grants truth, authority, or support.

Reconcile every parse, candidate IR, pass attempt, backend emission, validation, repair, retry, cache hit, failure, timeout, cost, human intervention, residual, and discarded artifact into complete denominators.

Run preregistered natural artifact campaigns against direct generation, schema-only prompting, workflow and planning pipelines, search, human-authored artifacts, conventional compiler/build tools, and strongest available synthesis or agent baselines under matched models, tools, sources, authority, budgets, repair opportunities, and horizons.

Use adversarial obligation deletion, paraphrase, ambiguity, authority and rights traps, validator collusion, stale caches, target-specific undefined behavior, repair blast-radius, and provenance attacks plus causal ablations, clean reproduction, independent implementation, and cross-target transfer to distinguish compiler benefit from extra structure, search, compute, feedback, or human repair.
interfaces: Intent-to-Execution Contracts and Planning supply an accepted, version-bound obligation and constraints; Cognitive Compilation may lower or reject it but cannot reinterpret, add, reorder, or widen the plan.

Context and Virtual Context ABI supply source artifacts and representations with exact identity, version, provenance, adequacy, taint, use, omission, and authority boundaries; the compiler cannot turn retrieved context into control.

Planning consumes compiler feasibility, failure, cost, and residual records for future scheduling, while the compiler consumes only admitted nodes and does not own candidate-plan selection or dispatch priority.

Labor OS and Runtime Adapters consume accepted target IR or artifact candidates as typed job or effect requests; a successful compile is not a job, capability, permission, effect, or completion receipt.
Artifact Graphs preserve source, IR, pass, target, validator, repair, rebuild, cost, failure, and residual lineage without treating provenance completeness as semantic correctness.
Verification and Scalable Oversight supply independent-enough source-target, artifact, outcome, and delayed-effect evaluations; compiler-owned checks remain labeled by their dependencies.
Security, Authority, Rights, Privacy, Licensing, and Publication owners decide whether compiled artifacts or requests may be used, executed, disclosed, or released.
Resource Economics supplies observed model, tool, compute, storage, latency, human, verifier, rebuild, recovery, and opportunity costs rather than compiler-declared estimates alone.
Evidence States and Readiness Gates own claim support, promotion, quarantine, and release; a lowering receipt or green build cannot move those states by itself.
Procedural Memory may retain qualified passes, backends, validators, and repair procedures, but reuse requires exact lineage, regression, authority, expiry, and consumer scope.

Supply-chain and model-weight custody owners bind compilers, models, dependencies, environments, artifacts, signatures, caches, and revocation; source hashes or pass names do not prove toolchain integrity.
invariants: Every translation binds one accepted obligation, consumer, purpose, source and target language, compiler/pass set, evaluator policy, environment, authority and rights ceiling, budget, horizon, and support ceiling.
Every material source obligation and non-goal is preserved, explicitly refined, split, merged, deferred, rejected, or residualized with reason and authority; none silently disappears or appears.
Stable semantic identity remains distinct from label, text position, hash, embedding, address, graph location, target identifier, storage location, and physical route.
Every source field and obligation has complete source-to-IR-to-target-to-artifact lineage with provenance, consumer, validator, allowed loss, observed disposition, and falsifier.
Unresolved, deferred, conflicting, or unknown ambiguity blocks strict lowering whenever it can change consumer meaning, authority, rights, effects, verification, or release.

A pass cannot widen authority, rights, consumers, purposes, means, effects, evidence claims, support, or release state; lowering may only preserve, narrow, block, or request a separately authorized change.
Well-formed source, IR, target, or artifact records remain distinct from semantic preservation, validator adequacy, usefulness, safety, execution, and support-state evidence.

Digest equality establishes only identity under its exact hash inputs; semantic equivalence requires a declared source-target relation and requirement-specific validation against actual content and behavior.
Every introduced assumption, normalization, approximation, undefined behavior, information loss, target constraint, and omitted distinction remains explicit and consumer-scoped.
Compiler passes, backends, and validators retain exact identities, versions, dependencies, inputs, nondeterminism, receipts, failure bundles, and reproducibility limits.
The compiler, generator, and target cannot be the sole semantic evaluator for their own output; evaluator dependencies and disagreement remain visible.
Declared repair scope is not locality: observed mutations, dependency closure, rebuilt descendants, invalidated caches, revalidation, and preserved failures determine the repair claim.
A source, compiler, pass, dependency, evaluator, target, policy, or environment change expires affected receipts and triggers dependency-closed rebuild or explicit residualization.
Semantic identity, address resolution, evidence state, physical routing, authority, execution, and support state remain separate lifecycle decisions.
All candidates, attempts, failures, retries, repairs, cache hits, human interventions, costs, discarded outputs, and residuals remain in the denominator.
A lower repair cost, faster build, shorter IR, cleaner artifact, or zero release is not semantic, useful, safe, or cost superiority without matched outcomes and opportunity.
Finite schemas, fixtures, theorem routes, synthetic evaluations, and local reversible traces establish only their exact records and cannot universalize semantic preservation or production transfer.
failure_modes: Source-meaning capture or compiler hallucination turns a mistaken interpretation into precise IR.
Obligation, non-goal, authority, rights, affected-party, evidence, stop, recovery, or residual loss occurs during parsing or lowering.
IR drift changes field, type, unit, quantifier, precedence, identity, dependency, or semantics across passes.
Schema and type theater treats well-formed records as correct meaning or acceptable artifacts.
Digest, embedding, address, graph-position, or token-similarity laundering relabels identity or proximity as semantic equivalence.
Ambiguity laundering resolves, defaults, or discards uncertainty without the required authority or consumer-specific adequacy.
Validator scarcity or proxy validation accepts fluent prose, compiling code, or schema-valid output while missing the source obligation.
Validator capture, shared-model correlation, self-verification, exposure, or evaluator gaming makes translation validation self-confirming.
Pass or backend authority laundering turns a successful lowering into execution, mutation, disclosure, publication, or support authority.
Target-specific assumption, undefined behavior, platform constraint, lossy normalization, or external dependency remains hidden.
Positional or unstable identity causes edits, insertions, merges, or splits to orphan obligations and repair history.
Repair-radius laundering reports proposed locality while actual mutations, invalidations, caches, descendants, or effects escape the declared scope.
Stale-build laundering reuses cached passes, validators, targets, or receipts after source, dependency, policy, evaluator, or environment change.
Failure-bundle erasure and best-artifact survivorship remove rejected parses, failed targets, repairs, timeouts, and discarded candidates from the record.
Compilation overhead, excessive fragmentation, verifier burden, or coordination cost dominates direct generation without improving useful outcomes.
Semantic-address or route laundering treats stable identity, atlas compatibility, certificate integrity, or semantic resolution as truth, capability, or execution permission.
Round-trip theater uses a correlated parser or evaluator to reconstruct the same mistaken structure and call it semantic preservation.
Portability and transfer theater infers cross-model, cross-language, cross-target, organizational, safety, or production validity from one synthetic toolchain.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is a record-and-bounded-reference scaffold: a semantic-atom schema; two valid and four rejecting hand-authored compilation traces; one blocked three-project semantic-preservation record and nine rejecting mutations; fourteen Lean theorem declarations grouped under three targets; and the QCSA reference package, whose 60 synthetic cases across 13 systems and three seeds produced 2,340 predictions plus one 13-stage zero-model temporary-file trace with ten rejecting adversarial paths and one byte-exact rollback. QCSA matched the selected baseline at 1.000 task accuracy while using 1.913386 times the operations, so matched advantage and resource gates failed; the active-question ablation is N2 proxy/regime evidence rather than an exact or broad refutation; several identity, certificate, migration, calibration, and plural-facet mechanisms earned bounded non-core dispositions; and the chapter core stayed at argument. No artifact is generated from a natural accepted source plan through a real cognitive backend and independently checked for semantic preservation. The next honest minimum must compile natural held-out source obligations into one concrete artifact class, compare direct, schema-only, workflow, human, and governed compiler routes under matched resources, use independently implemented source-target and artifact evaluators, exercise adversarial obligation loss and real incremental repair, reconcile all attempts and costs, rerun from locked artifacts, and transfer to a second target class.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature cognitive compiler is an obligation-preserving multi-level build system for cognitive work, not a larger prompt template. It accepts versioned plan obligations and source context; emits inspectable source, semantic, and target IR dialects; runs typed and reproducible passes; produces concrete code, prose, schema, proof, experiment, diagram, or job artifacts; validates each translation against actual target content and behavior; and rebuilds dependency-closed descendants after change. On prospectively sampled natural tasks it must beat or complement direct generation, schema-only prompting, strong workflows, planning and search systems, human-authored artifacts, conventional compiler/build tools, program synthesis, and current agent pipelines under matched models, sources, authority, tools, budgets, repair opportunities, and horizons. Independent implementations jointly measure obligation precision and recall, semantic preservation, ambiguity calibration, authority and rights retention, artifact validity and usefulness, repair locality, rebuild correctness, unsafe or unauthorized outputs, abstention and missed help, latency, compute, storage, verifier and human work, recovery, and total governance cost. Adversarial passes delete or paraphrase obligations, exploit target assumptions, poison caches, capture validators, widen authority, forge receipts, and escape repair scope; causal ablations isolate each mechanism; replication and transfer span models, languages, modalities, artifact classes, compilers, evaluators, organizations, jurisdictions, threats, horizons, and time. Promotion requires nonzero useful opportunity, strong matched baselines, independent evaluation, effect-bearing controls where relevant, predicted causal signatures, complete denominators, reproducible traces, transfer, and accepted claim-specific transitions; otherwise the exact claim is narrowed, null, negative, refuted, or blocked after full attempt.

chapters.title: The Virtual Context ABI: Typed Pages, Cells, and Certificates
chapters.problem: Between durable memory and a model's finite working context lies a static translation boundary: a consumer must request an exact purpose, object or address, snapshot, representation, authority, use, adequacy target, budget, and freshness horizon, while the system must disclose what it actually selected, transformed, omitted, certified, leased, denied, and left unresolved. Anonymous prompt text erases that boundary even when retrieval appears relevant or a context window is large.
chapters.insufficient: Long windows, RAG, graph retrieval, hierarchical summaries, prompt compression, persistent conversational memory, citations, adaptive retrieval, and OS-style memory tiers can improve access or performance in bounded settings, but none alone supplies a consumer-relative conformance contract for the actual materialized packet. Relevance is not identity, presence is not use, a source link is not support, a certificate can be false, a bounded graph can hide its frontier, and admission can coexist with inadequate, stale, unauthorized, poisoned, lossy, or cost-dominated context.
chapters.core_claim: The Virtual Context ABI should own the static, versioned, consumer- and purpose-relative request-to-materialization contract between durable memory and model-visible context. It resolves stable object, semantic-address, version, mount, and snapshot references into finite typed representation candidates, then issues a certificate and receipt that bind exact source and field lineage, transformations, omissions and loss, provenance and taint, authority and rights, permitted and prohibited uses, freshness, lease, revocation, selection and omitted frontier, requested and observed adequacy state, costs, faults, and residuals. Admission means only that the actual packet conforms to the frozen request and policy; it does not establish truth, verification adequacy, model use, usefulness, safety, or support. The ABI may deny, ask, abstain, refresh, broaden, narrow, or return a typed fault, but it does not update durable memory, own transactions, infer execution authority, adjudicate claims, execute effects, or move support or release state.
mechanism: Freeze consumer, purpose, task or claim, accepted obligation, object or expression identity, semantic-address policy, source and snapshot scope, representation need, authority and rights ceiling, permitted and prohibited uses, adequacy target, budget, horizon, support ceiling, and material-change triggers before resolution.

Keep durable object identity, plural semantic addresses, storage locations, retrieval routes, representation identities, consumer packets, physical execution routes, and claim-support states separate.
Resolve stable address, version, mount, snapshot, policy, generation, revocation epoch, and lease references against an exact durable-memory read boundary without granting write authority.

Generate and retain all eligible source-object, graph-neighborhood, excerpt, summary, redaction, translation, abstraction, contradiction, claim, decision, correction, event, and artifact representation candidates before selection.

Select the least sufficient packet under declared node, depth, edge, token, latency, privacy, authority, rights, and verification budgets while retaining rejected candidates and the exact omitted frontier.
Assign stable representation and field identities so source-to-packet lineage survives paraphrase, ordering, merge, split, readdressing, cache movement, and atlas migration.

Record every transformation with source fields, output fields, normalization, paraphrase, redaction, aggregation, introduced inference, omission, approximation, unknown, conflict, loss budget, and consumer-specific consequence.

Issue a representation certificate binding object and address refs, snapshot and source refs, provenance, taint, transformation and omission ledger, loss and use contract, authority and rights ceiling, verifier refs, validity, expiry, revocation, transaction refs, artifacts, costs, residuals, and non-claims.

Issue a materialization receipt binding the frozen request to actual selected content, byte or content identity, order and placement, cache and retrieval route, selection denominator, omitted frontier, policy decisions, faults, and resource use.

Validate certificate declarations against the actual source objects and emitted packet rather than trusting self-description, digest parity, address confidence, graph position, or round-trip reconstruction.

Separate resolver conformance, source freshness, representation fidelity, authority and use compliance, context admission, verification adequacy, downstream model use, outcome usefulness, safety, and support into different lifecycle states and owners.

Emit typed non-success states for missing, ambiguous, conflicting, stale, revoked, tainted, unauthorized, unlicensed, privacy-blocked, under-budgeted, unavailable, infeasible, inadequate, or migration-unresolved context.
Treat a mandatory miss as a blocking typed fault; treat an optional miss as an explicit request, fallback, abstention, or residual rather than invisible best effort.

Expire and revalidate affected certificates and packets after material changes to source, snapshot, address atlas, policy, authority, rights, evaluator, dependency, environment, consumer, or target use.

Instrument the actual model-facing or worker-facing packet and downstream use so available, selected, placed, attended-to, cited, relied-on, contradicted, and outcome-relevant context are not conflated.

Preserve every request, candidate, retrieval, transformation, certificate, admission decision, cache event, retry, human intervention, fault, cost, discard, downstream use observation, and residual in the denominator.

Carry QCSA's stable identity, plural address, certificate, migration, and typed-fault mechanisms only at their exact bounded synthetic dispositions; retain the accuracy tie, operation-cost failure, active-question refutation, and zero core support effect.

Compare the ABI against full-context, direct RAG, graph retrieval, hierarchical summary, prompt compression, persistent memory, adaptive retrieval, citation-oriented, OS-style tiering, human-curated, and no-context routes on natural tasks under matched models, sources, tools, authority, budgets, and horizons.
interfaces: Intent, Cognitive Compilation, and Planning own accepted purpose, obligation, consumer, and context demand; the ABI may satisfy or fault the request but cannot reinterpret or reprioritize it.

Durable memory and knowledge-lattice owners supply stable objects, source versions, provenance, lifecycle, and read snapshots; the ABI materializes consumer packets but cannot mutate, merge, delete, supersede, or forget durable state.

Context Transactions owns commits, branches, mounts, taint propagation, invalidation, deletion closure, and dynamic isolation; the ABI consumes exact transaction and snapshot state without claiming those behaviors.
QCSA or resolver components may propose identities and plural semantic addresses; address confidence and graph position cannot grant truth, use, or execution authority.
Verification Bandwidth and Scalable Oversight own adequacy and independent evaluation; ABI admission only records whether a packet conforms to the frozen static request and policy.
Claim Ledgers and Spinoza own evidence, contradiction, belief, and support transitions; source links, citations, certificates, and packet use cannot promote claims.
Labor OS and Runtime Adapters consume permitted packets and separately decide jobs, tools, approval, and effects; context relevance is never capability authority.
Security, Privacy, Rights, Licensing, and Publication owners decide disclosure, processing, retention, permitted use, declassification, and external release.
Artifact Graphs preserve source, request, candidate, certificate, packet, cache, model-use, outcome, cost, fault, and residual lineage without certifying correctness.
Resource Economics owns observed token, compute, storage, retrieval, compression, verifier, human, latency, recovery, privacy, and opportunity cost.
Evidence States and Readiness Gates own support, quarantine, promotion, and release; green resolution or admission has no direct support-state effect.
invariants: Every materialization binds one frozen consumer, purpose, obligation, object or expression, address policy, source and snapshot scope, representation need, authority and rights ceiling, use contract, adequacy target, budget, horizon, and support ceiling.
Durable identity remains distinct from name, text position, embedding, semantic address, graph position, storage location, representation, packet, retrieval route, physical route, and support state.
Source and field lineage survives every representation change; each field is preserved, transformed, split, merged, omitted, rejected, or residualized with reason and consumer consequence.
Every bounded selection stays within declared limits and discloses the exact selected denominator, rejected candidates, omissions, and reachable but unmaterialized frontier.
Summaries, redactions, translations, abstractions, and derived inferences cannot widen authority, rights, purpose, permitted use, evidence, effects, support, or release beyond their sources.
Loss, uncertainty, conflict, approximation, provenance, taint, permitted and prohibited uses, expiry, revocation, and residual risk remain explicit and consumer-relative.
A certificate is a falsifiable declaration about an actual source-to-packet relation; certificate presence, signature, digest, address confidence, or graph position is not truth or adequacy.
Resolver conformance, source freshness, representation fidelity, admission, adequacy, model use, usefulness, safety, and support remain distinct states with distinct evidence.
Mandatory misses and material ambiguity produce blocking typed faults; optional absence, low confidence, or infeasibility remains visible as request, fallback, abstention, denial, retry, or residual.
A context handle binds exact snapshot identity and digest, generation, policy, revocation epoch, provenance, issue and expiry time, revalidation receipt, and permitted use.
Revoked, expired, stale, policy-mismatched, migrated, tainted, or materially changed sources and packets cannot inherit current admission, adequacy, or support.
Materialized context is a finite derived consumer object, remains distinct from durable memory, and gains no write, merge, deletion, supersession, training, or execution authority.
Actual emitted bytes or content, order, placement, truncation, cache state, and consumer-visible packet remain inspectable; requested or selected context cannot substitute for observed delivery.
Available, selected, delivered, attended-to, cited, relied-on, contradicted, outcome-relevant, and support-bearing context remain different measured states.
Every candidate, retrieval, transformation, validation, retry, cache event, intervention, failure, cost, discard, use observation, and residual remains in the denominator.

Lower tokens, faster retrieval, higher recall, more citations, longer context, cleaner summaries, zero release, or fewer faults do not establish semantic fidelity, adequacy, usefulness, safety, or total-cost superiority alone.
Finite schemas, synthetic fixtures, bounded graph records, local reversible traces, and finite theorems establish only their exact scopes and cannot universalize context quality or transfer.
failure_modes: Anonymous-context laundering collapses sources, versions, roles, and permissions into flat prompt text.
Identity or address laundering treats a name, embedding, nearest node, semantic path, graph position, or physical route as the durable object itself.
Stale-handle laundering reuses an old generation, expired lease, revoked epoch, migrated address, mismatched policy, or unvalidated slice.
Summary or compression overconfidence hides qualifiers, negation, quantities, time, authority, citations, contradictions, minority evidence, or downstream reconstruction burden.
Provenance and field-lineage loss severs a packet from the exact source objects, transformations, omissions, and consumer consequences that bound its use.
Authority, rights, privacy, purpose, or license escalation through summarization, redaction, translation, caching, federation, or semantic similarity widens use beyond the source grant.
Certificate theater treats complete fields, a signature, a digest, self-validation, or a round trip as proof that the declaration is truthful.
Graph-completeness laundering hides rejected candidates, selection limits, unreachable material, or the omitted frontier and narrates a bounded slice as the store.
Admission laundering treats relevant, well-formed, fresh, or permitted context as adequate evidence for the target claim or decision.
Presence laundering treats context available in the window as selected, attended to, used correctly, or outcome-relevant.
Retrieval and citation laundering treats a high recall score, source link, or citation count as entailment, contradiction handling, or claim support.
Poisoning, conflicting sources, malicious instructions, future-answer leakage, and taint enter packets without visible quarantine or consumer-specific policy.
Mandatory misses, ambiguity, or infeasibility are silently replaced with best-effort nearest matches or hallucinated clean packets.
Cache and invalidation laundering serves content after source, snapshot, address atlas, policy, authority, evaluator, or environment change.
Durable-memory/context conflation lets a lossy consumer packet overwrite, merge, delete, train on, or become the durable source without separate authority and transaction lineage.
Failure and selection survivorship removes rejected candidates, retries, stale reads, compression losses, cache misses, human repair, faults, or residuals from denominators.
Efficiency theater reports fewer tokens, lower latency, higher retrieval, or longer nominal context while omitting verification, human, privacy, recovery, missed-help, and opportunity cost.
Portability theater infers cross-model, language, modality, memory store, organization, jurisdiction, threat, or temporal validity from one synthetic resolver and internally authored corpus.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is a static record-and-route scaffold: public context ABI, packet, adequacy, transaction-reference, semantic-page certificate, and bounded graph-snapshot schemas; three valid and five rejecting admission/adequacy fixtures; two valid and nine rejecting resolver/certificate routes; one bounded four-project certificate and eleven rejecting mutations; thirty-one Lean theorem declarations under six targets; and QCSA's exact bounded synthetic identity/address/certificate results. It does not run a deployed resolver, inspect natural source-to-packet fidelity, measure model use, execute memory transactions, enforce deletion, or establish useful advantage. The next honest minimum must materialize natural held-out source sets into actual model-visible packets through full-context, RAG, graph, summary/compression, human-curated, and governed ABI routes under matched resources; independently label source obligations, actual packet contents, model use, task outcomes, faults, costs, and delayed residuals; inject staleness, poisoning, omission, authority, cache, and frontier attacks; reproduce from locks; and transfer to a second model and memory backend.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature Virtual Context ABI is an independently testable context syscall layer: natural consumers request exact purpose-bound objects and representations; multiple durable stores and retrieval systems return finite candidates; the ABI emits actual model-visible packets with falsifiable source-to-packet certificates, complete selection and cost ledgers, least-sufficient authority and rights, typed faults, expiry, and revalidation; and independent observers distinguish availability, delivery, model use, outcome contribution, adequacy, and support. Prospectively sampled natural workloads compare full context, direct and graph RAG, hierarchical summarization, prompt compression, persistent memory, adaptive retrieval, citation systems, OS-style tiering, human curation, and no-context routes under matched models, sources, tools, authority, budgets, and horizons. Promotion requires useful opportunity, source and packet fidelity, calibrated abstention and missed-help, poisoning and stale-state resistance, measured model use and outcomes, complete total cost, predicted causal signatures under ablation, clean reproduction, cross-model and cross-backend transfer, and accepted claim-specific transitions; otherwise the exact claims narrow, remain null or negative, become refuted, or are blocked after a full attempt.
