mechanism: Freeze one job contract from an accepted contract and dispatchable plan node with exact type, objective, scope, inputs, expected artifacts, acceptance tests, failure behavior, consumer, consequence, risk, authority, rights, budget, deadline, and expiry.
Assign stable job, attempt, lease, workspace, adapter invocation, approval, effect, artifact, receipt, residual, and parent identities with causal lineage and idempotency keys.

Admit, refuse, defer, split, or quarantine work only after validating parent contract and plan versions, lifecycle state, required inputs, adapter eligibility, resource envelope, authority, rights, and open blockers.

Lock material job fields before dispatch and invalidate approval, permission, verification, or replay decisions after relevant contract, plan, input, adapter, policy, threat, environment, or consumer change.

Use typed lifecycle transitions for drafted, locked, awaiting approval, approved, dispatchable, queued, leased, running, paused, retrying, adjudicating, delivered, evidence ready, failed, blocked, quarantined, cancelled, compensated, replayed, expired, and retired states.
Request exact capability, tool, data, network, secret-handle, disclosure, side-effect, environment, and retention grants from their owners without minting or widening permissions locally.
Bind required accountable-human or policy approvals to exact job version, effect class, authority, scope, time, conditions, revocation, and reuse limits before high-impact dispatch.
Provision purpose-bound workspace, secret isolation, environment lock, runtime adapter, model/tool versions, input digests, observation hooks, and cleanup policy as separately inspectable boundaries.

Schedule admitted work under declared priority, fairness, dependency, capacity, deadline, preemption, starvation, and cost policies while keeping queue acceptance distinct from dispatch and completion.
Lease each dispatch attempt with bounded authority, expiry, heartbeat, retry policy, checkpoint, idempotency key, duplicate-effect guard, and exact resume or restart semantics.

Observe actual adapter calls, tool uses, reads, writes, messages, external effects, outputs, costs, failures, approvals, denials, interventions, and side records rather than trusting agent or scheduler status.

Resume and retry only from declared checkpoints with unchanged or reauthorized authority, reconciled effects, fresh leases, preserved attempt lineage, and explicit at-most-once, at-least-once, or compensated semantics.

Route failures, denials, timeouts, partial effects, lost workers, stale leases, policy changes, residuals, and unknown states to typed recovery, escalation, compensation, quarantine, or terminal custody.
Separate produced output, delivered output, adjudicated output, verified artifact capture, replay declaration, evidence-ready completion, user acceptance, support, and release.

Issue terminal receipts that bind observed pre/post state, attempts, approvals, permissions, adapters, effects, artifacts, tests, audit refs, delivery, evidence-readiness state, replay grade, costs, residuals, and non-claims.
Cancel, compensate, expire, revoke, garbage-collect, or retire jobs through owner-authorized transitions that preserve irreversible effects, artifacts, obligations, and historical receipts.

Return bounded feedback, failure signatures, reusable procedures, and learning candidates through Artifact Graphs and their owning gates without silently editing plans, memory, policies, models, or support.

Compare chat, ambient agents, agent swarms, scripts, queues, DAG orchestrators, durable workflows, BPMN/process engines, Kubernetes batch jobs, and governed typed jobs under matched work, resources, authority, faults, delayed outcomes, and total cost.
interfaces: Intent and Command Contracts supply authorized objectives and constraints; jobs cannot reinterpret intent or widen contract scope.
Planning supplies dispatchable plan-node identity, dependencies, acceptance conditions, and residuals; Labor OS owns execution lifecycle rather than plan correctness.
Proof-Carrying Claims and review owners supply bounded blockers, constraints, required actions, and human decisions; jobs cannot promote verdicts or support.
Security, Privacy, Rights, Licensing, and authority owners issue and revoke exact permissions, approvals, secret handles, disclosure limits, and retention obligations.
Resource Economics and scheduler policy supply capacity, priority, fairness, deadline, preemption, and cost constraints; queue state is not work completion.
Runtime Adapters and tool runners own actual effect execution and effect receipts; Labor OS dispatches and observes but does not declare external effects reversible.
Context ABI and Transactions supply exact input views, mounts, taint, deletion, and expiry state; jobs cite rather than mutate context outside authorized transactions.
Artifact Graphs own output identity, provenance, replay grade, audit reconstruction, and receipt faithfulness; Labor OS links attempts to produced and delivered artifacts.
Verification Bandwidth, Evidence States, and claim owners decide adequacy and support; evidence-ready is an eligibility state, not evidence acceptance.
Accountable humans own high-impact approval, exception, adjudication, intervention, appeal, and irreversible-effect decisions within exact authority.
Readiness and publication owners decide deployment, delivery claims, and release; a terminal job receipt is not release approval.
Temporal, Airflow, BPMN, Kubernetes, Talos, VIEA, GenesisCode, Software Magic, and MoECOT supply lineage and implementation comparators, not local scheduler or runtime evidence.
invariants: Every admitted job binds one exact contract and plan-node version, type, objective, scope, inputs, expected artifacts, acceptance tests, consumer, consequence, risk, authority, rights, budget, deadline, expiry, and owner.
Job, attempt, lease, workspace, approval, permission, adapter invocation, effect, artifact, receipt, residual, and parent identities remain stable, distinct, and causally linked.

Lifecycle transitions use the declared state machine; requested, admitted, queued, leased, running, paused, retrying, delivered, evidence ready, failed, blocked, quarantined, cancelled, compensated, replayed, expired, and retired states never collapse into a generic done state.

Material contract, plan, input, adapter, policy, threat, environment, authority, rights, consumer, or deadline changes expire affected locks, approvals, permissions, leases, verification, and replay decisions.

Permissions, approvals, budgets, data access, secret handles, network access, side effects, disclosure, retention, and release authority are explicit, least-privilege, purpose-bound, revocable, and non-escalating across dispatch, retry, resume, delegation, and replay.
High-impact or irreversible work cannot dispatch without the exact required approval for the current job, effect class, authority, scope, conditions, and time.

Queue acceptance, scheduler assignment, lease acquisition, agent response, tool return, file existence, delivery, and user acknowledgment are not equivalent to durable completion or evidence readiness.
Actual reads, writes, tool calls, messages, external effects, artifacts, approvals, denials, failures, retries, interventions, costs, and cleanup remain independently observable.
Retry and resume preserve attempt lineage, fresh lease state, authorization, idempotency or compensation policy, checkpoints, observed prior effects, and duplicate-effect handling.
Lost workers, expired leases, partial effects, timeouts, policy changes, unknown outcomes, and residuals cannot become silent success; they route to exact recovery or terminal custody.
Workspace, secret, context, adapter, and cleanup boundaries preserve taint, rights, authority, retention, and side-effect obligations through every produced derivative.
Produced, delivered, adjudicated, verified, artifact-captured, replay-declared, evidence-ready, user-accepted, supported, and released states remain distinct.

Evidence-ready completion requires exact approvals, permissions, observed effects, artifacts, verification refs, audit refs, residuals, replay or non-replay declaration, costs, terminal receipt, and non-claims for the declared boundary.

Cancellation, compensation, rollback, expiry, revocation, cleanup, and retirement preserve irreversible effects, external copies, disclosures, downstream artifacts, open obligations, costs, and residuals.
Feedback, procedures, memory candidates, policy changes, model updates, and support changes pass through their owning gates and are never inferred from job success.
Fairness, priority, starvation, deadline, preemption, and resource decisions remain inspectable with rejected, delayed, cancelled, and abandoned work in the denominator.

Every attempt, failure, denial, retry, timeout, lease loss, intervention, partial effect, compensation, quarantine, appeal, human work, privacy exposure, delay, cost, missed help, and residual remains in the denominator.
Schemas, authored fixtures, finite route theorems, documentation comparators, and local green checks establish only their exact record, formal, source, and environment boundaries.
failure_modes: Ambient-agent execution allows work or side effects without an admitted typed job, exact parent contract, authority, owner, or terminal receipt.
Contract or plan drift executes stale, widened, substituted, or outcome-edited work after the material boundary changed.
Lifecycle laundering treats queueing, leasing, running, output, delivery, acknowledgement, or idleness as completion or evidence readiness.
Permission and approval laundering widens grants, reuses stale approvals, self-approves, delegates beyond authority, or dispatches irreversible work without the current gate.
Tool, adapter, workspace, network, data, or secret overreach crosses the declared capability, purpose, context, isolation, disclosure, or retention boundary.
Unlogged-effect laundering omits actual reads, writes, messages, tool calls, disclosures, external changes, costs, or side records from receipts.
Scheduler theater reports a planned or available queue while work is starved, unfairly prioritized, repeatedly preempted, or never dispatched.
Lease and worker amnesia loses ownership, heartbeat, expiry, checkpoints, locks, prior effects, or terminal custody after crash or partition.
Retry duplication repeats non-idempotent effects, widens authority, reuses stale inputs, or resumes without reconciling prior partial outcomes.
Compensation and rollback laundering reports restoration while external effects, disclosures, artifacts, messages, model updates, or descendants remain.
Swarm explosion creates unbounded recursive jobs, duplicate work, circular dependencies, uncontrolled delegation, contention, or unauditable coordination.
Parentage loss severs job, attempt, contract, plan, approval, permission, context, adapter, artifact, and residual lineage.
Artifact and receipt theater accepts empty, stale, unverifiable, self-reported, wrong-environment, or reality-false outputs as completed work.
Delivery and evidence laundering converts user-visible output into verified, replayable, supported, safe, or released standing.
Failure and residual abandonment leaves blocked, quarantined, timed-out, partially effective, disputed, or expired jobs without owner, recovery, expiry, or terminal record.
Feedback and learning laundering turns successful work into silent plan edits, procedural memory, policy changes, model updates, or support promotion.
Failure and cost survivorship removes rejected, delayed, cancelled, retried, human-repaired, privacy-expensive, slow, missed-help, or residual work from comparisons.
Portability theater treats one authored fixture, scheduler, orchestrator, backend, model, domain, authority regime, fault schedule, or horizon as general Labor OS evidence.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current authored zero-model scaffold has one public typed-job schema, 3 valid/10 rejecting plan-execution fixtures, 2 valid/7 rejecting delivery/evidence-readiness traces, 2 valid/9 rejecting durable-lifecycle traces, one accepted no-promotion decision for the durable probe, and 27 Lean declarations under 5 public targets. Two declarations project modeled lifecycle/approval predicates, 23 prove finite route or negative-case consequences, and 2 restate authored probe summaries. No scheduler, worker, workspace, secret broker, permission service, approval service, adapter runner, external-effect observer, durable workflow engine, Temporal/Airflow/BPMN/Kubernetes integration, natural workload, useful advantage, causal ablation, reproduction, transfer, or core support effect exists.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature Labor OS must execute natural multi-step AI work through actual schedulers, workers, workspaces, permissions, approvals, adapters, artifacts, retries, crash recovery, compensation, delivery, adjudication, and evidence-readiness gates while comparing strong chat, ambient-agent, swarm, script, queue, DAG, durable-workflow, process-engine, and batch-job baselines under matched work, resources, authority, faults, and time. It must jointly improve useful throughput, authorization accuracy, side-effect fidelity, completion correctness, replay and recovery, false refusal, missed help, latency, privacy, human burden, fairness, and total cost; show mechanism-specific causal signatures; reproduce from locks; and transfer across models, domains, backends, schedulers, organizations, authority regimes, fault schedules, and time. Failed gates yield exact null, negative, narrowed, refuted, deprecated, or blocked dispositions rather than a deployed Labor OS claim.

chapters.title: Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay

chapters.insufficient: If outputs are not tied to inputs, tools, context, claims, and logs, later verification and improvement cannot know what happened.
chapters.core_claim: Execution should produce an artifact graph with audit logs, provenance, replay metadata, and links to claims and tests.

mechanism: Freeze an artifact admission envelope with stable artifact and revision identity, type, role, producer, parent job and attempt, intended consumers, consequence, authority, rights, retention, and expiry before reuse.
Compute content digests and, where policy matters, compound content-and-policy identities while treating paths, URLs, object keys, cache slots, and display names as mutable locations or projections.

Bind each artifact revision to exact source, dataset, contract, plan, job, attempt, context transaction, semantic certificate, model, prompt, tool, adapter, code, environment, policy, approval, permission, cost, and residual lineage that actually applied.

Record append-only or superseding audit events for creation, reads, writes, transformations, observations, verification, challenge, delivery, reuse, quarantine, revocation, deletion, export, and retirement with actor and clock boundaries.
Observe artifact bytes, external effects, command results, service responses, and side records through independently challengeable routes instead of accepting producer-authored receipts as reality.
Version derivations, revisions, merges, splits, semantic patches, alternate derivations, supersession, and invalidation without erasing prior bytes, decisions, failures, or disagreements.

Separate canonical artifacts and events from caches, indexes, summaries, dashboards, claim views, promotion labels, releases, and public pages, and bind every projection to its source digest, policy version, refresh state, and authority ceiling.

Maintain forward and reverse dependency indexes across artifacts, claims, tests, decisions, procedures, models, releases, and public surfaces so impact closure is computable rather than narrated after a defect.

Propagate quarantine, revocation, expiry, rights change, deletion duty, or discovered corruption through every reachable dependent unless a recorded valid alternate derivation preserves that descendant under the current policy.
Grade replay as retained-output playback, deterministic re-execution, semantic replay, partial re-execution, non-replayable, failed, or not attempted, with no vocabulary upgrade between grades.

Package replay with pinned inputs, code, model and tool versions, environment and dependency locks, seeds, external-service assumptions, irreversible-effect boundaries, evaluator identity, expected comparison, and retained failure output.

Reconcile asynchronous work through durable terminal receipts that bind accepted, running, failed, cancelled, compensated, delivered, and evidence-ready states to observed artifacts; queue acceptance or idleness is never completion.

Link artifacts to exact claim atoms, proof obligations, tests, benchmarks, decisions, downstream consumers, and support ceilings while requiring the owning Evidence and Claim Ledger gates for any support movement.
Challenge receipt faithfulness with independent byte, object, command, service, and downstream observations, trap receipts, mutation controls, sampled audits, and explicit attestation limits.

Declare an epistemic trusted computing base with minimal trusted components, roots, verifier versions, bounded delegation, recursion stops, compromise response, outside-TCB residuals, and no ambient self-verifier authority.

Carry privacy, confidentiality, licensing, consent, deletion, legal hold, redaction, disclosure, retention, and derivative obligations through lineage and revocation without pretending that an audit log authorizes collection or permanent storage.

Expose impact queries that identify every affected artifact, claim, test, procedure, model, release, public statement, owner, unresolved descendant, alternate derivation, and required remediation after change or challenge.

Compare folders, version control, event logs, provenance ontologies, dataset metadata, signed supply-chain attestations, workflow histories, lineage catalogs, and the governed artifact graph under matched natural work, faults, adversaries, costs, and delayed consequences.
interfaces: Labor OS supplies parent job, attempt, lease, observed-effect, delivery, compensation, and terminal-receipt lineage; Artifact Graphs owns output identity, derivation, replay, and record-reality custody rather than job lifecycle.

VCM and Context Transactions supply exact input views, source snapshots, transaction identity, taint, expiry, deletion, and semantic certificates; artifact records cite rather than silently absorb them.
Runtime Adapters and tool runners supply independently observable invocations, effects, outputs, errors, environment identity, and side records; an adapter success response is not artifact truth.

Source, data, model-weight, and supply-chain owners supply admitted inputs, signatures, attestations, usage conditions, compromise state, and lifecycle changes without granting downstream correctness or release authority.
Claim Ledgers and Evidence States own support transitions and defeaters; artifact links provide inspectable candidates and blockers only.

Verification, proof, benchmark, and evaluator owners supply exact mode-specific results, checker identity, assumptions, outcome limits, and expiry; Artifact Graphs preserves but does not validate every method.
Security, Privacy, Rights, Licensing, and accountable-human owners issue access, disclosure, redaction, retention, deletion, legal-hold, export, challenge, and exception decisions.
Procedural Memory may mine repeated traces only through declared quality, privacy, rights, contamination, and retirement gates; frequency is not reusable competence.
Readiness, Replacement, Rollback, and Publication owners consume impact closure, replay, residual, and revocation state while retaining deployment and release authority.
Resource Economics supplies storage, observation, replay, audit, human-review, latency, retention, deletion, and remediation budgets with complete failed-work denominators.
Accountable humans own contested identity, provenance gaps, destructive deletion, cross-party disclosure, exception, appeal, and irreversible remediation decisions within exact authority.

PROV-O, Croissant, in-toto, version control, workflow histories, and lineage catalogs supply interoperability and comparator vocabulary, not local integrity, completeness, usefulness, safety, or SOTA evidence.
invariants: Every admitted artifact revision has stable content identity, type, producer, parent work, role, consumer, authority, rights, retention, expiry, and owner; mutable locations never become identity authority.

Creation, observation, transformation, verification, challenge, reuse, quarantine, revocation, deletion, export, and retirement events are append-only or explicitly superseding with actor and clock boundaries.
Declared lineage distinguishes observed relations from producer assertions, inferred links, unknown parents, omitted effects, and unverified external records.
Canonical artifacts and events remain distinct from caches, indexes, summaries, dashboards, claims, promotions, releases, and public projections, each with source digest and refresh state.
Every derivation edge is traversable forward and backward; unknown, inaccessible, cyclic, or unresolved descendants remain blockers rather than disappearing from closure.
Upstream quarantine, revocation, expiry, rights change, deletion duty, or corruption invalidates all reachable dependents unless a valid policy-compatible alternate derivation is recorded.

Playback, deterministic re-execution, semantic replay, partial re-execution, failed replay, non-replayable, and not-attempted states remain distinct and preserve exact missing inputs and irreversible effects.
Replay claims bind inputs, code, models, tools, environment, seeds, external services, evaluators, expected comparison, observed divergence, cost, and residuals for the exercised attempt.
Queue acceptance, worker response, file existence, digest presence, command exit, delivery, and user acknowledgement never substitute for an observed terminal receipt and exact evidence gate.
A receipt cannot attest beyond independently observed bytes, objects, commands, services, effects, and trust roots; same-component agreement and receipt shape grant no additional authority.
Every verifier and attestation route names its trusted computing base, roots, version, bounded delegation, recursion stop, compromise response, and outside-TCB residual.
Claim, test, benchmark, support, readiness, procedure, model, and release consumers inherit artifact scope, replay grade, rights, expiry, defeaters, residuals, and non-claims.
Privacy, consent, confidentiality, licensing, deletion, legal hold, disclosure, redaction, retention, and export obligations propagate through derivatives and alternate copies.
Deletion or redaction preserves a non-sensitive tombstone and downstream obligation closure when permitted, without retaining prohibited content merely for audit convenience.
Every promotion, release, procedure, or model update affected by a changed artifact is discoverable through a bounded impact query with exact unresolved descendants and owners.
Alternate derivations preserve a descendant only when their complete current lineage independently satisfies the same consumer policy and does not reuse the revoked cause.

All observations, replay failures, missing artifacts, stale projections, rejected receipts, human interventions, privacy costs, storage costs, cleanup, and residuals remain in evaluation denominators.

Schemas, hashes, signed metadata, authored fixtures, finite theorems, green commands, CI records, and served pages establish only their exact record, cryptographic, formal, local, service, and time boundaries.
failure_modes: Artifact amnesia leaves useful or harmful work products without stable identity, parent work, source, context, tool, effect, claim, test, owner, or residual lineage.
Path and name authority makes relocation, cache movement, renaming, or object-store migration change identity or silently bind the wrong bytes.
Provenance theater records plausible asserted edges while actual inputs, transformations, side effects, omitted dependencies, or responsible actors remain unknown.
Audit-log theater accepts mutable, dropped, selectively written, unauthenticated, inaccessible, or producer-controlled events as a complete reconstruction.
Projection laundering lets a stale cache, report, dashboard, claim, promotion label, release, or public page outrank changed canonical state.
Incomplete reverse closure quarantines an upstream artifact while reachable claims, procedures, models, releases, copies, or public statements remain active.
Alternate-derivation laundering cites a nominal second path that shares the revoked source, compromised tool, stale policy, or unobserved transformation.
Replay-grade inflation calls retained playback deterministic re-execution, nondeterministic similarity semantic replay, or partial reconstruction complete reproduction.
Environment and service drift makes replay appear stable while model, tool, dependency, dataset, evaluator, network service, clock, or irreversible effect changed.
Queue-success laundering treats accepted, running, idle, delivered, or acknowledged work as terminally completed, compensated, or evidence ready.
Receipt-reality divergence preserves shape-valid but reality-false bytes, effects, commands, service states, or downstream outcomes.
Self-attestation laundering lets the producer, observer, verifier, trust root, and approver collapse into one component or dependency without a visible residual.
Trust recursion and verifier capture extend confidence indefinitely through fluent summaries, shared evaluators, compromised keys, circular attestations, or missing recursion stops.
Evidence laundering converts artifact existence, digest agreement, replay success, signed lineage, or audit completeness into correctness, usefulness, safety, support, or release.
Privacy and rights laundering retains prohibited content, exposes sensitive traces, ignores consent or deletion, or uses auditability as authority for permanent surveillance.
Procedural contamination turns repeated erroneous, unauthorized, private, adversarial, or obsolete traces into reusable memory or training data.
Cost survivorship omits failed replay, storage, indexing, challenge, human review, privacy work, deletion, remediation, downtime, and unresolved-descendant cost.
Portability and SOTA theater treats one repository, hash, fixture family, CI service, public page, model, organization, threat model, or time point as complete provenance or general superiority.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is two public artifact/projection schemas; 2 valid/6 rejecting replay fixtures; 1 valid/10 rejecting projection-revocation records; 1 valid/4 rejecting record-reality sequences; 3 valid/6 rejecting receipt-faithfulness records; a four-surface repository audit with 55 digest checks and five mutations; four deterministic challenges and five mutations; one live artifact with three observation routes and seven mutations; four sampled artifacts with twelve observation routes and eight mutations; 3 valid/6 rejecting epistemic-TCB records; one historical GitHub Actions service record; one historical public-page fetch record; eight accepted no-promotion decisions; and forty-three Lean declarations under ten public targets. These artifacts exercise authored record shape, finite route consequences, current or historical repository observations, digest and command challenges, exact no-promotion boundaries, and service-status facts only. They do not implement a deployed graph, complete event capture, open-world provenance, independent attestation service, transitive revocation runtime, replay engine, privacy/deletion closure, semantic correctness, causal benefit, or transfer.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature artifact-evidence plane runs a versioned content-addressed graph over natural multi-step AI work and independently observed effects. It compares folders, version control, workflow histories, provenance ontologies, data-lineage catalogs, Croissant-style metadata, in-toto-style signed layouts, and the full governed graph under matched models, tools, sources, authority, rights, storage, compute, faults, adversaries, and horizons. Independent observers measure lineage precision and recall, missing and false edges, receipt-reality error, replay fidelity by grade, reverse-closure completeness, revocation latency, alternate-derivation validity, stale-public-surface time, privacy leakage, deletion closure, useful downstream reuse, false blocking, incident reconstruction, human burden, latency, storage, remediation, and total cost. Prospective ablations remove content identity, independent observation, canonical/projection separation, reverse closure, replay locks, receipt challenges, trust-base boundaries, rights propagation, or complete denominators; reproduction and transfer vary repositories, artifact types, models, tools, workflow engines, storage backends, organizations, threat models, legal regimes, and delayed changes. The chapter advances only if the full graph is not dominated on that joint frontier and its unique mechanisms survive causal, reproduction, and transfer tests. This is a target campaign, not evidence that the repository has solved provenance, truth, auditability, reproducibility, privacy, safety, or state-of-the-art artifact governance.

proof_targets.target: A reachable artifact lifecycle preserves exact artifact, content, parent-job, source, context, transaction, certificate, tool, claim, test, policy, and consumer custody from registration through consumer-acknowledged admission without assigning support or external effects.
Missing provenance, replay, observation, cross-check, trap, attestation-limit, trust-root, verifier-separation, recursion-stop, residual, revocation, or consumer obligations block lifecycle progress.
The refinement preserves exact replay-packet custody and requires replay metadata, sufficient grade, limits, active certificates, and replay validation before reality review.
The independent consumer preserves the exact one-valid/four-invalid stale/partial/fresh record-reality sequence without assigning support.
The independent consumer preserves the exact three-valid/six-invalid receipt-faithfulness suite while requiring observation, independent cross-checks, trap challenges, and bounded attestation.
The independent consumer preserves the exact four-record/five-mutation repository audit while treating current digest and command checks as bounded observations rather than open-world truth.
The independent consumer preserves the exact four-response/five-mutation deterministic repository challenge while bounding fingerprint and digest observations to the sampled artifacts.
The independent consumer preserves one-artifact live attestation with three observation routes and seven mutations without treating a dirty or uncommitted target as attested.
The independent consumer preserves the four-artifact randomized attestation with twelve accepted observation routes and eight mutations without generalizing beyond the sample.
The independent consumer preserves the exact three-valid/six-invalid epistemic-TCB suite while requiring a bounded trusted core, roots, verifier separation, recursion stop, and outside-TCB residuals.

chapters.title: Runtime Adapters, Tool Permissions, and Human Approval
chapters.problem: Plans become real-world effects only through tools, runtimes, deployment adapters, and approval gates.
chapters.insufficient: Tool calling without a permission and approval model turns reasoning errors into side effects.
chapters.core_claim: Runtime adapters should enforce typed permissions, sandboxing, human approval, and post-action evidence capture.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports controlled runtime adapters through typed jobs, tool isolation, contract locks, human approval, blind secret handles, Digital SCIFs, evidence records, audit logs, replay, and promotion through tests/residuals/regressions.
claim_source_mappings.limits: No live adapter runner, approval service, sandbox, security test, or Talos runtime result has been reproduced here.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for contract locks, clearance checks, context slicing, blind handle use, sidecar permission checks, Digital SCIF isolation/wipe, security audit, rollback-as-negative-constraint, handle maps, RBAC policies, audit logs, immutable/auditable artifacts, proof bundles, replay, re-verification, and evidence audit. No live adapter runner, approval service, sandbox, handle broker, security result, replay result, or runtime execution is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports intent-to-runtime boundaries through structured command contracts, runtime adapters, verified outputs, deployment feedback, residuals, tools, benchmarks, and regression coverage.
claim_source_mappings.limits: No deployed runtime adapter, feedback loop, or verified VIEA execution trace exists in this repository.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for intent-to-execution framing, command-contract fields, risk-scaled command completeness, runtime adapter targets, target-specific verification, deployment gates with approval/rollback/monitoring, permission envelopes, least privilege, controlled external execution, benchmark runs, and feedback collection. mechanism: Freeze an invocation contract with stable invocation, job, attempt, adapter, target, operation, requester, consumer, consequence, risk, authority, rights, budget, deadline, expiry, policy, and environment identities before effect outcomes.

Classify target type, effect class, impact, reversibility, external visibility, affected parties, data sensitivity, secret use, legal or organizational consequence, and required recovery objective prospectively.
Validate exact contract, plan, job, attempt, principal, delegation, authority ceiling, approval policy, current lifecycle, and unresolved blocker parentage before an adapter can request capability.

Resolve a typed capability envelope over executable and digest, arguments, read and write objects or prefixes, network endpoints, API methods, data fields, clock, randomness, environment, secrets, hardware, rate, cost, retention, and downstream delegation.

Bind stable target and input identity, provenance, context transaction, taint, policy, rights, freshness, and pre-state without allowing aliases, semantic neighbors, summaries, or stale addresses to substitute for the authorized object.

Intersect parent authority, principal delegation, capability policy, target policy, security state, data and rights policy, risk tier, resource budget, and current revocation state; the adapter cannot mint or widen a grant.
Keep semantic resolution, certificates, tool discovery, model confidence, and intent interpretation separate from authorization; a correct operation label never grants effect authority.

Require a structured approval for applicable effects with exact target, operation, scope, inputs, risk, reversibility, conditions, evidence view, approver competence, authority, expiry, revocation, reuse, appeal, and non-claim boundaries.

Assess reviewer readiness and oversight degradation through workload, consecutive approvals, alert quality, rationale quality, contradiction, automation recommendation, independent evidence, conflicts, fatigue, rotation, delay, and escalation before accepting approval.

Issue a purpose-bound effect lease with exact capability, target, scope, authority ceiling, sandbox, secret handles, time, rate, cost, idempotency, retry, revocation, partition, and no-delegation conditions.

Provision and independently identify the enforcement environment, sandbox, process or container boundary, filesystem and network policy, secret broker, resource limits, observation hooks, cleanup, and attestation state before dispatch.

Capture pre-state, expected post-state, declared effect inventory, verification plan, checkpoint, rollback or compensation handle, irreversible residual forecast, incident route, and abort conditions before mutation.

Dispatch the exact adapter, executable, arguments, inputs, target, lease, and environment once all current gates pass, with duplicate-effect and confused-deputy defenses across delegation, retry, resume, and replay.

Observe actual reads, writes, messages, API calls, network flows, secret uses, costs, outputs, errors, side records, post-state, downstream effects, and policy decisions independently of model and adapter self-report.

Handle denial, timeout, crash, partial effect, partition, stale grant, revocation race, lease loss, observer loss, sandbox escape, secret exposure, unknown outcome, and human intervention through typed stop, quarantine, reconciliation, escalation, or incident custody.

Rollback, compensate, revoke, contain, clean up, or quarantine through the declared mechanism while preserving external copies, disclosures, messages, physical effects, descendants, recovery time, failed restoration, and irreversible residuals.

Issue a terminal effect receipt binding request, parentage, authority, approval, lease, sandbox, secrets, actual effects, pre/post state, verification, rollback, incidents, costs, audit refs, artifact refs, residuals, support ceiling, and non-claims.

Compare direct tool calling, prompt-only restrictions, ordinary OS/container sandboxes, capability systems, policy-mediated tool calls, human approval, workflow engines, CaMeL-like control/data separation, AgentDojo-like adversarial tasks, Inspect-like harnesses, and the full adapter under matched natural work, authority, faults, adversaries, resources, and delayed outcomes.
interfaces: Intent, Contracts, Planning, and Labor OS supply the exact authorized job, plan node, requester, consequence, risk, and lifecycle; Runtime Adapters own effect dispatch rather than objective or plan correctness.

Security kernels, identity, delegation, policy, and secret brokers issue and revoke principals, capabilities, authority ceilings, sandbox policy, and opaque handles; adapters consume but cannot widen them.
VCM and Context Transactions supply exact input views, target refs, taint, freshness, deletion, rights, and semantic certificates; availability and semantic resolution are not authority.
Artifact Graphs own input/output identity, actual lineage, replay grade, audit reconstruction, receipt faithfulness, and impact closure; adapters emit observations and receipts into that custody.
Accountable humans and approval-policy owners own high-impact approval, reviewer qualification, exception, appeal, override, incident, and irreversible-effect decisions within exact authority.
Privacy, Rights, Licensing, data owners, and affected-party governance constrain collection, access, use, disclosure, retention, deletion, export, redress, and derivative obligations.
Verification, proof, benchmark, and outcome evaluators own mode-specific correctness and utility judgments; an effect receipt reports what happened, not that it was wise or successful.
Resource Economics supplies compute, latency, rate, spend, storage, human-review, recovery, and displaced-work budgets with complete failed and denied effect denominators.
Inter-Stack Protocols supply remote principal, peer, credential, delegation, budget, revocation, dispute, and settlement records; remote protocol success is not local effect authority.
Replacement, Rollback, Readiness, and Publication owners decide promotion, deployment, recovery standing, external delivery, and release; adapter success grants none of them.
Incident response, monitoring, and accountable operators consume live denials, escapes, secret exposure, unknown outcomes, stale authority, rollback failure, residuals, and remediation duties.

Inspect AI, AgentDojo, CaMeL, OWASP, human-factors literature, CAP, workflow systems, sandboxes, and capability systems supply comparator and threat vocabulary, not local enforcement or SOTA evidence.
invariants: Every invocation binds one exact job, attempt, principal, delegation, adapter, target, operation, capability, consequence, risk, authority, rights, policy, budget, deadline, expiry, environment, consumer, and owner.

Requested executable, arguments, reads, writes, network, APIs, data fields, clock, randomness, environment, secrets, hardware, rate, cost, retention, and delegation remain within the intersection of current parent, principal, policy, approval, and lease scopes.
Semantic resolution, aliases, addresses, certificates, tool discovery, model confidence, and intent interpretation never create or widen authority.
Target, input, context, policy, approval, authority, capability, lease, sandbox, secret, executable, and pre-state identity are fresh and unchanged at dispatch or the gate reruns.
High-impact, externally visible, privacy-sensitive, credentialed, irreversible, safety-relevant, financial, physical, or institutional effects require exact current approval when policy says so.
Approval is purpose-bound, competent, informed, scoped, expiring, revocable, non-transitive, non-reusable by default, and distinct from request, execution, verification, support, and release.
Reviewer overload, rubber-stamping, alarm fatigue, automation bias, conflict, missing qualifications, missing evidence, or visible contradiction cannot route directly to high-impact dispatch.
Effect leases are least-privilege, purpose-bound, time/rate/cost bounded, revocable, non-escalating, partition-aware, and preserved across delegation, retry, resume, and replay.
Declared scope, simulated policy, cooperative process behavior, or process observation cannot be labeled OS or hardware enforcement without an identified independently observed enforcement instance.

Secret values remain inside the broker and authorized destination path; model context, logs, receipts, errors, artifacts, and downstream prompts receive handles or bounded disclosures rather than raw material.
Actual reads, writes, calls, flows, messages, secret uses, costs, outputs, errors, external effects, post-state, and policy decisions remain independently observable within declared limits.
Queue, dispatch, tool return, success status, file existence, API acknowledgement, human approval, and receipt shape never substitute for actual-effect observation and terminal custody.
Playback, deterministic simulation, bounded live reexecution, and deployed live reexecution remain distinct and bind exact executable, environment, effects, observers, and irreversible boundaries.

Revoked, expired, stale, partitioned, mismatched, over-ceiling, or unknown approval, authority, capability, lease, sandbox, secret, policy, or target state blocks mutation until fresh authority or independently observed no-mutation closure exists.

Rollback and compensation claims inventory every local, remote, informational, financial, physical, organizational, copied, disclosed, and descendant effect; unhandled effects remain explicit residuals.
Effect receipts separate request, approval, dispatch, observation, verification, rollback, incident, artifact, evidence, support, readiness, and release state with exact owners.

Every denial, false refusal, missed help, approval delay, human intervention, sandbox or observer cost, partial effect, secret exposure, incident, rollback failure, cleanup, and residual remains in evaluation denominators.

Schemas, authored fixtures, finite theorems, temp-file writes, exact local rollback, synthetic safety tasks, framework availability, risk-taxonomy coverage, and green commands establish only their exact record, local, formal, task, and time boundaries.
failure_modes: Ambient tool authority lets cognition invoke effects without an exact parent job, principal, capability, target, lease, policy, or terminal receipt.
Capability overreach widens executable, argument, object, path, network, API, data, clock, randomness, environment, secret, hardware, rate, cost, retention, or delegation scope.
Semantic or alias escalation turns a recognized operation, nearby address, tool discovery, fluent rationale, or certificate into authority over a different target or effect.
Confused-deputy and delegation laundering use adapter, service, human, or remote peer authority that the requesting principal never held.
Prompt and data injection lets untrusted content alter control policy, tool choice, arguments, destination, secret use, approval interpretation, or effect scope.
Approval bypass, laundering, reuse, expiry loss, coercion, capture, or self-approval converts a narrow or stale human decision into broad authority.
Oversight degradation makes overloaded, unqualified, rubber-stamping, alarm-fatigued, biased, conflicted, or evidence-starved reviewers a decorative gate.
Sandbox theater reports declared or cooperative scope as OS/hardware enforcement while escape, network, child process, side channel, or privileged host paths remain reachable.
Secret-handle failure materializes credentials into model context, logs, errors, artifacts, child processes, unauthorized destinations, or retained memory.
Stale and partitioned authority dispatches on revoked grants, expired leases, unsynchronized policy, healed caches, missing fresh receipts, or grant/effect races.
Unobserved-effect laundering omits reads, writes, calls, messages, disclosures, costs, external copies, physical actions, side records, or delayed consequences.
Success and receipt laundering turns tool return, API acknowledgement, file creation, approval, rollback handle, or structured receipt into correct, useful, safe, supported, or released work.
Replay laundering promotes record playback or deterministic simulation to live reexecution, or a disposable bounded probe to deployed equivalence.
Retry, resume, delegation, or duplicate dispatch repeats non-idempotent effects, widens authority, reuses stale approval, or loses prior partial outcomes.
Rollback and compensation laundering declares restoration while messages, payments, disclosures, deployments, remote state, physical effects, copies, descendants, or obligations persist.
Incident and residual abandonment leaves denial, escape, secret exposure, unknown outcome, partial effect, disputed approval, rollback failure, or affected party without owner and remedy.

Safety-by-refusal and denominator laundering suppress useful actions, hide false refusals, omit delayed approvals and human work, or exclude failed/unsafe effects to make the adapter look safe or efficient.

Portability and SOTA theater treats one temp-file trace, synthetic fixture, sandbox, policy parser, benchmark, model, tool family, organization, authority regime, or threat taxonomy as general adapter safety.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is one public invocation schema; a 2 valid/7 rejecting permission harness; one rollback-exact temporary-file effect with two no-mutation denial controls; 2 valid/12 rejecting adversarial-boundary records; 3 valid/7 rejecting human-oversight records and one accepted no-promotion decision; 3 valid/6 rejecting partitioned-authority records; one bounded/10 rejecting historical capability-envelope record; the adjacent QCSA synthetic authority and one reversible vertical-effect results at their exact non-core ceilings; and forty-nine Lean declarations under six public targets. These artifacts exercise authored record shape, finite route consequences, one public-safe local write/restore, synthetic authority, approval, injection-adjacent, revocation, reviewer, and partition controls only. They do not establish deployed adapters, real OS or hardware confinement, prompt-injection robustness, approval quality, reviewer correctness, secret isolation, distributed authority, target-service rollback, useful-effect advantage, production safety, independent reproduction, or transfer.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature capability firewall executes natural public-safe and deployment-like document, code, database, API, messaging, payment-simulator, service, hardware-simulator, and organizational tasks across multiple models, tools, sandboxes, capability systems, approval policies, secret brokers, and authority services. It compares direct tool calling, prompt-only controls, OS/container sandboxes, capability systems, policy-mediated tool calls, workflow engines, human approval, CaMeL-like control/data separation, AgentDojo-like adversarial tasks, Inspect-like harnesses, and the full adapter under matched information, authority, resources, faults, attacks, and horizons. Independent observers jointly measure correct useful effects, unauthorized or hidden effects, scope precision/recall, injection success, confused-deputy and escape success, secret exposure, approval calibration and degradation, stale/revoked dispatch, duplicate effects, effect-receipt fidelity, rollback and compensation completeness, false refusal, missed help, privacy, latency, human work, recovery, and total cost. Prospective ablations remove parent authority, typed capability, control/data separation, approval, reviewer-readiness checks, effect leases, observed enforcement, secret handles, pre-state, independent effect observation, revocation freshness, rollback inventory, receipts, or complete denominators; reproduction and transfer vary models, tasks, tools, targets, operating systems, sandboxes, organizations, authority and rights regimes, partitions, attacks, and time. The core advances only if the full adapter is not dominated on that joint frontier and its unique mechanisms survive causal, independent-reproduction, and transfer tests. This is a target campaign, not evidence of deployed adapter safety or state-of-the-art effect governance.

proof_targets.target: A runtime adapter invocation is valid only when job permissions include the adapter capability and its effect lease is active, scoped to that capability, and sandboxed.

A high-impact adapter call without approval or a required rollback handle is rejected, mismatched, expired, or unsandboxed effect leases cannot validate invocation, and modeled confused-deputy or sandbox-escape attempts cannot pass dispatch.

A modeled runtime-adapter effect replay routes missing permission, expired approval, missing no-mutation evidence, inexact rollback, missing receipts, repository/network side effects, and support-state effects away from accepted replay while admitting a public-safe rollback-exact replay.

A modeled runtime-adapter adversarial boundary review routes confused-deputy parent mismatches, authority ceiling overruns, approval scope mismatch, expired approval, sandbox escape, secret materialization, missing rollback, missing receipts, missing audits, support-state effects, and missing non-claim boundaries away from dispatch while admitting complete low-impact and scoped high-impact records.

A modeled runtime-adapter revocation review routes revoked approvals, revoked leases, and revoked authority receipts away from dispatch unless no-mutation denial evidence exists, while complete non-revoked records preserve receipt, audit, support-state, and non-claim boundaries.

A modeled human-oversight degradation fixture accepts scoped low-fatigue approval, routes overloaded reviewers to delay or rotation, blocks automation-bias cases, and rejects missing qualifications, rubber-stamped approvals, alarm-fatigue acceptance, support-state promotion, and missing non-claim boundaries.

open_evidence_gaps: One hand-authored five-project capability-envelope record is linked to the existing temp-file replay and rejects ten scope/replay/overclaim mutations; it does not observe an OS-enforced sandbox or deployed adapter.

No local result establishes production process confinement, network isolation, secret-handle safety, live approval-service quality, target-service rollback, deployed revocation propagation, or deployed replay equivalence.

chapters.problem: Local planning, context, artifact, and runtime contracts lose their binding when a request crosses to an independently operated protocol endpoint, agent, service, or value network without a record that separates peer identity, delegated authority, economic commitment, result evidence, revocation, and residual ownership.
chapters.insufficient: Runtime Adapters own local effects, Artifact Graphs own generic provenance and replay, Resource Economics owns local budget policy, Personal Compute Hives owns federation topology, and Security Kernel owns protected handles, but none owns the cross-stack contract that binds protocol structure, identities, credentials, delegation, consideration, receipts, disputes, and revocation before a remote handoff reaches local dispatch.
chapters.core_claim: A governed stack routes each cross-stack request through a versioned exchange contract that binds protocol and schema version, sender and receiver identities, endpoint and capability declaration, requested task or artifact, principal and delegated authority, credential verification, audience, scope, expiry, budget or consideration, expected receipt, dispute and revocation paths, and residual owner; an absent, mismatched, expired, revoked, unverified, or budget-unreserved required record blocks dispatch or routes accountable review, but does not itself establish peer trustworthiness, task or artifact truth, effect safety, payment settlement, legal validity, economic fairness, privacy, authorization correctness, or ASI.
mechanism: Freeze the protocol, schema, transport binding, session, endpoint, and allowed change triggers before interpreting an exchange.
Represent owner, operator, peer, endpoint, agent, user, principal, subject, issuer, holder, verifier, and payee as separable roles rather than aliases.
Bind task and artifact semantics to a namespace, version, digest, expected deliverable, acceptance predicate, and originating contract.
Record endpoint and capability declarations as expiring, provenance-bearing, untrusted advertisements rather than trust or competence evidence.
Resolve an identifier together with its method, controller, verification method, service endpoint, update history, and policy scope.
Verify each credential or presentation against its securing mechanism, issuer policy, status, freshness, audience, disclosure boundary, and correlation risk.
Build a bounded delegation chain with principal, audience, capability, scope, expiry, revocation, non-delegation constraints, and every intervening deputy.
Make a separate local authorization decision after identity, credential, and delegation checks; none of those checks authorizes an effect by itself.
Reserve budget or consideration with cap, denomination, fees, timeout, release, refund, settlement, and dispute terms while preserving every economic state separately.
Attach consent, confidentiality, rights, retention, deletion, export, and minimum-disclosure obligations to the exchanged fields and artifacts.
Negotiate versions, bindings, capabilities, downgrade policy, compatibility evidence, and migration behavior without silent fallback.
Route only a complete bounded request to the existing local owner; the exchange layer never performs or authorizes the local effect.
Bind each response to the request, contract version, responding peer, referenced artifacts, declared status, economic outcome, and residuals.
Require an independent result owner to test the response and artifacts against the intended consumer's acceptance predicate.
Handle timeout, cancellation, partial delivery, dispute, refund, retry, and idempotency as explicit terminal or recoverable states.
Propagate endpoint, key, credential, delegation, policy, budget, and revocation changes to every affected in-flight exchange.
Issue an audit record that names residuals, appeal and dispute routes, retained state, released reservations, and the accountable owner.
Compare MCP, A2A, direct APIs, queues or brokers, DID/VC identity, Interledger-like value exchange, and the full contract on matched natural workloads.
interfaces: Intent-to-Execution Contracts owns the originating intent and task contract; this chapter preserves principal, scope, and accepted task boundaries across a peer handoff.
Labor OS and Organizational Design owns roles, work assignment, and escalation; this chapter binds the cross-organization handoff without defining the organization.
Security Kernel and Digital SCIFs owns protected handles, secrets, and local authorization policy; this chapter transports only bounded identity and authority evidence.
Runtime Adapters, Tool Permissions, and Human Approval owns local effects; this chapter supplies a bounded remote request, not an effect lease.
Virtual Context ABI owns context provenance, admission, and taint; this chapter carries only a bounded context/artifact reference across the exchange.

Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay and Evidence Engineering own provenance, receipts, replay, and support; this chapter owns cross-stack identity, delegation, consideration, and exchange completeness.
Resource Economics and Token Budgets owns local capacity and cost policy; this chapter binds a remote consideration or reservation without claiming settlement or fairness.
Privacy, Rights, and Legal Constraints owns consent, rights, retention, deletion, export, and legal policy; this chapter carries their obligations across the boundary.
Human Collaboration and Dispute Resolution owns consequential review, appeal, and adjudication; this chapter supplies the disputed exchange record.
Readiness, Deployment, and Release Governance owns promotion and release; interoperability success cannot promote a system by itself.
Personal Compute Hives and Federated Edge Intelligence owns federation topology and placement; this chapter owns the contract across a selected peer edge.
MCP, A2A, DID Core, VC Data Model, and Interledger are external comparators and bindings, while QCSA is adjacent semantic-addressing evidence rather than an exchange authority.
invariants: Every exchange records exact protocol, schema, binding, session, endpoint, and negotiation versions.
Owner, operator, peer, endpoint, agent, user, principal, subject, issuer, holder, verifier, and payee remain distinct unless an evidenced mapping explicitly relates them.
Endpoint and capability declarations remain untrusted advertisements until separately qualified for the requested use.
Identity resolution and credential verification cannot silently become local authorization or proof of peer trustworthiness.
Delegated authority remains bound to principal, audience, capability, scope, expiry, policy, revocation, and non-delegation constraints.
Only the existing local authorization owner may release a request toward a local effect boundary.
Reservation, routing, delivery, acceptance, settlement, refund, and dispute remain distinct economic states.
A remote response remains a declared result until an independent owner tests the evidence required for the intended use.
Every disclosed field and artifact carries its consent, confidentiality, rights, retention, deletion, export, and minimum-disclosure policy.
Endpoint, key, credential, delegation, policy, budget, and revocation changes invalidate or re-review affected in-flight exchanges.
Version downgrade or binding fallback is explicit, policy-checked, and visible in the terminal record.
Retries are idempotent or carry a new exchange identity, and replayed messages cannot consume authority or value twice.
Partial delivery, timeout, cancellation, dispute, and refund retain custody and an accountable next owner.
Every terminal exchange names residuals, appeal paths, retained state, released reservations, and an accountable owner.
Evaluation denominators include every attempted peer, protocol, binding, task, identity method, credential path, threat, and economic outcome.
Protocol conformance does not imply semantic correctness, authorization correctness, result quality, privacy, safety, settlement, legal validity, or economic fairness.
A missing, mismatched, expired, revoked, unverified, or budget-unreserved required record routes to repair, review, dispute, or denial rather than ordinary dispatch.
Evidence language never exceeds the exercised peer, implementation, workload, threat, identity, economic, jurisdictional, and temporal envelope.
failure_modes: Capability-card theater treats a self-described endpoint as a trusted, competent, authorized, or safe peer.
Actor conflation treats an endpoint, owner, operator, agent, user, principal, credential subject, and payee as one actor.
Credential laundering turns a structurally valid or cryptographically verified presentation into trust, authorization, competence, or current status.
Delegation or deputy laundering widens a forwarded grant outside the originating principal's audience, capability, scope, expiry, or non-delegation rule.
Confused-audience routing accepts a credential, message, response, or payment instruction intended for another verifier or contract.
Replay or downgrade consumes authority, budget, value, or an effect twice or silently falls back to a weaker version or binding.
Semantic-equivalence laundering treats an alias, identifier mapping, schema translation, or migrated address as proof of task equivalence.
Prompt, data, or instruction injection crosses inside a protocol message or artifact and captures an unowned control surface.
Economic-reservation laundering turns a budget hold into consent, affordable execution, delivery, acceptance, settlement, or fair price.
Payment or settlement laundering turns a routed packet, connector acknowledgement, or balance update into legal finality or dispute-free transfer.
Result or receipt laundering turns a valid response shape, completion state, signature, or artifact receipt into task truth or permission to act.
A revocation race lets a stale endpoint, key, credential, delegation, policy, or budget state survive long enough to release a request.
Privacy and correlation leakage reveal unnecessary identity, credential, task, artifact, financial, or relationship data across peers.
Dispute and refund abandonment leaves partial delivery, cancellation, timeout, rejection, or remote failure without custody or remedy.
Availability or refusal failure blocks safe fallback, yields inconsistent terminal state, or pressures operators into bypassing controls.
Cascading multi-peer failure propagates a compromised message, poisoned memory, rogue agent, false declaration, or economic error across the federation.
Denominator and cost laundering reports successful interoperable cases while hiding failed peers, bindings, tasks, threat controls, human labor, latency, or fees.
State-of-the-art theater labels protocol conformance, one synthetic fixture, or cross-stack messaging as useful, safe, private, economically correct interoperability.
chapters.minimal_implementation: Nine digest-bound synthetic exchange records and eleven rejecting mutations bridge owned Lean routes for complete local handoff, missing sender, audience mismatch, expiry, invalid or revoked credentials, missing reserved budget, disputed receipt, and missing residual ownership. No peer, protocol, credential, payment, account, or remote effect ran.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature operational contract is a target tested through this program: The next claim-bearing campaign prospectively fixes matched natural tasks and runs public-safe implementations of MCP, A2A, DID/VC identity, multiple transport bindings, and a value reservation/settlement simulator or test network across multiple peers, vendors, models, protocol versions, identity methods, credentials, delegations, and operator domains. It compares direct REST or RPC, MCP or A2A alone, API-key or IAM-style controls, DID/VC, broker or workflow mediation, Interledger-like value exchange, and the full exchange contract. Joint metrics include useful interoperability, task and artifact correctness, authorization false acceptance and false denial, delegation drift, stale or revoked acceptance, downgrade and replay, privacy and correlation leakage, reservation, delivery, acceptance, settlement, dispute and refund correctness, latency, human effort, fees, residuals, and recovery. Prospectively fixed ablations must isolate identity, credential, delegation, authorization, semantic binding, privacy, economic, revocation, and independent-verification contributions; a separately implemented evaluator must reproduce conclusions across protocols, models, domains, organizations, jurisdictions, threat classes, and time. No stronger claim is available until that campaign passes.
codex_tests: Public-safe exchange contract workload with identity, delegation, expiry, revocation, budget, receipt, dispute, and residual negative controls

proof_targets.target: A finite requested cross-stack exchange with required invalid credential verification routes to denial rather than ordinary local dispatch, without inferring identity trust, task truth, authorization correctness, payment settlement, or ASI.

A finite value-bearing requested cross-stack exchange with all required identity and authority records but no reserved budget routes to budget repair rather than ordinary local dispatch, without inferring payment, settlement, economic fairness, authority correctness, or ASI.
A complete finite exchange record reaches the existing local dispatch boundary without proving runtime authorization or execution.
Missing sender identity routes to identity repair.
Audience or scope mismatch denies ordinary dispatch.
An expired request denies ordinary dispatch.
A verified but no-longer-current required credential denies dispatch.
A disputed expected receipt routes to accountable review.
Missing residual ownership routes to accountable review.

open_evidence_gaps: No ASI Stack protocol endpoint, peer discovery service, identity resolver, credential issuer or verifier, delegated-authority chain, payment connector, settlement workflow, dispute workflow, or cross-stack dispatch has run.

No local result establishes peer trustworthiness, identity binding, authorization correctness, task or artifact truth, effect safety, payment settlement, legal validity, economic fairness, privacy, or ASI.

The nine Lean routes and digest-bound fixture establish only finite record consequences; they do not establish any real protocol, identity, credential, policy, receipt, payment, peer, privacy, or task-quality result.

Run the prospectively fixed synthetic peer campaign with cached-versus-per-request qualification and false-dispatch, false-denial, stale-acceptance, dispute, latency, disclosure, operator-effort, and residual measures next.

mechanism: Freeze the target task family, intended consumers, authority boundary, risk, horizon, success criteria, and non-claims before mining traces.
Inventory the complete eligible trace universe with stable run, task, artifact, context, model, tool, effect, evaluator, and outcome identities.
Separate semantic facts, episodic observations, procedural traces, policies, skills, tools, and model-weight learning so one memory domain cannot launder another.
Verify source and effect receipts against artifact and runtime reality before any trace is eligible for learning.
Define comparability prospectively, then cluster by task semantics, pre-state, authority, environment, route, effect class, and outcome rather than superficial text similarity.
Preserve successes, failures, near misses, abstentions, interventions, repairs, and censored or missing outcomes in the candidate cluster.
Infer a candidate invariant together with counterexamples, alternative explanations, causal dependencies, and the scope it cannot cover.
Discover explicit parameters, latent context dependencies, defaults, ranges, units, interaction effects, and unresolved confounders.
Specify preconditions, postconditions, authority and rights requirements, side effects, resource bounds, failure semantics, and residual owners.
Synthesize a candidate procedure, checklist, workflow, program, or tool card with source lineage, environment lock, reproducible build, and rollback handle.
Run static, dynamic, adversarial, mutation, property, negative-control, and independent result checks appropriate to the candidate effect class.

Compare manual or interpreter execution, retrieval-only memory, prompt or checklist reuse, scripts, workflow engines, learned tool use, skill libraries, and the governed procedure under matched resources.
Run preregistered regressions, hidden holdouts, distribution shifts, counterfactual cases, and transfer tests while keeping every attempt in the denominator.
Measure useful success, error and unsafe reuse, false refusal, latency, token and compute cost, human effort, privacy, recovery, and residuals jointly.
Qualify the candidate through explicit trace, cluster, candidate, verified, routable, quarantined, revised, deprecated, and retired lifecycle states.
Expose a qualified procedure to Routing only through a scoped, fresh lease whose preconditions and authority are rechecked at invocation.
Monitor route drift, precondition decay, source or dependency change, regression failure, incidents, evaluator drift, and delayed harm after reuse.
Quarantine, roll back or compensate effects, revise or retire the procedure, preserve failure lessons, and invalidate affected descendants when qualification fails.
interfaces: Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay owns durable trace identity, lineage, observed receipts, replay grade, and impact closure.
Virtual Context ABI and Context Transactions own semantic, episodic, and working-memory state; this layer owns reusable execution structure only.
Claim Ledgers and Belief Revision owns propositional support and contradiction; a successful procedure cannot become a true fact by reuse.
Cognitive Compilation and Semantic IR owns executable representation and semantic lowering; this layer owns evidence-gated promotion from repeated traces.
Routing Heads and Specialist Cores owns selection among qualified procedures and fallbacks; this layer supplies lifecycle and applicability records.
Runtime Adapters owns authority-bound effects and effect observation; procedural memory cannot grant itself permission.
Verification Bandwidth, Spinoza, and Evidence Engineering own verification obligations, proof artifacts, evidence, and support decisions.
Benchmark Ratchets owns benchmark floors, anti-Goodhart controls, holdouts, and regression pressure used during qualification.
Stable Capability Fields, Replacement, and Readiness owns capability identity, promotion, quarantine, rollback, and release gates.
Security, Privacy, Rights, and Legal owners constrain trace admission, tool authority, disclosure, retention, deletion, and affected-party remedy.
Labor OS and Human Collaboration owns work roles, intervention, review, appeal, and organizational learning rather than hidden automation labor.
Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning owns dataset and model-state updates; compiled procedure reuse is not weight learning or erasure.
invariants: Every procedure retains stable identity for its complete eligible trace universe, included cluster, excluded traces, and sampling rule.
Semantic facts, episodic observations, procedural traces, policies, tools, and model-state learning remain distinct memory domains.
No declared source, intended action, or success label substitutes for independently observed source and effect receipts.
Comparability criteria are fixed before outcome inspection or every post-hoc change is recorded and re-evaluated.
Failures, near misses, abstentions, interventions, repairs, missing outcomes, and censored traces remain in the record and denominator.
The inferred invariant carries counterexamples, alternatives, assumptions, causal dependencies, and an explicit non-applicability envelope.
Parameters carry type, unit, range, default, provenance, interaction, and unknown-dependency records.
Preconditions, postconditions, authority, rights, side effects, resources, failures, and residual owners are machine-checkable at qualification and invocation.
The synthesized artifact is reproducible from bound source traces and cannot silently widen its task, authority, effect, data, or consumer scope.
Verification, regression, usefulness, safety, privacy, support, readiness, and release remain separate decisions with separate owners.
Matched baselines receive equivalent tasks, information, models, tools, authority, resources, human help, retries, and time.
Qualification denominators include every eligible, attempted, failed, refused, repaired, rolled-back, disputed, and delayed-outcome case.
Routable state requires clean current regressions, hidden holdouts, negative controls, active capability identity, rehearsed rollback, monitoring, and residual custody.
Routing rechecks task match, preconditions, authority, rights, freshness, risk, and resource ceiling for every invocation.
Dependency, policy, evaluator, environment, model, tool, source, or benchmark changes invalidate or requalify affected procedures and descendants.
Quarantine, revision, deprecation, and retirement remain reachable after promotion and block new ordinary routing when active.
Rollback claims inventory and observe all effects, copies, descendants, costs, disclosures, and delayed obligations rather than only restoring the procedure card.
Evidence language never exceeds the exercised tasks, traces, models, tools, effects, environments, threats, populations, organizations, and time.
failure_modes: Anecdote closure promotes one elegant or lucky trace without a comparable cluster.
Similarity laundering clusters superficially similar traces whose tasks, states, authority, environments, effects, or outcomes differ.
Memory-domain laundering turns facts, summaries, policies, model outputs, or intended actions into observed procedural evidence.
Receipt laundering accepts declared inputs, tool returns, or intended effects without checking artifact and runtime reality.
Success-only promotion removes failures, abstentions, interventions, repairs, missing outcomes, or negative examples.
Evaluator capture lets the same model, prompt, code, data, organization, or incentives generate and certify the procedure.
Hidden-parameter closure encodes latent context, defaults, units, or human knowledge as if the procedure were general.
Correlation-to-causation closure compiles a recurring shortcut or confounder instead of a mechanism that survives intervention.
Authority laundering lets a useful procedure acquire standing permission beyond the invoking principal, task, rights, or effect lease.
Semantic drift or dependency decay leaves a once-valid procedure routable after sources, APIs, policies, models, tools, or environments change.
Benchmark or regression overfitting preserves the visible floor while failing hidden natural work, transfer, or delayed outcomes.
Prompt, trace, or supply-chain poisoning installs malicious examples, dependencies, code, tests, monitors, or retirement logic.
Tool-bloat failure creates overlapping procedures whose retrieval, qualification, monitoring, and retirement cost exceeds saved reasoning.
Automation-complacency failure weakens human skill, review, exception handling, or responsibility while apparent throughput rises.
Route drift invokes a qualified procedure outside its task, precondition, authority, rights, risk, model, environment, or resource envelope.
Rollback theater restores a card or registry entry while effects, copies, disclosures, costs, descendants, or obligations persist.
Immortal promotion leaves quarantine, revision, deprecation, retirement, or descendant invalidation unreachable after routing begins.
State-of-the-art theater treats schema conformance, authored fixtures, finite routes, code generation, or reuse frequency as useful causal general procedural learning.
chapters.minimal_implementation: One public procedural-tool schema; three valid and six expected-invalid procedure-qualification packets; one bounded four-project trace-promotion record with ten rejecting mutations; and nineteen finite Lean declarations, classified as two direct predicate/projection checks and seventeen derived finite route, negative, or fixture consequences. No deployed trace miner, clusterer, parameter discovery, tool synthesizer, generated-tool correctness run, natural regression campaign, router, rollback execution, monitor, or retirement automation ran.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature operational contract is a procedure foundry tested on preregistered natural repeated work. It mines complete artifact and effect traces, separates memory domains, clusters comparable cases, preserves every failure and intervention, infers invariants with counterexamples and causal alternatives, discovers typed parameters, synthesizes reproducible candidates, and qualifies them through independent verification, matched baselines, hidden holdouts, adversarial tests, causal interventions, monitoring, rollback, quarantine, and retirement. Baselines include manual or interpreter execution, retrieval-only memory, prompts or checklists, scripts, workflow engines, learned tool use, program-library learning, skill libraries, and the full foundry under matched resources. Joint gates cover useful success, unsafe or wrong reuse, false refusal, generalization, transfer, route drift, authority, privacy, latency, token and compute cost, human effort, recovery, residuals, and total lifecycle burden. Predicted causal signatures must disappear when clustering, negative examples, parameter discovery, independent verification, regression holdouts, scoped routing, or retirement is ablated. A separately implemented evaluator must reproduce conclusions across models, tasks, tools, effect classes, environments, organizations, rights regimes, attacks, and time before any general procedural-learning or state-of-the-art claim is available.

proof_targets.target: A reachable procedure lifecycle preserves exact procedure, source-set, trace-cluster, abstraction, regression-suite, SCF, policy, and consumer custody from clustering through acknowledged routing and receipt-bound retirement without assigning support or external effects.

Missing comparable traces, negative examples, source/effect receipts, abstraction contracts, verification, regression clearance, benchmark floor, active SCF, rehearsed rollback, monitoring, residuals, non-claims, acknowledgment, or retirement custody blocks lifecycle progress or routes to quarantine.

open_evidence_gaps: No deployed trace miner, prospective comparability rule, loop clusterer, causal invariant learner, parameter-discovery system, tool synthesizer, independent generated-tool verifier, natural regression campaign, production router, or retirement automation has run.

The current schema, three valid and six invalid qualification packets, one four-project promotion record and ten mutations, and nineteen Lean declarations establish record and finite-route discipline only; they do not establish useful, causal, safe, general, private, efficient, or deployable procedural learning.

Run the preregistered natural repeated-work campaign against strong matched manual, retrieval, prompt/checklist, script, workflow, learned-tool-use, library-learning, and skill-library baselines with complete denominators and joint lifecycle metrics.

Require causal interventions, independent implementation and evaluation, and transfer across models, tasks, tools, effects, environments, organizations, rights regimes, attacks, and time before promoting the claim.
