chapters.title: Fast Generation Architectures

handoff_or_recovery_notes: 2026-06-24 browser-GPT fast-generation planning note; use as author-intent and literature-queue context only.

chapters.problem: The stack needs lower end-to-end time to useful accepted work without allowing raw token rate, synthetic proxy speed, aggregate serving throughput, or a cheaper unverified path to masquerade as intelligence, quality, safety, or evidence.
chapters.insufficient: Standard autoregressive decoding, isolated speculative or parallel methods, serving-only optimizations, and raw throughput leaderboards do not define a matched consumer/workload/model/hardware boundary, attribute verifier, repair, fallback, queueing, warmup, cache, memory, energy, human, and governance costs, or show that a faster route preserves quality and rights under load, failure, drift, and transfer.
chapters.core_claim: Fast-generation admission is consumer-, workload-, model-, hardware-, serving-policy-, and time-window-specific: a controller may route an eligible request through a named accelerated path only after prospectively binding the context, quality, risk, budget, metric, verifier, fallback, rollback, and expiry contracts; separating attempted, proposed, accepted, verified, delivered, and useful output; fully attributing queueing, prefill, decode, verification, repair, retry, fallback, cache, memory, bandwidth, energy, human, and governance burdens; and showing a meaningful end-to-end improvement over matched quality-equivalent baselines without violating safety, authority, rights, or residual gates. Raw tokens per second, FLOP estimates, aggregate throughput, synthetic templates, or unverified speed lifts alone cannot qualify a route.
mechanism: Freeze the consumer, permitted use, task cohort, input and output distributions, quality and safety predicates, model and tokenizer versions, hardware, runtime, serving policy, batch and concurrency regime, cache state, and evaluation horizon before observing candidate outcomes.

Register every autoregressive, speculative, multi-token, multi-head, feature-level, lookahead, diffusion, early-exit, recurrent, state-space, KV-cache, serving, or hybrid route by immutable implementation, configuration, dependency, checkpoint, compiler, and environment identity.

Choose matched quality-equivalent baselines prospectively, including an optimized autoregressive target-model route, an ordinary serving stack, and any stronger current method applicable to the exact workload and hardware.
Bind the exact VCM context packet, prompt and output contract, prefill state, cache provenance, warmup policy, seed policy, and invalidation conditions to each attempt.
Classify task risk, authority, affected-party rights, latency objective, deadline, reversibility, and minimum verification obligation before mode selection.
Define attempted, proposed, accepted, verified, delivered, and useful work as distinct denominators, with exact correctness, quality, safety, abstention, and selective-coverage criteria.

Let the planner request and the generation controller admit a mode only from prospectively qualified eligibility; neither route choice nor qualification may use hidden test outcomes or infer authority from semantic identity.

Execute every attempt with stable request, attempt, candidate, branch, seed, batch, tenant, device, and trace identities so retries, cancellations, partial outputs, and cross-request interference remain observable.

Measure queueing, scheduling, prefill, time to first token, inter-token latency, decode time, verification, repair, fallback, artifactization, and end-to-end completion, including median, tail, deadline-miss, and censored-failure behavior.
Record draft acceptance, rejection, correction, divergence, exactness claims, target-model distribution assumptions, and accepted-span length separately for every verifier or self-verifier path.

Run claim-, code-, citation-, artifact-, policy-, and outcome-appropriate verification, retaining evaluator identity, dependence, disagreement, false acceptance, false rejection, and abstention evidence.
Attribute verifier, repair, retry, rejection, fallback, escalation, and human-review work to the candidate route rather than charging it to an invisible external lane.

Account for compute, FLOPs or operation proxies, accelerator occupancy, memory capacity, KV-cache bytes, bandwidth, network, storage, energy, money, human effort, and governance work under matched load.

Observe batch and concurrency effects, tenant isolation, fairness, starvation, throughput-latency tradeoffs, cache eviction, and load-shedding so aggregate service gains cannot hide individual regressions.

Route uncertainty, failed acceptance, safety or rights violations, resource exhaustion, stale caches, verifier overload, and drift to an executable slower fallback, abstention, quarantine, or stop path with recovery receipts.

Estimate causal contribution through matched randomization or blocked assignment, strong negative controls, mode and verifier ablations, load interventions, and complete attempt denominators rather than before/after anecdotes.

Issue consumer-scoped evidence transitions only after preregistered joint quality, usefulness, safety, latency, resource, rights, recovery, and total-burden gates pass; retain all null, negative, and contradictory results.

Monitor qualification expiry, workload and model drift, hardware or runtime change, incident recurrence, descendant use, rollback and compensation, and owned residuals; requalify after every material change.
interfaces: Intent and Planning supply the exact request, consumer, task contract, risk, deadline, alternatives, and fallback objective without granting generation authority.
Virtual Context supplies the versioned model-visible packet, source and omission lineage, cache eligibility, adequacy state, expiry, and invalidation triggers.
Routing and Readiness expose eligible candidate modes, qualifications, capacity, leases, and abstention paths; Fast Generation owns the final request-specific speed-route admission.
Runtime Adapters bind model, tokenizer, checkpoint, compiler, device, serving engine, batch, concurrency, seed, clock, and instrumentation identities and enforce the admitted lease.
Resource Economics supplies prospective latency, compute, memory, bandwidth, energy, money, human, and governance budgets and receives the complete realized bill.
Security, Authority, and Rights gates bind permitted data, tenant isolation, side effects, disclosure, approval, reversibility, and affected-party constraints independently of speed.
The generator emits attempt, branch, proposal, acceptance, correction, cancellation, and resource traces rather than an undifferentiated final string.
Verification Bandwidth and Proof-Carrying Claims consume exact candidates and obligations and return bounded verdict, dependence, disagreement, cost, ignorance, and residual records.
Labor OS, Talos, and Artifact Graphs turn accepted output into typed delivered artifacts with job, effect, provenance, replay, and terminal receipt custody.
Benchmaxxing consumes complete attempt universes and produces matched quality, usefulness, latency, resource, tail, fairness, and uncertainty comparisons with hidden holdouts and failure retention.
Claim Ledgers and Readiness Gates own evidence transition, qualification scope, expiry, downgrade, quarantine, and no-change decisions; a benchmark or controller cannot self-promote.
Operators, incident response, and downstream consumers receive monitoring, rollback, compensation, appeal, residual, and requalification interfaces and may force a slower route or stop.
invariants: Every speed claim is scoped to an exact consumer, permitted use, workload distribution, model/tokenizer, hardware/runtime, serving policy, load regime, cache state, metric definition, and time window.
The workload, baselines, quality and safety gates, resource accounting, statistical plan, stopping rule, and promotion ceiling are frozen before candidate outcomes are inspected.
Every route and result names immutable model, draft, verifier, checkpoint, code, dependency, compiler, hardware, configuration, seed, clock, and artifact identities sufficient for replay.
A candidate is compared with strong quality-equivalent baselines under matched inputs, resources, evaluators, warmup, cache, batching, concurrency, retries, and measurement horizon.
Attempted, proposed, accepted, verified, delivered, and useful output remain distinct; none may be substituted for another in a headline metric.
End-to-end latency includes queueing, prefill, decode, verification, repair, retry, fallback, artifactization, and delivery; decoder-only time cannot support an end-to-end claim.
Verification, repair, fallback, human review, and evaluator disagreement are charged to the candidate route and preserved even when they erase the apparent speed gain.
Warm caches, compiled graphs, prefix reuse, batching, and startup amortization are declared and matched; cold-start and steady-state claims cannot be conflated.
Aggregate throughput, per-request latency, time to first token, inter-token latency, completion latency, and deadline success remain separate metrics with median and tail behavior.
Compute, memory, KV-cache, bandwidth, energy, money, human effort, governance work, displaced capacity, and opportunity cost remain visible beside latency and quality.
All failures, timeouts, cancellations, OOMs, verifier overloads, rejected drafts, retries, abstentions, fallbacks, and censored attempts remain in the denominator and artifact graph.
Fallback is executable, quality-checked, capacity-reserved, and included in latency and cost; a named but untested fallback does not close the route.
High-risk, authority-changing, rights-sensitive, or irreversible work may require a slower independent verifier or prohibit accelerated generation regardless of average speed.
A verifier sharing a model, training data, cache, prompt, or failure mode with the generator is not described as independent without tested dependency evidence.
Batching, tenant mix, cache eviction, load shedding, starvation, and fairness effects are reported so aggregate gains cannot be purchased by hidden individual harm.
No result transfers across workloads, models, tokenizers, hardware, runtimes, quantization, context lengths, organizations, rights regimes, or time without an explicit transfer test.
Qualification expires after material model, draft, verifier, workload, policy, hardware, runtime, serving, cache, or threat change and can be downgraded immediately by contrary evidence.

No raw token rate, FLOP estimate, theoretical parallelism, source-reported speedup, synthetic-template cost, local static import, finite Lean theorem, validator pass, or aggregate throughput alone promotes the chapter core.
failure_modes: Raw tokens per second or proposed spans replace accepted, verified, delivered, or useful work in the numerator.
The accelerated route is compared with an unoptimized, smaller-context, lower-quality, differently quantized, differently batched, or otherwise weaker baseline.
The task cohort is cherry-picked for high draft acceptance, short outputs, repeated prefixes, easy verification, or favorable hardware occupancy.
Average quality hides rare catastrophic, citation, code, policy, rights, multilingual, long-context, or high-risk regressions.
The verifier is slower, less capable, correlated, overloaded, or evaluated on a proxy, so acceptance does not preserve the claimed quality boundary.
Repair, retry, rejection, fallback, or human-review cost is omitted until the apparent latency advantage disappears in production-like accounting.
Warm caches, prefix reuse, compiled kernels, startup amortization, or precomputed candidates benefit only the accelerated route.
Aggregate batch throughput is reported as single-request speed, or time to first token is reported while completion latency and deadline misses regress.
Large batches, tenant mix, scheduler priority, or speculative branches improve averages by starving low-volume, long-context, accessibility, or high-priority requests.
KV-cache pressure, memory bandwidth, network transfer, energy, thermal throttling, or parallel-sampling cost erases gains outside the headline device or load point.
Draft heads, latent predictors, diffusion sketches, retrieval tries, recurrent states, or cached continuations drift out of distribution after model, data, prompt, or context change.
Multi-seed or hybrid generation multiplies candidate count without improving verified useful work per time and total resource burden.
Risk classification, authority, or rights constraints are weakened because the latency objective is treated as a release mandate.
Fallback is missing, capacity-starved, slower than the deadline, semantically inconsistent, unable to recover partial state, or never exercised under failure.
Clock choice, asynchronous synchronization, instrumentation overhead, excluded queue time, early stopping, or censored failures biases the latency comparison.
Failed attempts, cancellations, OOMs, rejected drafts, verifier disagreements, and null seeds disappear from the denominator or publication surface.
A speed result is overgeneralized across models, workloads, context lengths, hardware, runtimes, serving engines, load regimes, organizations, or time without transfer evidence.
Qualification remains routable after checkpoint, verifier, tokenizer, quantization, compiler, hardware, cache policy, workload, threat, or rights changes invalidate the evidence.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum contains three retained finite countermodels plus a seventeen-declaration, eight-stage, sixty-route request-to-closure lifecycle; an independent consumer rejecting 51/51 non-accepting mutations and digest-binding the two-valid/four-invalid baseline suite, three-route/four-task accounting bundle, and one-valid/six-invalid Theseus import; one reachable modeled fallback; and no support or external effect. Thirty-five assumed projections, copied fixture facts, and summary bridges are retired. This is bounded local policy/conformance evidence, not model speed, natural-workload usefulness, verifier independence, evaluator adequacy, deployed fallback, serving performance, transfer, or SOTA evidence.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature Fast Generation campaign preregisters natural chat, code, retrieval, structured extraction, long-context, multilingual, accessibility, agentic, artifact-producing, and high-risk workloads plus adversarial quality, cache, load, verifier, drift, rights, and failure cases. It compares optimized target-model autoregression, ordinary serving, speculative decoding, multi-token and multi-head drafting, feature-level drafting, lookahead, early exit, recurrent or state-space backbones, KV-cache and batching systems, diffusion, hybrid and conservative-slow routes, and the full governed controller under matched models, inputs, hardware, runtime, load, cache, evaluators, resources, retries, and time. Joint gates cover useful accepted success, exactness or quality, unsafe acceptance, false rejection, coverage and abstention, time to first token, inter-token and completion latency, deadline success, throughput, median and tail behavior, compute, memory, KV-cache, bandwidth, energy, money, human and governance work, fairness, rights, fallback and recovery, residuals, and total lifecycle burden. Prospectively fixed interventions isolate mode selection, accepted-output accounting, verifier independence, cache matching, full latency attribution, repair and fallback, resource accounting, high-risk override, load isolation, and expiry. Independent generator, verifier, evaluator, serving, and measurement implementations must reproduce conclusions and transfer them across models, workloads, languages, context lengths, hardware, runtimes, organizations, rights regimes, attacks, load states, failures, updates, and time. The current static import, deterministic template bundle, finite proofs, and source-reported systems define a useful negative boundary; they are not model-speed, serving, useful-solution-per-second, generality, or state-of-the-art evidence.

codex_tests.result_status: passes eight stages, all sixty routes, 51/51 non-accepting mutations, three digest-bound bounded suites, one reachable modeled fallback, and support/effect none; no model-speed, useful-throughput, deployed-fallback, serving, transfer, or support claim

proof_targets.target: Reachable admission binds task, context, consumer, mode, risk, verifier, baseline, quality, latency, resource, fallback, and rights records before fast selection.
Reachable accounting and decision routes block raw-speed promotion without accepted output, task success, matched baseline, complete costs, and an evidence transition.
Eight stages and sixty independently consumed routes govern context binding, selection, drafting, verification or fallback, useful-outcome accounting, decision, and closure.
A digest-bound external validator owns Theseus report counts while Lean owns general reachable raw-speed, fallback, promotion, and closure policy.
A digest-bound external validator owns the three-route task result while Lean owns general task-success, cost-separation, speed-proxy, support-transition, and closure policy.

open_evidence_gaps: The exact current evidence is one not-run schema fixture; one 18-mode/13-comparison Theseus import with five speed-lift warnings, zero task-passing or promotable comparisons, and six invalid controls; one two-command/ten-digest replay; one four-task deterministic cost-accounting bundle selecting a 264-unit verified template over a 632-unit baseline and rejecting a 176-unit latency proxy; one four-command/sixteen-digest/68-task aggregate with fourteen rejected controls and two no-change decisions; and thirty-eight finite Lean declarations. It establishes no model-speed, serving, route-selection, fallback-runtime, natural-workload, independent-verifier, or chapter-core support result.

A full attempt still requires actual autoregressive and accelerated model implementations on natural held-out workloads, matched strong serving and decoding baselines, immutable model/runtime/hardware identities, complete queue-to-delivery clocks, accepted and useful output measurement, full verifier/repair/fallback/resource attribution, failure retention, high-risk and rights gates, and executable recovery.

The causal campaign must compare speculative, multi-token, multi-head, feature, lookahead, early-exit, recurrent/state-space, KV-cache/serving, diffusion, hybrid, conservative-slow, and full governed routes under matched quality, load, cache, hardware, resources, evaluators, retries, and time, with preregistered ablations and joint quality/usefulness/safety/latency/tail/resource/fairness/rights/recovery gates.

Useful, general, verifier-preserving, resource-efficient, serving, or state-of-the-art speed claims remain blocked until independent generator, verifier, evaluator, serving, and measurement implementations reproduce the result and transfer spans models, workloads, languages, contexts, hardware, runtimes, organizations, rights regimes, attacks, load states, failures, updates, and time; all external reports remain comparators only.

claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Primary-source passages were reviewed for the stated comparator contribution and limits; no local implementation, reproduction, safety result, or support-state promotion is inferred.

chapters.problem: Additional inference can change a system's answer through search, revision, candidate aggregation, or verifier calls without a prospective rule for when extra compute is worthwhile, what evidence the verifier contributes, how initially correct answers are protected, when to stop or abstain, and what the resulting candidate may influence.
chapters.insufficient: Fast Generation owns low-latency route admission and Planning owns executable decomposition, while ordinary chain-of-thought, self-consistency, search, process reward, early-exit, and test-time-scaling methods do not by themselves provide consumer-specific risk and authority boundaries, complete candidate and cost denominators, independent outcome evaluation, initially-correct corruption accounting, executable stopping, or causal evidence that extra compute improved useful work.
chapters.core_claim: Governed deliberation is a consumer-, task-, risk-, model-, evaluator-, resource-, and time-specific inference lease: before outcomes, it chooses among direct generation, bounded revision, candidate search, or abstention; binds exact budgets, candidate/history custody, verifier scope and dependence, stop and escalation rules, initially-correct corruption and initially-incorrect repair metrics, downstream consumer, expiry, and residual owner; and admits only a bounded candidate to planning when matched natural and adversarial evidence shows useful gain after all branches, failures, verification, latency, compute, human, and governance costs. A trace, self-score, process reward, benchmark gain, or extra compute never establishes correctness, safety, capability, execution authority, or support movement by itself.
mechanism: Freeze the consumer, permitted use, natural and adversarial workload, task families, risk and authority classes, model and prompt versions, evaluator identities, horizon, metrics, and promotion ceiling before candidate generation.

Register direct, bounded-revision, fixed-search, adaptive-search, verifier-disabled, overcompute, abstention, and human-review modes with immutable policies, budgets, stopping rules, and dependencies.
Bind every request to exact time, token, branch, candidate, verifier, tool, compute, memory, money, human, and governance budgets plus protected review capacity.
Separate generation, public or private trace, candidate state, selection, verification, planning, and execution authority so no representation or score silently crosses owners.

Generate stable candidate and branch identities while retaining order, seeds, parentage, tool calls, context, prompt, model state, and all rejected, malformed, timed-out, cancelled, or abstaining attempts.
Record the first candidate before revision and score initially-correct corruption, initially-incorrect repair, unchanged correct and incorrect cases, and non-estimable cells separately.
Declare verifier obligations, evidence view, competence scope, model and data dependence, calibration, abstention, disagreement, and false-acceptance or false-rejection boundaries.
Measure causal trace faithfulness separately through sufficiency, completeness, necessity, perturbation, counterfactual prompt/tool changes, and action/final-answer deltas.
Estimate expected verification value and select a mode prospectively without hidden test labels, post-outcome prompt search, threshold tuning, or oracle routing.
Execute stopping on verified success, diminishing expected value, budget exhaustion, unresolved high-risk constraints, verifier incapacity, disagreement, or explicit abstention.
Route high-risk work without independent-enough verification to review and route exhausted or disputed work to residual escrow, never automatic planning or execution.

Measure final useful correctness, unsafe handoff, selective risk, calibration, abstention, latency, branch count, verifier cost, operator burden, and residual custody over the complete attempt universe.
Charge all extra candidates, revisions, verification, tools, retries, escalation, human review, displaced work, and opportunity cost to the deliberation mode.
Compare matched direct, bounded-revision, fixed, adaptive, verifier-disabled, overcompute, abstention, human, and full governed arms under identical information, resources, evaluators, and time.
Preserve the fifteen known extra-compute harms as regression cases while adding ambiguous cases where routing, fallback, abstention, and stopping must differ.
Run causal interventions on mode selection, budget, candidate history, verifier independence, stopping, faithfulness checks, residual ownership, and authority separation.
Issue only consumer-scoped no-change, narrow, refute, or support recommendations through Claim Ledgers and Readiness Gates; deliberation cannot self-promote.
Monitor calibration, corruption, drift, evaluator capture, cost, incidents, descendant use, expiry, rollback, and residual discharge or reopening after any admitted handoff.
interfaces: Intent and Planning supply the exact objective, consumer, permitted downstream use, risk, unresolved constraints, and alternatives without granting deliberation or execution authority.
Fast Generation supplies direct or low-latency candidate routes; Deliberation owns whether and how additional inference is leased.
Virtual Context supplies exact visible packets, omissions, source lineage, trace privacy class, expiry, and invalidation triggers.
Routing and Readiness expose qualified models, verifiers, tools, human lanes, capacity, and abstention paths without selecting outcomes.
Verification Bandwidth supplies obligation-to-capacity feasibility and receives exact candidate/evidence views and unresolved constraints.
Proof-Carrying Claims and evaluators return bounded verdict, dependence, calibration, disagreement, ignorance, faithfulness, and cost records without action authority.
Resource Economics reserves and bills time, tokens, branches, candidates, tools, compute, memory, money, human review, governance, displaced work, and opportunity cost.
Runtime Adapters enforce the leased mode, model/tool permissions, budgets, stopping, trace boundaries, and effect non-authority.
Artifact Graphs retain candidate parentage, traces, rejected and abstained attempts, evaluator receipts, costs, stop reasons, handoffs, and residual lineage.
Planning consumes only a bounded candidate or abstention receipt and independently decides whether further decomposition is warranted.
Claim Ledgers, Evidence States, Benchmaxxing, and Readiness Gates own comparison, transition, qualification, expiry, downgrade, and no-change decisions.
Humans, affected parties, incident response, and downstream consumers receive escalation, appeal, trace-disclosure, stop, rollback, residual, and requalification controls.
invariants: Every claim binds an exact consumer, use, workload, task family, risk class, model/prompt, mode policy, evaluator, resource envelope, metric, and time window.
Modes, budgets, verifier rules, stopping, workload, metrics, statistical plan, and support ceiling are frozen before outcome inspection.
Direct, first, intermediate, rejected, selected, final, abstained, malformed, timed-out, and cancelled candidates retain stable identities and complete denominators.
Initially-correct corruption and initially-incorrect repair remain separate; a workload with no initially correct cases cannot estimate corruption reduction.
Extra tokens, branches, revisions, traces, self-scores, process rewards, and benchmark scores are neither correctness evidence nor action authority by themselves.
Generator, selector, verifier, evaluator, planner, and executor roles remain distinct; shared dependencies are declared and tested before independence language.
Verifier obligations, evidence view, capacity, scope, calibration, disagreement, abstention, and false-decision risks remain visible beside final quality.
Trace plausibility, candidate quality, causal faithfulness, monitorability, and authority remain separate axes.
All candidate, verifier, tool, retry, escalation, human, latency, compute, memory, money, governance, displaced-work, and opportunity costs are charged to the arm.
High-risk work without independent-enough verification routes to review or abstention, never direct planning or execution.
Budget exhaustion, diminishing expected value, verifier incapacity, unresolved constraints, or disagreement triggers stop and residual custody without silent widening.
Matched arms receive identical information, resources, evaluators, tool access, retries, and time except for the preregistered intervention.
All fifteen known extra-compute harms remain regression cases; a higher average cannot erase any reintroduced harm.
Unsafe handoff, false acceptance, false refusal, missed help, calibration, coverage, latency, cost, human burden, and residuals are joint gates.
A planning handoff carries candidate and evidence, never hidden trace content, evaluator confidence, or an implicit permission to act.
No result transfers across models, prompts, tasks, domains, languages, tools, verifiers, evaluators, organizations, risks, or time without a test.
Qualification expires after material model, prompt, policy, evaluator, tool, workload, authority, rights, threat, or environment change and downgrades on contrary evidence.
No fixture, route theorem, synthetic task, oracle comparator, deterministic verifier, real-model trace, or validator pass alone promotes the core.
failure_modes: Self-score or correlated-verifier laundering turns shared failure modes into purported independent evidence.
Overthinking optimizes token, branch, candidate, or elapsed-time volume while useful verification and final correctness stall or regress.
Initially correct answers are corrupted by revision, search, majority pressure, or verifier error and disappear inside final accuracy.
Early exit removes necessary checking, while late exit consumes budget after expected verification value has collapsed.
Budget laundering silently widens time, tokens, branches, tools, retries, or human review after outcomes are visible.
Winner-only reporting drops rejected, malformed, timed-out, cancelled, abstained, disputed, or expensive candidates from denominators.
A persuasive or detailed trace becomes implied truth, planning evidence, monitorability, or execution authority.
Trace faithfulness is inferred from plausibility even when prompt information bypasses the trace or interventions show it is unnecessary.
A weak, miscalibrated, overloaded, gameable, or domain-mismatched verifier selects polished errors or rejects useful answers.
Adaptive selection peeks at hidden labels, tunes thresholds post hoc, or uses an oracle unavailable at deployment.
Parallel deliberation outruns verifier, reviewer, memory, tool, or downstream capacity and shifts cost or tail latency elsewhere.
Abstention is scored as failure or omitted, pressuring the controller to emit a polished but unsafe candidate.
The workload contains no ambiguous or initially-correct cases, making routing, stopping, fallback, corruption, or abstention effects unidentifiable.
Process reward or benchmark improvement does not transfer to useful natural tasks, rights-sensitive cases, or adversarial conditions.
High-risk, authority-changing, or irreversible work bypasses independent review because extra compute is mistaken for safety.
Unresolved constraints, evaluator disagreement, and residuals are erased when the best available candidate reaches planning.
A causal benefit is actually explained by extra information, tools, retries, evaluator access, compute, human work, or a weaker direct baseline.
Stale deliberation qualifications survive model, prompt, evaluator, policy, workload, threat, authority, rights, or environment change.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum contains two retained general countermodels plus an eleven-declaration, eight-stage, 59-route request-to-closure lifecycle; an independent consumer rejecting 51/51 non-accepting mutations and digest-binding the ten-case admission, three-seed post-v2 routing/deliberation result, and later actual-model 0/60 no-change result; one reachable residual-escrow route and one bounded planning handoff; fifteen preserved fixed-step harms, zero observed synthetic fallback activations, and no support or external effect. Eight flat route consequences are retired. This is bounded policy/conformance evidence plus a failed actual-model attempt, not useful language-model reasoning, natural-workload usefulness, verifier adequacy, trace faithfulness, calibrated routing or abstention, planning execution, transfer, or SOTA evidence.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: A mature Governed Deliberation campaign preregisters deliberately ambiguous natural and adversarial tasks with meaningful direct-answer, revision, search, fallback, abstention, and human-review opportunities; actual current model candidates; initially correct and incorrect strata; the fifteen known extra-compute harms; delayed and high-risk outcomes; and independent outcome and trace-faithfulness evaluators. It compares direct, bounded revision, fixed search, adaptive search, verifier-disabled, overcompute, self-consistency, process-reward, early-exit, human-review, abstention, and full governed policies under matched information, models, tools, resources, evaluators, retries, and time. Joint gates cover useful final correctness, initially-correct corruption, initially-incorrect repair, unsafe handoff, false acceptance and refusal, missed help, coverage, calibration, abstention, faithfulness sufficiency/completeness/necessity, latency, branches, tokens, tools, compute, memory, money, human and governance work, displaced work, recovery, and residual custody. Prospectively fixed ablations isolate mode selection, budgets, candidate-history retention, verifier independence, stopping, faithfulness checks, authority separation, abstention, and residual ownership. Independent model-runner, verifier, evaluator, faithfulness, cost, and handoff implementations must reproduce conclusions and transfer across models, prompts, tasks, domains, languages, tools, organizations, authority and rights regimes, attacks, updates, and time. Current fixtures, deterministic synthetic gains, and the 0/60 real-model null define valuable boundaries; they are not useful test-time scaling, faithful reasoning, safety, generality, or state-of-the-art evidence.

proof_targets.target: High-risk evaluation without an independent-enough review record is blocked before selection or planning handoff.
Budget or dispute exhaustion requires an owned residual before bounded planning handoff.
A complete seven-receipt lifecycle reaches a bounded planning handoff and closure without execution, support, or external-effect authority.
Candidate generation requires a prospectively bound budget and budget identity.
Candidate generation requires a registered deliberation mode policy.
Evaluation requires explicit obligations, verifier identity, evidence view, dependence, calibration, abstention, and false-decision boundaries.
Candidate generation requires first-candidate capture, complete history, trace privacy, and the complete attempt denominator.
Candidate generation and handoff require prospectively bound stop rules and a stop receipt.
No verified candidate, exhausted budget, or unresolved dispute routes through residual escrow and closure.
Raw scores, traces, and a planning handoff cannot grant support or execution authority.

open_evidence_gaps: The exact current evidence is ten synthetic admission routes with eleven mutations and fifteen preserved harms; one 300-example frozen setup; one deterministic three-seed result where adaptive stopping reached 179/180 in 236 operations, fixed search 154/180 in 540 and corrupted fifteen initially correct answers, direct generation 130/180, and fallback never activated; one later real-model five-arm result with 0/60 correct in every arm; two no-change decisions; and ten finite route theorems.

A full attempt still requires a deliberately ambiguous natural held-out workload with meaningful direct, revision, search, fallback, abstention, and human-review opportunities; actual current-model candidates; initially correct and incorrect strata; independent outcome and trace-faithfulness evaluators; delayed high-risk outcomes; and complete resource, human, governance, and residual accounting.

The causal campaign must compare direct, bounded revision, fixed, adaptive, verifier-disabled, overcompute, self-consistency, process-reward, early-exit, human, abstention, and governed arms under matched information, models, tools, resources, evaluators, retries, and time while preserving all fifteen harms.

Useful test-time scaling, faithful reasoning, verifier adequacy, safety, efficiency, generality, or state-of-the-art claims remain blocked until independent model-runner, verifier, evaluator, faithfulness, cost, and handoff implementations reproduce results and transfer spans heterogeneous models, prompts, tasks, domains, languages, tools, organizations, rights regimes, attacks, updates, and time.

chapters.title: RankFold, NeuralFold, and Artifact Compression
chapters.problem: The stack needs artifact-level and tensor-level compression strategies that remain honest about residuals and utility.
chapters.insufficient: Storage savings are not enough if compressed artifacts lose semantics, break downstream use, or require expensive fallback too often.
chapters.core_claim: A compressed artifact may enter a downstream route only through an artifact-, consumer-, use-, access-pattern-, decoder-, platform-, and time-specific admission lease that preserves the full source, separates representation, reconstruction, ratio, utility, latency, and evidentiary-authority claims, counts every byte and operation, exercises probes and fallback, and expires or quarantines on drift; RankFold/NeuralFold remains a bounded candidate implementation, and no compact form inherits the source artifact's authority.
mechanism: Freeze the source artifact digest, custody, rights, retention class, and preserved full-artifact fallback before encoding.
Declare the consumer, task family, access pattern, exactness class, permitted loss, risk class, and use horizon before choosing a compressed route.
Version encoder, decoder, arithmetic, serialization, platform, dependencies, parameters, seeds, manifests, and hardware assumptions.
Separate representation, reconstruction, serialized-ratio, downstream-utility, latency, evidentiary-authority, and support-state claims.
Account for payload, headers, indexes, manifests, parameters, code, environments, dictionaries, seeds, residuals, repairs, replicas, and fallback storage.
Use low-rank, quantized, residual, generative, semantic, hierarchical, or literal transforms only inside their declared reconstruction contract.
Retain residuals and omitted features as typed, queryable records rather than hiding them in an aggregate quality score.
Run deterministic decode, exact-replay, corruption, truncation, dependency-drift, and cross-platform checks where the use requires exactness.
Run task probes over common, rare, adversarial, provenance-sensitive, citation-sensitive, and rights-sensitive cases before admission.
Estimate probe coverage and preserve out-of-envelope detection instead of treating one passing probe as universal utility.
Route failed, uncertain, expired, or out-of-envelope uses to the preserved full artifact or to explicit refusal.
Measure encode, storage, transfer, decode, verification, probe, fallback, recovery, human, governance, and displaced-work burden together.
Compare against strong literal, standard-codec, quantized, sparse, low-rank, retrieval, summary, cache, and no-compression baselines under matched conditions.
Bind every admitted compact form to an artifact graph containing source, job, code, environment, receipts, probes, residuals, fallbacks, and descendants.
Keep previews and hierarchical summaries from serving as citations or exact evidence unless the declared evidence-use gate passed.
Expire admission when source, decoder, dependency, platform, consumer, access pattern, workload, rights, threat, or policy changes.
Quarantine decoder drift, reconstruction disagreement, residual loss, rare-case regression, provenance loss, and fallback unavailability.
Promote, narrow, refute, or deprecate each claim separately through explicit evidence transitions with residual ownership.
interfaces: Artifact Graph owns source and compact digests, lineage, descendants, retention, and replay references.
Compressed Artifact Record owns use envelope, codec identity, reconstruction contract, residuals, admission state, and non-claims.
Context owns whether a compact representation is adequate for the current transaction without replacing source custody.
Routing selects compact, full, or refusal paths by declared consumer, task, access pattern, risk, and current admission state.
Verification owns decode, reconstruction, corruption, exact-replay, probe, and evaluator receipts within declared scope.
Resource Economics owns complete byte, operation, latency, energy, human, governance, and displaced-work accounting.
Claim Ledgers keep representation, reconstruction, ratio, utility, latency, authority, deployment, and SOTA states separate.
Security owns decoder supply chain, parser isolation, malicious archives, dependency drift, and quarantine.
Rights and Provenance own lawful source retention, transformation permissions, deletion propagation, attribution, and audit access.
Readiness owns consumer-specific qualification, expiry, rollback, and residual escrow before use.
Runtime executes decoder, probe, fallback, refusal, recovery, and telemetry paths under bounded permissions.
Human review receives the preserved source, compact form, differences, residuals, probes, and authority boundary for consequential decisions.
invariants: The preserved full artifact remains retrievable and digest-bound for every admitted compact route unless an explicit lawful deletion policy says otherwise.
Admission is specific to artifact, consumer, task, access pattern, risk, decoder, platform, and time.
Representation success never implies reconstruction, compression advantage, utility, latency, authority, deployment, or SOTA.
Exactness names the target bytes or semantics, arithmetic, serialization, tolerances, platform envelope, and verifier.
Ratios include all metadata, code, parameters, environments, dictionaries, seeds, residuals, repairs, indexes, replicas, and fallback storage in scope.
Every omitted or lossy feature is inside the declared loss envelope or preserved as an owned residual.
Task probes include rare and adversarial cases and report their sampled coverage and blind spots.
A failed, uncertain, expired, or out-of-envelope probe cannot route the compact artifact to that consumer.
Fallback is executable, permissioned, observable, timed, and included in denominators rather than merely named.
Decoder and dependency drift invalidate affected exact-replay qualifications until reproduction succeeds again.
Compressed previews, summaries, and embeddings cannot substitute for source citations or legal evidence without a specific admitted use.
Every consumer can recover the source reference, codec manifest, evaluation receipts, residuals, and non-claims.
Failed, corrupt, cancelled, refused, quarantined, fallback, and human-reviewed attempts remain in accounting denominators.
Utility is evaluated on downstream outcomes, not inferred from reconstruction metrics or size alone.
Cost advantage is evaluated at the declared access frequency and horizon after encode, decode, verification, fallback, recovery, and governance burden.
Rights, deletion, retention, and provenance obligations propagate to compact forms, residuals, caches, backups, and descendants.
Support-state changes require an accepted evidence transition for the exact claim and scope.
Every expiry, quarantine, fallback failure, or unresolved residual has an owner and reopening condition.
failure_modes: Rare critical clauses, code paths, diagrams, formatting, or provenance are damaged while common probes pass.
The probe distribution is unrepresentative, leaked, too small, or optimized by the encoder.
Metadata, residuals, code, environments, indexes, replicas, and preserved fallbacks erase the reported ratio.
Encode, decode, verification, or fallback latency erases savings at the declared access pattern.
A WORM cold-archive assumption is reused for hot, mutable, or repeatedly queried artifacts.
Floating-point, hardware, dependency, compiler, or serialization drift breaks deterministic decode.
A compact preview or summary is cited as though it were the preserved source.
Lossy semantic utility is laundered into a lossless reconstruction claim.
A successful reconstruction metric is laundered into downstream task utility or safety.
A local ratio is laundered into system cost advantage, deployment readiness, generality, or SOTA.
Fallback is missing, unauthorized, corrupt, slower than declared, or recursively compressed under the same failure.
Residuals are omitted, unqueryable, detached from the source, or costed outside the denominator.
Archive parsing or decoder dependencies introduce malicious-code, supply-chain, or denial-of-service risk.
Rights, deletion, retention, attribution, or export obligations fail to propagate to compact copies and descendants.
Selective reporting removes corrupt, refused, quarantined, slow, fallback, or human-reviewed cases.
Shared encoder, decoder, probe, evaluator, and implementation bugs create false independent agreement.
Admission remains live after source, decoder, platform, workload, consumer, policy, rights, or threat drift.
The governance and verification tax exceeds any storage or transfer benefit while the compact route remains institutionally sticky.
chapters.minimal_implementation: Two retained finite probe/fallback and metadata countermodels plus an eight-declaration, eight-stage, 53-route artifact-to-consumption lifecycle; an independent consumer rejects 44/44 non-accepting mutations and digest-binds the exact compressed-artifact fixture, RAW0 replay, NEURAL0 metadata import, and two no-change decisions. One failed-probe route reaches fallback preparation, one qualified-use witness reaches closure, and support/effect authority remains none. Seventeen projections and theorem-per-record consequences are retired. This is bounded policy/conformance and artifact-identity evidence, not codec correctness, NeuralFold reproduction, useful compression, semantic preservation, downstream utility, deployed fallback, transfer, or SOTA evidence.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: Artifact compression needs an admission system before compressed forms can enter the stack. It treats compressed forms as routed candidates that must earn each use case instead of inheriting the authority of the full artifact. In the mature artifact-admission lane, compressed artifacts are candidates for specific uses, not replacements for the original, until probes, decode behavior, residual coding, fallback, and consumer policy accept them.

proof_targets.target: A reachable artifact-to-consumption lifecycle requires full-source custody, exact identities, reconstruction checks, consumer probes, executable fallback, observed outcomes, and closure before a qualified use can complete.
Failed probes route to fallback, exact-replay gaps block use, raw ratios cannot promote support, and missing evidence transitions block consumption.

Eight stages and 53 independently consumed routes govern registration, encoding, verification, probing, fallback, admission, observed consumption, and closure without support or external-effect authority.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports the artifact-compression claim through tensor-centric archival structure, per-artifact optimization, low-rank residual transforms, residual-coder cost alignment, WORM assumptions, manifests, codec parameters, deterministic decode, fuzz/integration tests, and reconstruction checks.
claim_source_mappings.limits: No local compression implementation, corpus benchmark, deterministic decoder, reproduced ratio, or utility probe exists here.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for WORM archival scope, heterogeneous artifact classes, per-artifact low-rank residual coding, NeuralFold inspection/probe/route/fallback, stream/container design, exact-reconstruction and residual conditions, full byte accounting, deterministic decode/evaluation requirements, decode-cost limits, and structure-dependent gains; no compressor implementation, deterministic decoder, ratio, corpus benchmark, or utility probe is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports low-rank/tensor compression lineage through vector-pair and factorized representations, adaptive rank, normalization, entropy-aware coding, shape optimization, recursive cascades, correctness, determinism, metrics, and CLI plans.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Lineage source with broad claims; no compressor, ratio reproduction, lossless reconstruction validation, or public matrix corpus test has been run.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache as lineage/source-intent context for vector-pair/factorized representation, adaptive rank, normalization, entropy-aware coding, shape optimization, recursive cascades, meta-selection, correctness/determinism ambitions, metrics, and CLI/benchmark plans. Broad source-reported losslessness, benchmark, ratio, latency, code, and reproducibility statements are not independently validated or promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports reconstruction and rate-accounting discipline for artifact compression through explicit contracts, exact repairs, bounded verification, fallback, interface costs, and proxy/final-rate separation.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not provide local RankFold performance evidence, artifact utility evidence, or codec correctness in this repo.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for reconstruction contracts, generate-verify-repair doctrine, exact repair streams, fallback, interface and total-rate accounting, local verification, repair hierarchy, search-time proxy versus final serialization separation, and proxy calibration; no RankFold performance evidence, artifact utility result, codec correctness, or BBVCA implementation result is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports residual-honesty framing for compressed artifacts through compact seeds, generated structure, residual channels, verification contracts, governance interfaces, and hidden-complexity accounting.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not prove that any compressed artifact preserves downstream utility or cost advantage.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for compact seeds, rule expansion, memory/state, residual/error channels, verification, governance interfaces, full cost accounting, residual honesty, fallback, and hidden-complexity concerns; no downstream artifact-utility preservation, cost advantage, or compression benchmark is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports resource-constrained implementation pressure through tiny/local cognition, storage paging, embedded workflow, and edge-efficiency concerns that make artifact size and runtime burden visible.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Speculative prototype-lineage source; no embedded runtime, paging behavior, artifact compressor, or hardware benchmark has been validated here.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for tiny/local hardware constraints, paging, storage hierarchy, brain/blob framing, semantic graph linearization, cache locality, edge-list delta compression, decompression/journaling, and resource-constrained implementation pressure; no embedded runtime, paging behavior, artifact compressor, or hardware benchmark is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supplies a hierarchical-retrieval comparator in which recursive clustering and abstractive summaries provide multiple retrieval levels, sharpening the distinction between a useful compact view and source-preserving reconstruction.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Metadata-first source note only; no paper result, tree build, summary-fidelity audit, source reconstruction, provenance preservation, invalidation behavior, downstream utility, or local RankFold/NeuralFold comparison is established.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed the bounded public source note for recursive abstraction and hierarchical retrieval, including provenance-loss and fidelity risks. Source-reported performance remains unimported; this mapping adds no local compression, utility, reconstruction, or support evidence.

open_evidence_gaps: The exact current evidence is one valid schema fixture; one fresh 3,936-byte synthetic RAW0 pack/verify/list/unpack replay with exact digest preservation, no compression advantage, a license-disabled NeuralFold boundary, and one rejected byte mutation; three imported NEURAL0 archive metadata observations for one 100,000,000-byte decoded digest with verifier summaries but no fresh compression run; two no-change decisions; and nineteen finite Lean declarations.

A competent full attempt still requires an enabled, independently inspectable compressor and deterministic decoder; a public-safe heterogeneous corpus spanning prose, code, tables, diagrams, citations, binaries, tensors, rare cases, provenance, and rights metadata; strong matched codecs and representation baselines; exact and lossy reconstruction contracts; task probes; executable fallback and recovery; complete bytes, operations, latency, energy, human, governance, and residual accounting; and adversarial archive and drift tests.

The causal campaign must compare literal storage, standard general-purpose and domain codecs, quantization, sparsity, low-rank, retrieval, hierarchical summary, semantic preview, RankFold/NeuralFold candidate, and full-artifact fallback routes under matched sources, consumers, access frequencies, platforms, resources, rights, evaluators, and time; ablations must isolate residual coding, probes, exactness, fallback, metadata, admission, and expiry.

Codec correctness, useful compression advantage, downstream utility, safe evidence use, generality, deployment, transfer, or SOTA remain blocked until independent encoder, decoder, probe, evaluator, cost-meter, security, and fallback implementations reproduce the result and transfer across artifacts, models, modalities, languages, organizations, platforms, rights regimes, attacks, updates, and time.

chapters.title: Resource Economics and Token Budgets

handoff_or_recovery_notes: simulation-fidelity-and-physical-constraints folded into this chapter as Simulation Fidelity and Claim Transport on 2026-06-30; old public URL preserved through a static historical stub.

chapters.problem: Compute, context, verification, simulation fidelity, and human attention are scarce resources that the architecture must allocate explicitly.
chapters.insufficient: Ignoring resource economics or fidelity boundaries makes high-quality verification unaffordable, encourages synchronized overload or hidden cost shifts, and lets synthetic results travel farther than their contracts support.
chapters.core_claim: Resource Economics owns a consumer-, task-, risk-, workload-, organization-, resource-, and time-specific allocation lease that admits, prices, schedules, defers, shrinks, escalates, or rejects work only after protected safety and rights floors, complete direct and displaced costs, uncertainty, useful outcome value, verification capacity, load and tail stability, simulation-transfer limits, recovery, and residual ownership are explicit; throughput, low token count, synthetic success, or a cheap route alone confers no quality, safety, economic-optimality, support, or deployment authority.
mechanism: Freeze consumer, task, value hypothesis, risk, workload, organization, authority, rights, horizon, success, and non-claims before allocation.
Inventory compute, memory, storage, network, energy, tool, verifier, reviewer, approval, security, rollback, and recovery capacity with units and owners.
Define protected safety, verification, security, rights, approval, replay, rollback, audit, and human-review floors that price pressure cannot erase.
Estimate direct spend, uncertainty, cost of error, verification tax, fallback, repair, incident, opportunity, reviewer, governance, and displaced future costs.
Keep attempted, admitted, scheduled, executed, verified, accepted, useful, failed, deferred, refused, cancelled, and recovered work in separate states and denominators.
Choose dispatch, defer, scope reduction, escalation, fallback, refusal, or residual routes under declared capacity and policy.
Use regenerative or budgeted capacity mechanisms only with bounded issuance, conservation, debt, expiry, and anti-hoarding rules.
Reserve scarce verifier and human capacity by risk and expected value without allowing low-risk volume to starve protected work.
Measure queueing, batching, cache, warmup, compilation, preemption, retries, tail latency, OOM, saturation, and tenant interference.
Separate aggregate throughput and memory efficiency from single-request verified useful outcomes and downstream value.
Attach Simulation Contract Records to every synthetic, simulated, benchmark, scenario, or modeled economic result used as evidence.
Bind simulation scope, fidelity, temporal semantics, assumptions, demand, resources, bottlenecks, omissions, instrumentation, and transfer decision before claim transport.
Compare strong greedy, risk-blind, fixed-budget, value-of-computation, queueing, serving, human-first, conservative, and full-governed baselines under matched information and resources.
Measure useful throughput, unsafe release, false refusal, missed help, fairness, latency, tails, resources, human work, governance cost, displaced work, recovery, and residuals jointly.
Ablate protected floors, verification pricing, displaced-cost accounting, review reservation, load smoothing, fallback, simulation contracts, and expiry to identify causal contribution.
Expire allocation and transfer qualifications when workload, prices, hardware, models, policies, rights, threats, organizations, or time change.
Reconcile estimates to actual spend and outcomes; record variance, debt, incidents, recovery, and opportunity cost without censoring failures.
Promote, narrow, refute, or deprecate each resource or simulation claim through explicit evidence transitions and independent reproduction.
interfaces: Intent and Planning provide consumer value, task scope, deadlines, dependencies, and acceptable deferral or reduction.
Resource Budget Record owns the allocation lease, units, capacity, floors, estimates, decision, actuals, variance, and residuals.
Routing provides eligible costed routes and receives dispatch, defer, escalate, fallback, or refusal decisions.
Runtime and Serving report queue, cache, batching, compilation, execution, tail, failure, retry, saturation, and recovery receipts.
Verification owns required checks, verifier capacity, independence, confidence limits, and accepted-output receipts.
Security, Rights, and Authority own protected floors that resource pressure cannot waive.
Labor and Human review own reviewer capacity, work conditions, intervention, disagreement, fatigue, and displaced labor.
Simulation Contract Record owns synthetic scope, fidelity, assumptions, resources, omissions, instrumentation, and transfer ceiling.
Evidence and Claim Ledgers keep proxy cost, useful outcome, safety, fairness, economic, simulation, deployment, and SOTA states separate.
Artifact Graph binds budgets, schedules, traces, meters, evaluations, transitions, incidents, and descendants.
Readiness and Incident response own qualification, expiry, rollback, recovery, and residual reopening.
Release consumes only resource and simulation claims whose exact use, evidence, rights, and support ceilings remain valid.
invariants: Budgets never override protected safety, verification, security, rights, approval, replay, rollback, audit, or human-review floors.
High-risk work either receives the required verification and review capacity or routes to scope reduction, escalation, deferral, fallback, or refusal.
Every resource quantity has a unit, meter or estimation method, time window, owner, uncertainty, and reconciliation path.
Cost savings travel with useful outcome, safety, fairness, latency, tail, failure, fallback, recovery, and residual results.
Aggregate throughput, cache efficiency, or memory reduction never implies single-request quality, verified usefulness, safety, or economic value.
Protected overhead is explicit and cannot be deleted, relabeled, or shifted outside the denominator.
Displaced costs and opportunity costs remain residuals until measured or accepted by a scoped transition.
Failed, deferred, refused, cancelled, preempted, timed-out, OOM, fallback, repaired, and human-reviewed work remains in denominators.
Capacity issuance and regeneration are bounded; no token, credit, or budget mechanism creates infinite spend authority.
Low-risk volume cannot consume the protected verifier or reviewer capacity reserved for blocked high-risk work.
Allocation comparisons match information, workload, models, tools, hardware, cache, load, resources, evaluators, rights, and time.
Simulation scope, fidelity, temporal semantics, resource bill, bottlenecks, omissions, instrumentation, and transfer ceiling are declared prospectively.
Synthetic or simulated results do not transfer beyond their declared support boundary by default.
Approximation liberties, parent-model assumptions, omitted variables, and instrumentation effects survive every summary and handoff.
Economic optimality is not inferred from a finite selector, modeled cost table, local replay, or one workload.
Allocation and simulation qualifications expire on material workload, price, hardware, model, policy, rights, threat, organization, or time change.
Support-state changes require an accepted evidence transition for the exact resource or simulation claim and scope.
Every budget shortfall, variance, overload, protected-capacity conflict, failed transfer, and undisclosed cost has an owner and reopening condition.
failure_modes: Verification, security, rights, approval, replay, rollback, audit, or human review is cut to make a route appear cheaper.
Synchronized arrivals, shared retries, cache misses, or failures produce load collapse and correlated quality degradation.
Low-value or strategically inflated work hoards compute, verifier, reviewer, tool, or approval capacity.
Aggregate serving throughput or cache savings are mistaken for lower single-request risk, better quality, or economic value.
Protected overhead is deleted, relabeled, hidden in another budget, or excluded from comparison.
Cheaper inference displaces cost into debugging, human repair, incidents, privacy exposure, evidence loss, or rollback difficulty.
A proxy value model rewards visible throughput while missing downstream usefulness, safety, fairness, or opportunity cost.
Failed, refused, deferred, cancelled, timed-out, OOM, fallback, or human-reviewed work is removed from denominators.
Risk labels, value estimates, or deadlines are gamed to capture priority and scarce capacity.
Static budgets ignore queueing, tails, tenant interference, price change, hardware saturation, or recovery debt.
Regenerative credits create inflation, circular issuance, hidden debt, or unbounded spend authority.
Review reservation protects the wrong work, creates starvation, or ignores reviewer fatigue and disagreement.
Simulation laundering turns clean synthetic results into real-world claims while omitting fidelity, bottlenecks, or transfer limits.
Sandbox-to-world transfer occurs without a declared fidelity, resource, instrumentation, and rights contract.
Parent physics, workload, price, policy, or organizational assumptions drift without expiring the result.
Shared scheduler, meter, evaluator, simulator, and organization create false independent agreement.
A local finite selector or replay is described as economic optimality, welfare improvement, production stability, or SOTA.
Governance cost exceeds benefit, but institutional lock-in preserves the allocation policy and hides the refutation.
chapters.minimal_implementation: Twenty-three retained countermodels and bounded computations plus an eight-declaration, nine-stage, 66-route allocation, execution, simulation-transport, reconciliation, and closure lifecycle; an independently implemented consumer rejects 57/57 non-accepting mutations and digest-binds twelve bounded source families. Thirty-five assumption projections and copied fixture summaries are retired. The model separates useful outcomes from raw resource proxies, blocks fidelity overclaims, requires failure and unsafe-release accounting, and grants no support or external-effect authority. This is bounded policy/conformance evidence over synthetic fixtures, repository replays, local timing, historical CI, and sanitized imports—not economic optimality, deployed scheduling, useful-throughput improvement, simulation adequacy, physical feasibility, transfer, or SOTA evidence.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: Resource economics matures into a risk-aware budget operating system and claim-transport layer for cognition. It decides when the stack may spend, defer, shrink, escalate, reject, residualize work, or downgrade synthetic evidence without letting local cost pressure delete verification, security, approval, replay, rollback, human review, fidelity limits, or transfer boundaries. In the mature budget OS, cognition has a budget and simulations have contracts: budgets cannot delete verification tax, protected overhead, review capacity, rollback cost, security cost, residual burden, or the requirement that simulated results travel only as far as declared scope, fidelity, resources, omissions, and transfer decisions allow.

proof_targets.target: A reachable allocation lifecycle requires a scoped request, explicit resource inventory and units, direct, displaced, verification, and uncertainty costs, protected floors, bounded capacity, reviewer and verifier capacity, queue policy, observed spend, useful-outcome accounting, verification, reconciliation, and closure.
Missing protected floors, reviewer capacity, failure retention, or evidence-transition accounting blocks the corresponding allocation stage; raw resource proxies cannot promote support.

The stronger lifecycle digest-binds the bounded four-route cost fixture while preserving its two eligible and two rejected routes and its fixture-specific 66.98% arithmetic without inferring economic optimality.

The stronger lifecycle digest-binds the three-step workflow result and requires queue policy, high-risk priority, protected overhead, residual ownership, actual spend, verification outcome, variance, incidents, descendants, and closure.

Reservation and scheduling require capacity, reviewer and verifier capacity, protected overhead, debt expiry, an owner, high-risk priority, tenant isolation, tail policy, fallback, and later variance and opportunity-cost reconciliation.
Observed resource bills and useful outcomes remain separate, and raw throughput, memory, or cost proxies cannot promote support without verification and evidence-transition accounting.

The lifecycle digest-binds the three-scenario governance-tax fixture while keeping its two governed choices and one low-risk shortcut explicitly synthetic, cost-complete only within the fixture, and non-promotional.
Transported simulation claims require explicit scope, fidelity, temporal semantics, resource bills, omissions, and a transfer decision before reconciliation.

A simulated claim above its declared fidelity support is blocked before reconciliation, and missing simulation scope, fidelity, temporal semantics, resource bills, omissions, or transfer decisions cannot pass.

The lifecycle digest-binds the sanitized five-scenario, six-receipt Theseus simulation result while preserving its seven invalid controls and excluding physical-feasibility, benchmark-transfer, native-parity, deployment, and support claims.

The lifecycle digest-binds the sanitized one-ready-export, three-format, seven-field Theseus trace result while preserving its seven invalid controls and excluding dataset-quality, replay-success, deployment, and support claims.
