open_evidence_gaps: The exact current flagship replays ten local commands over twenty-six tracked artifacts, preserves three accepted narrow transitions and five no-promotion decisions with zero chapter-core effect, and covers bounded selector, workflow, budget, capacity, timing, synthetic-load, CI, governance-tax, simulation-transfer, and sanitized Theseus-import records; it is not a deployed scheduler, production queue, real economic experiment, welfare study, or open-world simulator.

The strongest numeric observations remain scope-bound: 66.98 percent synthetic modeled selector cost reduction against the chosen finite baseline; 84.421 percent five-sample local validator-timing reduction for a narrower task while rejecting a cheaper no-op; five-to-zero modeled overload reduction by residualizing seven deferred-task ticks; and eight CI runs with five successes, three classified service failures, and a 131-second recovery boundary. None establishes general cost advantage, production stability, human productivity, or economic optimality.

A competent full attempt still requires natural multi-tenant work with real models, tools, verifiers, reviewers, serving, prices, rights, delayed outcomes, strong matched allocation policies, complete direct/displaced/opportunity costs and denominators, causal ablations, independent schedulers/meters/evaluators, and heterogeneous transfer.

Useful, safe, fair, stable, efficient, economically optimal, deployable, transferable, or SOTA resource claims and physical-feasibility or simulator-adequacy claims remain blocked until the natural campaign, independent reproduction, and transfer pass; all source-reported systems remain comparators only.

chapters.title: Mathematical and Search Substrates
chapters.problem: The source corpus contains exploratory mathematical/search substrates that may matter but must not be overclaimed.
chapters.insufficient: Novel substrates can become authority theater if they are not tied to baselines, adoption gates, and falsification criteria.
chapters.core_claim: Mathematical and Search Substrates owns a consumer-, use-, workload-, claim-axis-, implementation-, baseline-, resource-, and time-specific Substrate Adoption Lease: an unusual calculus, representation, recurrence, search procedure, latent world model, or sequence backbone may affect only the axes and consumers that pass matched ordinary and current baselines, negative controls, complete cost and rights accounting, falsification, fallback, independent reproduction, and transfer; structural elegance, a theorem, a source-reported benchmark, synthetic fixture validity, or one favorable axis alone confers no general quality, efficiency, safety, support, deployment, or SOTA authority.
mechanism: Register the candidate substrate, version, implementation, provenance, rights, declared mathematical object, and executable surface without granting route authority.
Bind the candidate to named consumers, intended uses, workloads, environments, and separately typed claim axes before evaluation.
Freeze ordinary, strongest-current, simple-ablation, and embarrassing negative-control baselines under matched information, resources, hardware, data, and evaluator access.
Declare the expected advantage, predicted causal signature, falsification threshold, narrowing rule, retirement trigger, and evidence horizon prospectively.
Package every structural theorem with its exact statement, assumptions, model, proof receipt, implementation relation, consumer boundary, and non-claims.
Implement an inspectable candidate and ordinary fallback whose identities, parameters, kernels, caches, search budgets, and failure behavior are reproducible.
Exercise missing-baseline, theorem-spillover, unmeasured-axis, failed-control, confounded-budget, unavailable-fallback, rights, and support-overclaim mutations before positive interpretation.
Run matched natural workloads across seeds, difficulty, lengths, modalities, and adversarial cases while retaining failures, refusals, timeouts, OOMs, fallbacks, and repairs in denominators.

Record structural validity, representation fidelity and rate, search quality, routing quality, reasoning and task utility, latency, memory, energy, verifier burden, safety, and rights as separate axes.

Account for training, inference, search, compilation, verification, storage, transfer, operator, debugging, fallback, recovery, and opportunity costs rather than reporting one favorable resource metric.
Evaluate attacks, pathological structures, distribution shift, privacy and licensing constraints, inaccessible states, and evaluator dependence before consumer admission.
Issue a least-authority consumer gate whose permission names the exact substrate version, axis, workload, environment, budget, expiry, evidence references, and prohibited inferences.
Canary admitted substrates beside an executable ordinary route with bounded traffic, live comparators, regression alarms, and no irreversible dependency.
Monitor axis-specific regressions, drift, tail failures, verifier disagreement, resource displacement, and downstream residuals without averaging failures into a favorable aggregate.
Fallback, quarantine, rollback, narrow, supersede, refute, or retire the substrate when its gate, negative control, budget, rights, or recovery condition fails.
Require an independent implementation and independently implemented evaluator to reproduce the claimed axis without sharing the decisive defect.
Test heterogeneous transfer across consumers, workloads, models, scales, hardware, data regimes, organizations, rights regimes, attacks, and time before broader qualification.
Expire every lease on material substrate, implementation, theorem, workload, baseline, model, hardware, data, policy, rights, threat, consumer, or time change, and assign every unresolved residual.
interfaces: Intent names the consumer objective and prohibited substrate inferences; Substrate Adoption owns no objective expansion.
Planning and Cognitive Compilation may request a calculus, IR, graph, or search substrate but retain plan semantics and downstream adequacy.
Context and Representation own source fidelity, reconstruction, taint, and semantic-loss boundaries; Substrate Adoption records only scoped axis evidence.
Routing consumes a least-authority lease and preserves an ordinary fallback; it may not infer an unmeasured axis from route admission.
Fast Generation owns request latency and accelerated decoding; sequence-backbone or recurrent-substrate evidence does not automatically establish generation benefit.
Deliberation owns extra-inference leases and stopping; search-substrate admission does not authorize unbounded compute or reasoning claims.
Resource Economics owns complete cost, capacity, queue, and opportunity accounting; a favorable substrate metric cannot waive displaced cost.
Verification and Proof Envelopes own claim adequacy, theorem receipts, runtime refinement, and evaluator independence; a proof travels only to its statement.
Security, Privacy, and Rights own adversarial, data, license, provenance, and access decisions; mathematical novelty creates no exemption.
Runtime and Artifact Graphs own executable bindings, versions, hashes, replay, fallback artifacts, and rollback closure.
Readiness, Incident, and Release own qualification, quarantine, recovery, and public authority; a substrate lease cannot promote support or deployment by itself.
Claim Ledgers and Evidence own atom status, baselines, negative results, reproduction, transfer, expiry, and residual lineage.
invariants: Every substrate lease is specific to consumer, use, workload, claim axis, implementation, baseline, resources, environment, and time.
Exploratory, structural-only, empirical-canary, qualified-for-scope, blocked, retired, refuted, and superseded states remain distinct and operational.
A theorem authorizes only its exact statement under its assumptions and implementation relation; it never supplies an empirical axis implicitly.
Structural, representation, search, routing, reasoning, task, resource, verifier, safety, rights, deployment, and SOTA axes remain separate.
Ordinary, strongest-current, simple-ablation, and embarrassing negative-control baselines are frozen before outcome inspection.
Information, data, compute, search budget, hardware, tuning effort, verifier access, retries, and evaluator opportunity are matched or residualized.
Failed hypotheses, negative controls, nulls, regressions, refusals, timeouts, OOMs, fallbacks, repairs, and excluded cases remain visible in complete denominators.
A favorable mean cannot hide tails, subgroups, pathological structures, verifier disagreement, or downstream regressions.
Training, inference, compilation, search, memory, storage, verification, human, fallback, recovery, and opportunity burdens stay in the same comparison.
A consumer cannot rely on an unmeasured, blocked, expired, non-transferable, or rights-incompatible axis.
Paper-reported results, schema validity, deterministic fixtures, theorem counts, and green validators remain comparator or record evidence, not local substrate benefit.
Every canary retains an executable, tested, and sufficiently provisioned ordinary fallback with bounded reversal time.
Qualification narrows or expires on material substrate, implementation, theorem, workload, baseline, model, hardware, data, evaluator, policy, rights, threat, consumer, or time change.
Independent reproduction must not share the decisive implementation, evaluator, data leakage, or benchmark defect.
Transfer claims require heterogeneous settings and preserve failures rather than averaging incompatible regimes.
Support, deployment, safety, efficiency, generality, or SOTA movement requires an accepted transition for that exact claim and scope.
Optionality cannot become permanent unfalsifiability: every live candidate has a dated run, narrowing, retirement, or blocked-after-full-attempt condition.
Every failed gate, hidden cost, theorem-model gap, fallback defect, transfer failure, and unresolved residual has an owner and reopening condition.
failure_modes: A beautiful notation, geometric metaphor, recurrence, or latent space is treated as evidence of usefulness.
A correct structural theorem is laundered into speed, quality, safety, compression, search, reasoning, or deployment authority.
A weak, outdated, undertuned, or resource-starved baseline makes the candidate appear useful.
Outcome-driven search over substrates, seeds, tasks, metrics, budgets, or plots is reported as a prospective test.
One favorable axis or average hides worse fidelity, tails, subgroups, verifier burden, safety, rights, or downstream utility.
Backbone throughput, linear scaling, cache savings, or active-parameter counts are treated as single-request quality or end-to-end economic evidence.
Search breadth, extra inference, retrieval, or evaluator calls are omitted from the candidate's resource bill.
Failed, refused, timed-out, OOM, fallback, repaired, or unscorable cases disappear from denominators.
A synthetic fixture, schema check, theorem count, or deterministic trace is described as a model, kernel, search, or workload result.
A consumer reads a structural-only or unmeasured axis because the substrate was admitted elsewhere.
The fallback is stale, unavailable, slower than the recovery window, or silently depends on the candidate.
The candidate becomes organizationally central before regression alarms, quarantine, rollback, and retirement are executable.
Data leakage, benchmark contamination, evaluator coupling, or shared implementation defects create false agreement.
Privacy, license, provenance, accessibility, or dual-use constraints are omitted because the object is mathematical.
A narrow consumer result is generalized across workloads, scales, models, hardware, organizations, or time without transfer evidence.
Negative results are relabeled exploratory indefinitely, making optionality an unfalsifiable parking lot.
Source-reported Mamba, Universal Transformer, Recurrent Transformer, V-JEPA, Circle, TreeLLM, coil, or calculus results are imported as ASI Stack results.
Governance burden exceeds substrate benefit, but sunk-cost or narrative commitment prevents retirement.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The current minimum is a schema-valid Substrate Adoption Record, a deterministic trace with four valid synthetic states and eight expected-invalid controls, and eleven finite Lean theorem declarations. It proves record and negative-case discipline only; it contains no implemented substrate A/B, learned model, kernel comparison, natural workload, measured benefit, independent reproduction, transfer, or chapter-core evidence transition.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature Substrate Adoption Exchange runs inspectable candidate implementations on natural bounded workloads against ordinary, strongest-current, simple-ablation, and embarrassing controls under matched resources; measures structural validity, fidelity/rate, search, routing, reasoning/task utility, latency, memory, energy, verifier burden, safety, rights, fallback, and recovery jointly; identifies each mechanism through causal ablation; requires independent implementation and evaluation; tests heterogeneous transfer; and retires candidates that do not earn a consumer-specific advantage. Mathematical novelty remains available, but no longer survives merely by staying optional.

Temporal Coil, Circle, Theseus, TreeLLM, CoilMoECOT, Mamba, Universal Transformer, Recurrent Transformer, and V-JEPA material remains process, structural, speculative, local-project, or external comparator context at its stated boundary; source-reported results are not reproduced or imported as ASI Stack results.

A competent full attempt requires inspectable candidate implementations on natural bounded workloads against ordinary, strongest-current, simple-ablation, and embarrassing controls under matched information and resources; joint axis and total-burden measurement; causal ablations; executable fallback and recovery; independent implementation/evaluation; and heterogeneous transfer.

Useful, efficient, safe, deployable, general, transferable, or SOTA substrate claims remain blocked until the exact consumer-specific campaign passes; no support-state movement is implied by schema validity, deterministic traces, theorem declarations, source review, or this semantic sweep.

chapters.title: Circle Calculus and Proof-Carrying AI Contracts
chapters.problem: The stack needs a way to turn selected mathematical and AI-infrastructure claims into theorem-linked, machine-readable contracts without treating proofs as model-quality evidence.
chapters.insufficient: Ordinary prose, tests, and diagrams can show intent, but they do not give downstream systems stable theorem IDs, proof status, receipt fingerprints, replay checks, and explicit non-claims.
chapters.core_claim: Circle Calculus and Proof-Carrying AI Contracts owns a theorem-, model-, artifact-, implementation-, consumer-, claim-, version-, and time-specific Proof Contract Transport Envelope: a finite formal fact may travel only with resolvable proof identity, exact assumptions and semantics, source and toolchain provenance, content fingerprints, deterministic recomputation or replay, least-authority consumer gates, expiry and revocation, and preserved non-claims; theorem validity, receipt readiness, archive integrity, or transport success alone confers no model-quality, runtime, memory, safety, deployment, transfer, support, or SOTA authority.
mechanism: Register the exact formal claim, theorem namespace and identity, finite model, assumptions, proof status, source version, toolchain, implementation object, intended consumers, and prohibited inferences.
Pin public-safe source, dependencies, compiler or prover version, build configuration, theorem manifest, schema, dictionary, and artifact digests before transport.
Build or verify the named formal target and retain command, environment, logs, failures, proof boundary, and compiled-declaration receipt rather than trusting a theorem label.
Resolve every theorem and dictionary reference to a unique compiled declaration, exact statement, assumptions, proof status, source location, and version, failing closed on ambiguity or absence.
Bind the engineering object to the formal model through explicit abstraction, representation, discretization, numeric, and implementation-refinement obligations, leaving gaps as residuals.
Canonicalize the contract and compute source, model, theorem-set, deterministic-field, receipt, policy, and pack fingerprints with collision and canonicalization policy declared.
Recompute deterministic fields and bounded reference outputs from pinned inputs, recording exact arithmetic, tolerances, numerical diagnostics, nondeterminism, and non-proof layers separately.
Attach explicit non-claims for every unproved semantic, empirical, causal, resource, safety, deployment, transfer, support, and SOTA axis.
Validate receipt and proof-target schemas, required fields, state transitions, provenance closure, consumer policy, staleness policy, failure behavior, and support-state effect.

Replay the complete contract in a clean environment from public-safe artifacts, checking source digests, theorem resolution, build result, deterministic recomputation, receipt fingerprint, and archived output.

Reject missing theorem, altered statement, digest mismatch, stale proof, ambiguous resolver, changed assumptions, forged status, missing non-claim, unsupported consumer, replay omission, and promotion-overclaim mutations.
Issue a least-authority consumer gate naming the exact fact, object, contract version, permitted use, prohibited inference, evidence state, expiry, verifier command, fallback, and residual owner.
Require separate natural workload, ordinary and current baselines, negative controls, metrics, full costs, evaluator independence, and result artifacts before any downstream empirical promotion.

Transport the envelope through artifact graphs with signatures or authenticated provenance, immutable lineage, dependency closure, custody, access and rights controls, acknowledgements, and consumer receipts.
Monitor theorem, source, toolchain, schema, implementation, consumer, policy, rights, and threat drift; revoke or narrow every affected descendant and cached receipt.
Fail closed, quarantine, fall back to the last verified envelope or ordinary implementation, repair, replay, and recover without silently preserving stale authority.
Reproduce resolution, build, recomputation, transport, invalidation, and recovery with independently implemented producers, resolvers, and consumers, then test heterogeneous transfer.
Expire every envelope on material change or horizon, retain supersession and revocation lineage, and assign every model gap, unverifiable dependency, replay failure, consumer misuse, and residual.
interfaces: Formal Methods owns theorem truth, statement, assumptions, proof status, compiled declarations, and prover soundness; Transport owns their versioned envelope only.
Executable Specifications owns the engineering-to-model refinement obligation; a receipt cannot invent semantic correspondence.
Artifact Graphs and Supply Chain own source, dependency, build, signature, custody, access, and lineage provenance.
Runtime owns executable binding and behavioral conformance; a structural receipt is not runtime behavior.
Context, Memory, Recurrence, Routing, Compression, and Cyclic Substrates consume only named structural facts within their gates.
Verification owns evaluator adequacy, theorem resolution checks, replay checks, negative controls, and dispute state.
Security, Privacy, and Rights own adversarial provenance, key trust, confidentiality, license, data, access, and revocation decisions.
Resource Economics owns build, resolution, recomputation, storage, verification, transport, human, recovery, and opportunity costs.
Claim Ledgers and Evidence own support states, transitions, non-claims, contrary evidence, reproduction, transfer, expiry, and residuals.
Readiness and Incident Response own admission, quarantine, rollback, recovery, and descendant invalidation.
Release owns public artifact and statement authority; receipt transport cannot publish or promote a claim by itself.
Private consumers may return public-safe receipts and boundaries, but private results remain private and cannot be inferred from contract acceptance.
invariants: Every transported fact is specific to theorem, statement, assumptions, model, artifact, implementation relation, consumer, use, version, and time.
Theorem identity resolves uniquely to a compiled declaration and exact statement under a pinned source and toolchain, or the envelope fails closed.
Proof status, schema validity, deterministic computation, numerical diagnostics, replay, archive integrity, consumer readiness, and empirical support remain distinct states.
A formal theorem authorizes only its statement under its assumptions; semantic correspondence and implementation refinement are separate obligations.
Fingerprints cover canonical content and declared dependencies; algorithm, canonicalization, collision policy, and trust root remain explicit.
Deterministic fields are reproducible from pinned public-safe inputs, and tolerances, nondeterminism, failures, and discarded attempts remain visible.
Every theorem, dictionary, artifact, source, dependency, schema, policy, and consumer reference is resolvable or explicitly residualized.
Non-claims survive quotation, summary, serialization, transport, caching, supersession, and downstream consumption.
Consumer permission is least-authority and cannot cross fact, object, use, claim axis, environment, version, or expiry boundaries.
Downstream quality, runtime, memory, safety, deployment, or transfer promotion requires separate natural workload evidence with strong baselines and controls.
Missing, ambiguous, stale, altered, revoked, unsupported, rights-incompatible, or unreplayable envelopes block use rather than degrade silently.
Every descendant, cache, citation, claim, artifact, and consumer receipt inherits invalidation when an upstream proof or dependency changes.
The ordinary or last verified fallback remains executable, tested, rights-compatible, and reachable within the declared recovery window.
Independent reproduction does not share the decisive producer, resolver, consumer, trust root, or implementation defect.
Private inputs and outcomes never enter public evidence through inference from a public-safe contract receipt.
Support, deployment, release, safety, transfer, or SOTA movement requires an accepted transition for that exact claim and scope.
Envelope qualifications expire on material theorem, source, toolchain, model, implementation, schema, consumer, policy, rights, threat, or time change.
Every unresolved theorem-model gap, unverifiable dependency, replay failure, consumer misuse, revocation, and residual has an owner and reopening condition.
failure_modes: A theorem name, badge, diagram, proof count, or green build creates authority beyond the exact statement.
A theorem ID resolves to the wrong declaration, changed statement, different assumptions, stale source, or incompatible toolchain.
The formal model is correct but does not faithfully represent the engineering object or deployed implementation.
Exact finite or discrete facts are applied to approximate, floating-point, probabilistic, continuous, or adversarial behavior without a bridge.
Canonicalization, hash coverage, collision policy, signatures, or trust roots omit a field that can change the fact or consumer decision.
A replay checks a copied receipt rather than rebuilding, resolving, and recomputing from pinned sources.
Generated fields, tolerances, nondeterminism, failed commands, or discarded attempts are hidden behind a passing summary.
Non-claims disappear in an appendix, summary, API conversion, cache, prompt, or downstream chapter.
Consumer readiness, archive integrity, or transport success is mistaken for workload success, model quality, or deployment safety.
A consumer silently broadens fact, use, claim axis, environment, version, or expiry beyond the lease.
Missing baselines, negative controls, evaluator independence, or complete costs are waived because the structural proof is strong.
Revocation or theorem drift fails to reach descendants, cached receipts, citations, releases, or private consumers.
Fallback depends on the invalid envelope, is stale, rights-incompatible, or exceeds the recovery window.
Producer, resolver, verifier, consumer, and trust root share one defect while appearing independently confirmed.
Private results or inaccessible artifacts are implied by a public-safe receipt or acceptance record.
A local clean checkout, archived pack, fixture, or finite Lean bridge is described as deployed proof-contract transport.
A narrow external Circle rope result is generalized to all Circle contracts, AI substrates, real RoPE, model quality, safety, or transfer.
Transport and governance cost exceed ambiguity reduction, but institutional commitment prevents retirement.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The exact current minimum contains two schema-valid proof/receipt records; one external clean-checkout Circle rope slice with a narrow non-core argument-to-prototype-backed transition; one ASI-side gate accepting one pinned receipt and rejecting four mutations with no core effect; one digest-verified archive of nine contracts and four policy receipts with five rejected controls and a no-change decision; and ten finite local Lean theorem declarations. It does not establish a locally built general resolver, end-to-end transport service, semantic refinement, descendant revocation, independent reproduction, downstream empirical benefit, or chapter-core transition.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature Proof Contract Transport Service carries exact formal facts from independently implemented producers through resolvers, canonicalizers, authenticated artifact graphs, clean replays, least-authority consumers, drift and descendant revocation, fallback, and recovery. It competes against signed manifests, hashes, schemas, tests, SBOM/provenance systems, and ordinary versioned APIs on natural multi-consumer workloads; measures resolution and semantic-refinement accuracy, false acceptance/rejection, replay determinism, tamper and stale-proof detection, revocation completeness, liveness, recovery, latency, cost, rights, and consumer misuse jointly; and requires adversarial mutations, causal component ablations, independent reproduction, and heterogeneous transfer while keeping every downstream empirical claim separate.

open_evidence_gaps: Appendix C records exact passage-reviewed mappings for all five assigned sources. They support proof and receipt shape, finite Circle contract families, exact/discretized RoPE boundaries, cyclic-compute lineage, and anti-overclaim discipline; none independently proves a general transport service or downstream empirical result.

The exact current minimum contains two schema-valid records; one external clean-checkout Circle rope slice with a narrow non-core prototype-backed transition; one ASI-side gate accepting one pinned receipt and rejecting four mutations with no core effect; one digest-verified archive of nine contracts and four policy receipts with five rejected controls and a no-change decision; and ten finite local theorem declarations.

No locally built general theorem resolver, producer-to-multiple-consumer service, authenticated transport, semantic refinement proof, descendant revocation, liveness or recovery campaign, independent implementation, heterogeneous transfer, downstream model-quality result, or accepted chapter-core transition exists.

A competent full attempt requires natural multi-consumer contract transport against signed manifests, hashes, schemas, tests, SBOM/provenance systems, and ordinary versioned APIs; exact resolution and semantic-refinement measurement; adversarial mutations; complete cost and rights accounting; revocation/fallback/recovery; causal ablations; independent producers/resolvers/consumers; and heterogeneous transfer.

Model quality, runtime, memory, safety, deployment, transfer, support, or SOTA remain separate empirical claims. No chapter-core support effect, local Circle proof ownership, private-result inference, or general proof-transport claim follows from the current fixtures, external replay, archive, or finite Lean checks.

proof_targets.target: A proof-carrying AI contract exposes theorem references, deterministic fields, and an explicit non-claim boundary before downstream use.
A downstream claim cannot be promoted solely from contract readiness without a workload, baseline, metric, and evidence artifact.

The public Circle consumer-gate fixture records one valid receipt, four rejected mutation controls, seven required theorem IDs, pinned digest fields, blocked support movement, no chapter-core promotion, and no deployed-transport claim.

chapters.title: Coil Attention, Cyclic Memory, and Recurrence Contracts
chapters.problem: Memory, attention, and recurrence mechanisms need finite structural contracts for aliasing, coverage, freshness, active work, loop exits, and overthinking boundaries.
chapters.insufficient: Long context, sparse attention, ring buffers, and recursive loops can silently hide stale reads, uncovered lags, duplicate slots, alias collisions, or unbounded work.
chapters.core_claim: Coil Attention, Cyclic Memory, and Recurrence Contracts owns a memory-object-, state-version-, request-, consumer-, workload-, structural-axis-, budget-, and time-specific State-Carry and Recurrence Admission Lease: a slot read, cyclic address, KV reuse, sparse edge, fanout schedule, or recurrent step may be admitted only when authority, provenance, residue and winding, freshness, coverage, alias and collision state, active work, progress, exit, fallback, expiry, and residuals satisfy the exact consumer contract; structural validity, synthetic fixtures, receipt replay, cache presence, or reduced scheduled work alone confers no retrieval, reasoning, context-length, quality, speed, memory, safety, deployment, transfer, support, or SOTA authority.
mechanism: Register the memory object, state and model version, request and tenant, consumer, workload, authority, rights, structural axes, budget, horizon, and prohibited inferences.
Bind every carried state to source provenance, VCM packet or context lineage, write and read epochs, model and tokenizer identity, cache layout, device, and revocation state.
Represent cyclic address with residue plus winding or a visible alias residual, never residue alone when wrap identity matters.
Compute slot occupancy, collision and alias load, duplicate candidates, overwrite order, source identity, and unresolved ambiguity before a read or route is eligible.
Check token-, request-, version-, policy-, model-, and time-specific freshness, distinguishing physical presence from authorized current meaning.
Compute declared sparse lag, interval, fanout, window, and path coverage, exposing the first gap, uncovered set, collisions, repair cost, and dense fallback.
Bind recurrence to a declared state transition, active and inactive work schedule, maximum steps, progress measure, stop rule, periodicity, and prohibited self-extension.
Reserve compute, memory, bandwidth, verifier, and reviewer budgets before recurrence or memory expansion, accounting for cache maintenance, lookup, repair, fallback, and displaced work.
Run malformed alias, stale read, wrong request, version mismatch, incomplete coverage, duplicate fanout, missing progress, missing exit, hidden work, and absent fallback controls before admission.
Issue a least-authority lease for the exact read, edge, reuse, fanout, or recurrent step, with permitted consumer, structural facts, expiry, non-claims, fallback, and residual owner.
Execute canary memory and recurrence behavior beside ordinary full-attention, no-cache, recompute, FIFO/LRU, retrieval, fixed-depth, and direct baselines under matched information and resources.

Measure structural correctness, stale and alias events, coverage, task retrieval and reasoning utility, calibration, latency and tails, memory, bandwidth, energy, verifier burden, and human repair separately and jointly.
Ablate winding, provenance, freshness, repair, recurrence state, progress, stopping, and fallback to identify the claimed mechanism rather than correlate a receipt with success.
Monitor drift, cache poisoning, cross-request leakage, stale descendants, coverage gaps, loop stagnation, overthinking, tail growth, and resource displacement without censoring failures.
Refuse, residualize, repair, recompute, fall back, quarantine, rollback, or terminate when authority, freshness, coverage, progress, budget, rights, or recovery fails.
Invalidate all dependent caches, recurrent states, routes, receipts, and claims on source, model, tokenizer, layout, policy, authority, rights, or threat change.
Reproduce with independent memory and recurrence implementations and evaluators, then transfer across tasks, sequence lengths, models, hardware, organizations, attacks, and time.
Expire every lease and assign each stale read, alias ambiguity, coverage gap, repeated state, hidden work, fallback defect, failed transfer, and unresolved residual.
interfaces: Context and VCM own semantic objects, packet lineage, authority, adequacy, taint, revocation, and materialization; this lease consumes those facts without widening them.
Proof Contract Transport owns theorem identity and structural receipt integrity; this chapter owns admission consequences for a named memory or recurrence consumer.
Runtime and Serving own actual cache, device, kernel, batching, paging, and request-isolation behavior.
Routing owns route choice; a structurally eligible memory or recurrence lane is not automatically selected.
Deliberation owns extra-inference purpose and stopping policy; this chapter checks recurrence state, progress, work, and exit conformance.
Resource Economics owns complete compute, memory, bandwidth, energy, verifier, reviewer, repair, fallback, and opportunity cost.
Verification owns semantic adequacy, retrieval correctness, reasoning outcome, evaluator independence, and dispute state.
Security, Privacy, and Rights own poisoning, cross-request leakage, access, data, license, retention, deletion, and revocation decisions.
Artifacts and Replay own state snapshots, hashes, dependency lineage, commands, receipts, and descendant invalidation.
Readiness and Incident Response own canary admission, quarantine, rollback, recovery, and escalation.
Claims and Evidence own support state, empirical transitions, contrary evidence, reproduction, transfer, expiry, and residuals.
Release owns public and deployment authority; a structural lease cannot promote or publish a capability claim.
invariants: Every lease is specific to memory object, state and model version, request, tenant, consumer, workload, axis, budget, environment, and time.
Authority, provenance, taint, revocation, rights, and deletion state never widen through cache or recurrence reuse.
Residue-only addressing never erases winding, wrap count, slot identity, collision, overwrite, or alias residuals when they matter.
Physical cache presence, address validity, freshness, semantic adequacy, retrieval utility, reasoning quality, and deployment readiness remain distinct.
Every read binds source, write epoch, request, model, tokenizer, layout, policy, and consumer expectations or fails closed.
Sparse coverage exposes exact domains, first gaps, uncovered sets, collisions, repair cost, and fallback rather than a single ratio.
Fanout budgets use unique effective candidates and preserve duplicates, shortfalls, unreachable states, and selection cost.
Every recurrent transition has typed input, state, and output, active work, progress, maximum steps, stop rule, and fallback.
A recurrence cannot self-authorize more steps, preserve hidden state, reset its progress measure, or exceed its lease.
Failed, stale, aliased, uncovered, duplicated, timed-out, OOM, fallback, recomputed, repaired, and refused cases remain in denominators.
Scheduled work savings remain modeled structural quantities until measured end-to-end against matched baselines with maintenance and verification cost.
A favorable structural result or mean cannot hide tails, cross-request leakage, rare aliasing, subgroup failures, or downstream regressions.
Every admitted path retains an executable ordinary fallback with bounded recovery time and sufficient resources.
Source, model, tokenizer, layout, policy, authority, rights, threat, or time change invalidates affected states and descendants.
Independent reproduction does not share the decisive cache, scheduler, recurrence controller, evaluator, or trace defect.
Support, retrieval, reasoning, context length, efficiency, safety, deployment, transfer, or SOTA movement requires a separate accepted transition.
Private Theseus or other consumer outcomes cannot be inferred from public-safe structural receipts.
Every stale read, alias, gap, repeated state, hidden cost, fallback defect, invalidation failure, and residual has an owner and reopening condition.
failure_modes: Residue-only addressing hides winding, wraps, overwrites, collisions, or aliased sources.
A cache entry from another request, tenant, model, tokenizer, policy, or epoch is accepted because its slot looks fresh.
Physical presence or timestamp freshness is confused with authorized, relevant, correct, or adequate content.
Sparse lag or path coverage is treated as semantic dependency coverage, retrieval quality, or long-context understanding.
A coverage average hides the first gap, unreachable tail, collisions, repair burden, or dense fallback cost.
Fanout counts duplicates as useful candidates and hides effective-budget shortfall or selection work.
Recurrence repeats a state, makes no progress, resets its budget, self-extends, or lacks a reachable exit.
Active-token or scheduled-work reduction is called speed, memory, energy, or economic benefit without end-to-end measurement.
Cache lookup, maintenance, invalidation, repair, verification, transfer, and fallback costs disappear from comparison.
Failures, stale reads, aliases, uncovered cases, timeouts, OOMs, fallbacks, recomputations, and repairs disappear from denominators.
A receipt, theorem, schema, synthetic trace, or external structural fixture is called a learned-memory or reasoning result.
Weak no-cache, full-attention, retrieval, recurrent, state-space, fixed-depth, or direct baselines make the mechanism appear useful.
Shared controller, evaluator, cache implementation, trace, or synthetic generator creates false independent agreement.
Poisoned, revoked, private, rights-incompatible, or deletion-targeted state survives in caches or recurrent descendants.
Fallback is stale, depends on the invalid state, lacks capacity, or exceeds the recovery window.
A narrow Circle memory, KV, sparse, fanout, or recurrence receipt is generalized to deployed behavior or model quality.
External recurrent-model results are imported as local cyclic-memory correctness, useful context, or compute benefit.
Governance and structural checking cost exceed benefit, but cache or recurrence lock-in prevents retirement.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The exact current minimum is one schema-valid cyclic-memory record; three valid and six expected-invalid synthetic contract traces; five public-safe external Circle structural receipt slices for cyclic memory, KV-cache freshness, recurrence scheduling, sparse-attention gaps, and strided fanout, all with explicit no-promotion boundaries; and six finite local Lean theorem declarations. It proves no deployed cache, sparse attention, recurrence controller, useful memory, retrieval, reasoning, context-length, latency, memory, independent reproduction, transfer, or chapter-core result.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature State-Carry and Recurrence Admission Layer runs natural long-context, retrieval, tool-state, streaming, and recurrent-reasoning workloads against full-attention, no-cache/recompute, FIFO/LRU, retrieval, Transformer-XL-style, compressed-memory, RetNet/state-space, Universal/Recurrent Transformer, fixed-depth, and direct controls under matched resources. It measures structural integrity, stale/alias/leakage events, coverage and repair, progress and overthinking, useful retrieval and task outcomes, calibration, latency/tails, memory, bandwidth, energy, verifier and human burden, fallback/recovery, safety, rights, and residuals jointly; identifies mechanisms through causal ablation; requires independent implementations and evaluators; and transfers across tasks, lengths, models, hardware, organizations, attacks, and time.

chapters.title: CoilRA, MultiCoil RoPE, and Cyclic Mixers
chapters.problem: Position encodings, adapters, route heads, and mixers need a place for cyclic or block-cyclic structure that separates structural invariants from quality and runtime claims.
chapters.insufficient: Parameter-count, equivariance, or exact phase facts can be mistaken for better model behavior unless baselines, hardware costs, alias and load diagnostics, and failure cases are separated.
chapters.core_claim: CoilRA, MultiCoil RoPE, and Cyclic Mixers owns a model-, layer-, mechanism-version-, workload-, baseline-, kernel-, hardware-, claim-axis-, and time-specific Cyclic Mechanism Tradeoff Packet: a cyclic adapter, phase bank, rotary scheme, route head, circulant operator, or block-cyclic mixer may enter a canary only when exact residue/winding, phase horizon, alias/collision/load, dense-reference parity, parameter and operation accounting, numerical error, kernel availability, complete cost, quality, failure, fallback, and rights evidence is matched against strong ordinary controls; equivariance, finite proofs, receipt validity, parameter reduction, or structural parity alone confers no quality, context-length, speed, memory, stability, efficiency, safety, deployment, transfer, support, or SOTA authority.
mechanism: Register the exact cyclic mechanism, model and layer, parameterization, implementation and kernel version, workload, consumer, hardware target, rights, claim axes, and prohibited inferences.
Declare why cyclic structure is native to the data, task, geometry, recurrence, position, routing, or computation rather than an aesthetic substitution.
Freeze dense, LoRA, ordinary RoPE, learned-position, recurrent, state-space, nonperiodic, random-phase, wrong-period, and no-mechanism controls under matched budgets.
Pin structural receipts, theorem identities, abstraction limits, source versions, fingerprints, deterministic fields, and exact/discretized versus real-valued boundaries.
Compute residue plus winding, phase tuples, joint repeat horizons, relative-phase invariants, alias and collision sets, route load, and unresolved periodic ambiguity.
Check functional parity against a dense or literal reference on bounded inputs, recording numeric precision, tolerances, error distributions, nondeterminism, and counterexamples.
Account for trainable and frozen parameters, optimizer state, activations, KV or recurrent state, storage, traffic, compile work, operations, padding, and fallback artifacts.
Build or select real kernels for the declared hardware and shapes, recording compilation, fusion, vectorization, occupancy, transfer, batching, warmup, and refusal behavior.
Preregister natural workloads, data splits, sequence lengths, scale, seeds, tuning budget, metrics, failure cases, expected causal signature, falsifier, narrowing, and retirement rule.
Run alias, collision, wrong-period, phase-wrap, load-imbalance, numerical-drift, dense-parity, parameter-accounting, unsupported-shape, missing-kernel, and overclaim mutations.
Execute matched training and inference canaries while preserving failures, divergence, OOM, timeout, fallback, repair, and excluded cases in complete denominators.

Measure task quality and calibration, context use, robustness, convergence and stability, latency and tails, throughput, memory, bandwidth, energy, verifier and operator burden, safety, rights, and residuals jointly.

Ablate cyclic tying, phase banks, winding, block structure, circulant operation, relative rotation, and hardware specialization while matching parameters, operations, tuning, and evaluator opportunity.
Issue a least-authority tradeoff packet and canary permission only for the exact mechanism, layer, workload, hardware, kernel, axes, budget, expiry, and fallback that passed.
Monitor alias/load tails, numeric error, kernel drift, compiler changes, quality regression, training instability, hardware mismatch, fallback health, and displaced cost.
Refuse, residualize, fall back, quarantine, rollback, narrow, supersede, refute, or retire on structural, empirical, hardware, rights, recovery, or budget failure.
Reproduce with independent mechanism and kernel implementations and independent evaluators, then transfer across tasks, models, scales, hardware, compilers, organizations, attacks, and time.
Expire every packet on material change and assign each alias, parity gap, numerical error, unsupported shape, kernel defect, quality regression, failed transfer, and residual.
interfaces: Mathematical and Search Substrates owns general adoption; it consumes this chapter's cyclic-specific packet without duplicating its diagnostics.
Proof Contract Transport owns theorem and receipt integrity; this chapter owns their cyclic mechanism and empirical tradeoff interpretation.
Representation and Context own semantic fidelity and position meaning; cyclic structure cannot redefine them by construction.
Routing owns route selection and load policy; a cyclic route feature or head is only an eligible input within its lease.
State-Carry and Recurrence owns memory and recurrence admission; this chapter owns internal cyclic parameterization and kernel tradeoffs.
Training and Policy Optimization own optimizer behavior, convergence, checkpoint selection, and learned-quality evidence.
Runtime and Serving own compiled kernels, devices, batching, cache behavior, throughput, and production conformance.
Resource Economics owns complete parameter, state, operation, memory, bandwidth, energy, verification, human, fallback, and opportunity cost.
Verification and Benchmarks own task adequacy, evaluator independence, contamination, baselines, controls, and dispute state.
Security, Privacy, and Rights own attacks, data, license, access, provenance, dual use, retention, and deployment constraints.
Readiness, Incident, and Release own canary admission, quarantine, rollback, recovery, deployment, and public authority.
Claims and Evidence own support states, transitions, non-claims, reproduction, transfer, expiry, and residual lineage.
invariants: Every packet is specific to mechanism, model, layer, implementation, kernel, workload, baseline, hardware, axis, budget, environment, and time.
Cyclic structure has a declared workload-native justification and remains optional when the structure is absent or unhelpful.
Structural, functional-parity, numerical, parameter, operation, quality, context, runtime, memory, stability, safety, deployment, transfer, and SOTA axes remain separate.
Exact integer or finite phase-bank facts never silently extend to floating-point RoPE, learned representations, trained behavior, or deployment.
Residue never replaces winding where wrap identity matters; alias, collision, joint-horizon, and load tails remain visible.
Dense-reference parity is scoped to declared inputs, precision, tolerance, implementation, and environment and is not quality or speed evidence.
Parameter accounting includes trainable/frozen weights, optimizer state, activations, cache or recurrence state, storage, padding, metadata, and fallback.
Operation and cost accounting includes compile, transforms, indexing, traffic, synchronization, verification, warmup, batching, transfer, repair, and opportunity cost.
Strong ordinary and negative controls receive matched data, parameters or operations, tuning, seeds, hardware, kernel effort, retries, and evaluator access.
All divergence, OOM, timeout, unsupported shape, missing kernel, fallback, repair, failed seed, and excluded cases remain in denominators.
A favorable mean cannot hide alias/load tails, rare numerical error, subgroup regression, training instability, or hardware-specific failure.
A lower parameter count, structural parity, or theoretical operation count cannot imply end-to-end latency, memory, energy, throughput, or economic benefit.
Every canary retains a tested ordinary fallback with sufficient resources and bounded recovery time.
Qualification expires on mechanism, model, layer, implementation, kernel, compiler, workload, baseline, hardware, policy, rights, threat, or time change.
Independent reproduction cannot share the decisive mechanism, kernel, evaluator, data leak, compiler defect, or benchmark flaw.
Support, quality, context, efficiency, safety, deployment, transfer, or SOTA movement requires an accepted transition for that exact claim and scope.
Private Theseus or other consumer outcomes cannot be inferred from public-safe structural packets or receipts.
Every alias, parity gap, numerical error, unsupported shape, kernel defect, quality regression, fallback failure, and residual has an owner and reopening condition.
failure_modes: Cyclic elegance, prime periods, or equivariance substitutes for workload-native justification.
A finite theorem or receipt is laundered into trained-model quality, usable context, runtime, memory, safety, or deployment authority.
Exact phase-bank or integer collision results are generalized to floating-point RoPE or learned positions without a numerical and behavioral bridge.
Residue-only diagnostics hide winding, wraps, aliases, collisions, joint repeat horizons, or load concentration.
Dense parity on toy inputs is described as model parity, training equivalence, or deployment correctness.
Parameter savings omit optimizer state, activations, caches, metadata, padding, fallback, storage, or reconstruction burden.
Theoretical operation savings disappear in indexing, memory traffic, transforms, synchronization, compilation, or verification.
A custom kernel is compared with an untuned baseline, different shapes, different precision, different batching, or unequal engineering effort.
Unsupported hardware or shapes silently use a slow path while the preferred-path claim remains attached.
Wrong-period, random-phase, nonperiodic, dense, LoRA, ordinary RoPE, learned-position, recurrent, or state-space controls are weak or absent.
Outcome-driven selection over periods, blocks, ranks, seeds, lengths, tasks, metrics, or kernels creates false discovery.
Failed seeds, divergence, OOMs, timeouts, fallbacks, repairs, and excluded shapes disappear from denominators.
A favorable average hides rare aliasing, load tails, numeric instability, subgroups, long-length collapse, or quality regressions.
Source-reported Circle, RoPE, LoRA, Mamba, RetNet, or Theseus results are imported as ASI Stack results.
A packet accepted on one model, layer, kernel, or accelerator is generalized across scales, hardware, compilers, organizations, or time.
Fallback is stale, unavailable, underprovisioned, or silently depends on the cyclic mechanism.
Structural or parameter wins survive despite causal ablation showing no useful mechanism-specific effect.
Kernel and governance cost exceed benefit, but aesthetic or sunk-cost commitment prevents retirement.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The exact current minimum is one schema-valid cyclic-mixer evaluation record; one inherited public-safe RoPE structural receipt boundary; one public-safe Circle cyclic-mixer receipt with bounded dense-reference parity and parameter accounting; one public-safe MultiCoil phase receipt with finite phase and relative-shift facts; two no-change decisions for the local cyclic slices; and seven finite local Lean theorem declarations. It includes no trained cyclic model, natural workload, baseline matrix, real kernel or hardware benchmark, measured quality/context/runtime/memory/stability benefit, independent reproduction, transfer, or chapter-core transition.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature Cyclic Mechanism Evaluation Lane implements CoilRA, MultiCoil RoPE, circulant and block-cyclic candidates plus tuned dense, LoRA, ordinary RoPE, learned-position, recurrent, state-space, wrong-period, random-phase, and nonperiodic controls on natural workloads and real hardware. It measures structural and numerical integrity, quality and context use, convergence and stability, latency/tails and throughput, complete memory/traffic/energy/parameter/operation burden, safety, rights, fallback, recovery, and residuals jointly; identifies each cyclic mechanism through matched causal ablation; requires independent mechanism, kernel, and evaluator implementations; transfers across models, scales, accelerators, compilers, tasks, attacks, and time; and retires cyclic candidates that do not earn a scoped advantage.

open_evidence_gaps: All five assigned sources have exact passage-reviewed mappings. The exact current implementation is one schema record, one inherited RoPE structural boundary, one bounded cyclic-mixer structural/accounting receipt, one finite MultiCoil phase receipt, two no-change decisions, and seven finite record-level theorem declarations; none changes chapter-core support.

The Circle cyclic-mixer slice records `max_abs_dense_delta=0` and `block_to_dense_ratio=0.0625` only for its bounded fixture. There is no cyclic mixer benchmark, no model-quality result, and no support-state transition.

The Circle MultiCoil phase slice records `joint_repeat_horizon=35` and `relative_phase=3` only for its finite fixture. There is no MultiCoil phase benchmark, no model-quality result, and no support-state transition.

No trained CoilRA, MultiCoil, circulant, or block-cyclic model has been run here on a natural workload; no matched dense, LoRA, ordinary RoPE, learned-position, recurrent, state-space, wrong-period, random-phase, or nonperiodic baseline matrix exists; and no real kernel or hardware benchmark establishes quality, context, convergence, stability, runtime, memory, traffic, energy, parameter, operation, safety, or deployment advantage.

Argument exit requires preregistered natural workloads, matched strong and negative controls, complete failure denominators, causal ablations, independent mechanism/kernel/evaluator implementations, heterogeneous transfer, working fallback, rights clearance, and an accepted claim transition. Until then all broad quality, context-length, efficiency, safety, deployment, transfer, support, and SOTA claims remain blocked.

parts.title: Part IV - Evidence, Implementation, and the Living Book
parts.part_job: Turn the architecture into an accountable research program: executable specifications, Lean proof envelopes, benchmark ratchets, reference traces, implementation roadmap, living-book method, and bibliography discipline.

chapters.title: Executable Specifications and Lean Proof Envelope
chapters.problem: The book needs to decide which architecture claims should become executable specs or Lean proofs.
chapters.insufficient: Formal methods are ineffective when applied to vague philosophical claims instead of operational predicates and transitions.
chapters.core_claim: Executable Specifications and Lean Proof Envelope owns a proposition-, predicate-, abstraction-, artifact-, verifier-, consumer-, implementation-, version-, environment-, and time-specific Formal Artifact Authority Lease: a schema, executable model, Lean theorem, model-checking result, runtime monitor, behavior test, benchmark, or external theorem may authorize only the exact consumer statement whose operational semantics, abstraction map and losses, assumptions, dependency closure, verifier result, semantic adequacy, implementation binding, limitations, non-claims, expiry, and revocation path are recorded; artifact existence, field presence, a finite route, proof depth, a green build, a passing fixture, or an external theorem identity alone confers no deployed enforcement, empirical truth, system safety, source correctness, support promotion, transfer, or SOTA authority.

mechanism: Register the exact proposition, quantifiers, scope, owner, intended consumer statement, authority sought, support axis, artifact and implementation versions, environment, and expiry.
Decompose broad safety, alignment, capability, governance, quality, or deployment language into the smallest load-bearing predicates and explicitly leave the remainder unproved.

Route each predicate to Lean, model checking, schema, executable model, runtime monitor, behavior test, benchmark, external theorem, normative review, or research backlog without laundering one lane into another.

Define an abstraction map from prose and implementation terms to formal symbols, representations, states, and transitions, recording every omission, quotient, approximation, idealization, and semantic loss.

State trusted bases and assumptions about identity, clocks, ordering, concurrency, completeness, nondeterminism, failures, adversaries, observations, environment, rights, and closed- versus open-world behavior.
Specify valid and invalid states, transition semantics, safety and liveness obligations, resource bounds, recovery behavior, residual states, and counterexamples before proving a preferred case.
Freeze stable proof-target and theorem identities, dependency edges, consumer links, supersession rules, required artifacts, verifier commands, and expected authority in the generated manifest.
Build the formal or executable artifact in a pinned environment and preserve source, toolchain, command, output, version, digest, warnings, timeouts, failures, and nondeterminism.

Check refinement or translation from specification to executable model and from executable model to implementation using an independently implemented checker or observational relation where the claim requires it.
Run vacuity, inconsistency, unreachable-state, assumption-restatement, missing-field, mutation, countermodel, adversarial-schedule, stale-artifact, and wrong-consumer controls.
Audit the declaration and target graph for direct projections, duplicated lemmas, unused theorems, circular dependencies, orphan targets, hidden axioms, unconsumed receipts, and misleading names.

Perform a semantic adequacy review against the prose obligation, strongest counterclaim, reachable implementation behavior, intended consumer, excluded cases, and required lane before any authority is granted.
Bind runtime claims to a named implementation, build, deployment, monitor, trace source, observation boundary, and independent evidence; otherwise retain formal-model-only language.

Issue a least-authority receipt stating the exact predicate, artifact lane, verifier result, adequacy state, consumer language, allowed uses, prohibited inferences, dependencies, support effect, expiry, and fallback.

Monitor changes in proposition, source, theorem, dependency, toolchain, specification, implementation, environment, policy, threat, rights, consumer, and evidence that can stale or invalidate the lease.

Refuse, residualize, quarantine, revoke, migrate, roll back, supersede, refute, or retire any artifact whose semantics, verifier, dependency, implementation binding, consumer match, rights, or recovery path fails.

Reproduce decisive claims with independent formalizations, proof assistants or model checkers where appropriate, executable models, implementations, evaluators, and adversarial reviewers, then test transfer across environments and time.
Expire every lease on material change and preserve lineage, negative results, retired declarations, countermodels, unresolved semantic loss, unproved remainder, and residual owners.
interfaces: Chapter and subsystem owners define the prose obligation, intended consumer statement, scope, strongest counterclaim, and unproved remainder; this chapter controls formal-artifact authority only.
Claims and Evidence owns support states and accepted transitions; a proof receipt cannot promote empirical or chapter-core support by itself.
Proof Contract Transport owns theorem identity, artifact integrity, fingerprints, replay, and consumer delivery; this chapter owns semantic adequacy and authorized interpretation.
Schemas and Protocol Specifications own record shape and conformance language; shape validity is not event occurrence, transition semantics, or implementation behavior.
Lean, model checkers, and formal semantics own derivations inside declared models and trusted bases, not source truth or deployed enforcement.
Executable specifications and reference models own bounded traces and refinement candidates, not production binding without a named implementation relation.
Runtime, Serving, and Security own actual enforcement, monitors, devices, failures, recovery, and operational conformance.
Benchmarks and Verification own empirical tasks, baselines, evaluators, contamination, uncertainty, and performance or capability evidence.
Source, Artifact, and Supply-Chain governance own provenance, licenses, toolchains, dependencies, attestations, and reproducibility inputs.
Resource Economics owns proof engineering, solver, runtime-monitor, verification, human-review, maintenance, fallback, and opportunity cost.
Readiness, Incident, and Release own canary admission, public wording, quarantine, rollback, correction, and post-release revocation.
Living-Book and Change Governance own stable tags, generated manifests, chapter/outline/appendix synchronization, changelog lineage, expiry, and successor work.
invariants: Every lease names one exact proposition, scope, artifact version, verifier result, consumer statement, implementation-binding state, environment, and expiry.
Broad safety, alignment, capability, deployment, or SOTA language is decomposed and the unproved remainder remains visible.
Schema, process, Lean, model-checking, executable, monitor, behavior, benchmark, external-theorem, normative, and research lanes retain distinct authority.
Every formal symbol resolves to a prose or implementation term through an explicit abstraction map with declared losses and exclusions.
Trusted bases, axioms, identity, clock, ordering, concurrency, completeness, nondeterminism, adversary, observation, and environment assumptions are explicit.
Safety, liveness, progress, recovery, resource, and empirical obligations remain separate and none is inferred from another without a recorded bridge.
A green build establishes only that the named artifact checks in the pinned toolchain; it does not establish semantic adequacy or runtime truth.
Field presence, direct projection, finite route, derived invariant, executable-model bridge, implementation binding, and independently observed effect remain distinct semantic depths.
Vacuous, inconsistent, unreachable, assumption-restating, duplicated, orphaned, unused, or unconsumed declarations cannot carry consumer authority.
Every accepted theorem and target has stable identity, dependency closure, source and consumer links, artifact refs, verifier command, limitations, and non-claims.
External theorem references retain exact identities, hypotheses, versions, licenses, trust boundaries, and local non-ownership; citation is not local proof or transfer.
Runtime language requires a named implementation, build, observation boundary, trace or monitor, refinement relation, and independently checked effect.
Failed builds, timeouts, solver unknowns, countermodels, mutation survivors, flaky results, and semantic disputes remain visible and cannot be censored from denominators.
Least-authority consumers may use only the exact statement and scope granted by the lease; downstream composition requires explicit dependency and interference review.
Every material proposition, artifact, dependency, toolchain, implementation, environment, policy, threat, rights, consumer, or evidence change expires affected authority.
Revocation reaches dependent claims, generated surfaces, runtime consumers, releases, caches, descendants, and public wording with a tested fallback and recovery path.
Independent reproduction does not share the decisive formalization error, proof kernel assumption, model checker, implementation, evaluator, trace generator, or benchmark flaw.
Every semantic loss, unproved remainder, disputed adequacy judgment, unsupported implementation binding, failed transfer, retired declaration, and residual has an owner and reopening condition.
failure_modes: Formal notation or theorem counts decorate an argument whose operational predicate and consumer use were never defined.
A broad safety, alignment, capability, quality, deployment, or SOTA claim is hidden inside a trivial finite record predicate.
A passing schema, process validator, behavior test, benchmark, or external citation is described as a Lean proof or equivalent formal guarantee.
A direct projection or assumption restatement is marketed as a derived semantic invariant.
A finite route over hand-constructed records is described as exhaustive real-system enforcement.
An executable model or fixture is called implementation binding without a named build, refinement relation, runtime trace, or independent observation.
The abstraction map silently drops identity, concurrency, missing events, nondeterminism, failures, adversaries, resources, rights, or open-world behavior.
Safety is inferred from liveness, liveness from safety, record completeness from field presence, or empirical utility from formal structure.
Vacuous truth, inconsistent assumptions, unreachable states, or an overconstrained model make every desired theorem easy.
Theorem names, proof tags, dependencies, toolchains, generated manifests, or consumers drift while stale authority remains attached.
Duplicate, orphan, unused, or unconsumed declarations survive because counts and green builds are treated as progress.
An external theorem is borrowed without exact hypotheses, version, license, theorem identity, artifact resolution, or applicability proof.
Failed builds, timeouts, unknown solver results, countermodels, mutation survivors, flaky checks, or adequacy disputes are omitted.
A consumer uses a true theorem for a stronger statement, different scope, changed implementation, incompatible environment, or unauthorized decision.
Proof engineering, solver, monitor, runtime, human-review, maintenance, and fallback costs are excluded from the claimed benefit.
A formal artifact changes support state, readiness, or release wording without an accepted claim transition and synchronized descendants.
Revocation stops at the theorem record while derived claims, releases, runtime consumers, caches, and public wording remain stale.
Sunk-cost attachment to a large proof corpus prevents merging, replacing, refuting, or retiring declarations that do not constrain a real consumer.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The exact current minimum is one proof-target schema and valid fixture; one generated 298-target proof manifest; a 65-module Lean workspace whose complete build reports 68 jobs and 1,151 theorem declarations classified as 952 derived/decomposed, 187 direct/projection, and 12 unknown/mixed; one proof-readiness classification of 13 adequate finite-record, 212 useful-but-narrow, 18 richer-state-machine, 35 executable-test-first, 18 empirical-test-first, and two research-agenda targets; one wiring-only proof artifact audit; one semantic-adequacy review surface; one blocked formal-semantic-depth record with ten rejecting mutations; and seven local ProofEnvelope declarations classified as two direct projections and five derived negative cases. It provides no complete semantic formalization of the stack, deployed enforcement proof, general implementation refinement, independent formal reproduction, empirical result, chapter-core transition, or SOTA result.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature Formal Claim Admission and Proof-Rationalization Program audits every proposition, all 298 activation-baseline proof targets, and all 1,151 activation-baseline theorem declarations for semantic necessity, vacuity, reachability, assumptions, dependencies, consumers, and implementation binding. It replaces load-bearing projections with richer state-machine, temporal, concurrency, noninterference, resource, recovery, probabilistic, or refinement models where warranted; moves behavior and performance obligations to executable and empirical lanes; validates implementation relations and runtime monitors; runs countermodels, mutations, adversarial schedules, and negative controls; requires independent formalizations, tools, implementations, and evaluators for decisive claims; measures proof and governance cost; propagates expiry and revocation through every consumer and release surface; and preserves narrow, null, refuted, retired, or blocked-after-full-attempt outcomes instead of optimizing theorem counts or green builds.
open_evidence_gaps: All twelve assigned sources have exact passage-reviewed mappings, but they support formalization and claim-boundary design only; no source-reported system, proof corpus, deployment, runtime, security, capability, or performance result is imported as ASI Stack evidence.

The current surface contains 298 targets and 1,151 declarations, but the activation-baseline rationalization is not complete: projections, assumption restatements, vacuity, reachability, duplicate/orphan status, consumer use, richer-model needs, executable and empirical handoffs, implementation binding, independent reproduction, and transfer must still receive terminal dispositions under the roadmap.

The local ProofEnvelope module has seven finite declarations—two direct projections and five derived negative cases—and the semantic-depth harness has one blocked record plus ten rejecting mutations. Neither proves full semantic adequacy, deployed enforcement, system safety, implementation refinement, empirical truth, support promotion, transfer, or SOTA.

chapters.title: Benchmark Ratchets and Anti-Goodhart Evidence
chapters.problem: The architecture needs a way to move capability claims through evidence without overfitting to fixed benchmarks.
chapters.insufficient: Benchmarks can saturate, be gamed, erase regressions, or fail to represent real requirements.
chapters.core_claim: Benchmark Ratchets and Anti-Goodhart Evidence owns a construct-, task-, dataset-, metric-, harness-, model-, checkpoint-, output-, evaluator-, baseline-, retry-lineage-, budget-, environment-, claim-axis-, and time-specific Benchmark Instrument Lease: a score or evaluation event may update only the exact claim whose construct validity, target capacity, data and metric provenance, output binding, contamination and public-calibration boundary, strong baselines and negative controls, complete selection and failure lineage, regression floors, frontier state, uncertainty, costs, causal checks, transfer, residuals, and decision authority survive review; a green fixture, synthetic probe, source-reported result, leaderboard gain, held-out score, saturation label, or archived winner alone confers no capability, safety, readiness, deployment, unlearning, support, transfer, or SOTA authority.
