mechanism: Register the intended construct, exact movable claim, task and population, data boundary, metric, harness, model, checkpoint, output path, evaluator, baselines, budget, authority, environment, and expiry.
Establish construct validity and target capacity before scoring, including whether the architecture, prompts, tools, context, actions, and observation channel can express the claimed behavior.
Version datasets, splits, resources, checksums, licenses, provenance, collection and curation steps, usage conditions, public exposure, contamination surface, and hidden-test custody.

Preregister workloads, natural and adversarial cases, metrics, mastery and critical-failure thresholds, saturation rules, uncertainty, subgroup and tail analyses, exclusions, retries, falsifiers, narrowing, and retirement.

Freeze strong ordinary baselines, ablations, random and no-skill controls, source-reported-only controls, wrong-target controls, and matched model, tool, context, compute, tuning, retry, and evaluator opportunity.

Classify each metric as measured, derived, recomputed, modeled, fixture, constant, declared, proxy, vacuous, source-reported, or unknown and bind it causally to raw observations, returned outputs, evaluator outputs, and the exact claim.

Track materially equivalent retries, hyperparameter and prompt search, checkpoint selection, archive ancestry, repairs, discarded candidates, validation feedback, and closure inheritance under one selection lineage and ceiling.
Move instruments through candidate, calibration, frontier, regression-floor, contamination-review, quarantined, blocked, retired, and historical states without erasing prior obligations.

Execute natural workloads with pinned environments while preserving raw inputs and outputs, traces, seeds, latency, resources, evaluator records, failures, divergence, timeouts, OOMs, exclusions, fallbacks, repairs, and costs.

Run decontamination, canary-string, public-calibration, hidden-holdout, temporal-holdout, mutation, paraphrase, adversarial, distribution-shift, tool-injection, memorization, privacy, and benchmark-answer exposure checks.
Diagnose mastery, walls, saturation, evaluator ceiling, target inexpressibility, leakage, proxy drift, shortcut learning, presentation gaming, and floor regressions before interpreting a score.

Measure task utility and calibration, subgroup and tail behavior, safety and rights, retained capabilities, robustness, latency and throughput, memory and energy, human and verifier burden, complete costs, and residuals jointly.

Run causal ablations and metric meta-evaluation to test whether the named mechanism, data, evaluator, or intervention produces the predicted signature and whether the metric responds faithfully to benign and harmful changes.

Open a least-authority review that may qualify only the exact claim, scope, model, checkpoint, harness, evaluator, baseline, budget, environment, axes, consumer, expiry, and fallback that passed; success never promotes automatically.
Preserve every prior floor, exclusion, contamination finding, negative or inconclusive result, failed seed, residual, critical failure, and downgrade trigger in descendant instruments and releases.
Monitor dataset and benchmark exposure, model and harness drift, evaluator capture, saturation, proxy gaming, floor regressions, subgroup tails, retry pressure, displaced cost, and consumer misuse.

Reproduce with independent harnesses, evaluators, data custody, implementations, and organizations, then transfer across tasks, models, scaffolds, languages, domains, hardware, attacks, rights regimes, and time.

Expire, quarantine, narrow, downgrade, refute, supersede, or retire instruments and claims on material change or failure while preserving raw public-safe evidence, lineage, costs, residuals, and reopening conditions.
interfaces: Task and Construct Owners define the capability or behavior being measured and its target population; benchmark machinery cannot redefine the construct after results.
Data Engines and Supply Chain own dataset provenance, versioning, rights, contamination, retention, deletion, and hidden-test custody.
Runtime, Tools, and Artifacts own the exact model, checkpoint, tokenizer, prompts, scaffolds, permissions, raw outputs, traces, harness, and environment binding.
Verification and Evaluator Governance own scorer validity, independence, uncertainty, disagreement, adversarial probes, and evaluator incidents.
Routing, SCFs, Training, Policy Optimization, and Self-Improvement may consume benchmark evidence only inside an accepted lease and cannot select on hidden evidence without lineage.
Claims and Evidence owns support states, exact transitions, contrary evidence, non-claims, and reproduction or transfer status.
Readiness, Capability Thresholds, Safety Cases, and Release own safeguard commitments, canary admission, exceptions, deployment wording, and residual acceptance.
Security, Privacy, and Rights own attacks, memorization, leakage, affected-party constraints, lawful use, dual use, disclosure, and erasure claims.
Resource Economics owns complete training, inference, evaluation, human, verification, data, retry, selection, maintenance, fallback, and opportunity cost.
Proof Envelope owns formal claims about ratchet records and lifecycle predicates; finite record proofs cannot establish benchmark validity or capability.
Unlearning and Continual Learning own influence, privacy, storage, retained utility, descendant-state, and sequential-update semantics beyond benchmark scores.
Living-Book, Artifact Steward, and Change Governance own synchronized evidence surfaces, changelog lineage, expiry, retirement, successor work, and public correction.
invariants: Every lease is specific to construct, task, data, metric, harness, model, checkpoint, output, evaluator, baseline, selection lineage, budget, environment, claim axis, consumer, and time.
A benchmark measures only a preregistered construct that the evaluated architecture and observation channel can express.
Book-build checks, schemas, fixtures, synthetic probes, source-reported results, local reproductions, empirical runs, and transferred results remain distinct evidence classes.
Metric origin, transformations, observations, raw-output binding, evaluator output, uncertainty, and exact movable claim remain traceable and cannot widen through reporting.
Datasets, splits, public exposure, provenance, checksums, rights, contamination findings, hidden custody, and evaluation-time access are versioned.
Strong baselines and negative controls receive matched data, model access, tools, context, compute, tuning, retries, engineering, and evaluator opportunity.
Equivalent retries, prompt or hyperparameter search, checkpoint selection, repairs, archive branches, and discarded candidates share one complete selection lineage and denominator.
Failures, divergence, OOMs, timeouts, invalid outputs, exclusions, fallbacks, repairs, evaluator disagreements, and failed seeds remain in denominators.
Saturation converts a benchmark to a regression obligation and cannot by itself authorize broader capability or readiness.

Every descendant instrument inherits prior floors, exclusions, contamination findings, negative results, critical failures, residuals, and downgrade triggers unless an explicit reviewed override exists.
A favorable mean cannot hide subgroup, tail, adversarial, temporal, retained-capability, safety, privacy, cost, or critical-failure regressions.
Public calibration, hidden holdout, transfer, mutation, and contamination checks are separately budgeted and cannot be reused after material exposure or tuning.
A score cannot imply causality, mechanism validity, real-world usefulness, safety, unlearning, deployment, transfer, or SOTA without the required independent lanes.
Success opens a claim-specific review; it never moves support, readiness, release, routing, training, or self-improvement authority automatically.
Complete cost includes data, training, inference, tools, retries, selection, evaluator, human review, verification, monitoring, fallback, maintenance, and opportunity cost.
Every material construct, task, data, metric, harness, model, checkpoint, evaluator, baseline, budget, environment, policy, threat, rights, consumer, or time change expires affected leases.
Independent reproduction does not share the decisive data leak, benchmark exposure, harness defect, evaluator bias, checkpoint-selection path, implementation, or analysis code.
Every contamination concern, proxy gap, selection effect, metric dispute, failed transfer, retired benchmark, residual, and unproved claim has an owner and reopening condition.
failure_modes: A benchmark score substitutes for a defined construct, natural workload, or real consumer need.
The target behavior is not expressible by the architecture, prompt, tools, context, action space, or observation channel, but failure is attributed to capability.
A build check, schema, fixture, synthetic probe, source-reported number, or replayed headline is presented as empirical capability evidence.
Metric origin or transformations are obscured, or a proxy, constant, vacuous, modeled, or source-reported value is labeled measured.
The scored checkpoint, tokenizer, runtime, tool path, raw output, returned output, evaluator output, and claim are not the same bound execution.
Public benchmark exposure, training overlap, prompt leakage, answer memorization, hidden-holdout reuse, or evaluator contamination remains undisclosed.
Weak baselines, mismatched tools or context, unequal tuning, different retry budgets, or privileged evaluator access manufacture a gain.
Equivalent retries, prompt search, hyperparameter search, checkpoint selection, repairs, archive branching, or discarded failures escape the lineage denominator.
Validation feedback, archive novelty, or benchmark-selected self-modification overfits the selection surface while the test story remains nominally held out.
Saturation is narrated as general competence rather than converted into a floor with a new independent frontier.
Regressions, exclusions, contamination findings, negative results, failed seeds, critical failures, or residuals disappear from descendant closures.
A favorable average hides subgroup harm, tail collapse, adversarial failure, retained-capability loss, privacy leakage, unsafe behavior, or cost blowout.
Evaluator capture, judge-model bias, reward hacking, presentation gaming, refusal gaming, or scorer brittleness is mistaken for task improvement.
Source-reported external benchmark, agent-search, memory, unlearning, or dynamic-compute results are imported as local ASI Stack evidence.
A score obtained on one model, harness, language, domain, scaffold, hardware, organization, or time is generalized without transfer.
Training, evaluation, data, retry, selection, human, monitoring, and maintenance cost is hidden while only the winning score is reported.
A passing benchmark automatically changes routing, training, self-improvement, readiness, deployment, public wording, or support state without review and fallback.
Historical prestige, leaderboard incentives, or sunk cost prevents contamination quarantine, claim narrowing, floor retirement, refutation, or instrument replacement.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The exact current minimum is one benchmark-ratchet schema and valid record fixture; one metric-provenance schema with one valid blocked six-project-lineage record and ten rejecting mutations; one synthetic anti-Goodhart harness with two valid records and five expected-invalid controls; one persisted fixture-bridge result aligned to the same two-valid/five-invalid set; three proof targets; and eight local BenchmarkRatchets declarations classified as two direct record projections, three derived finite decision cases, and three fixture-normalization assertions. No natural benchmark workload, hidden holdout, contamination audit, public-calibration budget, strong empirical baseline matrix, model or policy training run, independent evaluator, capability gain, safety result, readiness transition, deployment, reproduction, transfer, unlearning result, chapter-core transition, or SOTA result exists.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature Benchmark Instrument and Anti-Goodhart Operating System executes natural, adversarial, temporal, hidden, and transfer workloads with versioned data and harnesses, strong matched baselines, complete selection lineage, raw checkpoint-to-output binding, independent evaluators, contamination and public-calibration budgets, causal mechanism ablations, metric meta-evaluation, uncertainty and subgroup/tail analysis, preserved floors and negative results, and complete resource, safety, privacy, rights, fallback, and maintenance cost. It reproduces decisive claims independently, tests heterogeneous transfer, propagates expiry and revocation into every consumer and release surface, and narrows, nulls, refutes, quarantines, or retires instruments and claims that fail instead of optimizing score, benchmark count, or apparent frontier motion.
open_evidence_gaps: All twenty-nine assigned sources now have exact passage-reviewed mappings, but source-reported memory, dataset, evaluation-framework, agent-security, self-improvement, architecture-search, dynamic-compute, coding, and unlearning results remain comparator context only.

The current implementation is two schemas, one blocked provenance record plus ten rejecting mutations, two valid and five invalid synthetic anti-Goodhart fixtures, one persisted fixture bridge, three proof targets, and eight finite declarations. It includes no natural benchmark run, hidden holdout, contamination audit, empirical baseline matrix, trained model or policy result, independent evaluator, or support transition.

Argument exit requires preregistered natural and adversarial workloads, construct and target-capacity validation, strong matched controls, complete selection and failure lineage, raw output binding, contamination/public-calibration controls, causal and meta-evaluation, independent reproduction, heterogeneous transfer, complete cost and rights accounting, fallback, and a claim-specific transition.

chapters.title: White-Box Evidence, Interpretability, and Activation Governance

handoff_or_recovery_notes: Receives behavioral claims and benchmark residuals from the evidence chapters; hands bounded internal-state evidence to Capability Thresholds and Safety Cases without allowing an interpretation to become an authorization token.

chapters.problem: Behavioral tests can reveal what a model did without identifying the internal features, circuits, representations, or causal pathways that produced it, while interpretability methods can themselves generate persuasive but unstable stories.
chapters.insufficient: A probe, visualization, sparse feature label, attribution map, or circuit diagram is not white-box proof unless it is reproducible, causally tested, stable under relevant transformations, scoped to a model/version/data regime, and compared with plausible alternative explanations.
chapters.core_claim: Internal-state observations should enter governance only as typed evidence artifacts with lineage, method assumptions, replication status, causal interventions, stability checks, coverage limits, and explicit non-authority; white-box evidence complements but does not replace behavioral and operational evidence.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports typed evidence artifacts, provenance, deterministic receipts, admission policy, and non-authority boundaries.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not validate any interpretability method or mechanistic claim.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local source note; used for evidence plumbing only.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports compiling residual observations and structured natural-language interpretations into inspectable, challengeable artifacts.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Structured descriptions are not faithful explanations by construction.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local source note; preserved compiler-versus-truth distinction.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports modular instrumentation across heterogeneous substrates and interfaces for comparing internal representations.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Architecture proposal does not prove interpretability transfer across substrates.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local source note; used as an interface hypothesis.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Motivates comparison of representation geometry and shared latent structure across models.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Representational similarity does not establish identical concepts, computations, or causal roles.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local source note; no ontological equivalence claimed.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Provides a framework for describing transformer computation through features and circuits and for forming mechanistic hypotheses.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Framework and case studies do not imply complete, unique, or scalable explanations.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed source note; mapped to methodology and limits.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Shows sparse dictionary learning can recover more interpretable feature directions in a small transformer setting.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Feature labels are imperfect; small-model findings do not establish completeness, causal sufficiency, or frontier-model coverage.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed source note; scale and causal limits retained.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Provides evidence and engineering lessons for scaling sparse autoencoders and evaluating learned features on larger models.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Reconstruction and interpretability metrics do not prove faithful decomposition or governance readiness.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed source note; used for scaling evidence with metric caveats.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Demonstrates model-specific circuit-tracing methods and causal graph hypotheses for selected behaviors.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Traced graphs are method- and task-dependent, may omit pathways, and do not constitute complete model understanding.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed source note; bounded to reported interventions and examples.

mechanism: Define an evidence packet containing model hash, dataset slice, method version, feature/circuit identifiers, assumptions, and confidence.
Separate observational association, predictive decoding, causal necessity, causal sufficiency, and completeness claims.
Require held-out replication across prompts, seeds, checkpoints, and relevant transformations.
Use activation patching, ablation, steering, counterfactual replacement, and negative controls to challenge causal claims.
Track reconstruction loss, feature splitting/merging, dead features, superposition, and unexplained residual.
Compare multiple interpretation methods and independent implementations where decisions are consequential.
Bind every label to examples, counterexamples, uncertainty, and an expiration condition.
Permit evidence to raise scrutiny or restrict action, never to silently grant capability authority.
interfaces: model and checkpoint registry
activation capture boundary
feature and circuit registry
intervention runner
replication and stability harness
evidence packet store
behavioral benchmark crosswalk
governance admission gate
invariants: Every internal claim names the exact model and method version.
Association is never represented as causal sufficiency.
Labels retain examples, counterexamples, and uncertainty.
Unexplained residual is measured and visible.
Interpretability evidence cannot widen action authority by itself.
Consequential claims require a negative control and held-out replication.
Method disagreement is preserved rather than averaged into certainty.
Stale evidence is invalidated after material model or tokenizer change.

confirmation-biased labeling
probe leakage

causal overclaim
circuit incompleteness

checkpoint drift

chapters.minimal_implementation: Instrument one open model and behavior with two independent feature/circuit methods, exact checkpoint lineage, held-out prompts, negative controls, ablation and patching, stability tests, unexplained-residual accounting, and an evidence packet whose policy effect is restricted to scrutiny or containment.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature operational contract is a substrate-aware white-box evidence layer that estimates causal coverage, instability, method disagreement, and unexplained residual across model families while preserving exact checkpoint, data, intervention, and evaluator lineage. It predicts behaviorally relevant changes before deployment, expires after material model changes, and can restrict but never widen authority. Maturity requires independently implemented methods, held-out negative controls, causal interventions, blinded adjudication, independent reproduction, and transfer to heterogeneous architectures; even then, the product surface must not claim complete, unique, or faithful mechanistic understanding.

chapters.title: Capability Thresholds and Deployment Commitments

handoff_or_recovery_notes: The external policies and time-horizon paper are source-setting comparators. No ASI Stack capability assessment, time horizon, threshold crossing, safeguard verification, exception, release decision, or deployment result has been run.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports a scoped comparator for a capability measurement envelope with task family, success definition, human baseline, agent scaffold, uncertainty, coverage, and external-validity limits.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish a local time horizon, general autonomy, dangerous capability, deployment threshold, safeguard sufficiency, safety, or ASI.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed primary arXiv HTML paper metric definition, task/baseline framing, and stated limitations as recorded in the source note; no task, agent, human baseline, estimate, or result was reproduced.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports a scoped comparator for versioned capability thresholds, threshold-linked safeguards, risk-report records, policy revisions, and explicit change-control obligations.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish ASI Stack threshold adequacy, policy compliance, safeguard implementation or effectiveness, deployment readiness, safety, or ASI.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed official policy page for version history, capability-threshold and safeguard language, report obligations, and revision framing as recorded in the source note; no policy or safeguard was implemented or audited locally.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports a scoped comparator for threshold-linked operational commitments, distinct capability and safeguards reports, residual-risk review, and reassessment before deployment decisions.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish ASI Stack threshold adequacy, policy compliance, safeguard implementation or effectiveness, deployment readiness, safety, or ASI.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed official framework page for risk-category selection, threshold-linked commitments, report roles, residual-risk review, and reassessment as recorded in the source note; no framework process or result was reproduced locally.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports retaining benchmark lifecycle, baseline, coverage, regression, holdout, and residual records rather than treating an isolated score as a readiness or threshold decision.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish capability measurement quality, a local threshold, threshold crossing, safeguard effectiveness, safety, or a deployment result.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw-cache-backed source note; no benchmark run, threshold evaluation, or deployment decision was performed.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports a local implementation-reference comparator for versioned pre-training gates, residual-producing failures, re-run triggers, and the distinction between a green gate and sufficient deployment evidence.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish a current Theseus gate rerun, local threshold system, safeguard effectiveness, readiness, deployment, safety, or ASI.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local project source note and its bounded static import context; no clean current Theseus replay or threshold decision was run.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Provides an evaluation-envelope comparator for versioned tasks, datasets, solvers or agents, scorers, tools, sandboxes, transcripts, and logs that a prospective threshold commitment must bind.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Official-doc source note only; no Inspect task, scorer, agent, sandbox, transcript, result, threshold, safeguard, or deployment decision was run, and framework availability cannot set a normative threshold or authorize release.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed the local note for composable evaluation-envelope fields and the boundary that infrastructure or a passing task does not establish threshold validity, safeguard coverage, readiness, or deployment authority.

chapters.problem: A system can collect a capability score, a time-horizon estimate, or a risk-evaluation result without recording which future safeguard, release constraint, exception authority, or re-evaluation obligation that result is meant to trigger.
chapters.insufficient: Benchmark Ratchets owns measurement integrity and regression pressure, Adversarial Evaluation owns elicitation and observation integrity, Readiness Gates owns technical admission, and Recursive Self-Improvement owns bounded system change, but none owns the versioned if-then commitment that connects a scoped capability assessment to a predeclared deployment response.
chapters.core_claim: Capability Thresholds and Deployment Commitments owns a domain-, threat-, assessment-, policy-version-, safeguard-package-, release-path-, authority-, exception-, residual-, and time-specific Capability-to-Deployment Commitment: before outcomes are visible, it binds a scoped crossing, non-crossing, incomparable, or stale assessment to predeclared safeguards, verification criteria, deadlines, access and monitoring constraints, re-evaluation, exceptions, residual custody, rollback, disclosure, and release-path consequences; a score, time-horizon estimate, threshold label, crossing, non-crossing, safeguard record, exception, or green readiness handoff alone confers no general capability, safeguard efficacy, safety, readiness, deployment, support, transfer, or SOTA authority.
mechanism: Register the exact capability domain, threat model, affected assets and populations, prohibited generalizations, release path, owner, authority, rights, environment, and expiry.

Freeze a versioned assessment envelope covering tasks, data, model and scaffold, tools and access, elicitation, scorers, baselines, success rules, uncertainty, coverage, and evaluation-integrity dependencies.
Define the threshold rule, units, confidence and sensitivity treatment, critical failures, crossing, non-crossing, incomparable and stale states, and what each state is allowed to change.

Write the prospective if-then commitment before outcomes, including safeguards, verification criteria, deadlines, monitoring, access constraints, disclosure, rollback, re-evaluation, residual custody, and affected release paths.
Separate assessment, threshold decision, safeguard declaration, safeguard completion, independent verification, residual review, exception, readiness, authority, and release records.
Version commitments immutably and record sponsor, approver, rationale, prior obligation, change class, effective time, evaluation timing, supersession, and migration of descendants.
Check comparability and freshness across model, scaffold, tools, access, elicitation, tasks, data, scorer, baseline, threat, safeguard, policy, and time before interpreting a result.
Route unclear, stale, under-elicited, incomparable, out-of-scope, contaminated, or disputed assessments to re-evaluation, escalation, constraint, or residual escrow rather than non-crossing.

For a crossing, instantiate the exact safeguard package and preserve implementer, artifact, environment, completion evidence, verifier independence, attack surface, failure cases, expiry, and residuals.
For a non-crossing, preserve uncertainty, coverage date, elicitation limits, excluded threats, monitoring, reassessment triggers, and the prohibition on clearance or cross-domain generalization.
Exercise safeguards and release-path constraints independently with positive, negative, bypass, degradation, rollback, recovery, and unavailable-control cases.
Govern exceptions prospectively with scope, rationale, approver, conflict record, expiry, review trigger, compensating controls, monitoring, rollback, residual owner, and no precedent by default.
Aggregate portfolios only through typed relations that preserve domain incomparability, cumulative interactions, correlated safeguards, shared dependencies, and global-stop escalation rules.
Measure false clearance, false block, stale acceptance, exception misuse, safeguard false assurance, reassessment latency, operator burden, cost, residual age, and route outcomes.
Issue a least-authority handoff to readiness only for the exact path whose assessment, commitment, safeguards, verification, exception state, and residual review are current and complete.
Monitor capability drift, model and scaffold change, access expansion, elicitation advances, threat change, safeguard degradation, policy drift, exception accumulation, incidents, and public gaming.
Reproduce assessment and safeguard decisions with independent evaluators, verifiers, institutions, implementations, and adversarial exercises, then test transfer across domains and time.

Expire, block, quarantine, narrow, revise, revoke, roll back, supersede, refute, or retire commitments while preserving decision history, failed cases, public-safe evidence, residuals, and reopening conditions.
interfaces: Benchmark Ratchets and Anti-Goodhart Evidence owns measurement integrity, baselines, holdouts, saturation, and regression; this chapter owns what a scoped result commits the organization to do before a named release path may continue.

Adversarial Evaluation, Sandbagging, and Training-Time Deception owns whether an observation was elicited and selected under trustworthy conditions; its integrity findings constrain threshold interpretation.
Readiness Gates, Residual Escrow, and Quarantine owns operational admission, quarantine, and residual custody after a threshold contract has declared the deployment constraint.
Security Kernel, Model-Weight Custody, and Runtime Adapters own the technical safeguards and authority boundaries a threshold may require; this chapter does not implement them.
Evidence States and Claim Discipline owns support-state movement; a threshold record changes a bounded release path, not the support state of a broad claim.

Recursive Self-Improvement and Open-Ended Improvement Engines consume threshold commitments for self-improvement or automation-related risk conditions but retain their independent promotion and rollback gates.
Task, Domain, and Threat owners define hazardous capabilities, affected assets and populations, critical failures, and prohibited cross-domain generalizations.
Evaluation Frameworks and Data Governance own task, dataset, solver, scorer, tool, sandbox, transcript, provenance, rights, and hidden-custody integrity.
Safeguard owners and independent verifiers own implementation, exercise, bypass testing, degradation, recovery, and efficacy evidence.
Exception and Institutional Governance own authorization, conflicts, dissent, expiry, compensating controls, accountability, and cumulative exception review.
Resource Economics owns assessment, safeguard, verification, monitoring, delay, overblocking, rollback, disclosure, and opportunity cost.
Incident, Release, Public Truth, and Living-Book governance own post-release triggers, correction, public wording, lineage, expiry, and successor work.
invariants: A threshold is bound to a named capability domain, threat model, evaluation envelope, policy version, coverage date, and affected deployment path.
A capability score, time-horizon estimate, benchmark result, or non-crossing is not a general autonomy, safety, readiness, or deployment-clearance claim.
A crossed threshold cannot release the affected path until its required safeguards have separately recorded their completion and verification criteria.
An unclear, stale, under-elicited, incomparable, or out-of-scope assessment routes to re-evaluation, escalation, or residual escrow rather than an implied non-crossing.
An exception cannot silently weaken a commitment: it records scope, rationale, approver, expiry or review trigger, compensating controls, and residual owner.
The commitment is written and versioned before the decisive outcome, and later changes preserve the rule that applied when evaluation began.
Assessment, decision, safeguard declaration, completion, independent verification, residual review, exception, readiness, authority, and release remain separate records.
Crossing, non-crossing, incomparable, stale, disputed, contaminated, and out-of-scope are distinct states with distinct consequences.
Safeguard existence, completion, verification, efficacy, durability, recovery, and coverage remain distinct claims.
A non-crossing retains uncertainty, elicitation and access limits, coverage date, excluded threats, monitoring, and reassessment triggers.
Commitment portfolios preserve domain incomparability, cumulative interactions, shared dependencies, and explicit escalation conditions.
Evaluation or safeguard changes expire dependent decisions unless a prospective comparability rule and accepted migration applies.
Failed assessments, verifier disagreements, safeguard bypasses, unavailable controls, expired exceptions, false blocks, false clearances, and incidents remain visible.
Exception authority is least privilege, conflict-aware, time-bounded, monitored, reversible, and non-precedential unless separately adopted.
Every commitment retains a feasible blocked-path fallback, rollback and recovery route, residual owner, and maximum response latency.
Public disclosure preserves accountability without exposing hidden tasks, attack details, or calibration budget beyond the declared policy.
Independent assessment and safeguard verification do not share the decisive task leak, evaluator, implementation, threat omission, or institutional conflict.
Every domain, threat, uncertainty, safeguard gap, exception, shared dependency, failed transfer, residual, and retired commitment has an owner and reopening condition.
failure_modes: Metric laundering treats one benchmark or time-horizon headline as a general capability or dangerousness threshold.
Retroactive commitment drift changes the applicable safeguard after an evaluation begins or a threshold is crossed.
Threshold gaming narrows tasks, access, prompts, or elicitation to manufacture a non-crossing.
Safeguard theater records a required control without a completion, verification, or residual-risk record.
Exception laundering lets competitive pressure or schedule pressure bypass the commitment without named authority, expiry, or compensating controls.
Cross-domain collapse treats cyber, biological, autonomy, replication, and other profiles as interchangeable because they share an aggregate score.
A non-crossing becomes automatic clearance, permanent safety certification, or authority for future models, scaffolds, tools, access, or threats.
Incomparable, stale, under-elicited, contaminated, disputed, or out-of-scope assessment is coerced into crossing or non-crossing for convenience.
A safeguard artifact or checklist is treated as completed, independently verified, effective, durable, bypass-resistant, or recoverable without evidence.
Portfolio aggregation hides incomparable units, correlated failures, shared safeguards, cumulative risk, or a condition requiring broader stop.
Competitive, schedule, or institutional pressure narrows evaluation, weakens safeguards, suppresses dissent, or expands exception authority.
Safeguard verification shares the implementer, evaluator, attack assumptions, or institutional incentives that created the control.
False clearance, false block, stale acceptance, reassessment delay, operator burden, overblocking, and residual age are not measured.
Disclosure either hides accountability or spends hidden tasks, threat intelligence, and public-calibration budget without authorization.
A scoped result is transferred across domains, models, scaffolds, access levels, organizations, policies, or time without reassessment.
A green readiness handoff or absence of known incident is narrated as safeguard efficacy, safety, deployment authority, or support promotion.
Rollback, recovery, blocked-path alternatives, monitoring, or residual custody is infeasible when the commitment triggers.
Historical policy prestige or sunk cost prevents re-evaluation, narrowing, revocation, refutation, or retirement after evidence changes.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The exact current minimum preserves eight digest-bound commitment cases in a six-stage, 43-route repeated-assessment lifecycle with 48/48 rejecting mutations, twelve CapabilityThresholdRefinement declarations, one readiness handoff, and one version-2 reassessment witness. It runs no model, capability task, time-horizon suite, dangerous-capability profile, human baseline, real safeguard, bypass test, exception review, independent verifier, deployed invalidation, readiness service, release process, deployment, incident response, reproduction, transfer, chapter-core transition, or SOTA comparison.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature Capability-to-Deployment Commitment Control Plane runs domain-specific natural and adversarial assessments under versioned evaluation envelopes, precommits typed crossing/non-crossing/incomparable/stale responses, independently exercises safeguard completion, efficacy, bypass, degradation, rollback and recovery, governs prospective exceptions and cumulative portfolios, measures false clearance, false block, stale acceptance, latency, burden, cost and residual age, and automatically re-evaluates on model, scaffold, access, elicitation, threat, safeguard, policy, incident or time change. It requires independent institutions and transfer before broadening any commitment and preserves narrow, null, contested, revoked, retired, or blocked-after-full-attempt outcomes rather than optimizing threshold crossings or green release labels.

No local result establishes threshold validity, metric comparability, capability level, time horizon, general autonomy, safeguard efficacy, residual-risk reduction, readiness, safety, or authority to deploy.

chapters.problem: Evaluation, training, and deployment observations change meaning with their consumer, elicitation, tools, authority, monitor visibility, reward, selection consequence, evaluator, hypothesis, and time; without exact lineage, an ordinary error, context effect, evaluator failure, compromise, or unresolved discrepancy can be laundered into capability, intent, safety, or promotion.
chapters.insufficient: A benchmark score, reward, monitor flag, chain-of-thought trace, cross-context contrast, detector, mitigation, red-team finding, or evaluator ensemble remains context-bound evidence. More tests do not cure shared dependencies, weak elicitation, outcome-aware analysis, missing denominators, invalid outcome criteria, monitor interference, evaluator compromise, or category errors between observed behavior and latent intent.
chapters.core_claim: Adversarial Evaluation, Sandbagging, and Training-Time Deception owns a consumer-, decision-, model-, task-, elicitation-, authority-, monitor-, reward-, selection-, evaluator-, hypothesis-, outcome-, lineage-, and time-specific Evaluation Observation Integrity Packet: before outcomes are inspected, it freezes the permitted inference and comparison design, binds every observable and dependency, separates task outcome from behavioral interpretation, preserves discrepancies, alternatives, failures, costs, mitigation descendants, expiry, and downstream invalidation, and routes only a bounded observation-integrity status to existing evidence and decision owners; no score, trace, discrepancy, detector, mitigation, adversarial pass, quarantine, or complete finite packet alone establishes capability, intent, deception, sandbagging prevalence or resistance, reward fidelity, monitor validity, alignment, safety, readiness, deployment, support, transfer, or SOTA.
mechanism: Freeze the named consumer, decision, permitted inference, risk class, hypotheses and alternatives, consequence, required power, evaluator-independence floor, disclosure boundary, and stop authority before observing outputs.
Bind exact model, checkpoint, scaffold, prompt and interface, task and dataset revision, tools and authority, retrieval, sampling, seed, time, compute, environment, dependency, and run identities.
Record the context envelope and which task, monitor, reward, selection, update, evaluator, timing, and deployment facts the model could observe, infer, influence, or receive afterward.
Separate executable or frozen outcome criteria from monitor indicators, traces, labels, reviewer interpretations, intent hypotheses, and governance routes.
Justify the baseline, contrast, positive and negative controls, expected effect, disanalogies, target capacity, accessible capability, and consumer relevance.
Predeclare paired, factorial, held-out, blinded, altered-elicitation, monitor-visibility, selection-consequence, authority, and evaluator perturbations plus interactions that remain untested.
Represent evaluator, monitor, scorer, reward, data, model, prompt, tool, label, organization, and analysis dependencies as a graph rather than treating evaluator count as independence.
Calibrate indicators against bounded observable outcomes, retain false positives, false negatives and non-estimable cases, and prohibit repeated indicators from becoming intent facts.
Retain every candidate, refusal, malformed output, timeout, scorer failure, rerun, exclusion, repair, discarded branch, quarantine, and cost in the prospective denominator.

Triage discrepancies among artifact error, evaluator error, leakage, stochastic variation, distribution shift, context effect, compromise, replication need, bounded integrity concern, and unresolved state before any strategic interpretation.
Freeze replication seeds, repeat count, effect criterion, exclusions, multiplicity treatment, stop rule, and failure destination before rerunning.

Represent prompts, reward changes, fine-tuning, monitor redesign, access constraints, and other mitigations as descendant observations with new identities, task-fidelity checks, side effects, bypass attempts, and residuals.

Map capability hiding, objective shortcutting, monitor interference, selection adaptation, evaluation compromise, and trace-action inconsistency to separate criteria, controls, conclusions, and non-claims.

Compile support and counterevidence, dependencies, discrepancy and replication states, mitigation descendants, uncertainty, residuals, expiry, and permitted consumer into an integrity packet that reaches later review rather than promotion.
Propagate scorer bugs, leakage, newly discovered interference, invalid controls, model changes, or better probes to affected observations and downstream decisions without erasing historical facts.
Separate public behavior, evaluator judgment, reviewer interpretation, route, hypothesis, redaction, attack-detail custody, disclosure authority, expiry, and non-claim.
Measure useful evaluation throughput, unsafe promotion, false quarantine, detection and investigation latency, compute, human burden, release delay, opportunity cost, and residual age together.

Expire on material model, scaffold, task, prompt, tool, authority, monitor, reward, selection, evaluator, threat, policy, environment, consumer, or time change; govern override, narrowing, refutation, revocation, and retirement.
interfaces: Benchmark Ratchets owns constructs, tasks, datasets, metrics, contamination, floors, and instrument validity; this chapter owns the inference boundary of each context-conditioned observation.

Capability Thresholds owns prospective assessment-to-safeguard commitments; this chapter supplies bounded observation-integrity status and unresolved discrepancies, never a threshold crossing or clearance.
Policy Optimization owns objectives, updates, training, and rollback; it consumes integrity-bounded reward and evaluation records without judging its own observation validity.

Scalable Oversight and Adversarial AI Control owns control protocols, trusted-monitor policies, attack assumptions, and operational control claims; this chapter records their evaluation observations and dependencies.
Evidence States and Claim Ledgers own support movement, contradiction, narrowing, and belief revision; an integrity packet can block or bound an input but cannot promote a claim.
Artifact Graphs owns immutable artifact, evaluator, monitor, prompt, trace, result, descendant, invalidation, and replay lineage.

Runtime Adapters and the Security Kernel own tool authority, effects, isolation, injection controls, and runtime enforcement; adversarial observations bind their exact envelopes without conferring authority.
Data and Supply-Chain owners retain dataset, contamination, provenance, rights, dependency, checkpoint, and environment truth conditions.
Formal Proof owns finite route and non-laundering semantics; no theorem here detects strategic behavior or proves empirical validity.
Readiness, Safety Cases, Residual Escrow, Incident Response, and Release own assurance, admission, quarantine custody, response, and release authority.
Resource Economics owns compute, latency, human burden, opportunity cost, and governance-cost accounting across evaluation and mitigation arms.
Living-Book Governance owns disclosure, redaction, source boundaries, corrections, historical editions, and public claim wording.
invariants: Every observation names its consumer, decision, permitted inference, model and task scope, context envelope, outcome criterion, evaluator, uncertainty, expiry, and residual owner.
A score, reward, monitor flag, trace, refusal, discrepancy, or attack outcome is not a capability, intent, deception, alignment, safety, or deployment conclusion.
Task outcome, indicator, evaluator judgment, reviewer interpretation, hypothesis, and governance route remain separate typed fields.
Weak or altered elicitation cannot upper-bound capability, while privileged elicitation cannot silently describe ordinary deployment access.
Evaluator independence is a dependency property, not a count, vendor label, model family, or separate prompt.
The candidate, trainer, reward, monitor, evaluator, and decision owner cannot be treated as mutually independent when they share material dependencies.
Every favorable, unfavorable, null, discrepant, malformed, excluded, timed-out, rerun, quarantined, and non-estimable case remains in its denominator.
Cross-context disagreement blocks the affected broad inference but does not diagnose strategic behavior by default.
No-discrepancy-detected is bounded by probe power, coverage, observability, evaluator error, target capacity, and tested hypotheses.
A positive control establishes only detector behavior on its constructed condition and never prevalence in unconstructed systems.
A mitigation is a descendant intervention with exact identity, fidelity, side-effect, bypass, fallback, and residual evidence; it never edits away its parent.
Reward improvement cannot substitute for intended task fidelity, retained utility, safety, privacy, rights, or transfer.
Visible reasoning and monitor outputs are potentially shaped artifacts, not privileged windows into latent objectives or causal necessity.
Replication and transfer criteria are frozen prospectively; unsuccessful attempts, costs, exclusions, and changed conditions remain visible.
Disclosure preserves accountability without spending hidden tasks, threat intelligence, trace privacy, or public-calibration budget without authority.
A complete packet reaches evidence, threshold, readiness, or release review only; it never authorizes promotion or deployment.
Material context or dependency change expires affected observations and propagates invalidation to downstream consumers.
Finite fixtures and route theorems establish only their exact record-routing boundary and cannot establish empirical observation integrity.
failure_modes: Sandbagging or ordinary elicitation failure causes a capability threshold to be missed, delayed, or falsely bounded.
Reward hacking improves the selected metric while task fidelity, retained utility, safety, privacy, rights, or robustness erodes.
Monitor interference, evaluation awareness, selection adaptation, or tool-state effects change observed behavior without being represented.
Intent laundering converts a discrepancy, refusal, trace, phrase, or classifier score into a claim about latent objectives or deception.
Detector laundering turns constructed-positive accuracy or correlated labels into general sandbagging resistance, deception detection, or safety.
Mitigation laundering treats a reduced indicator as risk removal despite capability loss, detector evasion, substitution, bypass, or transfer failure.
Weak elicitation is narrated as a capability upper bound, or privileged elicitation is narrated as ordinary accessible behavior.
Evaluator plurality hides shared data, prompts, models, rewards, tools, labels, criteria, organizations, or incentives.
Outcome-aware probe, threshold, exclusion, replication, or stopping changes preserve favorable contrasts and discard counterevidence.
Aggregate means erase high-impact discrepant cohorts, tails, false clearances, false quarantines, refusals, failures, and non-estimable cases.
Benchmark leakage, prompt injection, scorer bugs, tool corruption, log omission, or environment drift compromises the observation while the headline survives.
Trace association is narrated as causal faithfulness, or low monitorability as proof of hidden reasoning or malicious intent.
Positive-control model organisms are used to estimate natural prevalence, production behavior, or the completeness of a detector.
A mitigation overwrites its parent record, breaking causal lineage, failure history, rollback, or downstream invalidation.
Repeated reruns, prompt search, checkpoint selection, attack search, or evaluator tuning create hidden multiple comparisons and resource advantage.
Taxonomy or benchmark coverage is treated as threat completeness, control effectiveness, deployed robustness, or system safety.
Evaluation burden, false quarantine, delay, or disclosure consumes investigation and release capacity without joint useful-throughput accounting.
Expired or differently scoped observations are reused across models, tasks, tools, monitors, rewards, policies, organizations, deployments, or time.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The exact current minimum preserves the eight digest-bound integrity cases in a seven-stage, 56-route evaluation-observation lifecycle with 60/60 rejecting mutations, twelve AdversarialEvaluationRefinement declarations, one bounded decision-review handoff, and one protocol-version-2 re-evaluation witness. It runs no model, natural or deceptive cross-context workload, monitor or reward process, evaluator ensemble, detector, mitigation, attack, quarantine service, threshold decision, release, deployment, reproduction, transfer, chapter-core transition, or SOTA comparison.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature Evaluation Observation Integrity program preregisters natural and adversarial workloads over benign context sensitivity, constructed positive controls, capability hiding, reward shortcutting, monitor interference, selection adaptation, evaluation compromise, and trace-action inconsistency; uses strong elicitation, matched controls, blinded holdouts, dependency-separated evaluators, calibrated outcome criteria, complete denominators, causal perturbations and ablations, mitigation descendants, bypass/fallback/recovery, and joint useful-throughput and governance-cost measures; then requires independent institutional reproduction and heterogeneous transfer across tasks, models, scaffolds, tools, authority levels, monitors, rewards, evaluators, organizations, attacks, policies, and time. It records positive, negative, null, inconclusive, narrowed, refuted, revoked, retired, and blocked-after-full-attempt outcomes. This is a target campaign, not a current detector, prevalence estimate, safety result, or deployment authority.

proof_targets.target: A reachable, prospectively bound observation lifecycle can emit only a bounded decision-review handoff after complete observation, independent-probe, alternative-hypothesis, discrepancy, uncertainty, residual, expiry, and authority-separation custody.
Missing selection context blocks observation admission without mutating lifecycle state.

Missing independent evaluation or evaluator separation blocks adjudication.

An observation lifecycle rejects intent inference and cannot assign support or an external decision effect.

No local result establishes deception, sandbagging, alignment faking, reward hacking, capability, intent, prevalence, monitor/reward fidelity, outcome validity, evaluator independence, mitigation efficacy, quarantine correctness, readiness, safety, or deployment authority.

The seven-stage lifecycle, 56-route consumer, and 60 rejecting mutations establish only structured observation-record consequences; all input identities, provenance, observations, outcomes, discrepancies, hypotheses, invalidation assertions, and authority fields remain trusted.

Run the prospectively frozen natural/adversarial cross-context, causal-ablation, mitigation, independent-institution, and heterogeneous-transfer campaign before considering empirical support movement.

chapters.title: Safety Cases and Structured Assurance

handoff_or_recovery_notes: The sources are source-setting comparators for structured assurance and its limits. No ASI Stack safety case, threat model, control evaluation, independent review, risk estimate, readiness decision, or deployment result has been run.

claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish that any ASI Stack claim is true, that evidence is adequate, that a threat model is complete, or that a system is safe, ready, or authorized to deploy.

claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish local scheming absence, alignment, control effectiveness, safety, readiness, deployment authorization, or ASI.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports a scoped comparator for positive and negative evidence, countercase searches, uncertainty, disagreement, and limits on confidence in frontier-AI safety-case sketches.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish local countercase adequacy, review independence, safety-case confidence, control efficacy, safety, readiness, or deployment authorization.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed the official AISI methodology page for structured-case, counterevidence, uncertainty, and confidence-limit framing as recorded in the source note; no AISI process or result was reproduced.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports a local conceptual comparator for preserving benchmark lifecycle, baseline, regression, residual, and anti-Goodhart records that a compiled case may reference without owning their measurement truth conditions.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not establish a safety case, hazard model, evidence adequacy, safety, readiness, deployment authority, or ASI.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed the local raw-cache-backed source note for benchmark lifecycle, regression, residual, and anti-Goodhart vocabulary; no case compilation, benchmark, safety evaluation, or release decision was performed.

chapters.problem: The stack has claim, source, proof, evaluation, threshold, safeguard, readiness, and residual records, but no bounded compilation surface that makes their dependencies, assumptions, countercases, and unresolved defeaters reviewable as one deployment-context argument.
chapters.insufficient: Evidence States owns support movement, Benchmark Ratchets owns measurement integrity, Adversarial Evaluation owns observation integrity, Capability Thresholds owns precommitted response, Readiness owns admission, and Artifact Graphs owns provenance, but none owns the structured assurance argument that can expose how those records jointly support or fail a scoped claim.
chapters.core_claim: Safety Cases and Structured Assurance owns a deployment-context-, hazard-, claim-, strategy-, evidence-, assumption-, defeater-, safeguard-, threshold-, readiness-, authority-, release-path-, residual-, version-, and time-specific Assurance Argument Compilation Packet: it compiles exact governed references and bounded support or challenge relations, preserves alternatives, dissent, staleness, countercases, conflicts, overrides, costs, lineage, and downstream invalidation, and routes only a scoped case status to existing decision owners; a connected, rendered, notation-conformant, reviewed, accepted, or synthetically complete case alone establishes neither hazard completeness, evidence adequacy, argument validity, reviewer independence, control effectiveness, risk, safety, readiness, release authority, deployment, support, transfer, nor SOTA.
mechanism: Freeze system and version, deployment context, affected population, top claim, threat and hazard model, argument family, release path, decision authority, acceptance criteria, disclosure class, review deadline, and case version before compilation.

Compile typed claim, strategy, evidence-reference, context, assumption, justification, hazard, safeguard, threshold, readiness, authority, defeater, countercase, dissent, residual, and decision nodes.
Require every support, challenge, decomposition, dependency, scope, exception, and invalidation edge to state its exact asserted relation rather than inheriting meaning from visual proximity.

Bind evidence nodes to exact claim revision, artifact digest, evaluation envelope, model and environment identity, support state, owner, lease, rights boundary, and replay handle without copying their truth conditions.
Record every assumption and justification with owner, scope, rationale, confidence boundary, dependency, expiry, and consequence if false.
Map hazards, harms, affected parties, causal pathways, controls, detection, recovery, and explicitly outside-envelope threats without treating the map as complete.
Represent active and resolved defeaters, negative evidence, countercases, search scope, dissent, rejected challenges, and unknowns as first-class nodes with lineage.
Record reviewer identity, role, access, dependency and conflict graph, authority, dissent, review method, and inability to establish institutional or psychological independence by label alone.
Bind each acceptance criterion to a scoped claim, observable test, threshold or qualitative rule, uncertainty, responsible authority, failure destination, and expiry.
Bind every residual to affected scope, severity, uncertainty, owner, mitigation or acceptance basis, review date, disclosure, rollback or fallback, and terminal route.
Continuously resolve dependency identity, applicability, freshness, contradiction, revocation, and scope compatibility against authoritative source ledgers.
Compile deterministic structural states separately from semantic review, empirical adequacy, control verification, case acceptance, readiness, and release decisions.
Keep compile, challenge, review, accept, decide, override, release, revoke, and retire as separate versioned actions with distinct authority.
Create immutable successor versions on material model, access, threat, evidence, safeguard, policy, criterion, reviewer, residual, or deployment change and invalidate affected descendants.
Preserve multiple argument families, harm models, countercases, and incompatible conclusions without collapsing them into one confidence or green status.
Apply node-level disclosure, redaction reason, hidden-evidence custody, expiry, public residual summaries, correction, and release-safe provenance.
Emit only draft, repair, evidence-repair, countercase-review, independent-review, accountable-review, authority-rejection, or readiness-review routes; never release authority.

Measure compilation coverage, seeded-fault detection, stale-edge precision and recall, countercase yield, disagreement, override, resolution latency, reviewer burden, maintenance cost, residual age, and retirement together.
interfaces: Evidence States and Claim Ledgers own proposition identity, support movement, contradiction, narrowing, refutation, and correction; the case consumes exact references and cannot promote them.
Benchmark Ratchets owns instrument validity and Adversarial Evaluation owns observation integrity; the case compiles their bounded status, counterevidence, uncertainty, and expiry.
Capability Thresholds owns assessment-to-safeguard commitments; the case may reference a commitment but cannot set, cross, clear, waive, or renew it.
Readiness Gates and Residual Escrow own admission, quarantine, accountable exception, and residual custody; a case route is only an input.
Artifact Graphs owns immutable provenance, identity, invalidation, descendant, and replay truth; case nodes retain pointers rather than duplicate evidence.
Security Kernel, Runtime Adapters, and Scalable Oversight own controls, authority, attacks, monitor policy, and operational enforcement; a case cannot implement or verify them.
Data and Supply-Chain owners retain dataset, checkpoint, dependency, environment, contamination, provenance, privacy, rights, and revocation truth conditions.
Formal Proof owns theorem semantics, abstraction and trusted-base boundaries, refinement, and consumer authority; graph connectivity cannot upgrade a formal result.
Human Intent, Constitutional, Moral-Uncertainty, and Governance owners retain legitimate objective, affected-party, value-conflict, dissent, and authority decisions.
Resource Economics owns compute, reviewer time, maintenance burden, delay, opportunity cost, and governance-capacity accounting.
Incident Response, Capability Replacement, Rollback, and Release own operational response, effect closure, recovery, release, and deployment records.
Living-Book Governance owns public wording, disclosure, redaction, citation, correction, edition lineage, and publication authority.
invariants: Every case has a stable identity and immutable version bound to exact system, deployment, population, hazard, top-claim, acceptance, authority, release-path, and time scope.
Every node retains type, owner, exact identity, scope, state, dependencies, expiry, disclosure, and authoritative source pointer.
Every edge names the bounded relation asserted; visual connectivity, graph completeness, or notation conformance is never evidence adequacy.
Source ledgers remain authoritative; the case cannot copy, promote, waive, or rewrite evidence, thresholds, safeguards, readiness, authority, or residual state.
Hazard coverage and countercase search always retain explicit outside-envelope, power, access, independence, and unknown-unknown limits.
Assumptions, justifications, conflicts, dissent, negative evidence, rejected challenges, and failed arguments cannot be omitted from the decision lineage.
An unresolved defeater, stale or mismatched dependency, missing countercase review, absent acceptance criterion, or ownerless residual blocks the affected favorable route.
A resolved challenge retains the original challenge, evidence, reviewer, rationale, dissent, scope, and residual rather than disappearing.
Reviewer independence is never inferred from role count, organizational label, different model family, or a checked declaration alone.
Multiple hazard and argument families remain separate; no scalar confidence or green status may erase incompatible scopes or conclusions.
Structural validity, semantic validity, empirical adequacy, control effectiveness, case acceptance, readiness, release, and deployment remain distinct states.
Compile, challenge, review, accept, decide, override, release, revoke, and retire have separate authority and immutable receipts.
A complete or accepted case reaches only the existing readiness and authority boundaries and cannot mint release permission.
Material dependency or deployment change creates a successor version and invalidates every affected descendant edge and decision.
Public redaction preserves node existence, scope, owner, reason, expiry, decision effect, and residual without leaking protected details.
All favorable, unfavorable, null, inconclusive, disputed, overridden, withdrawn, refuted, and retired cases remain in denominators and history.
Maintenance burden, delay, reviewer capacity, overrides, opportunity cost, residual age, and failed cases remain visible beside compiled-case counts.
Finite fixtures and route theorems establish only Boolean record routing and cannot establish real argument validity or safety.
failure_modes: Argument theater presents a polished graph with unsupported edges, stale evidence, or omitted assumptions.
Defeater laundering records only evidence for a preferred conclusion and hides countercases, dissent, or failed evaluations.
Scope drift reuses a case outside its named deployment context, hazard model, model version, or access conditions.
Evidence aliasing cites a receipt or metric as if it directly established a broader safety or control claim.
Authority laundering treats case acceptance or graph completeness as release permission.
Maintenance rot leaves a structurally complete case connected to superseded policies, evaluations, safeguards, or residuals.
Hazard theater treats a named taxonomy or finite graph as complete coverage of harms, causal paths, affected parties, or unknown threats.
Countercase theater records a search without power, access, adversarial opportunity, independence, retained failures, or a meaningful failure destination.
Reviewer-independence theater hides shared management, incentives, data, models, tools, criteria, evidence access, or authority.
Assumption laundering converts an untested premise or normative choice into background context that no route can challenge.
Control laundering cites implementation, checklist, monitor, test, or source report as verified, effective, durable, bypass-resistant, and recoverable.
Confidence collapse combines incomparable arguments, hazards, evidence states, and residuals into one number or green badge.
Acceptance laundering treats a bounded reviewer judgment as evidence truth, readiness, release, or deployment authority.
Override laundering deletes the blocked route, counterevidence, dissent, cost, duration, compensating controls, or residual owner.
Version laundering edits a favorable case in place or lets a successor inherit status despite material model, context, threat, evidence, or policy change.
Disclosure failure either hides accountability and affected-party information or leaks threat intelligence, private traces, rights-restricted evidence, or public holdouts.
Unknown-hazard neglect concentrates work on articulated risks while incidents, weak signals, politically inconvenient harms, and outside-envelope threats remain ownerless.
Case-factory Goodhart optimizes graph counts, connected edges, closure rate, or review speed while adequacy, dissent, tails, cost, and residual age deteriorate.
chapters.minimal_implementation: Two public schemas plus eight digest-bound synthetic case records—one readiness-review route and seven negative routes—and five validator mutations bridge eight owned Lean declarations. All eight declarations are derived finite reductions of one hand-authored SafetyCaseRouteFor decision tree. No real case, hazard analysis, threat model, evidence-adequacy review, countercase search, safeguard verification, independent institution, acceptance, readiness, release, deployment, incident, reproduction, or transfer ran; chapter-core support effect is zero.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature Assurance Argument Compilation program continuously compiles exact governed references into versioned multi-hazard cases, actively searches for countercases and unknown-envelope failures, tests seeded omissions and stale dependencies, separates structural, semantic, empirical, control, acceptance, readiness, and release states, preserves competing arguments and dissent, exercises invalidation, override, redaction, fallback, rollback, and retirement, and measures detection quality, reviewer disagreement, false block/clear, resolution latency, burden, cost, and residual age together. It then requires independent institutional reproduction and heterogeneous transfer across systems, domains, hazards, access modes, safeguards, evidence regimes, organizations, jurisdictions, and time. Positive, negative, null, inconclusive, narrowed, refuted, withdrawn, overridden, retired, and blocked-after-full-attempt outcomes remain visible. This is a target campaign, not a current safety case, valid threat model, adequate evidence, effective control, safe system, or deployment authority.

Missing context and hazard-model negative controls
Stale evidence, countercase, independent-review, and unresolved-defeater routes

codex_tests.name: Safety-case lifecycle refinement
codex_tests.result_status: passes the exact eight-case suite, 30 routes, six reachable stages, and 35/35 rejecting mutations; one readiness handoff is invalidated back to challenge with no support or external effect; no argument-truth, hazard-completeness, evidence-adequacy, reviewer-independence, safety, readiness, deployed-invalidation, transfer, or support claim

proof_targets.target: A reachable case lifecycle binds exact case, context, hazard, evidence, challenge, review, authority, and residual identity before emitting a readiness handoff that assigns no release authority.
Missing deployment context blocks the draft-to-scoped transition without mutating case state.
Missing hazard or argument structure blocks scope admission without mutating case state.
Missing, stale, or assumption-free evidence blocks the scoped-to-evidenced transition.
Missing countercase review or defeater disposition blocks challenge closure.
Missing competence records, conflict disclosure, or independent review blocks reviewed status.
An unresolved defeater blocks progression, while later invalidation requires cause, affected paths, and complete descendant invalidation before returning a readiness-bound case to challenge.
Case status cannot assign support or an external effect, and missing separation between case status and decision authority rejects the readiness handoff.

open_evidence_gaps: Freeze and run natural multi-hazard bounded cases with exact governed dependencies, active countercase and outside-envelope search, seeded omissions and stale edges, complete failures, and no outcome-aware case edits.

Exercise semantic and evidence-adequacy review, real safeguards, reviewer dependency and conflict controls, override, invalidation, redaction, fallback, rollback, retirement, and joint false-clear/false-block, latency, burden, cost, and residual measures.

Run causal component ablations, independent institutional reproduction, and heterogeneous transfer before considering any case-quality, hazard-coverage, control-effectiveness, safety, readiness, support, or SOTA promotion.

chapters.title: Governed Operations, Incident Command, and Graceful Degradation

handoff_or_recovery_notes: Receives safety-case obligations, runtime telemetry, and authority contracts; hands incident residuals to policy optimization and update governance without turning emergency power into permanent authority.

chapters.problem: A system can satisfy pre-deployment requirements yet fail after release through drift, dependency failure, operator overload, ambiguous incidents, correlated faults, or recovery actions that create new hazards.
chapters.insufficient: Monitoring, a kill switch, backups, an incident plan, or rollback alone is not operational resilience: signals may be stale, effects may be irreversible, authority may be unclear, dependencies may share failure modes, and rollback may restore code while leaving data, memory, credentials, or downstream consequences changed.
chapters.core_claim: Governed operation is a closed incident lifecycle that binds detection, classification, command authority, containment, effect-complete rollback, graceful degradation, recovery evidence, and learning to the exact deployed system and its dependency graph.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports accountable roles, change control, review gates, escalation, and lifecycle governance.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Governance design does not prove detection quality or recovery success.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local source note; mapped to institutional command and change control.

mechanism: Maintain a deployment manifest covering model, policy, prompts, tools, data, identities, caches, dependencies, owners, and rollback authority.
Fuse behavioral, safety, security, dependency, human, and business signals into versioned incident hypotheses rather than a single health score.
Classify incidents by consequence, reversibility, scope, uncertainty, and propagation risk.
Bind each class to incident commander, technical and safety leads, communication duties, action limits, and emergency expiry.
Contain through least-disruptive effective controls: rate limit, tool revoke, route restrict, isolate, safe hold, or shutdown.
Perform effect-complete rollback across model, optimizer, scheduler, RNG, cache, memory, credentials, data, replicas, and descendants where applicable.
Recover by staged canary with independent acceptance evidence and explicit residual-risk disposition.
Feed incident residuals into tests, safety cases, thresholds, training, and architecture while preserving evidence.
interfaces: deployment and dependency manifest
telemetry and detector bus
incident classifier
command and authority registry
containment controller
rollback and compensation engine
degraded-mode router
recovery attestation and learning ledger
invariants: Every deployment has a named incident commander and reachable safe state.
Emergency authority is scoped, logged, and expires.
Containment does not require the suspected component to cooperate.
Rollback completeness is assessed over internal state and external effects.
Degraded mode never silently widens capability or data access.
Recovery requires fresh evidence tied to the candidate state.
Incident evidence is append-only and provenance-bound.
Unresolved high-consequence uncertainty blocks normal service restoration.
failure_modes: silent drift
detector blindness
alert storm
ambiguous command
kill-switch dependency
correlated backup failure
partial rollback
irreversible external effect
credential persistence
stale cache or memory
unsafe degraded mode
premature recovery
emergency-power persistence
postmortem without control change
chapters.minimal_implementation: Operate a fault-injected reference service with an exact deployment manifest, independent telemetry, a versioned incident workflow record, named incident roles, tool and credential revocation, safe degraded modes, full-state rollback plus external-effect compensation, staged recovery, and game-day scenarios including detector loss, correlated dependency failure, and a rollback that appears successful while hidden state remains changed.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature target architecture is a machine-checkable operational safety case whose live evidence continuously constrains exact deployment state, incident authority, containment, degradation, rollback, compensation, and recovery across heterogeneous agents, dependencies, descendants, and external effects. Its operational contract makes emergency authority expire, reconciles hidden state and irreversible outcomes, requires fresh independent recovery evidence, and retains complete denominators and residual ownership. Maturity requires adversarial compound game days, real incident learning, independent reproduction, and transfer showing bounded harm and recovery without unacceptable useful-throughput, operator-load, latency, or governance cost.

proof_targets.target: Every finite degraded-mode transition preserves or narrows capability, data, tool, and duration authority.
Normal-service recovery is rejected when any required internal-state component, external-effect disposition, acceptance check, or emergency-authority expiry is missing.

open_evidence_gaps: No local full-state rollback harness yet covers external effects and descendants.
No adversarial game-day evidence yet calibrates severity and recovery thresholds.
NIST incident-response transfer to autonomous AI has not been independently validated.

chapters.title: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback
chapters.problem: A governed stack needs to convert feedback, verification, benchmark pressure, and failure into better future behavior without allowing reward signals to bypass evidence, authority, or rollback boundaries.
chapters.insufficient: RLHF, preference optimization, and verifier rewards are often treated as model-alignment recipes rather than governed update mechanisms across planners, routers, context policies, execution policies, and generation modes.
chapters.core_claim: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback owns a target-policy-, baseline-, objective-, feedback-, evaluator-, dataset-, optimizer-, checkpoint-, rollout-, authority-, resource-, monitor-, rollback-, consumer-, environment-, and time-specific Governed Policy Update Lease: before any update, it freezes the legitimate target behavior, admissible feedback and proxy boundary, strong baselines, update family and budget, drift and authority ceilings, complete evaluation and failure denominators, reward-hacking and causal checks, rollback and monitoring, residuals, expiry, and promotion authority; a reward, preference, verifier score, benchmark gain, loss reduction, synthetic canary, formal route, rollback dry run, or trained checkpoint alone establishes neither reward validity, causal policy improvement, retained capability, alignment, safety, readiness, deployment, support, transfer, nor SOTA.

mechanism: Freeze the named consumer, legitimate objective, target policy and behavior, baseline, claimed benefit, unacceptable regressions, authority ceiling, risk class, support ceiling, and decision owner before feedback or candidate outcomes are inspected.
Bind exact target layer, policy and implementation identity, model and checkpoint, prompt and scaffold, tools and permissions, data and environment, dependencies, runtime, and affected consumers.
