Separate the proxy, preference, reward model, judge, process signal, and final target property; specify when the proxy is informative, gameable, distribution-bound, conflicting, or ineligible.

Choose SFT, trust-region or proximal RL, critic-free or group-relative RL, offline preference optimization, RLVR, process reward, contextual bandit, controller search, or deterministic policy change only after target and feedback fit are declared.

Freeze optimizer, loss, hyperparameters, seeds, sampling, rollout policy, reference or baseline policy, clipping or trust region, regularization, budget, stopping, checkpoint selection, and repair rules.

Bind training, validation, hidden holdout, contamination, temporal, adversarial, transfer, regression, tail, subgroup, refusal, abstention, and malformed-output evaluation sets before selection.
Probe reward hacking, evaluator gaming, sycophancy, shortcut learning, length or style bias, monitor interference, reward tampering, authority expansion, and proxy-target divergence.
Retain every rollout, candidate, seed, checkpoint, retry, repair, exclusion, timeout, crash, failed baseline, rejected update, unsafe artifact, and compute or human cost in complete denominators.

Use matched component ablations, feedback substitutions, reward perturbations, target evaluations, causal mediation tests, and counterfactual rollbacks to distinguish update effect from data, selection, extra compute, or evaluator effects.
Emit a policy-delta receipt binding old and new behavior, feedback, training lineage, evaluation, authority effect, costs, failures, residuals, monitor window, promotion decision, and non-claims.
Keep experiment, shadow, canary, qualified, default, quarantined, rolled-back, deprecated, refuted, and retired states distinct with prerequisites, owners, receipts, and failure destinations.
Exercise effect-complete rollback over policy, checkpoint, optimizer, scheduler, RNG, cache, routes, credentials, downstream state, descendants, monitors, and external reliance before stronger use.

Monitor natural outcomes, drift, distribution shift, reward-model change, new exploits, regressions, incidents, residual age, user impact, and policy/evaluator feedback loops after any bounded promotion.
Expire and re-evaluate on material objective, feedback, evaluator, data, model, scaffold, tool, authority, threat, environment, consumer, policy, or time change; govern exceptions and revocation.

Adjudicate useful throughput, target quality, calibration, safety, privacy, rights, authority, latency, compute, energy, human burden, opportunity cost, residuals, reproduction, and transfer jointly rather than optimizing one reward.
interfaces: Benchmark Ratchets owns construct, dataset, metric, contamination, floor, selection-lineage, and instrument validity; policy optimization consumes bounded signals without redefining them.
Adversarial Evaluation owns context-conditioned observation integrity, sandbagging, monitor, reward, and evaluator discrepancies; the update lease retains its blockers.
Evidence States, Claim Ledgers, Spinoza, and UAT own claim support, verifier epistemics, contradiction, and review; reward cannot mint evidence.
Planning, VCM, Routing, Generation, Verification, Execution, Governance, and generation-mode owners define target-policy behavior and consumer-specific constraints.
Data and Supply-Chain owners retain dataset, label, preference, provenance, consent, rights, contamination, checkpoint, dependency, and environment truth conditions.
Artifact Graphs and Talos retain training, rollout, evaluator, checkpoint, tool, receipt, descendant, invalidation, and replay lineage.
Security Kernel and Runtime Adapters enforce authority, tool, credential, isolation, side-effect, approval, and rollback boundaries that training cannot expand.
Stable Capability Fields, Capability Replacement, and Readiness own qualification, substitution, canary/default transition, residual custody, quarantine, and admission.
Capability Thresholds and Safety Cases consume scoped policy evidence but retain commitment, assurance, exception, and release-path authority.
Resource Economics owns compute, latency, energy, human burden, opportunity cost, and governance-capacity accounting.
Formal Proof owns finite update, route, drift, authority, and rollback predicates; theorem success cannot establish empirical learning or safety.
Incident, Release, and Living-Book Governance own response, deployment, disclosure, correction, public claims, editions, and publication authority.
invariants: Every update names the legitimate objective, target policy, baseline, consumer, permitted behavior delta, prohibited effects, authority ceiling, evidence ceiling, owner, and expiry.
Reward, preference, verifier output, benchmark score, loss, style, length, latency, or cost is never identical to the target property or evidence by default.
Feedback provenance, incentives, rights, contamination, uncertainty, evaluator dependencies, and proxy boundary travel with every update and claim.
No policy, checkpoint, optimizer, trainer, reward model, evaluator, or candidate can solely author and judge its own promotion, incident closure, or rollback success.
Training cannot expand tool, data, credential, network, approval, release, support, or governance authority by behavioral side effect.
Optimizer family and acronym never substitute for target definition, reward validity, baselines, complete denominators, or causal evidence.
Every candidate, rollout, seed, retry, repair, exclusion, failure, unsafe artifact, and discarded checkpoint remains in selection and cost denominators.
Strong baselines receive matched model, data, tools, context, compute, tuning, retries, evaluator access, and engineering effort.
Holdouts, contamination checks, target evaluations, reward-hacking probes, regressions, tails, subgroups, and stopping rules are frozen before selection.
A reward improvement without target-property improvement is blocked; a target improvement with unacceptable regressions, authority drift, or hidden costs is not promoted.
Faster, shorter, cheaper, more compliant, or more preferred behavior is not better unless useful task outcomes and all declared floors survive.
A learned verifier, monitor, planner, router, or governance policy cannot validate the feedback process that trained it without independent bounded evidence.
Promotion status remains distinct from training completion, checkpoint existence, synthetic eligibility, canary status, readiness, release, deployment, support, and SOTA.
Rollback covers full causal state and downstream effects; a fixture reset or weight restore alone is never effect-complete recovery.
Post-update monitoring retains natural outcomes, failures, incidents, distribution shift, reward-model changes, regressions, costs, and residuals.
Material objective, feedback, evaluator, data, model, scaffold, tool, authority, environment, consumer, policy, or time change expires affected leases.
Reproduction and transfer use independently implemented trainers, evaluators, data custody, environments, and institutions without shared hidden advantages.
Finite schemas, fixtures, route predicates, and rollback dry runs establish only their exact record boundary and cannot prove causal policy improvement.
failure_modes: Reward hacking or verifier gaming increases the selected signal while the target property, retained utility, safety, privacy, rights, or robustness degrades.
Preference overoptimization learns style, agreeableness, verbosity, brevity, persuasion, or annotator artifacts instead of truth or task success.
Planner, router, context, verifier, execution, governance, or generation-mode policies learn hidden shortcuts that evade local evaluation.
Latency, token, or cost rewards suppress necessary retrieval, verification, abstention, escalation, approval, or recovery.
Policy or behavioral drift crosses capability-field, authority, readiness, safeguard, or deployment boundaries without an explicit decision.
Reward-model or evaluator feedback loops amplify shared errors, sycophancy, monitor blindness, institutional incentives, or self-confirming labels.
Offline preference data hide selection, annotator disagreement, affected-party exclusion, consent, distribution shift, and counterpreference.
Online RL hides unsafe exploration, discarded rollouts, environment resets, tool effects, and tail harms outside the reported checkpoint.
Checkpoint cherry-picking, seed search, repeated retries, reward tuning, and undisclosed repairs manufacture a favorable update.
Baseline starvation gives the candidate more model capacity, data, tools, context, compute, tuning, retries, or engineering effort.
Holdout, contamination, reward-hacking, regression, tail, or subgroup checks are changed, exposed, omitted, or interpreted after outcomes are visible.
Authority laundering treats learned compliance, a governance reward, or a policy classifier as permission to act, release, or waive review.
Training-completion laundering treats a stable loss, saved checkpoint, source-reported method, or synthetic canary as policy improvement.
Rollback theater restores weights or a fixture while optimizer, scheduler, RNG, caches, routes, credentials, descendants, effects, monitors, or reliance remain changed.
Catastrophic forgetting, capability erosion, calibration drift, mode collapse, refusal shift, or hidden specialization is erased by aggregate reward.
Mitigation substitution lowers a measured proxy while moving the exploit, reducing capability, disabling the detector, or creating a new failure channel.
Update churn and governance burden consume compute, reviewer capacity, energy, release time, and opportunity without useful-throughput improvement.
A result is transferred across tasks, policies, models, scaffolds, tools, authority, evaluators, organizations, environments, or time without requalification.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The exact current minimum preserves eleven inherited contradiction/guard theorems and the deterministic six-sample, two-holdout, five-candidate lease probe inside a seven-stage, 63-route governed-update lifecycle with two composed Lean traces, 73/73 route mutations, 3/3 cross-stage mutations, one bounded lease, and one protocol-version-2 readmission witness. It runs no optimizer, model, trainer, preference dataset, reward model, natural workload, real policy update, deployed canary, live rollback, independent reproduction, transfer, chapter-core transition, or SOTA comparison.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature Governed Policy Update program preregisters natural planner, context, router, verifier, execution, generation, and governance workloads; compares strong online-RL, offline-preference, verifier-RL, deterministic, no-update, and simpler baselines under matched opportunity; preserves complete rollout and selection lineage; tests reward validity, target outcomes, contamination, tails, authority, safety, privacy, rights, resource cost, forgetting, reward hacking, evaluator dependence, causal mechanisms, effect-complete rollback, and natural post-update monitoring; and requires independent trainer/evaluator/institution reproduction plus heterogeneous transfer. It records positive, negative, null, inconclusive, narrowed, refuted, rolled-back, deprecated, retired, and blocked-after-full-attempt outcomes. This is a target campaign, not a current trained policy, reward-quality result, safe optimizer, capability gain, deployment, or SOTA claim.

chapters.title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
chapters.problem: A self-improving stack needs to decide which data and feedback may enter future learning without confusing an intake decision with a capability result, a privacy guarantee, or proof that a model has forgotten a record.
chapters.insufficient: Training corpora, synthetic-data pipelines, replay buffers, and deletion requests are often handled as separate operational concerns, leaving provenance, contamination control, retention, distributional trade-offs, downstream descendants, and evidence effects without one governable lifecycle.
chapters.core_claim: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning owns a datum-, cohort-, provenance-, rights-, split-, contamination-, learning-lane-, retention-, checkpoint-authority-, full-state-inventory-, descendant-, deletion-request-, claim-axis-, consumer-, environment-, and time-specific Data-and-Descendant Custody Lease: before learning or deletion, it binds admissible use, evaluation exclusions, synthetic and transformation lineage, coverage and distribution residuals, model/optimizer/scheduler/RNG/cache/backup/descendant state, prospective checkpoint authority, retention and replay, deletion propagation, verification, rollback, expiry, and terminal custody; behavioral cohort change, causal influence reduction, privacy leakage reduction, lineage invalidation, legal compliance, and storage or backup erasure remain separate claims, and no receipt, checksum, exclusion, invalidation, benchmark score, rollback match, or synthetic campaign alone establishes model quality, forgetting, privacy, erasure, safety, readiness, deployment, support, transfer, or SOTA.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports treating deletion as a lifecycle with training-time structure, affected-artifact scope, retraining cost, accuracy trade-offs, and residual boundaries rather than as a bare forget command.

mechanism: Freeze the consumer, purpose, cohort, learning lane, requested claim axes, rights envelope, state inventory, and deletion scope before admission or update work begins.

Inventory model, adapters, optimizer, scheduler, scaler, RNG, sampler, dataloader, curriculum, checkpoints, caches, retrieval and indexes, logs, backups, replicas, credentials, evaluations, publications, and descendants.
Choose checkpoint-selection and rollback authority prospectively and retain every saved, selected, discarded, restored, and superseded state needed to audit the choice.
Run learning campaigns with frozen data, algorithms, seeds, strong baselines, budgets, stopping and checkpoint rules, natural tasks, hidden holdouts, regressions, tails, and costs.

Bind each deletion request to authority, target, legal basis where claimed, deadline, method, state inventory, descendant graph, requested claim axes, verification protocol, exceptions, and residuals.

Propagate deletion and revocation through datasets, checkpoints, adapters, caches, retrieval stores, indexes, evaluations, distilled artifacts, publications, releases, and declared external transfers.

interfaces: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback may consume only lease-bound data and feedback candidates, and must return update-policy, reward, checkpoint, rollback, and evidence records without changing custody truth.

Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay binds sources, transformations, generators, datasets, checkpoints, evaluations, descendants, and deletion events into replayable lineage while the data engine owns custody semantics.

Benchmark Ratchets and Anti-Goodhart Evidence supplies frozen partitions, exposure logs, contamination probes, strong comparators, regressions, and promotion blockers without deciding data rights or deletion closure.

AI Supply-Chain Integrity and Model-Weight Custody receive digest-bound datasets, checkpoints, replicas, backups, and transfer records while retaining separate artifact and hardware custody authority.

Virtual Context, Context Transactions, Procedural Memory, Retrieval, and Runtime Tools consume governed artifacts with purpose, provenance, retention, taint, revocation, and deletion boundaries instead of free-floating examples.

Evidence States, Claim Ledgers, Executable Specifications, and Lean proofs record claim-axis-specific evidence and finite route consequences without treating records or theorem counts as empirical proof.

Stable Capability Fields, Capability Replacement, Readiness Gates, Residual Escrow, and Release Governance retain exclusive authority over capability belief, rollback acceptance, deployment, and publication.
Security Kernel, Digital SCIFs, and Runtime Permissions enforce access, disclosure, transfer, and deletion operations but do not infer lawful authority or successful erasure from execution alone.

Human Intent, Constitutional Substrate, Moral Uncertainty, and Governance specify affected-party, rights, appeal, and normative constraints while the lease records their operational scope and unresolved disagreement.
Resource Economics and Token Budgets receives complete compute, storage, human-review, privacy, deletion, retraining, rollback, and externality costs for policy comparison.
Incident response consumes poisoning, leakage, rights, drift, regrowth, replica, and descendant residuals and can revoke or narrow a lease without promoting a replacement claim.
The Living Book consumes only adjudicated claim-axis records, stable anchors, and public-safe artifacts; publication cannot convert an admission or deletion operation into stronger evidence.
invariants: Eligibility is scoped to an exact datum or cohort, consumer, purpose, learning lane, rights envelope, partitions, retention window, state inventory, claim axes, environment, and time bound.

Metadata, a checksum, a provenance assertion, consent, a license, or an authority field does not by itself establish truth, completeness, integrity, lawful use, representativeness, fitness, privacy, or safety.
Every transformation and synthetic, translated, summarized, distilled, replayed, retrieved, inferred, or mixed descendant retains source and activity lineage plus unresolved provenance residuals.
Train, validation, public calibration, hidden holdout, contamination, temporal, adversarial, deletion, and transfer partitions and every exposure path remain distinct and frozen before evaluation.
Replacement, accumulation, replay, reweighting, synthetic augmentation, quarantine, retraining, sharding, fine-tuning, and no-change remain explicit competitors rather than post-hoc labels.

Coverage, duplication, contamination, poisoning, privacy, rights, utility, tail, forgetting, deletion, descendant, and cost results retain complete denominators, failures, exclusions, seeds, and decision history.

Full state includes model, adapters, optimizer, scheduler, scaler, RNG, sampler, dataloader, curriculum, checkpoints, caches, retrieval and indexes, logs, backups, replicas, credentials, evaluations, publications, and descendants.

Checkpoint-selection, rollback, deletion, verification, appeal, exception, and terminal-custody authorities are chosen prospectively and cannot be reassigned after outcomes are known without invalidating the decision.

Behavioral cohort change, causal influence reduction, privacy leakage reduction, lineage invalidation, legal compliance, storage erasure, backup erasure, and external-descendant closure are separate claim axes.

A deletion request, exclusion from a new dataset, changed output, lower memorization score, tombstone, checksum change, lineage invalidation, or retraining receipt cannot be used to cross-promote another deletion claim axis.

Deletion and revocation propagate through every declared descendant; undeclared or unreachable descendants, transfers, publications, replicas, backups, and regenerating processes remain explicit residuals.

Storage erasure requires identity-bound action, replica and backup scope, regeneration controls, independently checked postconditions, and residual accounting; a logical deletion flag is insufficient.

Admission, quarantine, curation, training, invalidation, deletion, restoration, or rollback cannot by itself promote capability, benchmark, privacy, safety, readiness, deployment, support, transfer, legal, or SOTA claims.

Every learning and deletion conclusion is bounded to the exact data, algorithms, models, seeds, checkpoints, metrics, evaluators, consumers, environments, institutions, descendants, and time window actually tested.

Natural utility, calibration, subgroup and tail behavior, forgetting, interference, privacy, poisoning, drift, sequential deletion, regrowth, descendant closure, latency, and total cost remain visible together.

Rollback is effect-complete only when the frozen state inventory, external effects, replicas, backups, descendants, credentials, publications, and evidence state are restored or explicitly residualized.

Every lease expires or re-enters review after material changes to data, rights, partitions, transformation lineage, model or optimizer state, consumers, environments, descendants, evidence, or time bounds.

Schema validity, deterministic probes, finite Lean reductions, local digest matches, and green validators establish only their exact artifact boundary and never semantic, empirical, causal, privacy, legal, deployment, transfer, or SOTA proof.
failure_modes: Provenance laundering relabels transformed, distilled, generated, retrieved, replayed, inferred, or mixed data as origin-complete source material.
Metadata theater treats a valid schema, checksum, license field, or PROV/Croissant-shaped record as proof of integrity, representativeness, lawful use, fitness, privacy, or safety.
Exact-overlap checks miss semantic leakage, benchmark memorization, near duplicates, indirect exposure, contamination, subgroup loss, or distribution shift.
Synthetic replacement collapses coverage and tails, while indiscriminate accumulation retains poisoned, private, obsolete, low-quality, or revoked material.
Curation Goodhart optimizes filters or teacher scores that remove difficult but valuable cases, reward benchmark leakage, or hide denominator and tail losses.
Partition laundering changes splits, reveals hidden holdouts, or reclassifies exposed examples after results are known while preserving a clean-test label.
State-inventory truncation omits optimizer, scheduler, RNG, sampler, cache, retrieval, backup, replica, credential, evaluation, publication, or descendant effects.
Checkpoint-authority drift selects, discards, or restores states after outcomes are visible and then presents the choice as prospective.
Behavioral forgetting is laundered into causal influence reduction even though the removed cohort may still affect parameters, representations, or descendants.
Privacy laundering treats lower memorization, changed answers, or an exclusion receipt as evidence that membership, extraction, or other leakage risk was reduced.
Lineage invalidation is laundered into storage or backup erasure while bytes, replicas, derived artifacts, logs, publications, or regenerating processes remain.
Deletion theater records a request or tombstone while checkpoints, adapters, caches, retrieval stores, indexes, backups, replicas, publications, and external descendants remain unverified.
Retraining theater reports a new model without matched retained utility, influence, privacy, state, descendant, replica, backup, regrowth, or cost checks.
Rollback theater restores a checkpoint digest while external effects, optimizer state, caches, credentials, descendants, publications, or evidence state remain changed.
Aggregate utility hides subgroup and tail regressions, catastrophic forgetting, interference, privacy leakage, poisoning, drift, or sequential-deletion failure.
Promotion laundering treats admission, a benchmark gain, an invalidation, a rollback match, or a synthetic campaign as readiness, safety, deployment, support, transfer, legal, or SOTA evidence.
Cost externalization omits compute, storage, reviewer, privacy, deletion, retraining, rollback, affected-party, environmental, and downstream-custody burdens.
Unsupported transfer generalizes a local synthetic or single-institution result across data regimes, models, languages, domains, institutions, descendants, or time.
chapters.minimal_implementation: The exact current minimum preserves all fifteen legacy data-admission, full-state, and unlearning-claim route theorems; the four-scenario admission probe; the 24-surface/15-transaction full-state bridge; and the three-seed/twelve-arm small-model update result inside an eight-stage, 82-route custody/update/deletion lifecycle with two composed Lean traces, 96/96 route mutations, 3/3 cross-stage mutations, one bounded custody handoff, and one protocol-version-2 readmission witness. Influence remains unestablished, storage erasure remains zero, all four affected claims remain no-change, and no source/rights truth, semantic contamination, foundation-model learning, privacy/legal/backup/external-descendant erasure, production rollback, transfer, chapter-core transition, or SOTA result exists.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature operational contract requires an argument-exit campaign over natural heterogeneous learning and deletion workloads with frozen partitions and matched strong replacement, accumulation, replay, reweighting, synthetic-augmentation, quarantine, retraining, sharding, fine-tuning, and no-change policies; inventory and restore model, adapter, optimizer, scheduler, RNG, sampler, cache, retrieval, replica, backup, credential, evaluation, publication, and descendant state; and jointly measure coverage, contamination, poisoning, leakage, rights, natural utility, calibration, tails, forgetting, causal influence, privacy, sequential deletion, storage and backup erasure, regrowth, external-descendant closure, effect-complete rollback, latency, and total cost. Independent trainers, custodians, privacy evaluators, storage verifiers, institutions, and transfer sites must reproduce the terminal outcomes, including null, negative, blocked, narrowed, and refuted results. Until then this is not evidence of production custody, forgetting, privacy or legal erasure, storage closure, capability promotion, deployment, transfer, or SOTA.

proof_targets.target: Missing bound provenance, rights, or authority blocks the reachable custody lifecycle before data admission.
Missing split exclusions or a contamination-check record blocks the reachable custody lifecycle before admission.
A complete admission record advances only to full-state binding and does not establish data quality, training, deletion, or support.
Complete declared full-state custody and a prospectively fixed selection rule advance only to an update receipt, not promotion.
Missing optimizer state blocks full-state binding.
Missing scheduler state blocks full-state binding.
Missing RNG state blocks full-state binding.
Missing cache state blocks full-state binding.
Missing backup state blocks full-state binding.
Missing descendant state blocks full-state binding.
Missing prospective checkpoint authority or selection rule blocks full-state binding.
A declared-surface rollback mismatch blocks the update record and cannot be generalized to semantic or production recovery.
Behavioral cohort change cannot serve as evidence of causal influence reduction in deletion-claim adjudication.
Behavioral cohort change cannot serve as evidence of privacy protection or erasure in deletion-claim adjudication.
Lineage propagation or invalidation cannot serve as evidence of physical storage or backup erasure in deletion-claim adjudication.

open_evidence_gaps: No natural heterogeneous data and deletion campaign has frozen complete partitions and denominators, compared strong matched replacement, accumulation, replay, reweighting, synthetic, quarantine, retraining, sharding, fine-tuning, and no-change policies, or prevented outcome-aware protocol change.

No real campaign jointly establishes coverage, semantic leakage, contamination, poisoning, privacy, rights, full-state restoration, effect-complete rollback, sequential deletion, replica and backup erasure, regrowth control, external-descendant closure, and complete cost accounting.

The eight-stage lifecycle, 82-route consumer, and 96 rejecting mutations establish only finite authored custody consequences; all digests, rights, inventories, checks, rollback receipts, deletion statuses, monitors, and invalidations remain trusted.

No independent trainer, custodian, privacy evaluator, storage verifier, institution, or transfer site has reproduced the bounded local results or adjudicated the separate behavioral, influence, privacy, lineage, legal, storage, backup, and external-descendant claim axes.

chapters.title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance

handoff_or_recovery_notes: sources/inbox/artifact_steward_agents_browser_note_2026-06-25/

chapters.problem: Durable artifacts need continuity across planning, funding, work assignment, release, maintenance, governance, and eventual sunset without turning an AI manager into the owner of the project.
chapters.insufficient: Issue trackers, CI systems, bots, DAOs, funding pages, and project-management tools each cover a slice of artifact lifecycle, but none by themselves preserve mission, memory, authority, treasury limits, contributor credit, evidence, and death protocol as one governed project steward.
chapters.core_claim: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance owns a project-, artifact-, mission-, owner-, authority-, roadmap-, work-contract-, event-, treasury-, compute-, contributor-, evidence-, governance-, release-, federation-, sunset-, consumer-, environment-, and time-specific Artifact Steward Continuity Lease: it may observe, propose, prepare, coordinate, execute, reverse, archive, or retire only through a versioned charter, taint-aware intake, scoped work contracts, separated contribution ledgers, bounded treasury and compute policy, verification and release gates, appeal and fork or exit paths, effect-complete rollback, and explicit sunset authority; it never acquires ownership, governance legitimacy, evidence authority, funding rights, release authority, legal standing, or permission from useful motion, a green workflow, a vote, a balance, a score, a fixture, a theorem, or its own prior action.

mechanism: Freeze artifact identity, mission, non-goals, protected principles, owners, affected parties, license, jurisdiction, consumers, environments, success criteria, and sunset criteria in a versioned charter.
Bind the exact authority hierarchy, roles, delegations, separation of duties, approval thresholds, appeal routes, fork and exit rights, emergency powers, and prohibited self-ratification.

Ingest issues, pull requests, comments, webhooks, dependencies, worker outputs, benchmarks, funding events, votes, and release events with identity, provenance, signature, trust, taint, injection, and review state.

Maintain versioned project memory for sources, decisions, assumptions, roadmaps, contracts, artifacts, releases, finances, contributors, incidents, disputes, open questions, corrections, and residuals.

Compile mission-level goals into dependency-aware roadmap items and scoped work contracts with outputs, files, systems, tools, forbidden actions, data, budget, deadline, verification, payment, rollback, and non-claims.
Dispatch humans, agents, personal or project hives, CI runners, rented compute, reviewers, and maintainers only through contracts that cannot transfer project authority.
Budget treasury, compute, storage, sponsorship, grants, bounties, recurring operations, reserves, payments, and externalities under prospective caps, approvals, freezes, and reconciliation.
Execute only allow-listed reversible actions with least privilege, isolated credentials, protected-asset gates, time bounds, logs, kill switches, and explicit external-mutation authority.
Verify submitted work against contract outputs, tests, source and claim evidence, security and privacy checks, regressions, costs, residuals, reviewer conflicts, and acceptance authority.
Record authorship, review, evidence credit, compensation, reputation, governance influence, conflicts, and corrections in separate ledgers that cannot mint truth or authority from one score.
Build release candidates with exact artifact identity, source and dependency state, tests, evidence, changelog, rights, residuals, approval, rollback, publication, and attestation records.
Route governance changes through typed proposals, notice and delay, quorum, voting or maintainer rules, conflict disclosure, guardian limits, appeal, audit, fork, exit, and terminal decision records.
Federate projects and workers through scoped contracts, data and tool boundaries, budget approval, evidence bundles, dispute routes, provenance, and independently rejectable terms.

Monitor mission fit, user value, maintainer and contributor continuity, useful throughput, false action and blocking, finances, resources, security, injection, capture, concentration, opacity, incidents, and residual age.
Freeze, revoke, contain, rotate credentials, suspend spend or work, roll back effects, correct ledgers, notify affected parties, and reopen governance after incidents or authority violations.
Move among inception, bootstrap, build, release, maintenance, manual, assisted, bounded-autonomous, community-governed, decline, transfer, archive, and sunset modes only through recorded gates.

When sunset criteria trigger, stop ordinary work, freeze optional spend, inventory artifacts and obligations, preserve or delete according to authority, offer transfer or fork, archive public-safe history, and retain appeal and recovery paths.

Expire, narrow, revoke, replace, archive, or retire the lease when mission, ownership, authority, law, funding, infrastructure, threat, governance, evidence, consumers, environment, or time bounds materially change.
interfaces: Human Intent, Constitutional Substrate, Moral Uncertainty, and System Authority own mission legitimacy, affected-party rights, protected principles, consent, appeal, and authority ceilings; the steward records and obeys them.
Planning, Cognitive Compilation, and Talos or Labor OS own decomposition and typed job semantics; the steward owns project continuity and contract dispatch, not task truth.
VCM, Context Transactions, Procedural Memory, and Artifact Graphs own governed memory, taint, lineage, replay, and revocation while the steward maintains the project-level index and lifecycle.
Runtime Tools, Security Kernel, Digital SCIFs, Supply Chain, and Weight Custody enforce least privilege, isolation, credentials, dependency and artifact integrity, and protected-asset boundaries.
Evidence States, Claim Ledgers, Spinoza, Executable Specifications, and Lean own evidence and proof semantics; the steward may prepare review packets but cannot promote support.
Benchmark Ratchets, Adversarial Evaluation, Safety Cases, Thresholds, Readiness, and Residual Escrow own evaluation, assurance, capability, release-readiness, and residual acceptance.
Policy Optimization, Data Engines, and Capability Replacement own learning, data custody, model updates, and capability rollback; steward activity cannot authorize them.
Resource Economics, TokenMana, treasury, sponsorship, fiscal hosting, and compute providers expose budgets and costs without converting money or resource access into roadmap or governance authority.
Contribution, maintainer, community, and governance systems own authorship, review, compensation, representation, voting, delegation, conflicts, quorum, appeal, fork, and exit decisions.
Personal and Project Compute Hives, CI, rented nodes, and federated projects accept or reject scoped contracts and return evidence bundles without inheriting project ownership.
Incident, rollback, release, publication, archive, and sunset authorities own external mutations and terminal outcomes; the steward can route only within explicit approval.
The Living Book consumes adjudicated, public-safe project records and preserves roadmap, evidence, release, correction, and sunset history without treating maintenance activity as proof.
invariants: The steward is a continuity service, never the artifact owner, rights holder, legal principal, maintainer monopoly, treasury sovereign, evidence authority, or governance source.
Every action is scoped to exact artifact, charter version, authority basis, work contract, tools, data, budget, credentials, approvals, consumer, environment, and time.
Mission, non-goals, protected principles, ownership, authority ceilings, success criteria, and sunset criteria remain durable, versioned, human-readable, and independently inspectable.
Plans, helpful outputs, prior actions, workflow success, votes, balances, scores, tests, fixtures, theorem passes, and steward recommendations cannot self-authorize later action.
Untrusted event text, metadata, code, artifacts, worker output, funding signals, and votes remain identity- and provenance-bound tainted input until the required independent review clears exact uses.

Workers, agents, hives, runners, providers, and federated projects receive least authority through scoped contracts and never inherit project ownership, treasury, evidence, release, or governance rights.

Spending, compute, storage, bounties, sponsorships, grants, recurring operations, payments, and reserves are bounded actuators with prospective caps, approvals, freezes, reconciliation, and complete costs.
Authorship, review, evidence credit, compensation, reputation, governance influence, conflicts, and corrections remain separate ledgers; no composite score changes claim support or authority.
Evidence, benchmark, safety, readiness, capability, legal, rights, finance, governance, and release decisions remain with their named owners; stewardship may prepare but cannot promote them.

Protected branches, credentials, funds, publications, releases, governance rules, licenses, ownership, personal data, and external systems require explicit action-specific authority and reversible execution where possible.

Release requires exact artifact identity, tests, evidence, source and dependency state, changelog, rights, residuals, approval, rollback, publication scope, and post-release observation; a green workflow is insufficient.
Governance changes preserve notice, delay, quorum or maintainer rules, conflict disclosure, guardian limits, appeal, audit, fork, exit, dissent, and terminal decision lineage.
Every accepted, rejected, failed, blocked, timed-out, rolled-back, disputed, appealed, corrected, transferred, archived, and sunset action remains in complete denominators and history.

Human-readable maps, work queues, decisions, finances, contribution records, release notes, recovery procedures, and maintenance paths remain usable without permission from or private memory inside the steward.
Monitoring measures mission fit, useful throughput, false action, false blocking, maintainer burden, capture, injection, opacity, incidents, recovery, residuals, latency, and total cost together.
Rollback is effect-complete only when repository, credentials, funds, compute, external services, notifications, ledgers, releases, governance, and reliance are restored or explicitly residualized.

Triggered sunset criteria block ordinary activity until an authorized review chooses continuation, narrowing, transfer, fork, archive, deletion, or retirement; activity volume cannot defeat the trigger.

Schema validity, deterministic routes, finite Lean reductions, documentation, source mappings, and green validators establish only their exact record boundary and never stewardship quality, legitimacy, safety, capture resistance, deployment, transfer, or SOTA.
failure_modes: Mission drift and activity Goodhart optimize issue, commit, release, contributor, funding, or benchmark counts while user value and protected purpose decay.
Authority laundering converts a helpful proposal, prior approval, role, token, vote, workflow, fixture, theorem, or emergency action into general permission.
Workflow injection lets untrusted issue, pull-request, comment, webhook, dependency, artifact, benchmark, or worker text become steward control input.
Credential and protected-asset capture exposes branches, releases, registries, domains, keys, wallets, personal data, or external systems beyond the contract.
Treasury and compute drain wastes funds through bounty spam, low-value work, sybil claims, recurring costs, rented nodes, storage growth, or hidden externalities.
Contribution gaming collapses authorship, review, evidence, compensation, reputation, and governance into a score that can be bought, farmed, or self-awarded.
Governance capture concentrates power in holders, delegates, early maintainers, guardians, funders, or steward-controlled ranking and suppresses conflicts, dissent, appeal, fork, or exit.
Evidence and release laundering treats CI, theorem counts, schema passes, citations, steward summaries, or approvals as proof of semantics, safety, readiness, rights, or quality.
Planning and contract theater produce detailed roadmaps and records without useful work, adequate verification, complete denominators, or terminal outcomes.
Federation trust laundering lets external workers, projects, hives, providers, or rented nodes inherit data, tool, treasury, release, or governance authority.
AI maintainer monopoly hides mission, memory, finances, decisions, recovery, and maintenance procedures in private context that humans cannot inspect or operate.
Human rubber-stamping makes nominal approval ineffective through overload, dark patterns, missing alternatives, time pressure, conflicts, or irreversible bundles.
Rollback theater restores repository state while credentials, funds, external services, releases, notifications, votes, ledgers, or downstream reliance remain changed.
Incident concealment optimizes uptime or reputation by suppressing failed actions, injection, compromise, disputes, cost overruns, rollbacks, and residuals.
Sunset evasion changes criteria after outcomes, manufactures users or work, spends reserves, or invokes continuity rhetoric to avoid transfer, archive, or retirement.
Premature sunset or hostile takeover uses captured criteria, funding withdrawal, false inactivity, or emergency powers to erase a useful project or its history.
Cost and burden externalization omits maintainer attention, reviewer fatigue, affected parties, legal and tax work, privacy, security, compute, environment, recovery, and opportunity cost.
Unsupported transfer generalizes local fixture routing or one project workflow across missions, communities, legal regimes, funding models, repositories, institutions, threats, or time.
chapters.minimal_implementation: Seven schema-and-fixture record families; sixteen Lean declarations derived from seven hand-authored finite predicates or route functions; one deterministic public-safe lifecycle probe with two valid routes and six expected-invalid controls; and one adjacent synthetic release evidence-and-approval handoff. No steward bot, real event intake, treasury or compute executor, contributor service, governance runner, federation harness, protected-branch action, release runner, sunset protocol, natural project workload, independent reproduction, deployment, or chapter-core support effect exists.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The argument-exit campaign must run natural, heterogeneous, months-long project workloads with frozen mission and authority charters and matched strong human-maintained, ordinary automation, workflow-platform, and no-steward baselines. It must exercise real tainted events and injection, scoped dispatch, protected branches and credentials, treasury and rented compute, contributor and compensation ledgers, governance proposals and capture pressure, federation, release, incident containment, effect-complete rollback, appeal, fork or exit, maintainer handoff, and genuine sunset decisions while jointly measuring useful throughput, quality, false action and blocking, maintainer burden, legitimacy, concentration, security, recovery, continuity, latency, and total cost. Independent maintainers, contributors, treasurers, security evaluators, governance institutions, projects, legal and funding regimes, and transfer environments must reproduce positive, negative, null, narrowed, blocked, and refuted outcomes. Until then the mature Artifact Steward Continuity Lease is not evidence of safe autonomy, ownership or legal authority, governance legitimacy, treasury safety, capture resistance, project quality, release readiness, deployment, transfer, or SOTA.

proof_targets.target: A steward-managed work contract records objective, authority, allowed tools, forbidden tools, verification requirements, budget, and non-claims before dispatch.
A steward action that exceeds treasury policy, changes governance rules, or touches protected assets cannot execute without explicit approval evidence.
A stewarded release publication requires test, evidence, changelog, residual, and approval records.
When sunset criteria are met, ordinary work generation is blocked until a sunset review is opened.

A steward lifecycle route sends tainted unreviewed events to quarantine, sunset criteria without review to sunset review, autonomy escalation without charter approval to approval, and over-policy treasury spend to approval.

A steward contribution ledger keeps authorship, review, evidence, compensation, reputation, governance effect, and conflicts separated; collapsed governance scoring is rejected and support-state changes require evidence-transition records.
A steward federation contract requires scoped work contracts, bounded worker authority, tool/data/budget gates, external-spend approval, and evidence-bundle requirements before dispatch.

open_evidence_gaps: No natural multi-project campaign has compared a bounded steward against strong human-maintained, ordinary automation, workflow-platform, and no-steward baselines under frozen missions, authorities, workloads, budgets, release criteria, sunset criteria, and complete success and failure denominators.

No real event-taint, prompt-injection, protected-branch, credential, treasury, compute-rental, contributor, governance-capture, federation, release, rollback, appeal, fork or exit, maintainer-continuity, or sunset campaign jointly measures useful throughput, governance quality, false action and false blocking, recovery, cost, and authority leakage.

No independent maintainer, contributor, treasurer, security evaluator, governance institution, project, or transfer environment has reproduced the record-level results or shown that stewardship improves continuity without ownership, evidence, funding, release, or governance capture.

chapters.title: Integrated Reference Architecture
chapters.problem: Readers need to see how the layers operate as one machine from intent to governed action and improvement.
chapters.insufficient: Layer chapters can still feel disconnected unless the book traces the complete control flow and artifacts.
chapters.core_claim: Integrated Reference Architecture owns a trace-, run-, request-, intent-, authority-, artifact-, parentage-, layer-, canonical-state-, material-effect-, terminal-receipt-, evaluator-, evidence-, residual-, rollback-, consumer-, environment-, and time-specific Cross-Layer Trace Join Contract: it proves integration only when every participating owner remains distinct and its typed input, output, authority delta, state identity, observed effect, acknowledgement, evaluation, evidence delta, residual, stop, repair, rollback, and non-claim remain joinable across approved, blocked, failed, revoked, rolled-back, and quarantined paths; a diagram, shared prompt, interface name, projection, green service, replay, fixture, theorem, or locally successful slice cannot establish whole-stack execution, semantic preservation, governance enforcement, safety, capability, deployment, transfer, or SOTA.

mechanism: Freeze the trace purpose, request, initiating intent, affected parties, consumers, environments, time bounds, participating owners, expected effects, acceptance criteria, and explicit non-claims.
Bind the authority chain, scope, delegation, ceilings, approvals, revocations, stops, exceptions, and terminal authority at every handoff without allowing downstream widening.

Assign stable identities and digests to parent artifacts, layer inputs and outputs, policies, canonical states, projections, material effects, receipts, evaluations, evidence events, residuals, rollbacks, and descendants.

Represent each layer as a typed owner contract with preconditions, input schema, output artifact, authority effect, state transition, observation surface, failure routes, rollback, expiry, and non-claims.

Trace intent through constitutional and governance interpretation, command contract, planning, context, routing, claim review, work dispatch, execution, observation, evidence, readiness, release, and improvement without collapsing roles.

Carry compression, procedural memory, learning, data, capability replacement, artifact stewardship, resource, security, and incident subtraces as linked contracts rather than hidden internal activity.
Record positive, blocked, failed, timed-out, revoked, quarantined, rolled-back, appealed, and abandoned paths with complete denominators and terminal acknowledgements.
Join request-derived input, policy decision, canonical-state selection, material effect, acknowledged receipt, evaluator result, and claim boundary in a name-to-effect spine.
Keep canonical state authoritative over dashboards, reports, caches, indexes, summaries, claim views, deployment pointers, and other projections, and detect drift in both directions.
Bind request, policy, state, effect, receipt, evaluator, evidence, residual, and decision digests so orphan effects, stale receipts, and false completions cannot join.
Observe effects independently of the actuator where feasible and record evaluator identity, method, dependencies, conflicts, blind spots, calibration, disagreement, and independence class.
Apply authority monotonicity, revocation-before-effect, evidence-transition integrity, residual conservation, causal order, lifecycle validity, and bounded liveness checks to the joined trace.
Run validation commands, semantic checks, negative controls, fault injection, replay, differential comparison, and mutation tests against both approved and blocked traces.

Compare the governed trace with matched direct, monolithic-agent, workflow-orchestrator, service-composition, and no-governance baselines under equal task, model, tool, context, compute, tuning, and retry opportunity.
Reconcile observed outcomes into exact retain, narrow, refute, deprecate, repair, retry, rollback, quarantine, release, or no-change decisions without promotion from activity.
Propagate corrections, revocations, rollback, evidence downgrades, and residual changes through every dependent artifact, projection, consumer, release, and descendant trace.

Monitor join completeness, orphan rate, authority drift, state/projection divergence, unacknowledged effects, evaluator capture, evidence latency, residual age, rollback closure, useful throughput, failures, and total cost.
Expire, replace, archive, or retire the contract when owners, interfaces, schemas, authority, states, evaluators, dependencies, threats, consumers, environments, or time bounds materially change.
interfaces: Human Intent, Constitutional Substrate, Moral Uncertainty, and System Authority supply legitimate initiating intent, affected-party and rights constraints, authority ceilings, exceptions, appeal, and terminal governance without surrendering ownership to the trace.
Intent-to-Execution Contracts, Planning, Cognitive Compilation, and Labor OS supply command contracts, plan DAGs, semantic IR, jobs, resources, acceptance tests, and rollback requirements.
VCM, Context Transactions, Verification Bandwidth, Procedural Memory, and Retrieval supply source-bound context, taint, snapshots, adequacy, revocation, learned tools, and memory lineage.

Routing, specialist cores, deliberation, mathematical/search substrates, compact systems, and model mechanisms supply bounded candidate computation and expose selection, extra compute, residuals, and failure routes.

Claim Ledgers, Evidence States, Spinoza, Executable Specifications, Lean, and proof-carrying contracts supply claim identity, support transitions, proof scope, assumptions, countermodels, and non-claims.
Runtime Adapters, Security Kernel, Digital SCIFs, Supply Chain, Weight Custody, and Hives supply tool, credential, isolation, artifact, hardware, node, and external-effect enforcement.
Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, Replay, and Data Engines supply parentage, canonical state, transformation, descendant, custody, deletion, and replay identities.

Benchmarks, Adversarial Evaluation, Safety Cases, Thresholds, and Scalable Oversight supply observation, challenge, assurance, elicitation, hazard, and evaluator records without inheriting release authority.
Policy Optimization, Capability Replacement, Recursive Improvement, and Open-Ended Improvement supply update, candidate, rollback, causal, and lifecycle records without self-promoting.

Stable Capability Fields, Readiness, Residual Escrow, Incident, Release, and Publication supply qualification, terminal decision, residual acceptance, external mutation, attestation, and rollback authority.
Resource Economics, Steward Agents, governance, and contribution systems supply budgets, costs, coordination, project continuity, representation, conflict, appeal, fork, exit, and sunset records.

The Living Book consumes adjudicated cross-layer traces, source and proof boundaries, corrections, negative results, and release identities while keeping the reference architecture a join contract rather than a super-owner.
invariants: Integration never creates a super-owner: every layer retains its exact authority, semantics, artifacts, failure routes, residuals, and terminal decisions.
Every trace is scoped to exact request, intent, authorities, owners, artifacts, state, effects, evaluator, consumers, environments, dependencies, and time.
No downstream handoff widens authority; revocation, denial, expiry, quarantine, or exception reaches the actuator before or at effect time under the declared precedence.
Every named layer joins its typed input and parent artifacts to the canonical state transition and material output it claims to own; interface presence is insufficient.
Canonical state remains authoritative over every projection, and projection drift, stale reads, missing writes, success-shaped identifiers, and ignored errors remain visible.
The request-to-effect receipt binds request, policy, canonical state, material effect, terminal acknowledgement, evaluator, evidence transition, residual, and decision identities and digests.
No material effect is accepted without a request and authority parent, and no terminal success is recorded without an observed effect or exact no-effect acknowledgement.
Evaluator identity, method, implementation, data, dependencies, conflicts, calibration, disagreement, blind spots, and independence class remain visible before any claim boundary.
Evidence changes only through accepted transitions; fixtures, validators, theorem passes, replay, projections, approvals, release activity, and report coherence cannot promote support by themselves.

Residuals are conserved: every created or discovered residual is open, transferred with an accepting owner, discharged with evidence, superseded with lineage, or archived without being silently erased.
Causal order and lifecycle state remain valid across intent, authority, plan, context, route, work, effect, observation, receipt, evaluation, evidence, release, rollback, and correction.
Approved, blocked, failed, timed-out, revoked, quarantined, rolled-back, appealed, and abandoned paths remain in complete denominators and retain terminal acknowledgements.
Self-improvement, learning, replacement, release, and deployment remain downstream of their own evidence, authority, readiness, residual, rollback, and monitoring gates.

Rollback is effect-complete only when canonical state, artifacts, projections, credentials, external effects, descendants, evidence, releases, notifications, and reliance are restored or residualized.
Natural usefulness, quality, safety, rights, latency, resources, governance burden, false blocking, failures, recovery, residuals, and total cost remain visible together.
Every claim is bounded to the exact joined path and cannot transfer across models, tools, tasks, organizations, infrastructures, legal regimes, environments, or time without new evidence.
Interface, schema, policy, evaluator, dependency, authority, threat, consumer, environment, or time changes expire affected joins and trigger revalidation.

Schema validity, a coherent diagram, deterministic fixtures, local slices, finite Lean reductions, green builds, and validator counts establish only their exact boundaries, never whole-stack execution, safety, capability, deployment, transfer, or SOTA.
failure_modes: Interface theater names layers, services, tools, schemas, or gates whose outputs never causally reach the material effect.
Super-owner collapse lets the integrator, shared prompt, controller, steward, or trace service silently inherit planning, evidence, governance, release, or legal authority.
Handoff laundering converts an untyped summary, hidden prompt state, cache entry, dashboard field, or success-shaped identifier into a trusted cross-layer artifact.
Planning/execution collapse lets the planner execute, the executor reinterpret goals, or a work queue claim completion without joined effects and receipts.
Canonical/projection inversion lets dashboards, reports, caches, indexes, summaries, or claim views overwrite or conceal authoritative state.
Request-to-effect drift joins the wrong request, policy, state, effect, receipt, evaluator, evidence transition, residual, or terminal decision.
Orphan effects occur without request or authority parents, while phantom completions acknowledge success without an observed material effect.
Authority drift, revocation races, exception laundering, or downstream widening allow an effect after permission narrowed, expired, or was withdrawn.
Evaluator laundering hides shared models, prompts, data, tools, organizations, implementations, selection, conflicts, or blind spots behind an independence label.
Evidence laundering treats a fixture, proof, replay, approval, coherent report, benchmark score, release, or green validator as support for a broader semantic or empirical claim.
Residual erasure drops failures, unknowns, rejected paths, open obligations, outside-model uncertainty, or downstream effects from the joined trace.
Replay theater reproduces recorded inputs or outputs without reproducing causal state, external effects, authority timing, evaluator conditions, or terminal acknowledgement.
Rollback theater restores a repository or checkpoint while projections, credentials, services, descendants, releases, notifications, evidence, or reliance remain changed.
Monolithic-loop opacity hides role conflicts, state mutation, tool effects, selection, retries, exceptions, and discarded candidates inside one agent transcript.
Happy-path integration omits blocked, failed, revoked, quarantined, timed-out, appealed, and rolled-back paths or uses weak attacks that cannot falsify the joins.
Integration overhead and false blocking consume latency, compute, reviewer attention, and user value without improving semantic preservation, recovery, or safety.
Embedded-model closure treats the finite trace as an omniscient world model and ignores logical uncertainty, self-reference, subsystem objectives, and outside-model effects.

Unsupported transfer generalizes a local repository slice, historical-project fixture, or source-reported architecture across models, services, tasks, organizations, infrastructures, legal regimes, environments, or time.
chapters.minimal_implementation: One trace schema and valid protocol fixture; a deterministic reference-trace harness with three valid and six expected-invalid fixtures; one local validator replay bound to thirteen tracked artifact digests; one blocked five-project name-to-effect lineage with eight rejecting mutations; one disposable governed repository-change slice over nine scenarios and eight named attacks where the direct baseline produced eight false accepts while the governed path produced zero false accepts, zero unsafe releases, and three rollback attempts; one finite invariant extraction over three authority handoffs, three timed effects, nine evidence events, two residuals created, one discharged, one open, and four rejected mutations; and sixteen Lean declarations across two modules. No language-model planning or generation, deployed multi-service runtime, production authorization or rollback service, independent evaluator, natural heterogeneous workload, external reproduction, transfer, whole-stack deployment, or chapter-core support effect exists.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature operational contract requires an argument-exit campaign that executes natural heterogeneous tasks through independently operated layer services and matched direct, monolithic-agent, workflow-orchestrator, service-composition, and no-governance baselines with equal models, tools, context, compute, tuning, and retries. It must test semantic preservation, authority monotonicity and revocation races, canonical/projection integrity, material-effect and terminal-receipt completeness, evaluator independence, evidence-transition integrity, residual conservation, lifecycle and bounded liveness, incident containment, effect-complete rollback, correction propagation, learning and release gates, embedded/outside-model residuals, useful throughput, failures, latency, resources, governance burden, and total cost under natural and adversarial paths. Independent implementers, operators, evaluators, institutions, infrastructures, legal regimes, and transfer environments must reproduce all positive, negative, null, narrowed, blocked, and refuted outcomes. Until then the Cross-Layer Trace Join Contract is not evidence of a deployed integrated stack, semantic or governance correctness, safety, capability, readiness, release, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.

proof_targets.target: Every accepted finite cross-layer trace joins exact parent artifacts and canonical state across typed layers, preserves the authority ceiling, and cannot widen active authority.

A cross-layer transition with a parent/state fork, missing governance gate, residual erasure, effect after revocation, unacknowledged terminal effect, missing receipt, or incomplete rollback is rejected or contained in quarantine.

The source-anchored finite consumer composes approved, blocked, rolled-back, and quarantined traces while preserving joined artifact/state lineage, authority, effect acknowledgement, evidence, residual, terminal-receipt, and no-promotion boundaries and rejecting targeted mutations.

open_evidence_gaps: No natural heterogeneous campaign has compared the joined stack with matched direct, monolithic-agent, workflow-orchestrator, service-composition, and no-governance baselines under equal models, tools, context, compute, tuning, retries, frozen authority, and complete denominators.

No deployed multi-service trace jointly establishes semantic preservation, authority timing, canonical/projection integrity, effect and receipt completeness, evaluator independence, evidence and residual integrity, lifecycle and bounded liveness, incident containment, effect-complete rollback, correction propagation, embedded residuals, useful throughput, failures, latency, governance burden, and total cost.

No independent implementer, operator, evaluator, institution, infrastructure, legal regime, or transfer environment has reproduced the local fixture and repository-slice results or established whole-stack execution, safety, capability, readiness, release, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.

chapters.title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
chapters.problem: The book needs a concrete implementation-reference chapter for how the ASI stack can be operated as report-first local machinery instead of only conceptual layer diagrams.
chapters.insufficient: A prototype roadmap is not enough unless readers can see how goals, plans, arms, reports, gates, residuals, checkpoints, compute, and operator surfaces interact in a working research system.
chapters.core_claim: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference owns a source-project-, pinned-revision-, report-family-, command-, environment-, artifact-, lineage-, evidence-state-, replay-, public-safety-, publication-permission-, reviewer-, consumer-, and time-specific Implementation-Reference Evidence Packet: it binds every imported or replayed report, configuration, ledger, work-board summary, registry, gate, crosswalk, trace, retained artifact, command, environment note, digest, missing artifact, decision, residual, and non-claim to source-note-only, imported, replay-ready, replay-failed, locally reproduced, stale, runtime-blocked, or archived lineage; dashboards and latest files are projections only, and no GREEN gate, complete registry, module card, pointer row, metadata snapshot, parity manifest, command replay, fixture, theorem, or sanitized import alone establishes current runtime truth, clean live replay, model quality, capability, benchmark validity, safety, deployment, support, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.
