mechanism: Freeze the source project, pinned revision, report families, publication permissions, public/private boundary, intended consumers, environment, and time window before importing any implementation-reference claim.

Inventory reports, configurations, tools, work boards, registries, ledgers, checkpoints, retained artifacts, dashboards, generators, and responsible owners without treating inventory presence as operational proof.
Bind every source note or local path to its digest, version, timestamp, generator, exact replay command, environment, dependencies, input boundary, expected output class, and failure behavior.

Classify every artifact as source-note-only, imported, artifact-missing, replay-ready, replay-failed, locally reproduced, stale, runtime-blocked, or archived lineage, preserving transitions and their adjudicators.

Sanitize public packets under an explicit permission boundary with zero training rows, raw prompts, tests, solutions, traces, scores, candidate code, private paths, credentials, or payloads unless separately authorized.
Keep dashboards, latest files, pointer tables, summaries, and status labels as projections over retained canonical artifacts; never allow a projection to replace lineage or currentness checks.

Crosswalk each ASI Stack layer and material claim to the exact Theseus report, configuration, tool, gate, residual, command, artifact, missing artifact, evidence state, and non-claim that bears on it.
Bind originating intent, approval, delegated agency, resource bounds, rollback requirement, work-board item, review decision, and residual to each imported or replayed packet.
Replay eligible commands in locked environments and retain stdout or structured output, exit status, artifact digests, failures, duration, resource use, dependency state, and any repository mutation.

Validate report-bundle completeness, lineage, crosswalk coverage, gate mapping, work contracts, visible gaps, public-safety boundaries, and intervention ordering with valid and expected-invalid fixtures.
Import static gates, registries, module summaries, crosswalks, metadata snapshots, parity manifests, and traces only at their exact pinned digest, revision, state, and stated non-claim boundary.

Compare packet fields with model cards, dataset datasheets, AI-service FactSheets, and reproducibility programs while preserving that documentation standards are comparators rather than local evidence.
Keep benchmark, calibration, private, and public partitions, external inference, teacher use, task access, training-row status, selection, retries, and accepted no-promotion decisions visible.
Gate capability, self-evolution, heavy training, deployment, and chapter-core promotion on separate evidence transitions rather than inheriting them from a GREEN report or complete packet.
Retain negative, null, failed, stale, missing, blocked, sanitized-only, no-promotion, and superseded results with complete denominators and exact lineage.
Propagate correction, revocation, expiry, staleness, permission changes, and evidence downgrade through crosswalks, appendices, dashboards, consumers, releases, and descendant claims.

Monitor replay age, dependency drift, missing artifacts, stale snapshots, public-safety violations, private-data pressure, promotion pressure, failures, latency, resource use, review burden, and total cost.

Expire, replace, archive, or retire the packet when the source project, revision, report format, generator, command, environment, dependency, permission, reviewer, consumer, threat, or time bound changes materially.
interfaces: The pinned Project Theseus checkout, source notes, report generators, configurations, tools, registries, ledgers, work board, checkpoints, and retained artifacts supply source-project truth at exact revisions.
The source inventory, source notes, source-to-chapter matrix, source queue, and Appendix G supply bibliographic and local-project provenance without promoting imported claims.
Human Intent, the plan compiler, agency contracts, work contracts, and authority records supply goal, plan, permission, resource, rollback, and acceptance boundaries.
Labor OS, the durable work board, operator command vocabulary, node registry, Artifact Steward agents, and feedback routes supply owned work and continuity records.
Artifact Graphs, supply-chain controls, data engines, weight custody, residual escrow, and replay supply identity, parentage, custody, retention, deletion, and lineage surfaces.

Benchmarks, generation modes, simulation, routing, specialist arms, external-inference controls, and teacher governance supply workload and model-computation records without inheriting evidence authority.

Claim Ledgers, Evidence States, formal methods, proof-carrying contracts, Appendices C and E, and accepted evidence transitions supply claim identity, proof scope, support state, defeaters, and non-claims.

Readiness, Stable Capability Fields, Capability Replacement, Recursive Improvement, and self-evolution gates consume exact packet facts without converting local report state into capability or release claims.
Security Kernel, runtime adapters, Digital SCIFs, public-safety filters, rights records, and publication permissions constrain commands, payloads, environments, and disclosure.
Resource Economics and compute governance supply budgets, accelerator surfaces, external-service accounting, duration, energy or compute records, and cost boundaries.
Model cards, datasheets, FactSheets, and reproducibility programs provide external reporting and artifact-review comparators without certifying Project Theseus.
The Living Book, outline, appendices, roadmap, status records, release surfaces, and readers consume only adjudicated packet claims and their currentness boundaries.
invariants: Every packet is scoped to an exact source project, pinned revision, report family, command, environment, artifact set, permission, reviewer, consumer, dependency set, and time window.
A retained report artifact, not dashboard prose, a latest-file alias, pointer table, status badge, or chapter summary, owns the imported fact at its exact digest.
A current-state claim requires a current artifact or clean bounded replay; a historical import never silently becomes current runtime truth.
Latest files and regenerated summaries never erase prior versions, failed runs, stale snapshots, corrected claims, negative controls, or accepted no-promotion decisions.
Every imported or replayed fact remains bound to source path or note, revision, digest, generator, exact command, environment, dependency boundary, timestamp, and reviewer decision.
Source-note-only, imported, artifact-missing, replay-ready, replay-failed, locally reproduced, stale, runtime-blocked, and archived-lineage states remain distinct and machine-visible.
Sanitization proves only that the selected packet satisfies its declared public boundary; it does not establish source truth, completeness, reproducibility, capability, safety, or fitness.

Public packets contain zero training rows and zero raw prompts, tests, solutions, traces, scores, candidate code, private paths, credentials, or payloads unless exact permission and purpose are recorded.
Private, public, benchmark, and calibration partitions plus external inference, teacher use, selection, retries, and task access remain visible wherever they bear on interpretation.
Missing artifacts, stale snapshots, failed commands, runtime blockers, dirty checkouts, dependency drift, and unresolved gaps remain visible and block broader currentness or support claims.

GREEN, complete, ready, parity, wired, pointer, registered, retained, replayed, or current labels never cross-promote into model quality, capability, benchmark validity, safety, deployment, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.
Architecture, readiness, resource, public-safety, benchmark, and promotion gates are necessary within their declared scope but never sufficient for chapter-core support.

A report bundle is implementation-reference-shaped only when it includes goal and work contracts, plan or compiler artifact, gate record, residual, artifact lineage, replay state, review note, publication boundary, and non-claims.
Private source artifacts and public-safe packet projections stay separated; redaction is explicit, loss is recorded, and public consumers cannot infer access to private evidence.
Human approvals, delegated agency, work-board ownership, kill and rollback authority, reviewer identity, conflicts, and accepted terminal decisions remain visible.
Corrections, revocations, expiry, staleness, permission changes, and evidence downgrades propagate to every crosswalk, appendix, dashboard, consumer, release, and descendant claim.
Replay age, duration, resource use, external service use, failure count, review burden, storage, and total cost remain visible beside positive results.

Finite record implications, fixture normalizations, validator passes, report coherence, and theorem counts establish only their exact record boundaries, not source truth or empirical system performance.
failure_modes: Latest-file overwrite or dashboard theater replaces canonical lineage, hides stale or failed runs, and makes a projection look like source truth.
Report-shape laundering treats a complete schema, polished packet, checklist, or documentation standard as proof that the underlying report or system is correct.
Stale-snapshot currentness promotes historical work-board, registry, gate, readiness, or runtime metadata as a statement about the current project.
Digest, path, revision, generator, command, environment, or dependency drift joins an imported fact to the wrong artifact or makes replay irreproducible.
Sanitized-import truth laundering treats successful redaction and fixture validation as verification of private source contents or broader implementation claims.
Private-payload leakage exports prompts, tests, solutions, traces, scores, candidate code, credentials, private paths, training rows, or other unauthorized material.
Benchmark-headline laundering promotes task counts, zero regressions, gate passes, speed warnings, or useful-solution summaries without tasks, denominators, baselines, selection, and uncertainty.
GREEN-gate promotion converts architecture or readiness compliance into capability, model quality, safety, deployment, or training-success evidence.
Module, registry, work-board, pointer, or lifecycle completeness becomes a claim that the corresponding runtime behavior exists or works correctly.
Accelerator-parity manifest promotion converts named surfaces and zero hard failures into full CUDA, MLX, Metal, scheduler, or production-routing parity.
Pointer-crosswalk promotion converts a link, source-sync decision, or chapter mapping into truth of the referenced artifact or claim.
Replay theater reruns only book-side validators, cached outputs, metadata, or a narrow gate while implying clean live whole-project reproduction.
Teacher-use or external-inference hiding makes locally governed work, model independence, benchmark validity, or training provenance look stronger than it is.
Artifact-retention promotion converts exact-hash replay, compression, storage, or artifact-graph records into behavioral correctness, model quality, or deployed-system proof.
Self-evolution promotion advances architecture, training, capability, or deployment claims from intervention-ladder compliance without causal outcome evidence.
Negative, null, stale, missing, runtime-blocked, rejected, and no-promotion artifacts are suppressed from denominators or overwritten by later positive-looking summaries.
Publication-permission drift exposes private material or lets a consumer infer rights, approval, currentness, review, or certification that was never granted.
Unsupported transfer generalizes local Project Theseus reports across models, tasks, institutions, infrastructures, legal regimes, environments, reviewers, or time.
chapters.minimal_implementation: Ten exact source-to-claim mappings; twelve proof targets implemented by fifty-four Lean declarations that are finite record implications, reductions, fixture normalizations, and explicit overclaim controls rather than empirical projections; one report-crosswalk schema and fixture; a static 14/14 architecture-gate import; an 18-mode and 13-comparison generation-mode import with zero promotable comparisons; a two-command ten-artifact support replay with no transition; one valid and seven invalid report-bundle audit cases; a metadata-only 64-task public bundle with zero public training rows; a selected support aggregate with four command replays, sixteen artifact digests, sixty-eight task records, fourteen rejected controls, and two accepted no-promotion decisions; bounded artifact-retention, 22-module definition-of-done, 5,662-path registry, 53-pointer crosswalk, stale 130-task work-board, 19-record-type assistant trace, seven-surface accelerator parity, and one clean-checkout pre-training-readiness currentness imports. Several narrow non-core imports are prototype-backed, but no clean live whole-project replay, current work-board or runtime proof, model-quality or capability result, benchmark-validity or useful-throughput result, production routing, deployment, safety, chapter-core support effect, independent reproduction, or transfer exists.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature operational contract requires an argument-exit campaign that freezes a public-safe Project Theseus release or report bundle and supports independent clean-room replay from documented commands, environments, dependencies, artifact digests, current work-board and registry state, permissions, and retained failures. It must execute natural workloads and matched ordinary baselines through actual model, routing, training, tool, benchmark, resource, operator, rollback, and self-evolution paths; disclose teacher and external-inference use; measure useful throughput, quality, false promotion and blocking, safety, currentness, failures, latency, compute, storage, review burden, recovery, residuals, and total cost; and test staleness, digest drift, private-payload pressure, gate and dashboard laundering, missing artifacts, negative-result retention, and correction propagation. Independent implementers, operators, artifact reviewers, evaluators, institutions, infrastructures, legal regimes, and transfer settings must reproduce all positive, negative, null, narrowed, blocked, and refuted outcomes over time. Until then the Implementation-Reference Evidence Packet is not proof of current Project Theseus behavior, capability, model quality, benchmark superiority, safety, deployment, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.

proof_targets.target: An implementation-reference claim names the report, config, or tool surface and does not rely on dashboard prose alone.
A capability or self-evolution promotion is blocked when required gate reports are absent or failing.

A public-safe Theseus report-bundle audit fixture is accepted only when bundle components, replay-readiness rows, crosswalk rows, gate mappings, work-board contract fields, visible artifact gaps, intervention-ladder ordering, and no-promotion boundaries are complete.

A public-safe Theseus public task-bundle import fixture is accepted only when source reports, metadata-only public task boundaries, zero public training rows, zero exported prompts/tests/solutions/traces/scores/candidate code, operator and benchmark gates, visible artifact gaps, no clean-live-replay claim, and no-promotion boundaries are complete.

The selected Theseus/Fast support-lane aggregate carries 4 command replays, 16 tracked artifacts, 68 public task records, 14 expected-invalid or rejected controls, 2 accepted no-promotion decisions, the four included support surfaces, and explicit no-clean-live-replay, no-model-quality, no-generation-speed, no-useful-solution-model, no-chapter-core-promotion, and no-support-state-promotion boundaries.

A sanitized Project Theseus artifact-retention replay import fixture records exact-hash retained-artifact replay, public-safe redaction, zero public training rows, zero external inference calls, required record counts, and rejects hash mismatch or chapter-core promotion overclaim.

A sanitized Project Theseus module definition-of-done import fixture records a GREEN gate, 22 ready module records, 0 hard gaps, 0 warnings, source-backlog routing, public-safety boundaries, and rejects chapter-core promotion or capability overclaim.

A sanitized Project Theseus project-registry import fixture records 5,662 registered paths, 24 owned lifecycle surfaces, complete registry coverage, zero unregistered active sources, zero stale or missing report outputs, zero generated source artifacts, zero registry-governance violations, public-safety boundaries, and rejects unregistered-source, clean-live-replay, private-payload, and chapter-core-promotion overclaims.

A sanitized Project Theseus assistant reference-trace import fixture records 19 required trace record types, 27/27 gates, 2,203 route-validator view records, 12 selected VCM pages, zero public training rows, zero external inference calls, public-safety boundaries, and rejects missing-hop, private-payload, clean-live-replay, model-quality, benchmark-headline, and chapter-core-promotion overclaims.

A sanitized Project Theseus accelerator parity manifest import fixture records 7 surfaces, 7 MLX report summaries, 4 Metal report summaries, 4 artifact manifests, 4 scheduler-canary surfaces, zero hard failures, zero public training rows, zero external inference calls, no teacher use, locked model promotion and production routing, public-safety boundaries, and rejects full-parity, production-routing, model-promotion, and chapter-core-promotion overclaims.

A sanitized Project Theseus book-to-Theseus crosswalk import fixture records trigger state `GREEN`, 53 public-safe pointer rows, 20 backlog cards, 134 source-sync review decisions, public-safety boundaries, and rejects source-sync failure, public-safety failure, clean-live-replay overclaim, and chapter-core-promotion overclaim.

A sanitized Project Theseus work-board metadata import fixture records 130 durable task rows, 412 event rows, 133 evidence rows, five SQLite tables, stale-snapshot currentness blocking, public-safety boundaries, and rejects clean-live-replay, private-payload, public-training-row, and chapter-core-promotion overclaims.

chapters.title: Prototype Roadmap
chapters.problem: The architecture needs a build sequence that does not introduce agency or self-improvement before auditability and governance are credible.
chapters.insufficient: A roadmap that jumps to autonomous improvement skips source matrices, artifact graphs, claim ledgers, tests, and authority controls.
chapters.core_claim: Prototype Roadmap owns a program-, roadmap-, phase-, dependency-, artifact-, acceptance-gate-, authority-, evaluator-, evidence-transition-, phase-debt-, residual-, rollback-, reviewer-, consumer-, environment-, and time-specific Evidence-Gated Phase Unlock Contract: it binds every proposed phase to prerequisites, allowed work state, required artifacts, commands, environment, resource bounds, gates, independent evaluation, residuals, debt, rollback, retirement, evidence effect, and non-claims before later work may research, demo, integrate, promote, or release; no roadmap row, milestone, source report, dashboard, task count, passing fixture, theorem, validator, build, or locally useful prototype alone establishes phase completion, safe dependency order, capability, governance effectiveness, deployment, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.

claim_source_mappings.mapped_support: Supports starting the roadmap with durable artifacts, command contracts, claim ledgers, residuals, feedback, tools, benchmarks, and regression coverage before higher-agency layers are introduced.
claim_source_mappings.limits: Does not prove that any roadmap phase has been implemented, accepted, validated, or used to promote a capability claim.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for staged minimum viable VIEA, durable artifacts, command contracts, claim/support-state discipline, critique and release records, residuals, benchmark/regression coverage, and the small first vertical slice before broader specialists, workflow compilation, ratchets, and runtime adapters; no completed roadmap phase is promoted.

claim_source_mappings.limits: No benchmark harness, phase acceptance benchmark, mutation test, holdout run, contamination audit, empirical run, or phase acceptance result is recorded here.
claim_source_mappings.passage_review_note: Reviewed local raw cache for benchmark frontier-to-regression phasing, residual diagnosis, anti-Goodhart safeguards, mutation, hidden holdouts, benchmark/model ledgers, architecture-change caution, and the staged implementation phases for benchmark ledgers, model ledgers, residual analysis, diagnostic ladders, and frontier expansion; no benchmark run or acceptance result is promoted.

mechanism: Freeze the program objective, roadmap version, candidate phases, dependency graph, authority ceiling, consumers, environments, resource envelope, time horizon, and explicit non-claims before execution.

Assign stable identities to every phase, prerequisite, artifact, command, environment, gate, evaluator, evidence transition, residual, debt item, rollback path, retirement condition, reviewer, and descendant unlock.

Classify phases as proposed, blocked, research-only, demo-eligible, integration-eligible, promotion-eligible, release-eligible, failed, rolled-back, retired, or superseded without collapsing those states.
Build evidence infrastructure first: source inventory, claim atoms, artifact graph, lineage, schemas, validators, negative controls, replay, corrections, rights, and release identity.
Add intent, authority, work contracts, planning, context, typed jobs, runtime adapters, observability, and rollback only after their evidence and refusal surfaces exist.
Add routing, deliberation, procedural memory, benchmarks, simulation, policy updates, data updates, and capability replacement only under matched evaluation, causal, resource, and recovery gates.

Delay open-ended or recursive improvement, autonomous teacher use, high-risk training, broad tool authority, and external deployment until independent evaluator, containment, rollback, residual, and terminal-governance gates pass.
Express every dependency edge as typed required input, allowed work state, blocking condition, acceptance predicate, artifact output, authority delta, evidence effect, rollback, and expiry.
Issue a phase work contract naming deliverables, owners, commands, environment, resources, expected observations, stop conditions, review path, completion test, and forbidden claims.
Validate phase packets with schema, semantic, executable, negative, mutation, replay, proof, rights, publication, and release checks appropriate to the claimed unlock.
Require evaluator identity, implementation, data, independence class, conflicts, calibration, disagreement, blind spots, and acceptance thresholds before promotion-sensitive phases.

Record phase debt for every shortcut, missing source, provisional gate, manual workaround, private artifact, weak baseline, incomplete test, open incident, or unsupported claim, with owner, severity, due condition, and retirement rule.
Permit bounded research and demos under failed or provisional gates only when isolation, non-promotion, data, effect, cost, cleanup, and sunset boundaries are explicit.

Accept phase completion only from observed required artifacts and gates plus an authorized terminal decision; milestone names, progress percentages, green dashboards, and useful demos are insufficient.
Propagate failure, revocation, correction, expiry, rollback, evidence downgrade, and retired debt through all dependent phases, artifacts, claims, releases, consumers, and descendants.

Compare the governed dependency controller with matched calendar, feature-backlog, milestone, stage-gate, no-gate, and strongest ordinary program-management baselines under equal teams, tasks, tools, resources, and time.

Monitor useful throughput, time-to-evidence, false unlocks, false blocks, rework, incidents, residual age, debt, rollback closure, reviewer burden, compute, storage, opportunity cost, and total program cost.

Expire, replace, archive, or retire phase contracts when objectives, dependencies, interfaces, artifacts, gates, evaluators, authority, threats, resources, consumers, environments, or time bounds change materially.
interfaces: Human Intent, Constitutional Substrate, Moral Uncertainty, Agency Rights, and System Authority supply legitimate objectives, affected-party constraints, authority ceilings, exception, appeal, stop, and terminal decisions.

Source Inventory, Claim Ledgers, Evidence States, Artifact Graphs, Supply Chain, rights records, and the Living Book supply provenance, artifact identity, support state, corrections, and public truth.
Intent-to-Execution Contracts, planning, semantic IR, context systems, Labor OS, and Artifact Steward agents supply typed phase work, dependencies, ownership, continuity, and acceptance packets.
Runtime Adapters, Security Kernel, Digital SCIFs, observability, audit, replay, and incident systems supply bounded effects, containment, receipts, recovery, and rollback.
Routing, specialist cores, deliberation, procedural memory, mathematical/search substrates, and compact model systems supply candidate computation without inheriting unlock authority.
Benchmarks, behavioral tests, simulation, adversarial evaluation, scalable oversight, safety cases, and formal methods supply evaluation lanes without deciding phase promotion alone.

Policy Optimization, data updates, unlearning, Capability Replacement, Recursive Improvement, and Open-Ended Improvement consume accepted lower-phase artifacts and produce separate causal and rollback obligations.
Readiness, Stable Capability Fields, residual escrow, quarantine, release, deployment, and publication supply qualification and terminal transition authority.
Resource Economics, compute governance, weight custody, hardware roots, Hives, and external services supply budgets, substrates, custody, availability, and cost records.

Project Theseus, Circle contracts, VIEA, VCM, PlanForge, Benchmaxxing, SCF, BeastBrain, BugBrain, and MoECOT supply implementation lineage and candidate phase surfaces without local result inheritance.
NIST AI RMF, extreme-risk evaluation, Dafny, Copilot, SHOP2, SWE-bench, MMLU, CheckList, CodeBLEU, and QLoRA supply external gate and experiment comparators without certifying the roadmap.
Roadmap status, changelog, dashboards, releases, readers, builders, reviewers, and successor roadmaps consume projections of canonical phase records and cannot overwrite them.
invariants: Every phase contract is scoped to exact program, roadmap version, phase, dependencies, artifacts, commands, environment, resources, authority, evaluators, reviewers, consumers, and time.
Dependency direction is acyclic or explicitly iterative under bounded loop rules; later phases cannot silently satisfy their own prerequisites.
Evidence infrastructure and authority stops precede capability, broad agency, irreversible effects, deployment, and self-improvement authority.
Proposed, blocked, research-only, demo, integrate, promote, release, failed, rolled-back, retired, and superseded states remain distinct.
Every unlock cites observed required artifacts, passing gates, accepted residuals, retired or bounded debt, and an authorized terminal decision.
A phase milestone, task closure, progress percentage, dashboard, report, demo, build, fixture, theorem, or validator never establishes completion or evidence promotion alone.
Research or demo permission never implies integration, promotion, release, production routing, training authorization, or broader tool authority.
Authority is monotone non-widening across dependencies; denial, revocation, expiry, quarantine, or stop reaches every dependent phase before effect.
Evaluator identity, implementation, data, dependence, conflicts, calibration, thresholds, disagreement, and blind spots remain visible for promotion-sensitive gates.
Phase debt is conserved until discharged, transferred to an accepting owner, superseded with lineage, or retired by its declared condition; it cannot disappear from summaries.
Failures, null results, skipped work, manual workarounds, private artifacts, provisional gates, weak baselines, incidents, and abandoned phases remain in complete denominators.
Support changes only through accepted evidence transitions; a roadmap cannot grant support state, capability, safety, readiness, deployment, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.
Rollback restores or residualizes phase state, artifacts, authority, credentials, services, data, descendants, evidence, releases, notifications, and downstream reliance.
Corrections, revocations, expiry, rollback, downgrade, and debt changes propagate through every dependency, claim, consumer, release, and successor roadmap.
Useful throughput, false unlocks, false blocks, quality, safety, rework, incidents, latency, resources, reviewer burden, opportunity cost, residuals, debt, and total cost remain visible together.
Every result is bounded to exact phase graph, implementation, team, task, tool, model, evaluator, organization, infrastructure, legal regime, environment, and time.
Changed objectives, dependencies, interfaces, gates, evaluators, authority, threats, resources, consumers, environments, or time expire affected phase contracts and require revalidation.

Finite predicates, route reductions, fixture bridges, passing registries, phase tables, and coherent narratives prove only their exact record boundaries, never a functioning build controller or completed program.
failure_modes: Agency-first sequencing grants tools, execution, deployment, training, or self-improvement authority before evidence, containment, rollback, and terminal governance exist.
Dependency inversion lets a later phase manufacture, assume, or waive its own prerequisite or treat a cyclic dependency as progress.
Roadmap-as-evidence laundering treats rows, dates, percentages, status colors, task counts, reports, demos, builds, fixtures, or proofs as completed empirical outcomes.
Prototype theater presents a board, dashboard, agent loop, retrieval store, benchmark, or generated report without its acceptance packet, effects, failures, and evidence transition.
Milestone compression hides multiple hard gates inside labels such as agentic runtime, memory, alignment, readiness, or self-improvement.
Gate theater uses weak, stale, self-authored, circular, outcome-aware, or unconsumed checks that cannot block the intended failure.
Evaluator laundering hides shared models, data, prompts, tools, authors, organizations, selection, conflicts, or blind spots behind an independence label.
Phase-state collapse lets research-only or demo-eligible work silently integrate, promote, release, route in production, train, or widen authority.
Evidence-transition laundering lets a phase controller grant support, capability, safety, readiness, deployment, or SOTA status without the claim authority.
Phase-debt erasure drops shortcuts, missing sources, provisional gates, manual workarounds, private artifacts, weak baselines, incomplete tests, or incidents from later phases.
Happy-path sequencing omits rejected, blocked, failed, rolled-back, retired, superseded, appealed, and abandoned phases from denominators.
Source-result inheritance converts VIEA, Theseus, Circle, PlanForge, VCM, benchmark, formal-method, or external-paper results into local phase acceptance.
Rollback theater restores a repository or task status while authority, credentials, services, data, artifacts, descendants, evidence, releases, notifications, or reliance remain changed.
Operator-convenience drift weakens allowlists, approvals, TTLs, kill switches, resource ceilings, reviews, or non-claims to keep a useful prototype moving.
Roadmap staleness retains obsolete dependencies, owners, gates, commands, environments, threats, resources, or consumer assumptions after the program changes.
Over-gating and false blocking consume time, compute, reviewer attention, opportunity, and useful output without improving evidence, safety, recovery, or governance.
Self-improvement ratchet failure permits architecture, teacher, policy, data, or capability changes whose causal effect, rollback, containment, and residuals are unknown.
Unsupported transfer generalizes one local project sequence across teams, models, tasks, organizations, infrastructures, legal regimes, environments, or time.
chapters.minimal_implementation: Twenty-nine exact source-to-claim mappings; one prototype phase record schema and public-safe fixture; a deterministic dependency-gate harness with two valid fixtures and six expected-invalid controls covering missing artifacts, dependency inversion, self-improvement without an independent evaluator, support promotion without an evidence transition, phase debt without retirement, and missing non-claims; three proof targets implemented by eleven Lean declarations that reduce hand-authored finite predicates, route functions, and fixture summaries; and one synthetic readiness/residual expired-evidence rerun/reject check. No real phase is accepted or complete, no deployed build controller exists, no natural program workload or matched baseline has run, and no chapter-core support effect, capability, safety, deployment, independent reproduction, or transfer is established.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature argument-exit campaign must prospectively freeze a real multi-phase program, its dependency graph, teams, tasks, tools, models, resources, gates, evaluators, debt policy, rollback, stopping rules, and outcomes before execution. It must compare the Evidence-Gated Phase Unlock Contract with matched calendar, feature-backlog, milestone, stage-gate, no-gate, and strongest ordinary program-management baselines under equal opportunities; exercise natural builds plus dependency inversion, stale gates, evaluator dependence, research-to-release leakage, debt erasure, revocation, incident, correction, and effect-complete rollback attacks; and jointly measure useful throughput, time-to-evidence, quality, false unlocks and blocks, rework, incidents, recovery, residuals, debt, reviewer burden, compute, storage, opportunity cost, and total cost. Causal ablations must identify which gates help or harm, and independent teams, reviewers, institutions, infrastructures, legal regimes, environments, and successor programs must reproduce and transfer positive, negative, null, narrowed, blocked, and refuted results. Until then the contract is not a functioning build controller, proof of correct sequencing, capability, governance effectiveness, safety, readiness, deployment, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.

proof_targets.target: A roadmap phase can unlock a dependent phase only after acceptance gates pass.
A phase milestone cannot promote a claim without evidence artifacts.

A modeled prototype phase-gate fixture bridge accepts only the complete public-safe fixture summary and rejects missing non-claim boundaries while preserving no support-state promotion and no phase-completion claim.

chapters.title: Living Book Methodology
chapters.problem: The book itself must remain a living technical system rather than a static anthology.
chapters.insufficient: Static manuscripts cannot show source additions, claim-state movement, deprecations, proof updates, render status, or test history.
chapters.core_claim: Living Book Methodology owns a book-, edition-, change-, source-, claim-, proof-, test-, render-, audience-, derivative-, release-, rights-, reviewer-, consumer-, environment-, and time-specific Evidence-Preserving Publication Transaction: it binds every substantive intake, structural edit, claim change, proof or test change, render, reader or audio projection, release, correction, rollback, and successor handoff to canonical source state, provenance, authority, validation, evidence effect, residuals, non-claims, and immutable lineage; generated scaffolds, green validators, theorem builds, successful renders, local format artifacts, publication activity, or a polished release never by themselves establish source interpretation, editorial quality, accessibility, reader approval, chapter truth, capability, safety, external reproduction, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.

mechanism: Freeze the change objective, canonical source commit, affected book graph, audience profiles, authority, rights, release target, environments, time, and explicit non-claims before substantive work.

Assign stable identities to parts, chapters, sections, sources, passages, claims, mappings, proof targets, tests, schemas, fixtures, figures, reader projections, releases, residuals, corrections, and successor work.
Use `book_structure.json` as canonical part/chapter/source/claim/proof order and `docs/book_outline.md` as the drafting, source-queue, proof-scope, deliverable, and argument-exit control surface.
Ingest public, local, private, connector, conversation-mined, project, and external sources through explicit provenance, permission, source-note, passage-review, readiness, and non-claim states.
Separate source report, author intent, synthesis, experiment, proof, editorial judgment, open question, and machine-generated candidate so none silently inherits another lane's authority.
Atomize material claims and bind each to scope, falsifier, evidence lanes, source mappings, contrary evidence, current disposition, promotion ceiling, residual, and exact consumer surfaces.
Maintain proof targets, theorem dispositions, schemas, fixtures, executable tests, benchmark records, evidence transitions, and non-claims as linked but non-substitutable artifacts.

Express every substantive edit as a change packet naming affected artifacts, reasons, sources, claims, proofs, tests, validations, render, release target, audience, derivative boundary, support effect, residuals, and non-claims.

Regenerate scaffold, proof manifest, source and claim appendices, trust metrics, reader projections, status ledgers, and other declared outputs from canonical state rather than hand-editing projections.
Run schema, semantic, source, claim, proof, executable, negative, mutation, rights, accessibility, render, browser, release, reproducibility, and deployment checks appropriate to the changed surface.

Keep the canonical AI/research book, Human view, reader manuscript, HTML, DOCX, EPUB, PDF, companion, article, image, and audio artifacts as typed derivatives with exact source commit, strip policy, review, approval, evidence effect, and residuals.
Separate local generation, local inspection, accessibility review, editorial approval, release approval, deployment, public observation, immutable archive, and external publication states.

Accept claim or support changes only through exact evidence-transition records; validation, proof compilation, render success, publication, release, citation, or reader polish cannot promote claims alone.
Record positive, negative, null, failed, stale, blocked, deprecated, refuted, reverted, superseded, unpublished, and ready-not-published outcomes with complete denominators and immutable lineage.
Propagate source corrections, claim downgrades, proof retirement, test failure, rights changes, release revocation, derivative drift, and errata through every dependent artifact and public surface.

Monitor source freshness, mapping coverage, claim debt, proof usefulness, validator coverage, projection drift, broken links, accessibility, render and browser regressions, release identity, residual age, maintenance burden, and cost.
Require successor continuity: unfinished work, authority, terminal records, residuals, publication state, and the one active roadmap remain machine-readable across handoff and release.

Expire, replace, archive, or retire transactions and derivatives when source state, claims, proof models, validation, rights, audience, formats, release policy, consumers, environments, or time change materially.
interfaces: Human author intent, editorial judgment, audience needs, rights, accessibility, publication approval, and terminal governance retain human authority outside automated maintenance.
Source Inventory, source notes, passage reviews, raw caches, connectors, local projects, Appendix A, Appendix G, and external-source appendix supply provenance and readiness.
Book manifest, outline, chapter sources, figures, appendices, glossary, citations, and changelog supply canonical authored structure and historical narrative.
Claim registry, claim reviews, Appendix C, Evidence States, transition records, contrary evidence, residuals, and non-claims supply semantic support control.
Proof manifest, Lean modules, proof adequacy reviews, Appendix E, schemas, fixtures, validators, benchmarks, and result artifacts supply formal and executable boundaries.
Git commits, branches, diffs, tags, immutable archives, release records, citations, checksums, and reproducibility records supply version and release identity.
Quarto, HTML, Human view, browser checks, navigation, search, links, figures, styles, and GitHub Pages supply the canonical public research projection and observed deployment state.
Reader manuscripts, DOCX, EPUB, PDF, companion notes, article synopsis, header image, and audio workspaces supply typed derivatives without equal authority.
Validation registry, CI, supply-chain pins, rights inventory, accessibility checks, deployment attestation, and public-truth validators supply release gates without editorial or claim authority.
Roadmaps, status records, goals, work contracts, Artifact Steward agents, changelog, terminal records, and successor handoffs supply continuous maintenance and ownership.
Literate programming, Jupyter Book, Quarto, NIST AI RMF, frontier governance, HELM, LiveBench, contamination analysis, and OpenUnlearning supply external comparators without certification.
Readers, researchers, AI agents, builders, reviewers, citers, downstream repositories, mirrors, archives, and future editions consume exact projections and correction notices.
invariants: Every publication transaction is scoped to exact book graph, commit, change, sources, claims, proofs, tests, render, audience, derivatives, release target, rights, reviewers, consumers, environments, and time.

Canonical authored source and machine-readable records remain authoritative over generated appendices, dashboards, status prose, reader derivatives, format outputs, mirrors, and public-site projections.
Stable chapter, source, claim, proof, test, release, and derivative identities survive reordering, revision, deprecation, correction, rollback, archive, and successor handoff.
No source passage, citation, test result, proof status, benchmark, render, accessibility result, release, deployment, or external publication is fabricated or inferred from desired completion.
Source report, author intent, synthesis, experiment, proof, editorial judgment, and open question remain distinct lanes with explicit provenance and authority.
Every material claim has exact scope, falsifier, evidence lanes, mappings, contrary evidence, disposition, promotion ceiling, residual, and consumer boundary.
Claims change support only through accepted evidence transitions; source count, citations, green validators, theorem builds, renders, releases, and reader polish never promote by themselves.
Proof targets and declarations retain assumptions, model scope, direct/derived status, consumer, negative cases, adequacy disposition, and empirical non-claims.
Generated scaffold, proof manifest, appendices, trust metrics, reader projections, status ledgers, and release records remain synchronized with canonical inputs or visibly fail closed.
Each derivative names exact source commit, audience, strip or transform policy, included and omitted content, review state, approval, validation, evidence effect, residuals, and non-claims.
Local generation, inspection, accessibility review, editorial approval, release approval, deployment, public observation, archive, and external publication states remain distinct.
Negative, null, failed, stale, blocked, deprecated, refuted, reverted, superseded, unpublished, and ready-not-published outcomes remain visible with complete denominators.
Corrections, source revocations, claim downgrades, proof retirement, failed tests, rights changes, errata, release revocation, and derivative drift propagate to every consumer.
Public release identity binds commit, source tree, render, validation, artifacts, checksums, citation, rights, archive, deployment, observation, and terminal record without rebuild drift.
The public book distinguishes current living truth, immutable historical releases, unpublished local state, blocked formats, and future target artifacts.
Maintenance usefulness, editorial and accessibility quality, failures, latency, reviewer burden, compute, storage, hosting, opportunity cost, residuals, and total cost remain visible together.
Exactly one active roadmap and its authority, unfinished work, residuals, and terminal publication state survive every completion and successor transaction.

Schema validity, proof compilation, test passes, green CI, successful render, browser checks, release activity, and coherent prose establish only their exact boundaries, never chapter truth, capability, safety, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.
failure_modes: Canonical/projection inversion lets generated appendices, dashboards, reader editions, mirrors, status prose, or the deployed site overwrite or conceal canonical source state.
Manifest-outline-chapter-proof drift leaves order, source queues, claims, proof targets, files, appendices, navigation, or releases inconsistent.
Source laundering converts conversation memory, author intent, connector placeholders, local notes, citations, or source summaries into independently supported evidence.
Claim laundering treats source count, passage mappings, proof tags, validators, releases, or polished prose as semantic or empirical support.
Proof theater retains vacuous, assumption-restating, duplicative, unconsumed, misleading, or finite traceability theorems as if they prove chapter mechanisms.
Validation theater optimizes green counts while validators are stale, circular, weak, allow-listed, unconsumed, or unable to falsify the claimed failure.
Release laundering converts local generation, a green build, successful render, tag, deploy, or public URL into approval, accessibility, editorial quality, or claim truth.
Derivative equality lets Human view, reader manuscript, DOCX, EPUB, PDF, article, image, audio, mirror, or archive silently become a second canonical book.
Derivative drift drops claims, sources, uncertainty, negative results, figures, accessibility, rights, corrections, or non-claims during stripping or format conversion.
Latest-release overwrite or mutable deployment erases immutable history, exact citations, prior claims, negative results, errata, or reproducible artifacts.
Stale public truth exposes obsolete release identity, roadmap status, rights, citation guidance, source counts, support states, format readiness, or publication claims.
Editorial automation smooths uncertainty, disagreements, failed proofs, null results, blocked work, author voice, or difficult caveats into fluent unsupported prose.
Accessibility theater treats alt text, keyboard checks, accessibility trees, or automated scans as complete reader accessibility and approval.
Rights or provenance drift publishes unauthorized source material, private payloads, incompatible assets, uncertain ownership, or misleading license claims.
Correction failure leaves stale claims, proofs, citations, derivatives, releases, mirrors, consumers, or downstream reliance after evidence changes.
Maintenance bloat creates duplicate roadmaps, appendices, ledgers, tests, proofs, generated outputs, or historical status surfaces that obscure the current contract.
Successor discontinuity loses active authority, unfinished work, residuals, terminal records, publication state, or exact validation instructions between agents or releases.
Unsupported transfer treats one repository's local hygiene, render pipeline, release process, or maintenance history as proof that the method improves other books, teams, domains, or times.
chapters.minimal_implementation: Sixteen exact source-to-claim mappings; one living-book release-record fixture; a change-packet harness with three valid and six expected-invalid synthetic cases; a reader release-candidate bridge over the current blocked candidate, three additional valid routes, and five expected-invalid controls; manifest/outline/proof synchronization, claim and source appendices, Quarto HTML rendering, Human-view toggle, browser checks, changelog and release records, and an authoritative validation registry; and four proof targets implemented by twenty-one Lean declarations over hand-authored finite manifest, structural-update, release, derivative, change-packet, and reader-route records. These artifacts establish repository, record, build, render, derivative, and release discipline only; they do not prove source interpretation, editorial quality, accessibility approval, chapter truth, reader or audio approval, external reproduction, transfer, or a chapter-core support effect.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature argument-exit campaign must follow a prospectively frozen stream of natural substantive updates—new sources, conflicting evidence, claim promotion and refutation, proof retirement, schema changes, figures, accessibility fixes, corrections, rollbacks, releases, reader derivatives, and successor handoffs—performed by multiple independent human and AI maintainers. It must compare the Evidence-Preserving Publication Transaction with matched static manuscript, wiki, ordinary Git repository, literate-programming, executable-book, docs-as-code, and strongest conventional scholarly publishing workflows under equal content, tooling, time, and review opportunity; inject source revocation, citation error, claim drift, proof bloat, validator weakness, rights conflict, derivative drift, release mismatch, deployment failure, errata, and handoff loss; and jointly measure claim accuracy and calibration, source and proof traceability, error detection, correction latency, reproducibility, rollback, accessibility, reader comprehension, editorial quality, maintenance burden, reviewer time, compute, storage, hosting, opportunity cost, and total cost. Independent maintainers, editors, accessibility reviewers, readers, repositories, institutions, formats, hosting systems, and domains must reproduce and transfer positive, negative, null, narrowed, blocked, and refuted outcomes. Until then the transaction is not proof of superior scholarship, editorial quality, accessibility, reader value, governance effectiveness, external reproducibility, transfer, or SOTA publishing.

chapters.title: Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan
chapters.problem: The book needs a managed research backlog and bibliography plan so new papers can be inserted without destabilizing the architecture.
chapters.insufficient: A pile of sources or ad hoc citations does not tell future writing agents what to load, compare, prove, test, or defer.
chapters.core_claim: Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan owns a research-program-, source-or-gap-, backlog-item-, access-, provenance-, public-safety-, chapter-boundary-, claim-, proof-or-experiment-, deduplication-, evidence-transition-, owner-, next-action-, closure-, consumer-, environment-, and time-specific Research Backlog Admission and Closure Contract: every new paper, local project, missing artifact, conflicting result, proof idea, experiment, reproduction need, correction, or chapter proposal enters through exact intake, triage, assignment, preconditions, blockers, non-claims, and terminal closure before it changes prose or support; no title, citation, source count, inventory row, source note, queue, priority label, backlog size, fixture, theorem, validator, or completed reading task alone establishes citation accuracy, literature completeness, research quality, claim support, reproduction, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.

mechanism: Freeze the research-program purpose, intake window, source and gap classes, chapter graph, authority, access and rights policy, public-safety boundary, consumers, environments, time, and non-claims.

Assign stable identities to every source, version, passage, citation, author, organization, project, gap, backlog item, claim, chapter decision, proof target, experiment, reproduction, residual, correction, owner, action, and closure.

Inventory candidate material before use and record provenance, bibliographic metadata source, acquisition path, access state, storage policy, checksum or version, permission, public-safety state, and canonical source note.

Classify intake as unread, inventoried, cached, source-noted, mapped, passage-reviewed, integrated, tested, mechanized, reproduced, deprecated, refuted, inaccessible, private, rejected, duplicate, superseded, or retired without collapsing states.

Separate Corben-authored or supplied materials, recovered projects, local projects, conversation-mined intent, external literature, benchmark artifacts, proof artifacts, private evidence, and missing sources across exact appendix and storage boundaries.
Extract source thesis, mechanisms, evidence, failures, affected chapters, candidate claims, contrary evidence, citation data, and explicit limits before drafting or support use.

Triage each item to update an existing chapter, propose a distinct new owner, route to an appendix, create proof or experiment work, defer for access or verification, reject as duplicate or out of scope, or retire with rationale.
Apply the distinct-interface test and merge/split policy so terminology novelty, source count, project lineage, or author identity does not create anthology chapters.
Bind every chapter assignment to exact boundary rationale, source refs, affected atoms, non-claims, dependencies, alternative owners, insertion or merge decision, and reviewer authority.

Create proof, executable, empirical, causal, reproduction, transfer, and normative sub-backlogs with exact artifacts, baselines, controls, commands, environments, metrics, acceptance, costs, and stopping rules.

Record required pre-drafting work and evidence-transition preconditions so reading, note creation, mapping, coding, theorem writing, experiment activity, or citation addition cannot promote support alone.
Deduplicate editions, variants, mirrors, exports, overlapping papers, project snapshots, repeated claims, and superseded records while preserving all lineage and disagreements.
Verify direct citations, authorship, title, venue, year, DOI or URL, version, passage support, retraction or correction state, rights, and current availability before public citation.

Rehearse intake and closure on natural new papers and project artifacts, including conflict, inaccessible sources, private data, duplicate claims, negative results, corrections, retractions, and rejected chapter proposals.
Accept closure only when the declared action, artifact, review, citation, chapter change, evidence transition or no-change decision, residual, cost, and non-claims are recorded and consumed.

Propagate source corrections, retractions, access loss, rights changes, mapping errors, claim downgrades, proof retirement, failed reproduction, and chapter decisions through every dependent artifact and consumer.

Monitor unread and stale sources, unverified citations, mapping gaps, duplicate rate, backlog age, blocked access, orphan items, closure quality, claim impact, reviewer burden, compute, storage, opportunity cost, and total research cost.

Expire, replace, archive, or retire items and contracts when sources, versions, citations, chapter boundaries, claims, proof models, evidence, rights, consumers, environments, or time change materially.
interfaces: Human author intent, research priorities, affected-party concerns, rights, editorial judgment, chapter ownership, rejection, and terminal closure retain human authority.
Source inventory, raw caches, source notes, connectors, local projects, checksums, permissions, and acquisition records supply canonical intake identity and access state.

Appendix G owns Corben's corpus, supplied materials, recovered records, and local projects; Appendix H owns external literature; Appendix I preserves author intent and lineage without evidence authority.
Book manifest, outline, chapters, source queues, citation files, bibliography, glossary, figures, appendices, and changelog consume accepted assignment and insertion decisions.
Claim registry, claim reviews, Appendix C, evidence transitions, contrary evidence, residuals, deprecations, refutations, and non-claims consume exact source mappings without automatic promotion.
Proof manifest, Lean, proof adequacy, schemas, fixtures, executable tests, benchmarks, empirical studies, causal analyses, and reproduction artifacts consume sub-backlogs.
Artifact Graphs, supply chain, data engines, weight custody, security, rights, privacy, provenance, retention, deletion, and replay constrain stored research artifacts.
Prototype Roadmap, Living Book Methodology, Artifact Steward agents, work contracts, goals, roadmaps, status, terminals, and successors supply ownership and execution continuity.
Project Theseus, Circle, VIEA, SCF, VCM, PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, RMI, CGS, GenesisCode, Simulation Scaling, and MoECOT supply internal or local research lineage without result inheritance.
GraphRAG, HippoRAG, MMLU, GPQA, LiveBench, Lean, MoE surveys, and future external sources supply comparison and challenge queues without completed synthesis claims.
Release, publication, citation, reader, article, archive, public site, and correction surfaces consume verified bibliographic and claim changes under exact rights and state boundaries.
Researchers, readers, AI agents, reviewers, citers, implementers, evaluators, downstream repositories, institutions, and future editions consume source and backlog projections and correction notices.
invariants: Every backlog contract is scoped to exact research program, source or gap, version, access, provenance, public-safety state, chapters, claims, work lanes, owner, consumers, environments, and time.
No unread, inaccessible, title-only, connector-placeholder, private, missing, unverified, retracted, or merely cited source supports a public claim.
Bibliographic metadata is copied from inspected authoritative records or marked unknown; title, author, venue, year, DOI, URL, and version are never inferred for completion.

Corben-authored or supplied material, local projects, conversation intent, external literature, benchmark artifacts, proofs, private evidence, and missing sources remain distinct provenance and appendix classes.

Source lifecycle states remain distinct and monotone only through recorded transitions; inventory, note, mapping, passage review, integration, testing, mechanization, and reproduction are not synonyms.
Every source-derived material claim cites exact inspected passages or equivalent artifacts and preserves source limits, contrary evidence, disagreement, and scope.
Every assignment names an existing chapter or a proposed distinct owner that passes the interface test; terminology or source novelty alone cannot create a chapter.
Duplicate, variant, mirror, export, snapshot, overlapping, superseded, corrected, and retracted relationships preserve lineage and prevent independent-evidence double counting.
Backlog activity, reading, note creation, mapping, citation addition, code, proof, experiment, reproduction attempt, or closure count never changes support without an accepted evidence transition.
Proof, executable, empirical, causal, reproduction, transfer, and normative sub-backlogs retain distinct artifacts, baselines, controls, metrics, acceptance, costs, and non-claims.
Private or permission-gated sources never leak raw text, data, prompts, tests, solutions, credentials, personal information, or restricted artifacts onto public surfaces.
Negative, null, conflicting, failed, inaccessible, deferred, rejected, duplicate, retracted, deprecated, refuted, superseded, and retired items remain visible with complete denominators.
Closure requires an executed declared action, artifact, review, decision, residual, cost, consumer acknowledgement, and non-claims; status labels and task closure are insufficient.
Corrections, retractions, access loss, rights changes, mapping errors, claim downgrades, proof retirement, failed reproduction, and boundary changes propagate to every consumer.
Current citation state distinguishes latest source versions, historical versions, corrections, retractions, mirrors, inaccessible records, and local snapshots.
Research usefulness, novelty, error discovery, closure quality, failures, latency, reviewer burden, compute, storage, opportunity cost, residuals, and total cost remain visible together.
Every synthesis or result is bounded to exact sources, versions, passages, tasks, artifacts, models, evaluators, institutions, infrastructures, legal regimes, environments, and time.

Finite inventory, note, mapping, triage, fixture, theorem, validator, citation, and backlog counts establish only their exact administrative boundaries, never research completeness, claim truth, reproduction, or SOTA.
failure_modes: Citation drift points a claim to the wrong source, version, passage, author, venue, DOI, URL, correction, retraction, or access state.
Bibliographic hallucination fills missing metadata, passages, results, permissions, or relationships because a record looks incomplete.
Unread-source laundering promotes titles, abstracts, citations, snippets, connector placeholders, source-note summaries, or secondary descriptions as inspected support.
Inventory theater treats source counts, notes, mappings, passage refs, backlog size, or appendix coverage as research quality or literature completeness.
Anthology drift creates chapters one paper, project, author, metaphor, benchmark, or terminology variant at a time without a distinct owner or interface.
Duplicate-evidence inflation counts versions, mirrors, exports, snapshots, survey references, project notes, and overlapping papers as independent corroboration.
Source-result inheritance imports benchmark, model, proof, runtime, safety, governance, capability, or deployment claims without local reproduction or accepted literature evidence.
Private-evidence leakage publishes restricted source text, artifacts, prompts, tests, data, credentials, personal information, or unreleased project results.
External-reference laundering cites a reference named inside another source without direct metadata, passage, correction, retraction, and availability verification.
Triage theater assigns a plausible chapter or action without inspecting boundaries, alternatives, duplicates, conflicts, required work, or support effects.
Backlog rot accumulates stale, orphaned, ownerless, blocked, superseded, unactionable, or costless promises that no consumer uses.
Activity laundering treats fetching, reading, noting, mapping, coding, proving, experimenting, reviewing, or closing a task as a positive research result.
Negative and conflict suppression drops null results, failed reproductions, inaccessible sources, rejected proposals, retractions, contradictions, and no-change decisions.
Closure laundering marks an item done without consumed artifact, review, citation or chapter decision, evidence transition or no-change record, residual, cost, and non-claims.
Correction failure leaves stale citations, mappings, claims, proofs, chapters, appendices, derivatives, releases, and downstream reliance after a source changes.
Priority capture lets novelty, prestige, recency, hype, author proximity, easy access, or agent preference crowd out impact, uncertainty, risk, and tractable falsification.
Research overhead and false deferral consume time, reviewer attention, compute, storage, and opportunity without improving source truth, claims, proofs, or decisions.
Unsupported transfer treats one book's source graph, backlog workflow, local corpus, or successful intake as proof of completeness or research quality in other domains, institutions, or times.
chapters.minimal_implementation: Twenty-eight exact source-to-claim mappings; a source inventory and source-note corpus split across Corben/local, external, and author-intent appendices; one research-backlog record schema and public-safe fixture; one new-paper triage scenario fixture covering update-existing, propose-new-owner, defer-unread-external, and reject-duplicate decisions; source inventory, source-note, protocol, appendix, citation, and mapping validators; two proof targets implemented by four Lean declarations over hand-authored finite source-ingestion and chapter-assignment records; and an explicit active backlog including citation normalization, artifact reproduction, proof and experiment work, GraphRAG/HippoRAG overlap, and rejected or blocked items. No external-literature completeness, citation-accuracy study, natural intake campaign, real backlog-closure study, research-quality result, independent reproduction, transfer, or chapter-core support effect exists.
chapters.beyond_state_of_art: The mature argument-exit campaign must prospectively freeze a long-running natural research stream of new papers, local projects, inaccessible and private sources, conflicting findings, corrections and retractions, benchmark and proof artifacts, reproduction attempts, negative results, duplicate or overlapping claims, and chapter proposals handled by multiple independent human and AI researchers. It must compare the Research Backlog Admission and Closure Contract with matched reference-manager, systematic-review, evidence-map, issue-tracker, wiki, ordinary bibliography, ad hoc agent-research, and strongest conventional research-program workflows under equal sources, access, tools, time, and review opportunity; inject metadata errors, title-only sources, duplicate variants, hidden conflicts, private leakage pressure, stale citations, retractions, boundary ambiguity, chapter novelty pressure, failed reproduction, correction propagation, and backlog rot; and jointly measure citation precision and recall, source coverage and calibration, decision and claim accuracy, duplicate detection, error and conflict discovery, time-to-usable-evidence, closure quality, false admission and deferral, correction latency, reproduction yield, chapter churn, reviewer burden, compute, storage, opportunity cost, residuals, and total cost. Causal ablations must identify useful and harmful record fields or gates, and independent researchers, reviewers, repositories, institutions, disciplines, legal regimes, languages, and time periods must reproduce and transfer positive, negative, null, narrowed, blocked, and refuted results. Until then the contract is not proof of literature completeness, citation accuracy, research quality, superior prioritization, claim truth, reproduction, transfer, or SOTA research management.

codex_tests.name: Source-derived claim evidence gate
codex_tests.purpose: Check that a source-derived claim without a source note or ingested-source artifact is rejected at the finite record layer.
codex_tests.result_status: passes via Lean source-record negative case; no citation-accuracy or source-interpretation claim
codex_tests.status: implemented in AsiStackProofs.BibliographyPlan; no citation-accuracy claim

codex_tests.name: New-source assignment gate
codex_tests.purpose: Check that an accepted new-source assignment cannot target a nonexistent chapter id.
codex_tests.result_status: passes via Lean nonexistent-chapter negative case; no real new-paper triage-quality claim
codex_tests.status: implemented in AsiStackProofs.BibliographyPlan; no new-paper triage-quality claim

codex_tests.name: New-paper triage scenario
codex_tests.purpose: Check whether a synthetic intake case updates an existing chapter, proposes a new boundary, defers unread external literature, or rejects a duplicate variant without changing support state.
codex_tests.result_status: validated locally
codex_tests.status: implemented by protocol validation; validated locally

proof_targets.target: A source-derived claim requires a source note or equivalent ingested-source artifact.
A new source cannot be assigned to a non-existent chapter id.
